Right location, in one of the liveliest restaurants and entertainment areas, amazing bed, very clean, fully equipped, with nice balconies and all the hot spots at your feet. I highly recommend it!

We enjoyed our stay at Keramos! It was really quiet, clean and comfortable and the Kitchenette was well equipped. The room was connected to a communal patio which was a really great place to relax. The hotel was relatively close to the centre of Athens, Plaka and the Acropolis. Despite being close to the centre, the neighbourhood was really quiet. The key entry system was interesting and required an app on your phone, so make sure you have plenty of battery life when out and about. Overall, this was a great place and we would definitely stay again when visiting Athens!

We stayed here for 3 nights on the second week of July 2023. I actually had a lot of requests for changing our stay dates since our schedule changed a lot since I made the first booking, and they were very accommodating with all our schedule requests. Prior to our arrival they gave out detailed instructions on finding the place and checking in. We arrived in Athens too early for check-in, and they had a procedure for storing your luggage on your own, which we really appreciated. After a hot day walking around Athens we finally checked-in (an easy process) and we were very pleasantly surprised at how nice the room was. Everything we conceivably needed was provided, and we could cook and do laundry with no problems, should we wish to do so. The detergent tablet already preloaded into the dishwasher was a thoughtful touch, as well as having detergent available for the laundry washer. The body wash and shampoo provided were commercial brands (as opposed to the bulk types usually provided in hotels) which I really appreciated. The room was spacious, and they prepared the sofa as a bed for my daughter which she really appreciated. The living room/sofa bed arrangement included curtains so that the living room can be treated as a bedroom with privacy. The automated front desk also allowed printing wirelessly, which I thought was a really great touch as we needed to print some travel documents, and I have not encountered that type of service before without having to go through a person or even paying extra for the privilege. We had a great time staying here, and would stay here again should I have an opportunity to go to Athens again.

Several restaurants and at least one bakery and pastry shop was in the neighborhood, and the nearest metro station was only 15 minutes away by foot.

Well-designed, convenient, digital key, tourist guides, all you need for a few nights stay — at a very good price! Only thing I didn’t like was the water pressure in the shower was a bit weak.

Bit far from the main tourist area but there are good bars and fast food places nearby.

It was such a pleasant stay! We loved the beautiful and calm apartment, the area (so vivid, filled with cool places, music and nice people). Staff was really friendly and responded super fast to all our questions. Next time in Athens, the place to stay is already clear: KERAMOS Athens :) Thanks a lot for having us!

Excellent choice for my firts visit to Athens.
The studio was exactly as in the photos and had everything needed (even a washer-dryer!).  The bed was comfy.

The neighborhood was very animated, with nice restaurants and clubs,  close enough to the major tourist sites even by foot. The host was very helpfull and wellcoming.

Will definetly stay there again if I visit Athens again in the future.

Amazing Apartment. Great for 5 people (1 couple and three adults). The balcony had amazing views and the place became home for the 9 nights we booked. All beds were comfy enough and we were supplied with everything we need. Definetly reccomend 💃
Very nice cozy spot. Room was very clean, and if you get a chance to meet Hermes, consider yourself lucky, because he is the best!
Very clean and comfortable apartment with all comforts. Not the best location aesthetically but within an easy walk from the central attractions.
Very clean space with a fantastic view of the Acropolis. Bright, spacious, with parking available and quick access to central spots for nightlife in the city. Also suitable for work on the PC.
Great apartment, clean and quiet. I enjoyed the Acropolis view :)
We loved our stay — Great mattresses, great location, convenient to everything!
We had an amazing experience and found the house extremely clean. Very close to the center.
Very clean and modern house, full of natural light and amenities! Ideal for big groups. Just few minutes walking away from the metro station and the sweet Iraklio Square. We'll book again :)
Clean and Quiet. Friendly host and all the amenities you need. We had a great stay!

This place is a work in progress. A/C doesn’t cover much of the house. TV doesn’t work, remotes all dead. No basic bathroom products or shower curtains (so water sprays all over the place). Not enough water glasses. Crappy, rough towels. Overall, sub-par accommodations for the price.

Amazing villa right in the middle of Athens. Celebrated my birthday with a group of friends and had a private chef over. The house with private lift is massive with a nice garden, lots of space, great kitchen, 4 bathrooms and 1 extra toilet. Great to host a group of friends or a large family. Easy to explore Athens from this central location.
Excellent facility.  Central location, easily accommodated five of us.
Beautiful place but an airbnb not a hotel
Very nice service although the nearby area may not be very ideal.

My friends and I had a fantastic stay at Bespoke. It’s very centrally located, close to many restaurants & cafes. We stayed in 2 different rooms for our stay, and both were well-furnished, neat and squeaky clean! Konstantinos, the owner, is such a gentleman and he was very pleasant to interact with and was always responsive. He even helped arrange taxis for us to & from the airport & the port. Highly recommend this for a stay in Athens.

Amazing place… especially the apartment with 2 levels, children love it. Extra clean and roomy. Hosts are kind, generous and friendly…provided lots of recommendations for restaurants and bars. The place is well located, so practically we did need public transportation to get to city center and site seeings. Also, one of the best advantage of the apartment is private parking that is well secured. Having small kitchen with coffee machine, kettler, roster and refrigerator. But no cooking appliances. On the other hand there are plenty of pleasant places around the apartment where you could eat.

Wonderful rooms and apartments, the host was very kind and friendly!
The rooms are very clean and the ac is wonderful.
10/10
I'll definitely come back.

Konstantinos was super organised and offering to assist with transport arrangements. Very understanding of our late arrival time and self-check-in was very easy. The room was very clean and comfortable, mattresses felt great. The air conditioning works well. We would definitely stay here again. Thank you!

Great rooms with everything you need and a fun design. Location is also great, since you're in the middle of the city. Parking (for €7,- a day) is next to the building, and guarded by a gate, so the rental car was safe from busy streets of Athens.

Beautiful accommodation, recently renovated. It includes everything you can expect, even coffee and other things for breakfast. Disadvantages, the room has little natural light. The location can be improved, not only because of the distance to the historic center but also because of the surroundings.

It's a good option.

In a central but quiet location. Located in a small dead-end alley and therefore without traffic. Very nice and well furnished room. Masonry kitchenette. Well equipped. Small but nice bathroom.
Not suitable for disabled people as there are steps.

The worst experience we have ever had. He sells a “luxury” apartment and there is no paper to clean, brooms to sweep or cloths to wash. The rooms are located at the bottom of a dark and dirty passage, there is no attention from the owner upon arrival or in-person induction, just a WhatsApp with instructions.
And if we talk about breakfast, a shame. The cheapest bread with ham, jam and egg. If you don't ask him to replenish, he won't bring anything for the next day and you'll be left without breakfast... Luxury is no match for the locations (dirty streets, homeless people and little commerce)

A charming, very clean place with complete equipment. Nice location close to the center, many places nearby where you can eat dinner and drink wine in the evening. An interesting solution with ingredients you use to make your own breakfast - there could be a little more bread. The perfect place for a trip to Athens.
Although it is not the most central, but there are centers of interest nearby, new apartments equipped with everything, the host is available 24/7 for everything, he even helped us plan the trip

The apartment was amazing and spotless, completely brand new
The gym was well equipped and the pool was with a wonderful view of all Athens, the cherry on the cake. The apartment itself has a pretty big yard ,the beds are very comfortable even the pillows and the bathrooms are very nice and they leave you a shampoo a shower gel and a body lotion there in the 2 bathrooms. The hotel is completely new and the favourite part of my daughter was the main technological door😆

Terrible location, unsafe pool.
On arrival our taxi driver warned us not to be out in this area of Athens after dark because of “drug dealers and homeless people”. Being a 30min walk from the tourist areas this would have been difficult. After check in  we went to view the pool as we were travelling with children who were excited to use it. The pool and area  were totally unsafe with lights hanging off in the pool, missing tiles, broken glass on the floor and razor sharp exposed metal on the very few broken “loungers” available. We immediately complained checked out and found alternate accommodation. On contacting the holiday provider we were told we couldn’t have a re-fund as it’s outside of hotel policy. I’m not sure maiming their guests in an unsafe pool should be part of their policy either.

EDIT: In response to the owners comment below.
It’s also not right to call someone who you took money from but didn’t stay a liar.
Why would I lie? It cost me £500 to book an alternative hotel at no notice.
I don’t know the area but that’s what the taxi driver told us, a few times.
I saw the pool with my own eyes.
If this wasn’t true I would have just stayed.

Our stay at Athens Mosaico Suites & Apartments was an absolute fiasco. We've never felt the urge to escape a hotel as urgently as we did upon setting foot in this place. When we asked to cancel our booking and get our money back, the reception showed zero flexibility, effectively trapping us in this dump.

The photos you'll find online are a masterclass in deceit. Crafted from the most misleading angles, they create a false narrative so far removed from the grim reality that it's practically fraudulent. Walking into the actual rooms was a slap in the face—grimy, under-equipped, and wholly disappointing.

Now, let's talk about the so-called 'breakfast.' Forget a hot meal or even a continental spread; all you get are breakfast boxes filled with inedible slop. It's as if they deliberately sourced the worst food imaginable. Imagine eating cardboard—now make that cardboard taste worse, and you'll have a pretty good idea of what to expect.

The rooftop pool? Another massive letdown. It's small, filthy, and the limited seating area is so cramped you’d be better off standing. It’s practically a glorified puddle.

In summary, this has been one of the worst hotel experiences of our lives. If you value your time, money, and general well-being, avoid Athens Mosaico Suites & Apartments like the plague.

DO NOT STAY HERE! We booked this hotel via Expedia because of the good reviews. I'm now certain those were all fake reviews. The hotel itself may be nice but it is dirty and in no way a 4 star hotel! It is located in the worst part of Athens, we had cab drivers refuse to take us to the area. There was a car with broken windows right in front of the main door, homeless and drug addicts everywhere. You can't walk around in that area.
The room itself was nice, but as others have said the shower handle would not stay affixed to the wall which made showering a pain.
The power went out the first two nights, which also meant no AC or hot water. However, power was on throughout the rest of the neighborhood.
As far as cleanliness... upon our arrival we found feminine hygiene wrappers, old socks, soiled towels and foreign papers in our room. Two of our pillows didn't have pillow cases and one comforter was heavily stained with what looked to be dried blood. They never once cleaned our room, despite our countless requests to multiple people, and the request to clean sign on our door! Every time we inquired about it we were told they would get to us... never once did they! At checkout the person behind the desk said he wasn't the right person to complain to....
Breakfast is served via a third party, they don't have room services, in brown takeout boxes and didn't really taste good. For coffee we have a coffee machine in the room but no pods... They wanted to charge us 5 euros for a pack of pods.
And for the cherry on top... The pool, which they keep showing in all photos, is a joke, in need of serious repair. The pool lights were dangling on the bottom of the pool, and tiles were missing all over. When we told the front desk about the hazards in the pool area, which by the way only consists of 5 chairs, we were told to not worry as they pop right back on ....
PLEASE avoid this place!

The rooms are old the shower heads don’t stay up. The lifts are so slow no service lifts so if the staff are using them you will be waiting for ages. But above all the service is so bad. No one wants to help they just sit behind there desk. Could not even give me a hand to take my bags up as I had 3 kids with me. Location is not close to anything. The only saving grace is the view from the top room. The pool view is fantastic. But the pool is a lot smaller then the pictures. If there is 5 people up there then you don’t have much to swim or sit. I would not recommend this place at all. Stuff are so rude

We booked through Booking, but the description wasn’t entirely accurate, and the photos were mixed up. Due to a lack of transparency, we ended up renting a small room without a kitchen, featuring a King-size bed and another small bed. That was it.

The website states that reception is open until 6 PM. We arrived on a Sunday at 3 PM, and it was closed, so we had to check in online. The same thing happened at check-out; I went down to let them know we were leaving at 9 AM, but no one was at the reception.

The street is incredibly noisy, and the neighborhood is quite unattractive.

On the positive side, the apartment is very clean, and the reception staff is friendly.

I spent 5 days, arrived at 2:00 pm on a Saturday, and left Wednesday morning.

The room is clean, extremely clean actually.
I had a nice view from my room to the city and the acropolis.
Coffee, tea, water, fridge and a water heater were provided.
Towels and shower gels were also provided. 🙏🏻🙏🏻

Location from a distance perception is really good, it is 10 to 15 minutes walking distance from Gazi, Plaka and other areas.
However there were lots of homeless and junky people around the neighborhood, could be intimidating for solo travelers.

The receptionist was not friendly honestly.. he printed me a paper and told me here are the instructions, read them. Before looking at the paper, I simply asked if there is a card for the room, and he was like… “READ the INSTRUCTIONS.. we use codes not card” with a tone 🤣🤣🤣 I  have been traveling for 6 hours or more, tired and hungry, I don't feel like reading 2 pagers with an upside down print .. sorry ☺️
would have been totally fine for the receptionist to take it easy and just briefly explain the system… anyways ..
all in all my stay was good .. Thank you 🙏🏻🇬🇷❣️

The bald receptionist needs a serious attitude adjustment: rude, then tries to gaslight you to put it his provocative attitude back on you. Eventually stonewalled me when I asked to speak to his manager. Find someone competent to work with people.
The apartment was a great choice—big, clean, and had everything we needed for a short stay. The staff were helpful, and the location is convenient, near bus and metro stops

Overall a very nice and hospitable experience.
Couldn't ask for more for the price truly nice stay in the city of Athens.
Very simple place to stay. For the price, great for a short stay. The problem for me was the bed, the mattress was so terrible that I didn’t rest properly. Location was very good.

It is very close to the metro station, you can easily go anywhere, it would be better if there were cloth slippers and an iron in the room. There were towels, shampoo and soap. The beds were not very comfortable but you could sleep in. It is a perfect value for money room.
O local dispõe de um qto amplo, mas sugiro colocar uma mesa para trabalhar e uma cadeira.
The owner stops serving customers, totally dissatisfied, new country for me and I did not expect such bad treatment, I could not even enjoy the accommodation.

Metro station is 50m from the hotel, which is very convenient. The rooms are reasonably new and spacious.
Overall a good option for travelers on a budget who are after a 3 stars comfort. I personally wouldn't consider this a 4 stars hotel.
The only disappointing bit is the buffet: the croissants are not fresh, and the coffee is really really bad. If you want to order coffee from the bar, you need to pay extra...

For the price we paid for our holiday it was worth it. The area is not the best but haven't had any trouble. The metro is 30 seconds away from the front door so that's brilliant. Room was very clean. Came with air conditioning that worked well and it was away from you so just leave it on to keep the room cool. Had its own fridge which was surprisingly spacious for a mini fridge, can get big bottle's in it. Wardrobe with a safe in it that you put your own code in and it's own bathroom with shower. Read a review that said it had low water pressure but not had any troubles. If you are on a medium budget then this is a very good choice. Tons of food places around and on 2 stops away from the party district with the 360 bar that has an amazing view of the Acropolis

Small rooms, but no hot water in the shower. Breakfast is okay, better than average. There are no fancy views from the rooms. Neighborhood may be a bit unattractive, but it is safe in a way if you take your precautions. Staff is super helpful and always kind. The hotel is really close to the metro station, and going to Akropolis would take you max 20 minutes. Good value for money.

I booked a trip to Greece in this hotel in 2020, canceled because of the pandemic obviously. Now in July 2023, I am still asking for my refund. I call every week, and send emails with all the information, and still, no one has got back to me with a real solution. Won't recommend a hotel that doesn't refund their customers correctly and keep their money.

We had a great stay here this week. Rooms were extremely clean and nicely decorated. The bed was one of the comfiest I have slept in!
The staff were very helpful and friendly. The food in the restaurant was tasty and not very expensive.
Our stay was quiet and comfortable, and the easy access to the metro meant we were just far enough away from the centre but could get there quickly if needed.
I would definitely stay here again. Thank you for a lovely experience.

The accommodation at Athens City Themis is highly recommended. I have already had the pleasure of staying in two of the apartments in Athens. These accommodations are just right for those who want to stay comfortably and at a reasonable price. You can expect comfortable and clean beds, spacious bathrooms and a well-stocked kitchen that leaves nothing to be desired. In particular, the apartment near the Panepistimio metro station has a large, covered balcony that invites you to linger at any time of day. Each apartment is also very centrally located, making it an ideal starting point for tourist excursions. In addition, the areas can be described as family-friendly and quiet. I'm really convinced and would be happy to come back...otherwise I wouldn't have taken the time to write this review. Of course you should also leave positive feedback on Google, and not just use the comment function to get rid of your frustration.
Greetings, Tobias
Apartment is clean, good location and host is very friendly and communicates really well. Would recommend!👍

nice apartment, close to the metro station and the Museum and other tourist attractions.  Friendly owner, we could leave our luggage at an earlier hour.  I recommend it even though not everyone will like the Omonia district.
The toilet doesn't take toilet paper. the top bunk of the bunk bed almost fell apart (it was missing screws). Not that clean. Very mid
Nice place and so quite place you can’t be disturbed like hotels to night and you can sleep relax .

place_name: Confy Living Apartment Metaxourghio metro Station
review_text:
Charming space with thoughtful touches, immaculate cleanliness, and a host who goes above and beyond to ensure a delightful stay. The attention to detail and cozy ambiance made our Airbnb experience truly exceptional.
Conveniently located at the city center and easy access to the subway.
5⭐️

place_name: Confy Living Apartment Metaxourghio metro Station
review_text: A Very clean and beautifull apartment, located very close to metro station.

place_name: Confy Living Apartment Metaxourghio metro Station
review_text: Very warm apartment, with all the comforts for a short stay. Very close to everything and literally next to the metro! Mr. Alexandros was very helpful and answered all our requests

place_name: Confy Living Apartment Metaxourghio metro Station
review_text: Beautiful and clean room, Bravo

place_name: Confy Living Apartment Metaxourghio metro Station
review_text: Good location

This is review about failed stay. I had a made in advance, prepaied and approved  booking in these apartments. In 20 minutes before check in time I have received notification from booking service that my staying is not possible. As compensation they offered me overnight in hotel with not good customer reviews located in not safety city district, occupied with homeless people and addicted peoples. After my refusal and asking for other options they just stopped to answer me. At the end I was forced to find new hotel by myself. I wasted 2 hours of my time and 100 euro more as it is difficult to find new place immediately.

It is clear that accident situations are possible. But the way how apartments customer service solve it is very bad. I even do not know will my prepayment will be turned me back or no.

We spent 3 nights here, it is a historical architecture, but newly decorated. The room is spacious, clean, the bed is soft, we slept well, although it is noisy outside due to the traffic. Earplugs are provided, but we didn't use it.The location is good, just 30 min walk to Acropolis, but you have to pass several blocks which seems not so safe at night. I think it is good value for money.

A perfect apartment in a well-maintained and beautiful building, the owner is charming, welcoming and helpful with every request, including perfect recommendations!  A little far from the center there is a metro 200 meters from the room and a 10 minute walk from the center.  It is highly recommended!
The apartment at del taso 1850 was very nice and comfy. The building is a wonderful neoclassical, such a great view! Athens City center was 10 minutes by walk!
Good value clean and fabulous hosts
Nice view! Excellent location!

We only booked a day room for our last day in Athens, and it provided everything we needed. The room was okay, had air conditioning (although it was weak, but it made the room definitely cooler and more comfortable than the outside temperature). The bathtub was leaking a little, but it wasn't a huge issue. The location of the hotel is great - close to metro station and will make it easy for you to tourist around.
As there's no 24-hour reception, you might need to wait a bit at the door when you get there. After all, you get what you pay for and this offers good value for the price.

This is the worst hotel in the world. I was supposed to stay one night but I couldn’t because this hotel is horrible. Nothing worked, air conditioning was broken, safe box didn’t work, the bathroom had a leak in the ceiling and if you go out you must leave the room key. But the worst of all was the owner or the manager, a rude man. He insulted me and almost hit me because I told him the safe box was not working so I couldn’t leave the room keys.
I should have reported him to the police but I didn’t have enough time to do it cause I had a flight next morning.

Please don’t stay in this hotel!!!! The owner/manager is very dangerous

Not everybody's experience is the same, but I had a wonderful visit here. The room was adequate, with a nice open air balcony.  The service friendly, and the location was perfect to go down and visit the shops, museums, and sites.

first off picture on the net is photo shopped does not look anything like the picture. The only thing I liked about the hotel was the location and the fact that we had a balcony.  there are alot of great restaurants in walking distance especially Alexander the GREAT Restaurant which I highly recommend. breakfast was horrible for me. I don't know what kind of milk they served but it was not moo milk🤮🤮. they advertised a restaurant but was not opened. the owners were really friendly and very helpful 🙂

We stayed in the hotel for 7 nights. The hotel has a wonderful sphere. Our room wasn't big, but cozy, with lovely interior details. There was a bathtub in the bathroom, of a very comfortable shape. A small mini-fridge, so we could keep there berries and yogurt.  A lovely balcony with 2 chairs.   Comfortable mattresses on old stile beds,  sleeping there was nice. Air-co worked properly. Wi-fi worked perfectly everywhere. The owners/ personnel are friendly and helpful. After mentioning that I'd like to have tea in the room, the lady owner suggested to lend me a kettle for the room, and she lent us also a knife. I felt there at home, and would stay there again.

A very nice and modern hotel. We had a beautiful room with a lot of space. It took us 15 minutes to walk to Plaka.
But the best thing is the breakfast. You get a huge selection of hot, cold, sweet and savoury dishes with three different types of bread. Super! We would stay there again at any time.
Lily+Adam
One of the worst experiences I have ever had in a hotel. Ruined my vacation. The reception staff either wasn't there or, when they were, were very rude and unhelpful. The rooms where dirty and noisy.

Lengthy and unusual hotel credit card verification process with extensive communication via WhatsApp on top of my booking.com reservation. My creditcard was already updated and accepted by booking.
No problem with them, only a unusual payment procedure by the hotel sending verification links via WhatsApp, thats not a very serious behaviour! I cancelled my reservation.

The hotel is brand new and the room with kitchen has a reasonable size comfortable for two people. Particularly, I don’t like the bathroom open to the room, but this is more a personal note. The hotel has only service during the business hours what I like because you have your own privacy during night and the hotel is fully check-in contactless, which is a great thing. Breakfast is simple but with all the essentials you need, including protein options. Location is strategically close to the subway and with easy access anywhere in Athens. The negative point is the acoustic, which is really disappointing for a new construction like this one. They should change the hotel category for “Adults Only” which would prevent guests having kids noisy disturbing you during the stay, specially because the hotel is a young oriented design, so being in this category would certainly attract more couples and singles. They also should open the Spa premises for all guests without being necessary to book an overpriced massage.

Firstly, you have to remember that this building is located in a very suburban area of the city. Although modern, luxurious it is not.
Personal classification would not be a hotel, more serviced studio apartments.
Clean, functional rooms consisting of open plan bedroom/kitchen with separate toilet and shower room divided by a glass sliding wall.
Everything it provided for your stay and housekeeping comes with fresh towels every other day.
You are located with a residential area with a bar/restaurant opposite that does have live music and a street market every Wednesday.....so if it's peace and quiet your after, then I would suggest elsewhere.
Positives - Easy access to all forms of public transport. Modern feel including keyless entry. Price per night
Negatives - Rooftop bar not used. No dining area as breakfast is brought to your room making it very sterile.
Full time reception facilities unavailable (office hours only)

This establishment is a genuinely nice place with a lot to offer. The rooms are not only beautiful but also provide a sense of peace that's highly appreciated when traveling. Its location is superb, being conveniently close to both the train and bus stations, making it an ideal choice for those on the move.

However, there are some areas for improvement. The window blinds could be better, as they don't block out light effectively. The bed's softness might not suit everyone, and the pillow was notably too large and hard for my liking. Additionally, cleanliness under the bed sheet left something to be desired.

On the plus side, the inclusion of unify wifi is a fantastic addition that greatly enhances the overall guest experience.

In summary, this is a beautiful and well-located place that would benefit from some minor adjustments, particularly when it comes to bedding and cleanliness. Despite these shortcomings, it offers a largely pleasant stay.

Excellent apartments in a renovated neoclassical building with a dreamy interior patio! Fully furnished and equipped, stylish and quiet! 3 minutes walking distance from Metaxourgeio metro station. Highly recommended!

The apartment is an oasis of serenity with classy design. Beds are very comfortable and we loved spending time in the interior court.
George is extremely friendly and helpful. The check in is very smooth.
The only drawback is the sketchy neighborhood. It is safe though.

What an amazing place. It's an island of peace in the middle of the chaotic Athens. Great smily host (thanks George!!).
We got room number 2: cozy, very well decorated and spotless. Very nice interior with high ceilings , spacious toilet and very comfy bed (great bed linen). Good AC too.
The inner patio with aromatic plants (rosemary, jasmine, basil and mint) is great to relax.
We don't really understand the issue with the area: it's just a normal neighbourhood with local stuff. Not touristic at all but still with a certain charm. With few bars and groceries stores around, we never had a problem walking from the metro station to the premises (both morning and late evening).
Will definitely come back ;)

Amazing place, the owner has put a lot into making this place feel very elegant, modern and comfortable. All the furnishings are very high quality and I don’t see how anyone could say anything bad about staying here! The bed was extremely comfortable and the room had everything we needed. Location was ideal too. A lot of people said the area was rough, but we did not feel like this or that it was unsafe; it’s just a local neighbourhood and the Wyndham Grand is less than 5 minutes walk. There’s on street parking and the walk to all the tourist sites and restaurants is quite small, about 20 minutes. There’s nice bars and restaurants just around the corner too. When I come back to Athens I will definitely stay here again. Trust my review, as I’m usually very hard to please!

perfect for your "base" in Athens, kitchen with most important facilities, microwave, fridge/freezer, a washing machine with integrated dryer, easy access to nearby metro, bakery and mini market around the corner
Stayed 1 night. Excellent. Modern, very clean, very well organized. Close to train and metro stations. Highly Recommended. Thanks😎👍

AWAY! Honestly, I read the above comments and wonder! 4 girls we went alone, in an alley of Metaxourgeio and very close to Omonia, and it has been called the center of Athens! Only foreigners and prisoners in the neighborhood... fallen on the floor with pinches and not 1 prisoner there were more than 40 gathered together!!!!!! We got to ask if we would have a problem and he said no! Things are very serious there…. Tragic what our eyes saw! Also, the house was dirty... the cleaning lady was supposed to be there for 2 hours because she was cleaning and the bathroom was dirty.. also the atmosphere inside the house was smelly... and most important of all the sheets were not changed! He had hair from the previous ones, blood and crumbs!!!! I do not recommend it to anyone, you are at risk there!

Very good experience to live in this loft. Next to the metro, in the center and in a very quiet neighborhood. It has everything from amenities and everything is new. I will definitely choose it again for my stay in Athens.
Fantastic brand new loft, we were the first to enter. As if it was our own home! The host is perfect. Now whenever I go to Athens I'm not looking for a house...I have my loft

The hotel is a walkable distance from the metro station. There are quite a lot of mini marts also nearby.
The room was a little musty, but the service was really nice. All in all, it was of good value.

Avoid at all cost no value for money even as a two star. This is definitely the worst hotel I have ever experienced.

First of experienced one staff member to be rude in his mannerism, you would have thought he paid me to be here.

Rooms are outdated as well as dirty ants spiders probably bed bugs.

I'm not sure if you will get a good night sleep due to the random noise that occurs from the neighbours.

For what you pay the area is not in the best locations in terms of concerns of safety.

Myself is OK however a lady,  elderly couple, or a family with young children. I'm not so sure if that to be the case.

An unease amount of homeless people and alot of people in large amount of people on drugs down most streets.

Feel as though ripped of the price just not worth it because on a walk out you then come back to a none nice hotel.

Yes you do see alot of nice hotels in the same area but the experience changes if you pay that €10-15 more on a better decent hotel at least you feel comfortable when sleeping.

To clarify this was a stop over however your better of not doing that here.

Not sure what else to mention other than don't come here.

One of the best hotels of the city
I got there last minute with my father and sister and it was great.
What can I say,how cleaner it was ,how quiet it was and the staff was amazing.
The hotel was near the city center. I could walk to many places. I enjoyed my stay.
Definitely going there any time I visit Athens.

Upfront this hotel is probably circa 1970 so presents as “dated” however it was immaculately clean, the beds were very comfortable, air conditioning worked well, staff were exceptionally helpful and it has 24hr reception. We only spent 1 night here arriving at midnight and out by 10am. For its budget price it was very good value. Did find some decent places for food within a short walk.

This review is very much based on price point.

The first thing you should know, this is not a good area at all. For myself, I wouldn't care so much. However, I brought my girlfriend and very much wished I had booked into a more expensive hotel in a better area.

The area was very dirty, full of drug use, and definitely questionable for night walking.

Now on the price point of less than 50€ per night in Athens and only a 20 min walk from the Acropolis, this hotel is great!

The rooms are nice (for the price) and the service is good.
Very nice apartment!

The apartment is very nice and very comfortable!! It doesn't lack detail. Very spacious and complete rooms. And complete bathroom and toilet. The worst thing is the elevator. Only one person can go up, and when we arrived it was damaged by a storm. The area is very good because it is next to a metro station. But I don't recommend walking around the right side.
The modern, sustainable, clean place. With everything for your comfort. Two bathrooms, three rooms with classes, windows to look at Athens and it is close to the places of interest
Great location, everything within walking distance. Clean and tip top
Magnificent

This place deserves way better rating than current 2.6.
Considering the value for money and clear check-in.
Also,yes the neighbourhood is rather sus, but i'm halfway through my stay and haven't been stabbed by a trans hooker or anything yet. So far so good. Also not far from all the touristy stuff, cheap local stuff and public transport. Best of both worlds one might even say. The accommodation itself is decent, clean and maintained. Mattresses are cheap though and stone floors cold under feet.

Look, the rooms and the bathroom are light and clean, the bed was comfortable, the place is affordable, it has a small elevator and it has a door-code system, so you don't have to worry about the keys. But the surroundings are the worst neighbourhood I've ever stayed in. The streets in every direction are full of drug addicts, drunk criminal-like and homeless people. It was not my first time to travel alone in the world, but the first time I was worrying during the night getting back to the apartment. I was not attacked, but I was afraid that I would be. I'm sorry, because I see that the building was well refurbished, but I can't recommend it to anyone.

I had a terrible experience at this place. The room was tiny and reeked of cigarette smell, with an uncomfortable, dirty mattress. The toilet door wouldn’t close properly and would get stuck. Internet and TV were both non-functional, and the staff rarely responded to chats or phone calls. It seemed like they only cared about the money, with absolutely no hospitality at all. Avoid this place! 🙏🏻 DO NOT MAKE MY MISTAKE- MONEY VALUE IS ABSOLUTELY NOT WORTHY HERE

Its terrible place to stay! A very dangerous area, with lots of homeless, drug addict  and alcoholics living on the street! The smell of this neighbour is just horrible, urine and some rotten stuff. They have no luggage storage, so in case of late flight you cannot count on them. The room isn't small, it's ridiculously tiny, and looks more like a cage. And not too clean, I had lots of dust on the floor and I found lots of hair in my bed sheets which did not belong to me. Highly not recommended!!!
the room was nice but very small, and the location did not felt safe. but it's OK for a short stay as it's very affordable

We ate breakfast at this hotel and it was delicious. Coffee excellent too! And the service was amazing! The restaurant was very busy with lots of patrons and the servers were still 'on top of it all. Highly recommend!

The hotel is passable, if you are not expecting any lux. The shower cabin is suitable only for skinny women, so probably you will need to take a shower outside of it. The reception guys are polite and friendly. For the money I paid I guess this is what one can get. It's near the historical centre of Athens (25 min. walking distance) but the area is dodgy, dirty and noisy. I would not recommend to anyone.

Booked a room here and stayed for a week in December 2016. Booked a premium room but when we got to the room it was awful. The door to the balcony was broken. Hair all over the bed. The floor and the bathroom were dirty. Area not great but we knew that as my boyfriend is greek and knows the area and close to the metro. When we went to ask to change rooms the lady at reception told us there were no other deluxe rooms available. She said she had one other room and we asked to go and see it. It was a lot smaller with no balcony but cleaner however the shower didn't stay in position which was annoying but it was an improvement. Our room wasn't cleaned while we were there and when we asked to be refunded the difference of the two rooms we were refused which I personally think is awful seen as it wasn't our fault that we moved in the first place.

The area where's the hotel isn't so sure, especially in the night. The guys at the reception were very nice and they speak a good English. But the room was kind dirty, we've found a wet old towel on the top the bed, just to say. Also the toilet wasn't brilliant in cleaning. The price was low, but also the quality. Nope.

The hotel is really worth staying in. The location is convenient enough. Our room was clean, cozy and had everything you need to have in a hotel room. The staff was very friendly and was always willing to help, if there were any questions. Highly recommend to choose the hotel for your stay to be pleasant.

Place is clean,  spacious with big bathroom and huge tarase to enjoy cold drink after long day. Reception workers are helpful and polite. Room service... not too good, lock your belongs.  Neighbourhood not for single women. Street on the front is quiet indeed, but the next street close to theatre is full of drug users. They gather in huge group. We simply turned left going out from the hotel to avoid them and didn't have any issues, but we didn't feel safe or conformable with them by next street.

Clean, good balcony with table & 2 chairs , bathroom nice but you should not be a big man as it is pretty small .
Area: for us it was OK,close to metro but maybe if you come from quite small village and you are a solo female traveler choose another location , if you come from Berlin 😉its fine .
Very friendly reception ❤.

Hotel with good quality-price ratio, it is not in the center of Athens but you can easily get there by Metro or walking.

Sara at the reception was charming and very friendly. The room was super clean and had a small refrigerator. It is perfect to spend one or two nights in Athens.
Great little hotel. The room was marvellous and all the staff we met were superb! Our room was so clean, comfortable and had a fantastic balcony. Would definitely stay here again!

1. Re location, you must understand that though the hotel is in a dodgy area, many hotels around are in the same area. It is 100 meters from the metro station. And it is very easy to get there.
2. The staff are very nice and welcoming, the breakfast is taking place in the coffee place owned by the hotel, it is prepared for you, and has everything you will need. We really enjoyed it.
3. Lobby and Cafe  are nicely decorated. Good vibe.
3. Room, for the fairness, we got the last available room , and I understand there is a variety of rooms. Sadly, the room was not convenient, not only small but not organized well, old feel, early noises from the hallway (you hear everything, guests walking, cleaning carts, talking), very poor vibe. Good things: it was clean! The shower was good and new, but in a very very small bathroom.

So, as we loved the staff and breakfast, it is positive altogether.

We chose this apartment due to a concert one metro station farther. So it was perfect for us with a lot of nice restaurants directly around.
It's really clean and tidy with a fridge and a microwave. Bathroom is big enough and we were 3 persons.
Only the neighbourhood and the roundabout is pretty noisy, so it's possible you'll have a lack of sleep if you're sensitive.
But, they have different apartments. Our friends apartment wasn't that noisy.
The bed is absolutely comfortable and the price is top! 👍🏽

It was a good experience in connect suites. Good location, clear directions to find the apartment, and the gain access into the building. Lots of restaurants and attractions are nearby. The rooms are spacious, very neat and beautiful. All amenities were working fine. Lift was quite small, so I had to make multiple trips with my luggage.
Overall, it was a very good experience.

I'm utterly appalled by our experience at this property. We booked a 4-night stay based on the blatant lie on booking.com that promised a separate bedroom and living room. Traveling with a baby and an elderly family member, it's not a luxury to ask for the separate spaces that we booked and paid for; it's a necessity.

To our shock, we were met with a dingy studio apartment that was nowhere close to what was promised. To add insult to injury, the room's condition was downright disgusting. The linen box in the bed was filthy, suggesting a complete disregard for basic hygiene. The A/C was malfunctioning as well, leaving us to sweat heavily during a hot summer day.

Confronting the property owner about this nightmare only made things worse. His unhelpful and dismissive attitude was infuriating. He refused to issue a refund and made no effort to find us alternative accommodation, leaving us in a dire situation.

I strongly urge potential guests to think twice before considering this sham of a property. Misleading descriptions, unhygienic conditions, and an uncooperative host are the unfortunate highlights of our stay.

Spacious, clean appartement for up to 4 people. Smooth check-in/check-out process. Workers are nice & helpful. Surrounding area not top notch but nice, small, friendly PS restaurant next door, superb coffeebar on the corner and subway station just steps away. Worth the money! P.S.: Dishes & cutlery are limited to the minimum need. I'd love a corksrew in addition to taste the great greek wines before falling asleep.

Located in the central, metro station is just 70mts. The room was very clean and had very good amenities It made our stay very pleasant. 5 ⭐  for the customer service on giving guidance for the trams and subway.

We were very afraid of this accomodation at first aa it had no reviews on either Booking or google. However, the pictures looked great and we have decided to give it a go. I must say that we were very nicely surprised to find out that the apartment looked exactly as on the pictures! The room was very cosy, clean, nicely decorated, had a lot of kitchen appliances like the cooker, sandwich maker, coffee maker, fridge and it also had an amazing view on the Acropoli. The host also ensured our smooth check in.

What I would like to point out though, is that it is a rooftop flat, therefore it can get VERY hot up there so we had to have the AC running all day in order to keep the heat away.

For location, it is not the best looking part of Athens, but that is compensated by the fact that the apartment is very close to a metro and taxi station - it's only about 50-100 meters. The city centre is only about 2-3 metro stops away.

Would definitely come here again if we visit Athens again in the future!

The apartment is perfect for three people. The views from the terrace are very beautiful as you can see the Acropolis in the distance. There is a metro stop in the square but you can walk to Monasteraki Square without any problem. In the square where the apartment is located there were some junkies but we did not feel unsafe at ANY time, there was always a police car nearby. If I return to Athenas I will return to this apartment

Great location if you want to enjoy down town Athens, with a metro station in walking distance!
Beautiful studio apartment in excellant condition, brand new and super clean!
Definately recommended as a #1 choice for your stay in Athens.
It was a pleasant and comfortable stay. The apartment has all the necessary amenities. It is close to Omonoia, metro, and you can also go to the city center by foot within 15-20 minutes.

I loved staying in this apartment. It is a very beautiful apartment that had everything I needed.
It was also very clean and convenient being in a such a good location.

An Amazing place to stay in
Cozy awesome and accessible to everything 🙏

I suspected bc they can’t find our prepaid booking, we got a small room with an antique bathroom, just icky! Towels were stiff & old looking, didn’t use them. I used paper napkins I brought instead.

My husband was doing the booking and only looked at location, not the reviews nor pictures of the place. And because we arrived almost midnight to leave to cruise port at 9:30 am. It was doable for the night.
If you are on a budget and looking for alternatives to hostel, this could work for you. Be sure to check a day or 2 before arrival ensuring they have your reservation. Don’t want to be without a room if you arrived late like us

A perfect place to stay overnight in Athens.  The room was tired (cracked shower base and bathroom door with notches cut in it so it could be opened/closed without hitting the toilet), but it had everything I needed, including a fridge, air-conditioning and a comfy bed (the pillows were like bricks tbh!). I took my own food and drink.
The guy on reception was curt, but, thanks to Google maps, I found my way to Omonia station easily, and from there to the airport for only €9!

Good hotel, the price is very good for the service, you can say its the best hotel for that price and that area.
Highly recommended

Very basic hotel, quite clean. Old decor, shower is very small and the room did smell of smoke. The room has air conditioning and a balcony. Hotel staff were mostly friendly and helpful. The location isn't ideal especially for a solo female traveler. Closest square is Monastiraki which is a good 20-25 minutes away. Price is reasonable.

We came to Athens for a short city break, so we were looking for accommodation that was close to the centre and not too expensive. The hotel was good value for money, the room was small, but for such a short stay - when you just go home to sleep - it was fine. The problem was with the area, huge rubbish, dirt, just bad faces, scary people, druggies. It was such a bad neighbourhood that we didn't even go downstairs at night. Otherwise, I recommend it to everyone, but be prepared that you might not dare to walk down the street.

The hotel is a nice budget place, near the centre for a reasonable price. Breakfast is ok, but the same every day, enough not to be hungry. The good thing you do not to be  on time, all food is brought even if you are after  the time. The staff is  friendly and really nice, speak a lot of languages. The neighborhood is disgusting, be aware of your suitcases, immigrants and drug addicts may steal if you do not pay attention.

The staff in the hotel is good and the morning breakfast offered is quite tasty as well. The main attraction about the hotel is it's proximity to all major historical and archeological tourist attraction in Athens and it's competitive pricing. The locality around the hotel though feels a little unsafe, especially late at night. The room we used was basic and the decor was old, but everything was fully functional. Do not expect any view kind of good view as most of the rooms face other buildings or the congested street

In the winter (I can't speak for the summer), this is an excellent budget hotel.  At an exceedingly low price, I got friendly service, an ample breakfast, a decent room, a hot shower, and a location close to downtown.  The neighborhood is not upscale, but it is quite decent--far better than some comments would have you believe.  I forgot pants and 2 shirts in the closet when I left for Crete.  When I came for a second stay 3 weeks later, the staff, who had kept them not knowing that I would return, delivered them to my new room.  In the budget category, this hotel is hard to beat.

The staff is amazing. Some of the best people we have ever met. Main problem we had was that the a.c isn't working great in some rooms. Also it's not in the greatest of neighborhoods. We enjoyed this place despite those facts.

Decent enough. Breakfast includes eggs, sausages, cheese, cereal, basic items. Bedroom was a bit spare and only had one visible outlet halfway up the wall which was annoying. The hotel is located in a rather dismal looking area, but the main square and metro are less than ten minutes away.
BALKAN SEC is really good at following up with the client so as to ensure that they are never left in the dark, keep it up! 💪🏼💯
The best
A family business that is always by the customer's side! Whatever we've got is reliable! Well done keep it up!!!!!

just a disappointment. Pity. I just asked for information about beelonia and for no reason I heard a load of nonsense about the style of where you bought it etc. the funny thing is that it was bought from this supplier, I found it used. All this behavior prompted me to sell the ripening refrigerator I had bought from this supplier. After 40 years in catering and customer service, I am sorry...
I have no words really the best by far in the industry!! I bought a smoking oven and had a question about its operation and Mr. Panagiotis was willing to help me !!

I highly recommend these apartments. Located on the Boulevard,  which creats a bit of noise,  it is anyhow very clean and safe, about 500 m from Omonia square which otherwise has a bad reputation. I for myself did not feel any disconfort whatsoever. It's pure Athens at fair price
Very nice

In one word, it is a super place, it is very spacious, everything was thought out to the smallest detail, there was a television in both the bedroom and the living room, as well as air conditioning. The entrance to the house was reliable and could be entered with a password. It was right in the center of the city. I definitely recommend it. I stayed for 2 nights. I was very satisfied. It is now my favorite place in Athens.

So, nothing can be said about the apartment, excellent value for money, very nice and modern interiors, televisions in both rooms and a really nice bathroom.

The only flaw of this apartment is that it is not located in the three tourist districts of Athens, and therefore in the evening it is full of homeless people and bizarre people.

Obviously it is considered in the price because in this area, Omonia, you pay €520 for two 7 nights in Monastiraki, one of the three tourist districts, it costs around €1500/2000.
Then in any case you are close to the metro, trams and the main attractions of Athens are a 20 minute walk away.
Unfortunately Athens is like this.
Excellent service, beautiful and complete apartment, very close and easy to Omonia, great price, everything PERFECT!

This was an exceptional find when searching for a hotel within a 10-minute walk of the city centre. As a Level 7 Google Reviewer, I found it near impossible to fault this Hidden Gem in an ideal location in the capital city of Greece with such historical significance. The manager, Thanosis, and his staff were very professional, courteous, and most obliging when it came to accommodating my small tour group of 5's needs. It was also one of the few businesses that we visited where a staff member spoke English almost as well as a native speaker. Thanosis tells me the hotel's plan is to enhance the gluten-free options on their magnificent buffet breakfast in the very near future. BTW: The breakfast was complimentary and a treat for the eyes and discerning palate. As a coeliac for 16 years, I was suitably impressed and pleasantly surprised. The hotel was so impressive in every way, including, but not limited to, its creative and upmarket design, its contemporary vibe, the security, the convenience, and the staff going above and beyond their call of duty to make our stay a memorable one. I would highly recommend this property to anyone planning a short or long stay in Athens.

Great experience in a very convenient area. The staff amazing and helpful. The shops all near. No car needed, only for the beach or longer distances far from the center of Athens. They have a good parking lot that you get a discount as a costumer. Breakfast amazing and variable. In total great experience.✨

The hotel was fine, good price - quality relation. Stuff were very kindly. The hotel surroundings not good at all (dirty streets, not very secure). I recommend to use Uber / Taxi if you want to move to the most touristic places.

We stayed for one night. The hotel is very smart, well priced, not the best of areas at night , but possible to walk to the heart of Athens in half hour. The staff were brilliant! Small bar and restaurant for breakfast which was good. Room; aircon, clean and modern. Overall great value for money.

Hotel was clean, very nice staff. Enjoyed our short stay very much and definitely will stay next time at the same place! Excellent place, not quite in the eye of the city, but short distance to everyplace. Thank you! Hope to see you again.

It’s easy to walk to restaurants, train station and all the attractions that bring people to Athens. The staff was super helpful and kind. The air conditioner worked really well. The WiFi went in and out. The area is a bit dodgy. I wouldn’t want to walk around there at night. You get what you pay for

Okay hotel in a neighbourhood with many sketchy people, not recommended to arrive at too late times, especially because one day after arriving around 2:00 am the hotel doors were closed and the number I had to call was always busy after even 5 tries. Breakfast is 8 euros, and you would expect more variety or some warm food like scrambled eggs for this price. Rooms on the fourth floor are nice with nice view from balcony, but the cards have to be reprogrammed every day at the reception. In the second room they put me the AC wasn't working, the TV signal was horrible. I would only recommend it to brave people who have plan B when their hotel locks them out for the night.

So the city is just sadly so dirty, tagged and filled with the homeless. It felt dirty but not dangerous. The hotel itself was nice as a previous review art was interesting but it was lying around in places. It looked like they had not gotten done putting it up. The television did not  work and no idea how to call down. The staff was lovely and kind. We did not ever partake in the breakfast but there were so many awesome bakeries/coffee shops. It was easy to get around but we will probably stay in plaka or mythraoki next visit. It just felt more vacation spot.

For me a bed has to be comfortable and the AC needs to work and it’s quiet. It meets alll those needs. Breakfast is good and the service from the owner lady is 5*
They could use minor your ups like the grout was moldy looking in the shower but that’s common.
For the price it’s a great value and I keep coming back. Not in the core center and not the nicest neighborhood but again the Hotel is clean and safe.

Good hotel but a bit pricey for what it offers (approx €100 euros a night including taxes)
You need to walk up a set of maybe 5-8 steps to get to the hotel so not really wheelchair friendly.
Receptionist and housekeeping were respectful and helpful
Room was a bit tight and toilets had no lock and the door was glass albeit frosted glass.
They don’t have money exchange service.
Lift doors have no sensors so you must be careful.
Nice quiet rooms
Has a swimming pool
Cosy reception area

The apartment was perfect for our stay in Athens! Centrally located, close to all major attractions. The place was clean, spacious, and had a modern design. The host was always available and helped with everything we needed.

We stayed in a stunning apartment, suitable for couples and families. The location was really good - close to restaurants and public transportation, which made the trip easier. The apartment was clean, and the owners were available and kind.
Highly recommended for those looking for a simple and comfortable place for a vacation.

The apartment was great accommodation for 4 adults with big shared space.
Large Hamam bathroom and well designed.
Very responsive service and great location!

Close to the city centre
20 minutes walk from the Acropolis with great shops and restaurants all the way to.
High quality, secure apartments with code for the main and apartments doors.
I strongly recommend it

I found this accommodation at the last minute in Athens, where prices were sky-high. It turned out to be an excellent choice—clean, modern (with all entrances accessible via code), and with a thoughtful touch of providing lockers in the lobby so you can keep enjoying the city after check-out. The location is convenient, just a 10-minute walk from both Exarchia and Monastiraki. It’s also a great option for families or anyone who prefers not to eat out all the time, thanks to the fully equipped kitchen. One note of caution: it borders less touristy areas, and a nighttime stroll can feel different from the lively atmosphere of Plaka and Monastiraki. However, the overall impression remains one of safety, and I would definitely stay here again when I return to the city.

Absolutely beautiful place❤️
There is everything that you might thought you need. From the complimentary drinks that are waiting for you after the long flight to the laundry option .We’ve been pleasantly surprised to find the little gym next to our room and the terrace on the 7th floor from where you can see Acropolis
The receptionists are very positive and smilie. The whole service is up to the standards.
Down side is the area , that feels a bit unsafe , but we had no problems at all
Highly recommended

We were so excited to stay at this hotel in Athens, but we became very unwell after our flight from Australia and needed to change our plans. The hotel has refused to refund us, either fully or partially, and we are so disappointed.

Another hotel in Athens, with the same booking policy, has gladly refunded us without a fuss, as it's high-peak tourist season and their hotel has already been booked out again.

We requested a refund twice and have been told no. We highly reccommend booking different accommodation that has more flexible options for holidays- we definitely wish we had booked a different hotel/accommodation, as we have now lost over $1000 aud.

I do NOT recommend!!! My friend and I just stayed at this hotel for 4 nights. First of all, it’s more like a 2-star, not a 4-star. Our room was not great. We arrived and the room had no hand soap or shower soap and no towels! One of the sink stoppers was missing. The shower is completely open and floods the floor when you shower. The lighting in the bathroom was terrible. The staff was helpful, however, I have a big complaint. They are extremely unresponsive via email and phone. Before we left the US my friend tried sending emails 4 times asking about our reservation because we had 2 separate reservations but wanted to combine them. She also sent a separate email with another question. 4 emails and no answers to any. They don’t respond and it’s impossible to call them because you either get a number that’s not in service or it just rings and no one picks it up. Surprising because there was always someone at the front desk. I accidentally left a notebook at the front desk and tried NUMEROUS times to contact them explaining the issue but to no avail. I was happy to pay the postage costs. There was an older, lovely woman at the front desk who was kind enough to give me ice for my swollen ankle and hand from a bike accident. I thought surely she would answer but she did not😔Another comment, the hotel is in a getto area. The first night we walked to dinner and saw a number of •riot police”!!! Quite unsettling to say the least. We were told the riots have stopped and other the police presence. Run down neighborhood, graffiti and trash everywhere, sidewalks are broken and falling apart. Had we known the area was so bad we would have picked a different hotel. My hope is this review will wake them up and give others a heads up as to what they are getting into. The pool looks much better in photos and the other rooms as well. I wouldn’t go in that pool. I’m also hopeful they will read my email and respond about getting my book back. I don’t recommend staying here!

It’s not worth it!!!!!! You might think this hotel is a good deal, I promise it’s not. The location sucks. It’s in a creepy neighborhood with junkies and prostitutes. It does not feel safe at all. The hotel is awful. The swimming pools look nice on the pictures, they are not usable and disgusting. The rooms are old and everything is falling apart. The are no bed sheets, the safe is not safe. Someone broke into our room at 2AM in the middle of the night. How did they even give our key to someone else???? We are pretty sure that there were prostitutes in the lobby and in the rooms. We changed hotel and didn’t even complete our stay. This hotel should not be recommended by any sites!!!! There are so many better options in Athens.

Affordable hotel with decent, clean air conditioned rooms and good service. However be mindful that while it is close to some great sites and the metro, it is in a rather rough part of the city. (Though you walk for 5-10min and things get much better) I was randomly clawed on my arm by a homeless woman while walking  and there are vagabonds loitering nearby. This place pales in comparison to American ghettos and overall not too bad. Overall,  I would say this is a great option for groups on a budget but I would be a bit weary as a solo traveller staying here.
Looks good in photos, but is a different thing in person, not very clean rooms, bad management and is located in one of the worst area of Athens,

Not good location, with a lot of homeless around!
The room's clean is not that good
But the staff were very kind.

place_name: Palette Apartment Alice Blue – Athens Center
review_text:
Locality: not a very good area
Apartment: okay-ish but bathroom and toilets too crammed up

Budget price meets budget expectations
Pros- rooms relatively clean
Walking distance to metro
Generally friendly reception and works 24hours
Cons - omonia is not a great location - was okay as a couple in our 20s didnt get any trouble on the other hand lots of visible drug use on streets
Shower area a bit small but reasonable curtains keep touching when u shower

The manager tried to kick us out of the hotel because of a guy playing accordion out in the street, you can’t even flush the toilet with toilet paper in it. The walls are not sound proof, this hotel is so bad, worst hotel in Athens, never go there. The rooms are not cleaned, we found a used pack of honey in the desk, there was fentanyl usage all around the hotel, and again, this greek owner was the most unfriendliest ever, never ever choose this hotel in athens, you’ll regret it everything is horrible !

Man was very unfriendly, stains on beds, no towels, soap, no sheets, no place, very strict hotel and staff want to kill you. Won't recommend for any couple who want to have a child since the walls are paper thin. We even found leftovers.

Service was very friendly and informative, they even checked us in a few hours earlier than the stated time. Room was spacious, clean and had the essentials, an extra twin bed, and a patio. Only a 5 min walk to the metro rail and an overall 10 minute commute to the Acropolis station. Was a very reasonable, inexpensive price for the value and location. Area felt safe during the day but kind of sketchy at night, wouldn’t really recommend staying out too late.

We stayed there for just one night before a very early flight. Most of the hotels were booked so after reading the reviews we decided to stay there. It was decent and clean. We didn't need anything more just for a night's sleep so we were satisfied. Good service, very polite and helpful man at the reception.

We enjoyed our stay at Hotel Lozanni. The staff were friendly and very personable. They gave us a free upgrade because the bar was under construction, which was awesome! Room and linens were clean, comfortable and spacious. We appreciated being near a non-touristy side of town with easy access to the metro, and really liked the architecture of the building itself. Good shops in the area and close to the grocery. Loved American Laundry next door-what a wonderful service! Someone kept tossing our labeled food, which was a little annoying but wasn’t the hotel’s fault. If you’re looking for 5 star resort hotel, go elsewhere but this was a great place for us long term or regular travelers.

Excellent price/quality ratio.
The staff was beyond helpful, made the stay so much better. The room was clean and large. No view however, but we didn't mind.
The area is a bit weird at night but everyone minded their own business. Maybe not ideal for solo female travellers, but with a bit of planning and caution, all good.
The building itself is a bit scary at night if you have a shared bathroom, but for no real reason. Just because of the size of each floor, I guess.
A pleasant experience overall.

Nice enough place, IF you are on a budget and/or only want to spend a quick night. The personnel is very friendly and forthcoming. Free WiFi and air conditioning are also included. Keeping all that in mind, the room we had, however, was very small, and especially the bathroom seemed somewhat dirty (hair on the floor and in the sink) and the whole thing needed a paint job. HOWEVER, the friendly people made up for it. And for 23€ for a double room you will likely not get anything better. We were happy here.

Correct price/value ratio.
Smoking friendly hotel. Only one small towel per guest. Shower handle broken, and a lot of noise from the street outside. A bit of a dodgy area. Friendly staff and air conditioner work very nice. Location in respect of tourist attractions not to bad.

We didn't mind Hotel Lozanni too much. Sure - it's not in the nicest neighbourhood but we didn't feel unsafe. If anything, there's plenty of convenience stores around the hotel, so we felt there was always someone about. Also I prefer somewhere that felt more like locals rather than touristic. BUT the nearest metro station - not good - we got pickpocketed when we took the train to the airport. Nothing to do with the hotel, but part of our experience in Athens. Our friend also had incidents, so I do feel it's quite rampant.

The hotel itself - it is a bit shabby, and the rooms are run down. But the price is good, and it had some very effective air conditioning. Not the most convenient as you have to grab the remote from downstairs. Very basic setup, but enough.

The staff were characters! Very nice and also very big fans of smoking.  There was generally an eye on the entrance, so it feels somewhat reassuring once you get in. The rooms themselves smelt fine, and the shared bathrooms were acceptable (barely). Location is good, we walked to all the main sights from there and there was lots of thing to enjoy and eat nearby.

Value for money is good. The room was fine and clean. The elevator is tiny. The breakfast is very basic (2 toasts with cheese, one boiled egg, and a packaged croissant and instant coffee). I was glad they offered locker services for 7€. There’s no physical reception but communication was good thru email or WhatsApp.
- the shower hat was a little broken.
In general , you’ll be fine for 1-2 nights.

Bed bugs in the room! Don’t come here. I’ve just read a recent review from another guest saying that they got bitten by bed bugs and I can confirm because it happened to me too last night. I am currently covered in bites. I had to leave the room in the middle of the night to find a different hotel because I couldn’t stay there any longer (I had booked 3 nights and it happened during the first night).
They claim they have 24/7 reception but it’s a lie, there is no reception at all. There was no one I could warn about the bed bugs in the room. I tried to contact the owner via WhatsApp as soon as I found out about the bed bugs, but they replied to me the next morning and I had already left.
They weren’t even surprised when hearing about bed bugs. They just said “sorry about that”. My guess is that they already knew. Luckily they agreed to a full refund, so they canceled my reservation on Booking and I was able to, at least, get a refund.
Please don’t go there unless you want to be covered in bites.

Good for a day or two. But if you have kids dont prefer it. The elevator can fit more than 2 people max. We had travel bags, stroller etc. we had to use up and down three times. Also the storage for luggage’s was small but if you have 10kg bag its good.

BED BUGS!!!
Don’t come to this apartment. Service is awful, we got bitten by bed bugs a lot, got a severe allergic reaction. The stuff didn’t want to talk with us and just told us to check out. We had one more night so we had to find another apartment asap.
Rooms don’t look the same as on the photos. Very dirty, bad smell, the shower doesn’t work properly.
Don’t go here at any cost, better find something else!!!
Rooms are nice, area a bit sketchy,  especially  after dark.  Walk to center and museum  of archeology,  great..
The worst hotel I got in, there was no bad cleanliness and ventilation. I paid for 40 euros one night, and I didn't know the hotel was bad.

The hotel is the last resort if you can't find anything else. It's probably a "love hotel". WC is super dirty, flushing wasn't working, hair on the pillows, smelly, toilet papers on various places and unknown pills left on the table. I slept with my clothes on.
Not clean, bad treatment, a lot of dirt, and some of it does not deserve to be a hotel
There's this friendly Ethiopian lady i like her welcomed to me there. So I'm giving 3 out of 5.
Very inhospitable reception staff. Not recommended.
Excellent!

The apartment is very nice, new, modern and clean. It is on the 7th floor and the building has an elevator. The place is very comfortable with style and character. The terrace is very large and even has a big table for meals. Close to Metro and easy to explore Athens. The host/manager is great, a nice and helpful man. I hope to stay here again when I return to Athens. Highly recommend!!

It is a diamond in the heart of Athens! Value for money property! If you find free reservation dates do not hesitate to book them! Alexandros awesome, he response to any request!Very good hospitality and totally new apartment!
Sure will visit it again!
Fantastic apartment!

Excellent apartment in the city center with very good style, very clean, bright and quite quiet. I stayed 3 days there with my partner and we enjoyed our stay to the fullest. Alexander is awesome in everything and willing to serve us in everything. We will definitely meet again!

The stay is a unique experience! Alexandros always available, always willing, always helpful!
The house was really spacious and functional! It had all the necessary things in it and in fact, it also had treats that the owner was not obliged to provide!
Two steps from the center, the neighborhood is quiet and safe!

Alexander, see you soon!

We stayed in a comfortable and very clean junior suite.The reception staff was kind and helpful.The breakfast, included in the room price, was a bit small,but tasty with salty and sweet options.Espresso and Cappuccino are not included in the breakfast price,must be paid at the check out.
Unfortunately the hotel is located in a dodgy area of Athens.During the night the street was very loud and it was difficult to sleep because the hotel windows and walls  are not soundproofing,but in Athens is almost normal to hear noise from the street.
I rated the Hotel 4 stars because it deserves it,I hope with some time the street situation will improve.

We have enjoyed our staying in this hotel, even just for one night! The breakfast is very nice! The staff are amazing people, specially Vasia, such a nice and kind girl!!! Service, attention provided 10/10!!!
Clean, quite basic rooms. Friendly service with 24 hour reception. Quite a noisy area. Good restaurants close by!
a BAD Street.! Hotel is surrounded by Zombie like people allegedly doing drugs. Its on other reviews. Hotel staff challanged me about the review when asked for refund.

I had an exceptional experience at this hotel! Not only did they provide excellent service throughout my stay, but they also went above and beyond when I accidentally left behind a valuable bag. The staff took immediate action, securing and safeguarding the bag until I could retrieve it. Their attentiveness and care truly made my trip unforgettable. I highly recommend this hotel for their outstanding hospitality and genuine concern for their guests’ well-being. Thank you for an incredible stay!

The room was great! Clean, new, spacious, and comfortable. The shower was good too.

Apart from that, the facilities were all extremely basic. There is no lobby or comfortable place to sit/wait. The elevator is tiny.  The breakfast was very basic and felt a bit reserved, as if they count the number of olives they give you. There is nothing to write home about in that respect.

Regarding the staff, it didn't feel like they were going out of their way to be hospitable or to give us a good feeling, though they did help out by letting us leave our luggage at the hotel, after we checked out.
It should be said that the guard was extremely friendly!

The neighbourhood,  as many stated, is rough. You see many homeless junkies wandering around the area, day and night, though we didn't have any direct contact with them as they all minded their own business. We never felt threatened or in danger. However, our kids noticed used needles rolling around on the sidewalks, and we had to be very clear about not touching anything!!

This is definitely *not* a 4-star hotel. 3  is more accurate. If you stay here, just make sure you know what to expect...

I recently stayed here. The room itself was quite pleasant—clean, comfortable, and well-appointed for the price. The food at the hotel was also impressive, offering a good variety of tasty dishes for breakfast.

However, the neighbourhood surrounding the hotel left much to be desired. While the location is central and convenient for accessing many of Athens' key attractions, the area felt unsafe, especially after dark. We encountered several heavily drug-affected individuals and noticed used needles on the streets, which made the environment uncomfortable and unsuitable for families.

Overall, the Hotel  offers good accommodation and food, but the surrounding area significantly detracts from the overall experience.

I am sorry to have to rate two stars for this place. The room and service were nice but I had to check out without even staying a night (I booked three nights). It was because I had a child and an elderly with me and I wanted them to enjoy Athens by walking around the city. When we were in a taxi from the port, the taxi driver warned us not to go out at night time as the area (Omonia) is rough due to drug abuse and petty theft. The hotel itself is in a safe building with a security. I think it would be ok if you don’t mind being in a rough neighbourhood.
