I have stayed in Photo story apartment many times. Savvas is a great host, very kind and always willing to help. Apartment is very comfortable, clean, very well equipped and with a nice balcony that you can see Acropolis also. The view also from the terrace at night is simply amazing! Location is super, very close to city center and metro station, while also is in a friendly neighborhood, with no noise and full of restaurants and bars. I highly recommend photo story apartment ++++

I have recently stayed in Photo story art studio apartment and I have really enjoyed all of it! It is very cozy, comfortable, clean, very well equipped -it had even a baby crib!!- and has an amazing view from the balcony too. Location is also excellent, very close to city center and metro station, while also is in a friendly neighbourhood, full of life with plenty of choices of restaurants and bars, but at the same time not at all noisy. Savvas is also a great host, very kind and always there whenever you need any kind of help. Strongly recommend it !!!

Great apartment and hosting. We really enjoyed our vacations in Athens.
Very comfortable and super clean apartment with amazing view.
High recommended accommodation for any guest

Apartment is ok, looks bigger on the photos but in fact smaller in reality.  Our internet didn't work for the whole 3 days. As we tried to reach someone from the team there, but  noone answered or fixed the problem.  Very disappointing.
I was a guest togethter with B. Fenner and internet was NOT available and no service and no fixing of the problem . Thanks for this bad reaction.
Customer service is not your strength and also not a critic
So pleasant place , super clean, great location and excellent communication with the host.

Had a really nice and spacious apartment with a cozy backyard for enjoying morning coffees. Only one of the apartments has access to the backyard though, the others have a balcony.

There is no reception at the place, so call or sms the owner in advance to get keys. Also, since these are apartments there is no breakfast served. But they provide some basics that should keep you fed for a few days. And there is a supermarket less than 3 min away where you can get everything you need.

Brilliant. Would 100% recommend. Nice and spacious, comfortable bed. Cake, bread, eggs, butter & water were left for us. Apartment was nearby a couple of bars (no noise problems), supermarket, bakery, pharmacy etc. 15 minute walk from central athens.

Great location. Katerina and Marirtini are super helpful.  Our room size was a little tight for us and dimly lit but overall clean and comfortable. Breakfast every morning was beyond our expectations...all carefully curated and absolutely delicious! Easy to walk from hotel to other places/attractions. We walked everywhere each day. Would definitely stay there again.

From the moment I stepped into the hotel, I was greeted by Marina and the charming team of ladies at the front desk and kitchen, whose warmth and helpfulness defined my stay. They provided invaluable advice, detailed maps, refreshing drinks, and thoughtful guidance, ensuring every moment was filled with comfort and care.

Upon my early arrival, well before check-in time, I was graciously welcomed with coffee and cake in the serene ambiance of the hotel's open terrace, a tranquil retreat at the back of the property. This unexpected gesture set the tone for the exceptional hospitality that followed.

My spacious room exceeded expectations, offering ample space to effortlessly unpack my two suitcases. The attention to detail was evident, ensuring a seamless and comfortable stay.

During check-in, the staff thoughtfully inquired about my flight time, demonstrating their commitment to personalized service. Their offer to prepare a breakfast box for my early departure exemplified their dedication to guest satisfaction.

While the hotel's location may not be in the most picturesque neighborhood, the convenience of affordable taxi services made exploring Athens effortless. Coupled with the reasonable room rates, the value proposition was undeniable.

In summary, my experience at this hotel surpassed all expectations. The genuine warmth of the staff, coupled with the comfortable accommodations and thoughtful gestures, left a lasting impression. Without a doubt, I will eagerly return on my next visit to Athens.

As soon as I booked this hotel I was impressed with their service. Within half an hour I received a welcome email. Any time I sent emails requesting information, I reply was sent shortly thereafter with info on local restaurants and offers to help us with booking excursions and transportation.
We were warmly welcomed when we arrived. The front desk staff Marina, Katarina,  Arhodoula were super friendly and helpful. The breakfast offered was excellent, the courtyard dining room was beautiful. A peaceful setting. Each room has a different theme. We had the Santorini room. The room was a good size, very quiet, nice decor.
The neighborhood does have a lot of graffiti but that seems to be the norm for Athens. We didn't have any problems walking around the area. It takes about 15 minutes to walk to the downtown area where the hoho buses are. Takes about 30 to 40 minutes to walk to the acropolis. Recommend going to Rakor for dinner.
If we ever go back to Athens we will definitely book this hotel again.
We stayed only 1 night but after such a long flight Estia was amazing. Wonderful staff, very comfortable room. Morning breakfast was fresh and wonderful. We highly recommend.

My husband and I are in our late 50's. We loved being able to walk 15 to 20 minutes to most places. The hotel staff was amazing. The free breakfast was outstanding. I'm so glad we choose to stay here.

The house was very big and comfortable. All the furniture was new and very high quality. Very close to the center of Athens and the acropolis. The metro was very close by foot. The host helped us with everything we needed. It is worth noting that the house had Netflix as well as coffee pods.

Unbelievable three-storey maisonette, very large, comfortable, very modernly decorated, fully equipped with everything we needed and in a quiet place. The communication manager next to us in whatever we needed at any time and moment. Simply perfect and we will definitely prefer it again.
Stayed here for over a week.  Great spot and friendly owners that really made us feel at home.  Cannot recommend highly enough!

It's an ideal place to stay for a few days, since you cannot stay permanently. !!!
The environment and the facilities are just perfect.
The host behavior is exceptional.
I absolutely recommend it.

Amazing place close to city center of Athens  , close to coffee  stores & taverns   in Gkazi & Psurri area, very kindly hostess ,top destinstion to visiting  closest Archaeological sites antiquities .

It was a mixed experience. The apartment has a good location, near the metro station, and it was adequately equipped. However, it was really hard to communicate with the owner. I tried to contact them several times via email and phone to confirm my reservation but I could not get ahold of them. Finally, I could reach them an Whatsapp. For some reason I did not receive the code for the door beforehand (this was not necessarily their fault), and it took at least 1,5 hours until I could reach someone and get in to the apartment which was really uncomfortable.

I travel all over around word, and this experience was as awful as I ever had. While, the apartment is clean and has a good location with an attached private backyard. Management is unprofessional and doesn't care if something happens to you. So, I went out of an apartment in this lovely backyard, and than I realized that the door was locked because there is no handle outside.  I stayed more than 3 hours with no help, manager Maria, who I called many times, tell me that she is 3000 km away, promised to relieve me but finally, I escaped from my neighbor's door.  While she promised to send me the owner to help open my apartment,  I had to deal with a door service technician who I called myself and was requested to pay 50€ for opening the door, which was insane. I DO NOT recommend this apartment because the management doesn't care if something happens with their customers.
The management is unprofessional and non-responsive

The host is lovely
The apartment is very pleasant, clean and very practical with our children.
Next to the metro station (acropolis...)
We loved it, the little shaded terrace is perfect and put us in a cocooning atmosphere.
I highly recommend.
Thank you for everything.

I've lived in really bad apartments (unfortunately), but Lambda Vintage has surpassed everything
Looked different than in the pictures, everything was damp and super loud. Ultimately we had to change apartments

Awesome modern designed flat (as seen in photos) with everything you need for a short stay (wifi, kitchenette, AC, coffee, great shower)
The location is a mix of close to hip (5 min walk) and central (20-30 min walk) touristy area. But also 1-3 blocks from drug infused, homeless ridden streets.
The host was very communicative and helpful by pointing me to a garage to park the car as it doesn't feel safe to leave it on the street.

Advertises with "wifi is available in all areas", but both wifi and 4G signal are unable to reach the room in the basement.. service center was not willing to provide a solution, so I had no internet connection in my room for the full 6 days of my stay.
Loved Olala Homes! The property features amazing interior design and is located in an up and coming bohemian neighborhood. Will visit again!
Great holiday apartment in terms of cleanliness, the place is quiet, the check-in is done online, you don't have to be greeted by the host.

Great apartment, very big, all clean.
There is no body mirror!!

I stay here every time I am in Athens. It is very affordable and about a block from the metro. It is a 10 minute walk to Psiri and 15 to Monasteraki. The people are amazing. I highly recommend it. The pics show the view from my room.

The hotel is very simple as the breakfast. But the treatment of the receptionist was extremely rude at me when I notice that calling a Uber was half of her taxi, and when other costumers heard me she started to scream in front of everyone. Not classy at all and super disrespectful. Never staying there for sure. I do NOT recommend this hotel

Here’s the translation to English:

"Very modest rooms. For showering in the bathroom, I recommend bringing your own slippers. Breakfast is mediocre... All in all, acceptable for an overnight stay, nothing more..."

The hotel is clean, friendly staff, and has a delicious fresh breakfast.  Good internet.

It is located in a transitioning neighborhood across from Wyndham.

I definitely recommend the Apollo Hotel.

Really good hotel, close too the centre of athens. Around 20 mins walk to the centre so not so bad. The hotel itself is budget but we loved it, cost room, aircon, TV, bathroom with amazing shower. I would highly recommend this hotel. Perfect base for city exploring. The staff were nice and friendly and the cleaners were nice.

Reasonable cost and fairly central location (walkable to lovely restaurants in 15 minutes, or to Acropolis tour meeting locations in 25 minutes or so). The neighborhood it is located in is fairly seedy, but the locals did not harass us even when walking around past midnight. That said, I would be uncomfortable walking through here alone at night even though nothing actually happened.

The service desk folk were all very friendly and lovely. The room was immaculately clean, comfortable and modern in its decor. Other than the shower being a bit messy (the doors didn't close all the way so the floors do end up wet and slippery, be careful!) absolutely fantastic accommodations.

The room is clean, a quite new and with a good design.
The staff are amazing, they help you for everything and they are nice.
The location is not 5 stars because close there is a not very good neighborhood to walk at night (if you go around is perfect)

I booked a room for 2 people, with a bed & chaise lounge. When we arrived we requested to use the chaise to sleep on but were told it would be an additional 50 euros to do so. They refused to provide linens for the chaise unless I paid extra. Even though there was no mention of this on the website when booking. Which is a bait & switch. I also had to purchase more toilet paper for the room as their was no one staffing the front desk. I arranged airport pick up thru them & the driver never showed up nor answered when they tried to track them down. But despite this poor customer service, I was still asked to pay extra to have bedding. It's loud & noisy, subpar service & I'd never stay here again.

Very nice hotel with a rooftop with a excellent view at the Acropolis. Staff is very friendly and helpful. The rooms are very beautiful and access is via pincode, same with the front door. The location is excellent near metro, near pharmacy, near bakery and not far from a supermarket. Absolutely loved my stay at the hotel !

Convenient location as it's only a few meters away from a metro stop but the street that it is on is a bit dodgy. Also loud street noise at night. Rooms could use double glazed windows. Roof top is nice and has an amazing view of the Acropolis.

Before my booking i read many of the reviews here and i was really surprise when i checked in. is a boutique hotel, brand new everything its smarty and is really clean. Is one of the most clean city hotel that i have stay. The area is too safe, is part of the historical center, i walk all 3 days and its next to the sub way. Its the best option since the price its very low for this hotel. The hydromassage spa shower was very relaxing when i was return to the hotel and the breakfast at the terrace was amazing. Also you have amazing view to the Acropolis. I really be back, thank for everything to the front office. For couples its an ideal solution due to the glass bathrooms.

We stayed here for 1 night to explore Athens before starting our journey through the rest of mainland Greece. Rooms are very spacious, clean and new. Staff is very friendly and helpful. Breakfast is good as well.

Amazing hotel stay with city view

I am a great fan of Greece. I usually visit the city Athens like every year. I stay at Acropolis Plaza Smart and spa usually. This time i stayed here too. I must say this is one of the finest hotel with great location of city. The rooms are super.

Staff has been very cooperative here and always happy to help. I can recommend this hotel to anyone looking for a stay in Athens.

Prettiest view to acropolis, nice big rooms and bathrooms, friendly staff and nice moderate breakfast at the rooftop. Only thing the staff wasnt present when we arrived at 1 am from the airport and then we had to check ourselves in and when we found out the remote for the ac was missing they did not pick up. Further tgan that nice hotel to stay at!!

The room was great, modern and automated. The only issue I had was with check-in. I arrive around 6:30 and there was no one at the reception to even open the entrance door because the door is automated/digital, you use a code to get into the hotel. Well, I didn't have the code and there was no one at the reception to provide the code. You need a local phone number to call them the day of your check-in to get the damn code. After that the room looked great and the room door is also digital (you use a code to get in). The doors being digital/automated is a fantastic feature, but they need to improve how the give the damn codes. The location is what you make of it, it's in the trouble zone of Athens, but it's what you make of it. As far as the hotel: rooms are great and big, pretty safe once in the hotel premises because no one from outside can get in the hotel without the codes (it makes the place feel/be safe). One block (2-3 minutes walk) away from the Metaxourgeio terminal. The hotel looks amazing, it has a beautiful terrace and it has a smoking designated area (A plus for smokers). My only issue was with the door codes and the wifi password.

My recomendation for the hotel is: they should email the door codes prior to the guest arrival. Or have a pre-checkin website where the guest retrieves the door codes. Also, place a note inside the rooms with the Wi-Fi password. I had to ask another tenant for the password because there was no one at the reception and no one answers the phone during the night (supposed to be 24H call number and it's not). That's the reason of my 4 star, otherwise, I have given you a 5 star review.

It's a very unique low-cost Hotel perfect for a very cheap night in Athens Greece around 50 bucks a night very cheap and it's a good hotel for that price whatever you get that's what you pay for the rooms are decent every room comes almost every room comes with a ball can you so that is good with stunning beautiful views from the sundown and the mountains around Athens Greece decent bathroom shower TV small bar refrigerator plugs by the beds comfortable beds you know decent it's in the center it's literally 5 minutes from do you know these small streets the real vibes from Athens Greece and the nice lobby to chill out what's a good massage you're over there that was the job for you instead of a real massage
The service is great

Hotel was reasonable.
Bakery next door was to die for. Great food and sweets.
Location of hotel - was NOT safe when  walking around at night.
Average 3* hotel.  However the bathroom was filthy. Long black hair on shower walls and all over the floors.  This indicates that the room was not cleaned properly. Good service.

Good value hotel, clean room, nice bathrooms, and decent service with a rooftop bar that has a view of the acropolis  but if you are expecting anything above average than this isn’t the place for you. Just about everything is pretty average for a hotel. My biggest gripe is the WiFi in the hotel. It is atrocious, for the amount of money they make on tourist their WiFi is horrendous. I ran a speed test on my phone and didn’t get more than 5mb download. I couldn’t even load the airport webpage to check in with my flight. I couldn’t use FaceTime and webpages either didn’t load or took minutes to load. Using apps wasn’t as bad but the second you try to use something like chrome or explorer good luck. if you need WiFi for any business don’t even try their WiFi it will not cut it, you will have to use your phones hotspot and hopefully you have an international plan with unlimited data. I guess free WiFi is free WiFi.
Didn’t stay here, but went for brunch. €10 per person. It was a good continental breakfast with hot eggs and coffees.

Overall a pleasant experience. Fantastic rooftop bar and restaurant with great food and stunning views of the acropolis. The service staff were all very friendly and d helpful. The breakfast buffet leaves a bit of room for improvement. The rooms are clean and have a comfy bed and seem to be well soundproofed as I did not hear my neighbors which is often a nuisance in some other hotels. Would stay there again.

From reading some of these reviews a week before I arrived I thought I was going to be staying in the worst hotel on earth, the reality was very much the opposite.

The room was a really decent size for a European city hotel! The bathroom was fine, some room for improvement in that area but perfectly adequate!

The reception staff were extremely helpful and friendly, the breakfast buffet was also very good!

The rooftop is the star of the show, we ate at the restaurant twice during our week in Athens, the food there was exquisite and the view is sensational! Also the staff made us feel very welcome and special! I didn't use the pool but other members of my party did and have no complaints but it can get busy on certain days and times.

Location is perfect! The metro is right outside the hotel, which is very accessible! 4 stops to the acropolis, what more could you want?!

Also top tip, there's a cafe just on the corner of the hotel which is actually part of the Stanley, it's the perfect place to get a quick bite to eat!

The hotel is great. Best service. Rooftop facilities are the best!

The bar is too expensive. That's really a shame.
The rooms are okay (superior), bathroom way too small. Minibar with free drinks is actually just water refill daily. Expectstion on that benefit was too high.

Location is okay. Near the subway which is very nice.

The amenity space on the rooftop is the best feature of this hotel. The pool area is really large and very nice. It's very busy so it's not easy getting a spot to sit but it's worth the effort.
The free breakfast on the rooftop is also very good. Very good selection of food and the views are amazing.
The rooms themselves are dated and the bathrooms are so extremely small. One would expect a more luxury feeling for this price point in terms of being modern and larger. The front lobby feels dated as well.
Seems they have spent all their money on the rooftop.
There is a subway right out front so that's helpful but the area is a 5 out of 10 you really need to walk about 20 minutes before you get anywhere nice.

The best thing about this hotel is its rooftop pool that looks on to the acropolis. The showers were also incredible, but the beds weren't particularly comfy.

The food was amazing. We had this incredible orzo and shrimp dish for lunch. It was incredible.

After booking into an apartment that was the worst in our lives, we booked a room for one night in the Ceramian Apartments IN THE EARLY HOURS OF THE MORNING. We received an answer straight away in the morning and were able to check in early without any problems. The apartment itself was outstanding. Modern, super clean and simply beautiful. The large terrace with a view of the Acropolis was of course the highlight and unforgettable. This apartment saved our stay in Athens and transformed it from unbelievably bad to unbelievably beautiful within 24 hours. We are also so grateful for Constantina's service. She was always available immediately and, with a super friendly manner, made everything you needed to feel comfortable possible. Thank you again at this point. I can really only strongly recommend that everyone come here. In addition to the wonderful apartment, it is also super central and in an authentic district with beautiful cafes, bars and restaurants. If there is a room available here, book it! I would come again and again!

After booking two tickets to Athens I starting orientating for a hotel to stay in. Realising everything closest to the city center I started looking elsewhere. I couldn't have wanted it any other way. Finding Ceramian Athens was the greatest! I had not stayed in an appartement before. This superb experience was so good it set the bar super high. Everything was super clean, utilities worked fine. Even though I had only seen a staff member once in 4 days, which was just fine and a fresh breath of air as this was my first time staying in an apartment as I said earlier. The only contact I had when a question arose was so great. Fast response and great service. Would definitely recommend staying here and will recommend to friends to go here when visiting Athens.

Great view of the Acropolis from the rooms. Rooms were excellent and well furnished but a bit dusty; might be due to the renovations going on.

10-15 min easy walk into the city centre and relatively walkable to attractions.

Went over there for the 4th time already.

Lovely, spacious, very clean and luxurious apartments and rooms with dito terraces/balconies.
Great rooftop(bar) with stunning views (Acropolis and so much more).
Very good location: calm but very close to citycentre.
Very nice, warm and helpful staff.
Professionaly and warmhearted managed.

A must do when in Athens!

My trips to Athens would not be complete without staying at the Ceramian! ❤️

The hotel itself boasts a modern and stylish design that perfectly complements the vibrant energy of Athens. And let's talk about the view – waking up to a stunning vista of the Acropolis every morning is nothing short of magical.

Location-wise, you couldn't ask for better. Situated in the heart of the city, it's close to everything you'd want to see and do in Athens. Whether you're here for a short weekend break or an extended stay, this hotel is the perfect base.

And to top it all off, the value for money is exceptional. You get so much more than what you pay for here. You can’t miss out on this one as it ticks all the boxes – especially when the hosts treat you to best sweets in town on arrival 😊

Titika is very nice, forthcoming, and helpful.
We were a family of 4 adults.
We had the patio Apt.
It was very clean, nicely decorated and cozy.
It was cramped, the king-size bedroom had no room for the suitcase, never mind opening it...it has a translucent glass door which made it hard to keep our room dark.
The double bedroom and the living room-kitchen were cramped too.
The kitchen was well equipped.
Very tastefully decorated. It has a delightful garden with wonderful beds and pillows. You can easily reach anywhere by walking in 15-20 minutes. I advise.

Great Location, metro station is just a minute away. City center is also at walking distance. Staff was very helpful. Great breakfast spread. We had a early morning ferry so the staff gave us a bag with snacks since we would miss the breakfast.

Great little hotel we stumbled upon in a time of need very late at night when our other accommodation fell through. They were so helpful and accommodating to our family and nothing was too much trouble. Lovely facilities, great breakfast, fabulous service from all the staff and reception. Great location.

At first we were a little surprised… I came here with my mother who traveled the world in her youth but it is in the beginning stages of memory decline. The staff was BEYOnD kind and helpful. They gave us great recommendations, a lot of support, and even when night when we separated for a bit and there was confusions they cared for my mother as if she was their own family. I can not say enough. Also the voucher to the blue line hop on hop off bus was easy to navigate and got us all around the city for 2 days. I would recommend this to anyone! It was wonderful . The restaurant bar on the top was beautiful. Go there first and get a sense of where you are…. The are is a little sketchy right around the hotel but it is really quite safe as long as you are not afraid of humanity.

Someone next door to us just kept knocking on the wall.. didn’t know if it was a trafficking attempt or what, but was pretty scary and we did not feel safe. Made the front desk aware and nothing was done. The elevator to the room was tiny and reeked of piss. I didn’t expect this for a 5-star hotel. Was happy that we only had one night there.

REPLY TO MANAGEMENT:

Yes you were the last person we spoke to, and nothing was done. Thank you

The staff were helpful but this hotel is not up to the standards that we have come to expect from Wyndham. The rooms are dated, the floors a tacky plastic and the “swimming pool” is 40cm deep (41cm deep once I immersed myself). Based on our inquiries, similar standard hotels can be secured for around half the price. If you’re a Wyndham member, save your credits and pay cash for somewhere else in Athens. The Wyndham at Riviera near Rafina was much better and cheaper.

place_name: Το σπίτι της Περσεφόνης στην Αθήνα Persephones house in Athens
review_text: Very nice and large house in an excellent location near an electric station, the owner is kind and welcoming, I highly recommend it!

If you’re looking for a memorable stay in Athens, I can’t recommend this hotel enough. It’s truly a gem, made even better by the incredible team that ensures each guest feels valued. They were super helpful and supportive. Thank you to all of them for making my visit unforgettable—I can’t wait to return!

I recently had the pleasure of  organizing a business meeting in Wyndham Grand Athens hotel, and I can't recommend it enough! The organization was impressive. The staff was incredibly responsive to any inquiries, even changing the meeting room at very last minute.

The restaurant at the hotel served delicious food! The rooms were well-appointed and comfortable, providing a perfect retreat after a day of engaging discussions. Overall, my experience at Wyndham Grand Athens hotel was amazing!

At the Grand Wyndham, we had the absolute pleasure of meeting Niki at the travel desk! This dynamo of a lady was a travel wizard, conjuring up the most brilliant suggestions that turned our Athens adventure into a treasure hunt of epic proportions. From must-visit ancient ruins to hidden gems where the locals hang out, she had the insider scoop on it all!

With her delightful charm and unwavering support, she was like our personal travel superhero.
Thanks to Niki, we navigated the city like pros and made memories that will last a lifetime.

A massive thank you to Niki for making our trip a blast. You truly rock!

Lovely staff, beautiful hotel and good location as it’s near to the hotspots. Fantastic view of the acropolis. The rooftop pool was freezing, which was a shame as it was very beautiful. The WiFi was bad, hardly worked. The cocktails were okay but not very good for the price. Food was average. We paid extra for a city view and we had a wonderful view of the crackheads washing themselves in the fountain. The hotel was in crackville filled with brothels. Don’t walk there at night, get a cab. The staff will help you get a cab of your need it. They say they have a spa but it’s just a massage room, no sauna or steam room. Treatments were fantastic and worth getting.

Simply put, the staff is exceptional. As single parent traveling with young daughters, not only were the staff friendly, inviting and accommodating, they also made it their mission to make our stay perfect! Truth be told, this is our second stay and it was a non-negotiable that we stay at the Wyndham Grand! This coming from my daughters! Even in a neighborhood that shows all the facets of urban life, both the good and seedier side, we felt very safe and comfortable! The rooms were impeccable and the view from the terrace breathtaking!!!! I highly recommend!

Let me start by saying I have been traveling around Greece for the past month, and I have stayed at a lot of hotels some good some better some words, but none of them made me want to write a review like this place. The people of reception are great very polite and helpful. They are surprisingly there at 24/7, something I didn't expect from a three-star hotel. The bathroom is good but could be better. They have a giant rain style shower head but with no pressure, at least it had unlimited hot water that didn't stop which was nice. Half the lights in the bathroom didn't work so I was shaving in the some what dark. The most annoying thing about this hotel is the owner that doesn't stop yelling and smoking for 1 minute. I really felt bad for the staff. Butt what's annoying about the room is that they had a loud fan in the bathroom that was not able to be turned off. The staff said it's against the rules to turn it off. And the second most annoying thing about the hotel room is that you have to get permission to use the AC every single day from the reception downstairs. So if you came home after a long trip went to take a shower came out got in bed and realize you forgot to ask reception to unlock your AC. You have to ask them to unlock it every single day. From a strange point of view I was surprised how when I asked housekeeping if they could change my bed linens they said sorry they don't have any bed Linens. How does a hotel not have bed linens? I have stayed at one star hotels even motels that had beds on bricks, and even they had sheets. Finally they were able to find me some bad Linens. But getting anything simple here is really difficult. There's a teapot in the rooms but there's no tea or coffee to drink it. They advertising Wi-Fi but for some reason it doesn't work no matter how much you try. They have a smart TV but since there's non-existent Wi-Fi it's useless. But what's the cherry on top? The hotel is located in the worst part of the city, think of the Cesspool that is Detroit this is like Detroit of Greece. Drug addicts homeless people as far as the eyes can see and everything smelling like pee. We were able to find parking right outside our hotel which was nice. But if you don't like the sound of cars I wouldn't recommend this hotel because it's right in front of a busy intersection and s round about.

Also they serve breakfast here but I wouldn't recommended it, you can get better breakfast at a cafe around the corner.

Felt ripped off when checking in as a group of six. Been told that an okay was given by us for an “upgrade to a bigger apartment“ for one of the 3 double rooms reserved. It was a surcharge of €380 for three-night upgrade for that one room. In fact  there are two double rooms inside the apartment. It didn’t make any sense that we would have agreed to book 4 double rooms for three couples at an unnecessary surcharge. It was even more absurd that we didn’t have the liberty to arrange our group members in how to make use of these 4 rooms. Only two were allowed to stay in the big but expensive apartment (it is in fact more aged facility). Arriving with all the heavy luggage, we were basically forced to swallow the ripe-off. What an experience it was the first time in Athens?

Great service from the hotel ! They were really welcoming and the rooms were kept clean. You're lucky if you get a room with a view on the main street, because we got the view of the neighboring building's wall. Our room was small but it didn't matter for us because we aren't picky. Breakfast was fine, it kept me full.

Location gets points because it's right next to the Metaxourgeio metro station. (About a 3 minute walk) However, the nearing streets aren't really safe, especially at night. I would recommend walking with someone or keeping your distance from sketchy people on the street.

Overall, not bad for the price we paid last summer !

The rooms here are beautiful and the staff here is very helpful and compassionate to their guests. However I have to state how the owner was a great disturbance throughout our whole stay!! He was continuously cursing and calling derogatory terms to his staff throughout the day and basically just treating them so disrespectfully. He was constantly smoking inside the building which is very illegal here in Greece, and when asked to stop he responded with more disrespectful names to someone of my party. Can’t say I would stay here again

I’d like to start by saying none of the following applies to the staff and employees and that they have always treated my friends and I with respect and compassion (especially regarding the language barrier). HOWEVER… the boss of the hotel is beyond inappropriate and disrespectful. Throughout my stay, I had endure his constant yelling and upset, as well as the smell of his cigarettes 24/7 (something that is illegal to do indoors). It became more and more uncomfortable to walk through the lobby because he always sat at the entrance watching and smoking. I will never stay here again and would never recommend this place to anyone.

Hotel Katerina is outstanding. Exceeded our expectations. We've got a free upgrade to junior suite which was really great. The room was squeaky clean,  comfortable beds and freshly ironed sheets.
The breakfast was very tasty.
The safe hotel parking is extra helpful for the area. Pretty good walking distance to the centre. The staff was really polite and informative. They provided extra care and attention.  We would definitely stay here again.

Has aircon, location not that good, but Easy to get on the hop on, hop off bus route! Metro close by. Super breakfast. Extremely friendly staff. They go the extra mile. Giannis the Manager, Krista’s and Giorgos. We were able to leave our luggage in the reception area. Also has a laundromat across the road!
Robin and Dee from South Africa
its okay just the fact that homeless people are a few meters away from the hotel wich would be fine if they didnt smell

Staff all lovely and helpful with organising taxis. Breakfast bar has reasonably good variety. The rooms do seem outdated, they could probably change the curtains and paint the brown wooden furniture to a lighter colour to make it look fresh.
There is a metro nearby, but you could also walk 20-30 min to the centre. Some of the area seems dodgy, so we walked one day and took taxis another day. It costs no more than €10.
Personel at this hotel are very kind and supportive. The whole time was very clean and the breakfast is truly good.

Great hotel, made by the staff. There was one blunt woman on reception when we arrived, but all the others were wonderful.

There was a nice chap there most of the time and he couldn’t do enough for us. The cleaners,  restaurant staff, and especially the ladies working at (a terrific) breakfast every morning made such a difference.

Can’t believe the reviews moaning constantly. We stayed several nights, so let me address a few.

The lift was fine. Wasn’t particularly slow. The pool was great and it has to close at some stage during the day to be cleaned. You could swim in it and look across to the Parthenon. Cool! The food was good, including that we purchased in the top floor restaurant. Yes, the rooms are a little dated, but they are clean and spacious and importantly the shower and bed were decent.

What more could you ask…

Finally, Athens is a working city, populated by several million people in the urban area. It will have ‘rough’ areas. But the hotel isn’t in one, as some reviews say. It’s just off a main shopping thoroughfare. We walked back to it, late, every night. There are homeless people, but they didn’t even talk to us, let alone threaten us.

Personally, I felt safer late at night than I do walking through my home town in England. Also, while we were there, we saw wonderful people helping them with food and especially water.

This part of the city has almost a grid pattern and there are plenty of alternative routes to walk to and from the hotel on. We walked past them, as I say, many times.

If you want a solid hotel, with great staff, smashing breakfast, rooftop views, in the centre of a vibrant, colourful, friendly city, then I think you could do a lot worse. We liked it very much.

Recently, I stayed in the Novus City Hotel in Athens, Greece, for 6 nights. The hotel's location is awesome, just 200 meters from the metro station. The room is clean and provides all modern facilities. The buffet breakfast contains a lot of options, and the food quality is very good. The rooftop restaurant gives a clear view of the Acropolis at night, and I recommend having your dinner once to enjoy the view with Greek food/salad. Last but not least, jelly chocolates at the reception are an excellent way to welcome the guests to the Hotel. Overall, I found the Novus City Hotel to be very good for travelers and recommended it for your stay in Athens, Greece.

Unsafe area!!! Every local we met told us this is the most unsafe area in the city. One block up there are homeless drug addicts all around. They didn’t seem aggressive but it was unsightly and sad. This hotel looks very nice on the surface but everything is cheap underneath. The woman at the front desk was very rude unfriendly and unhelpful. All of the other staff were excellent especially the front of house manager he was very nice. The upstairs restaurant pool and view were all great. the food was good and portions were huge. The furniture in the room was falling apart and there’s a window in the shower!? 🤷🏽‍♀️ our key deactivated every day and we had to go to the desk to reactivate it. The elevator is extremely slow but it runs fine and is clean.

We stayed here for two nights before starting our island hopping tour. The rooms are clean and spacious. The breakfast ample with a good variety. It is in a bit of a down trodden area but we turned right out of the hotel as well as left and wasn't threatened, just very sad to see so many lonely people

Location  is not great from the point of view of security being 200m from a drug addiction colony.

However  the stass are fantastic and gave obe approach warnings not to turn right when leaving the hotel and only turn left.

The pool has a great view of the Acropolis at night

STAY AWAY

I stayed there last night !
bed was missing a plank (as the bed was bending) that was the balcony,I didn’t bother and I did it myself.
in the evening there was a heavy engine working all night (after midnight I arrived) that wouldn’t let us sleep. I tried to call to ask what’s happening but the phone was not working as well.
The flush was  not working in the bathroom as well in result to all the  room smell after I used the bathroom without being able to flush.The cleaning lady this morning said that on the high floors the flush doesn’t work in general.
if you check my profile I am not a complainer but despite the fact that the young lady at the reception was very kind, the manager on the phone was attacking and lying that everything works properly besides the phone which he said “in busy hotels these things happen”.

very disrespectfully he said “what do you want,I’ll give you 10€ discount” .
just describing my experience

I missed my bus to Karditsa when I landed in Athens and a friendly taxi driver brought me here.   I asked to take me to a clean inexpensive spot to sleep for the night.   I was not disappointed.  The staff spoke English and understood my poor Greek.  The room was clean and the balcony was nice.   My only complaint was that it seemed a little out of date and the air condition was not working.   Thankful for the nice breeze that night

Not a good spot. We told a girl the first night our room had bed bugs and asked if we could be moved. To her credit she was very nice and said they didn’t have any available that night, but the next night we could. We then asked the next day about changing and the owner said no. He also wouldn’t offer us any sort of refund for the bed bugs, and told us if we wanted a different room the following day (we had reserved for 3 nights) it would cost us more money. He also didn’t seem even slightly surprised / caught off guard when we told him about the bed bugs. The younger girl we dealt with was great and you could tell the next day she felt really bad when the owner showed zero sympathy. Shes the only reason we gave a 2 star rather than 1 for service, but I would strongly recommend staying anywhere else.

We are very happy to were there. The Service there is very good . They helped us many many Times. The 2 Girls which worked at the Reception are very friendly . Special Thanks from us to both and also a great Thank you to the girl from the Roomservice , it was excellent . Greetings from us to all There.
Carmen & Robert Ilias

I don't know why this Hotel does not have 5 stars, because this Hotel exeeds all of my expectations.

Super air conditioner, nice, clean room with view of THE ACROPOLIS and oh man, the reception person is very friendly.

Excellent Hotel in Athens for this price!🤩

Although located in a difficult area with many drug addicted lying on the streets, this hotel is well-kept, quiet, tidy and clean. The staff is very friendly and does their best to satisfy the clients' wishes. They do not offer breakfast but the coffee shop Dodici can be accessed by means of a passageway directly from the hotel. Besides the neighbourhood is full of coffee shops and eating facilities. The hotel is very close to the Metaxourgeio metro station and the OSE railroad station. The hotel has a favourable price-performance ratio.

A staple for me in the last 4 years, whenever available.
Never had a bad experience here; top notch cleanliness and very polite staff.
The surrounding area can be dodgy, especially at night, but at the end of the day this is a central point of a touristy metropolis.
Considering the price tag, couldn't recommend it more.

For the special rate of 56 euro for my overnight stay I was very satisfied 3 important things for me number 1 rooms were immaculately clean . 2. Front of staff Valentina and the Gentlemen are always friendly and welcoming and extremely helpful I was offerded a late complementry check out also  as it's also  my second visit to the Minoa. Regarding the Location is great and a few minutes walk to the Metro to connect you to Synigma square Acroplis ect, some reviews have implicated the surronding neighborhoods it's Athens harden up . I had no proplems this time around or on my last stay.
Arrived late (1am) and needed a place to sleep for one night, great 24hr check-in, clean rooms, very friendly and helpful people. Good value at the price point
Definitely good services, very close to the metro station, good price 💯 but back streets are dangerous u need to be careful

Warning. Bed bugs. You get what you pay for. It’s the cheapest rooms in the centre of Athens. So the rooms are small, man eating mosquitoes (I thought they were mosquitoes) noisy neighbours but it also has Wi-Fi tv wash basin in the room. Main thing is that it’s cheap.
Update : Two days after leaving Argo I have insect bites all over my body. I remember being bitten in the night but I though it must be mosquitoes but I didn’t hear any. Now I’m pretty sure it’s bedbugs. I’m covered in bites. Mosquito bites feels differently. Sorry, but never again.

There's no laundry,no internet in the room's,very dirty on the stairs & nobody  cleaned it all the week.Only one(good person from reception) speaking English-it's also making problems all the way.Sometimes there was no hot water,sometimes no light on some floors.
(-)5 stars!! I was eaten by bedbugs. Now i need to go on a hospital! This place must close by LAW!
Nice personel and bathroom in room but If you have bether place to sleep GO THERE!!!
Pretty old walls and furniture, but cheap.
It is beautiful and quiet, and there is a very beautiful park near it

Quiet place
Pure atmosphere

We found our stolen luggages in the hotel after we had a broken car window, we came after our air tag and found 100 stolen luaggages after the receptionist try to hide it from us. There is a lot of homeless uses drug near the hotel. Terrible 🤮

It was the worst hotel that i hav ever been.
They welcome us with deep smoke, and right after we enter the room , odor from the toilet said hello to us.
It was quite cold day but the hitter of the room never worked. So we just felt that we were the homeless without rain.

In the beginning we were shocked. The receptionist coulnd't even retourn 30euros to 50! there was dirt and stains on the walls, small and big, old dirt on the floor, the drawer was not even cleaned from fresh dirt! cut heating tubes leading to a hole in the top floor above, and a bathroom without any basin or carpet.... round the shower. only one lamp in the room, but we got lamps fixed above our beds,
and the receptionist war very kind and helpful, advising, ... !!!
we could use the fridge downstairs behind the reception,  ...
In the room it was very loud from street noise (1st floor and old windows) and very bright cause the curtains were not too thick.
At least the beds were super clean and comfortable!!!
Anyway  - it was a good impression to imagine how common greek people might live in athen - more than comfort... :)
Cheap hotel with nicotine smell everywhere. Friendly staff and clean blankets
Super hotel,and the girl at the reception..she is so beatiful.

Every time I visit Athens I’m looking for a nice accommodation in a well connected area. This flat a part from fulfilling these requirements, has really surprised me, not only for the space and the lovely terrace  but also for the care of the owner towards us. Amazing communication and tips. I highly recommend it!

Booked this place for friends before Greek islands trip. Host was helpful with clear instructions, recommendations, and the house matched its description perfectly. Conveniently located, clean, comfy, and reasonably priced. Will recommend to future Athens visitors.
We thoroughly enjoyed our time in Athens! The apartment was more spacious than we expected, clean and with a well equipped kitchen. Would definitely recommend it :)
Booked an Apartment in Silk Mill while visiting Athens on business. George is amazing, on the ball and very responsive. Location is central and next to great food and nightlife!
We really enjoyed our stay here while visiting Athens. Everything was super clean and exactly as described. Would love to come back :) The communication with the owner was great.

Very bad location, it's a street of homelessness' that doing drugs all the night, they don't hurt but it's very bas situation to walk in such street to your hotel at night.

There is no service at all, because this hotel have nothing to offer for customer, the elevator is small, 80*80cm max, the rooms old, the bed is bad and not comfortable, the water leaking outside from the bathroom.

The front desk staff was kind and helpful, but it's not their fault that I gave this review to this hotel.

Incredible small shower,  i gurss 40 X 40 cm. Room is ok, central in Athens,  15-20 minutes walk to almost everything.
Don't think about asking the Hotel for transfer to/from airport.  They don't do it, even if it say so on their page. I asked via email twice,  no reply.  When we left i asked the reception but they didn't want to do it.  An Uber is 42 €, so if you are familiar with metro, you save a lot of money
Friendly staff,
Clean room
Good value for the money.

Even if we paid for it pretty cheap I think the hotel could have been in other conditions. Our main door was broken, same the toilet door, we were unable to close it to have privacy, bad smell, bedsheet not clear at all and bed was not comfortable.
About the neighborhood was a mess, full of yonkis, homeless and people naked and yelling. Not recommended even if it’s cheap.

What can I say? It's a very affordable place to stay for a 1-2 persons, who need a clean, safe, friendly place to sleep, to take a shower and keep their stuff. My room was clean, bright and cozy, had a small balcony, two single-beds, AC, TV, internet and a bathroom - honestly I didn't need anything else. Staff was very friendly and reception works 24/7. One thing that I've found missing (and I would be willing to pay extra for) was breakfast - on the first floor I even saw a small dining hall with a bar(?), so I guess it wouldn't be such a huge problem to add it to the offer :)

And regarding the neighborhood... Yes, it's extremely dirty and disgusting. Especially during the late hours there are dozens of homeless folks, drunks & junkies. I always tried to return to the hotel before dark..... But I wouldn't necessarily blame it on the hotel itself - the entire block between Omonia and Larissa Station looks like a southamerican slums... Blame city authorities for it!

The hotel was clean but no kettle, no hairdryer, no basic things to enjoy your stay .  The shower is so small that you keep banging the side panels .  If you want luxury or a drink in the morning ... Do not stay here.  As for the neighbourhood , we'll DO NOT TAKE YOUR KIDS HERE ! .  Junkies are shooting up openly in this area, and needles are left  on the pavement .  It is the  Bronx of Athens .  Avoid this area and book somewhere nicer .   On a plus side, the lady in the hotel reception is a lovely lady .
An excellent experience, at a very good price. Congratulations to the team, who are young and full of enthusiasm for their work and communication with the guest.

I stayed in the upstairs apartment with my boyfriend for 4 nights. Everything was great, the apartment was brand new and clean and the host Dimitris was very helpful, he even suggested places to go and some streets to avoid at night since we were staying in the center. Very close on foot there was the meter which helped us a lot in our movements. I would definitely recommend this accommodation!

Brand new, luxurious and comfortable apartments a breath away from all the worldly spots of Athens!! I booked a 5 day stay for business in the city center and I was satisfied! The service team willing, polite, always there!! Great job guys!! Nice to meet you and Hope to see you again!!

The worst hotel ever stayed in. The room was too little for 2 people. The bathroom was dirty. Ventilation was very good. Sheets were dirty and torn. There was a pretzel on the armchair, a bottle tap on the ceiling and a chewing-gum on the window pane. When we said it to the host, he didn’t say sorry nor resfunded us. Don’t go there

So a five star review but 1 star on rooms and location!
The circumstances in which we arrived at the hotel was not ideal as this was not the hotel we booked. This lovely hotel owner opened his doors to 3 vulnerable females - myself, my 2 young girls- at 330am. We were tired, just landed, walked the streets of Athens for 3 hours, harassed and hounded by a Moroccan and very drunk man and propositioned by men. Was awful!
Kamal (Hotel owner), got rid of the crazy drunk man, settled us in and gave us a room.
The area was not good for tourists as there were drug users on the street, but no-one approached us.
Got a real "ghetto experience" of how deprived ppl live in different countries so I'm not knocking it completely!!
The hotel facilities were very poor and in need of desparate modernising. Cleanliness was basic. Maybe invest some money back into the hotel rooms just as a suggestion..
AIR CON was perfect - again very old system but did the job.
This hotel was in essence a classic example of "if it aynt broke, don't fix it". Lol.
But I cannot fault the service here. The owner calmed us all down and pushed us to go out and enjoy Greece and not to let the events disturb our trip. He showed us where to go and where to eat. His wife was also a very nice lady.
Defo recommend this hotel if your not fussy on mod cons and want to live basic. But because of the integrity, professionalism and kindness of Kamal, I'd rate this 5 star. Kamal all the best brother. Will never forget the kindness.

I don’t usually give bad reviews but unfortunately there is nothing good to say about this place. Rooms were filthy, everything smelled like cigarettes, bathroom couldn’t be worse. Would not go back, even if for free. The owner should care more for his guests and business.

It’s funny and shocking because some previous reviews were negative and very inhumane, common sense! you paid less so do you really expect to be serve like 5 star hotel? this people doesn’t know hotels and expect to be serve like king/queens but wants to pay very less. Let me tell you our story, The person in the reception is  very nice to offer us umbrella because it was raining, we also arrived late around 1 am,  he advised us where to go, the bread factory is 2 mins walking distance and is 24 hours open it  is offering a lot of greek food not just pastry. He is very kind and accommodating. The surrounding place is not that posh but its ok because it was cheap and very near to places like PLAKA , Acropolis and etc. The price is right/cheap and the service is good. This people commenting bad upsets me because they let down good people. Don’t kill small business please! Book in Marriott and pay 400 euros/night then complain lol!

Lovely and satisfying rooms with nearby restaurants

Wifi , TV , air conditioning ,  Laundry are provided

Rooms are too quite with outdoor view

Breakfast are included ( boiled eggs , cheeses, yoghurt and Tea )

Meeting Room are available

Very recommended! Very new, clean and comfortable apartment with AC and washing machine + dryer (shared). Administration very quick, friendly and helpful! Good location for bus hop-on-hop-off tours & underground transport. Very good restaurant MIRONI 5 minutes walk away. The street seems not be the best neighborhood, but we didn’t have any problem.
Ok apartments for a night or two.  Locations a bit sketchy, bedding super uncomfortable.  Otherwise it’s sufficient for a few nights.
A great place to spend holidays, accessible to Metro stations and a walking distance to major landmarks in Athens. Rooms are neat and clean and the staff is super friendly

This hotel is in the ghetto. Prostitutes on one corner and a park full of homeless on the other side. It's super loud in the hotel itself because everything is marble. The hotel keys are extremely hard to use and you have to lock yourself into the room with them because there's no lock knob, but you have to lock yourself in in the dark because the key has to go into the light switch. The neighborhood is not good and it's far from all the fun places. The bed is extremely and I mean extremely hard You might as well lay on the floor. Rooms are super small. And the shower head gets your entire bathroom wet.

We arrived very late at night and had the best check-in experience ever with John, who gave us water and helped us with all the luggage we were carrying. He was extremely available and polite. The hotel is strategically located, offers a delicious breakfast with fresh Greek products like Greek yogurt, Greek salad & fruits. The room was impeccably clean. We will return.

Unsafe area! Solo female travelers should avoid staying here! Many homeless people and drug users around, lots of shit on the street 🙄. The room was passable, but I won’t stay here again. Lots of noise at night, next to a huge multi-floor parking lot. One night I heard the police nearby because some people were arguing/fighting on the street. The breakfast was simple, and the coffee tasted terrible. Not enough seats during breakfast so some people had to wait for some time.

The rooms are clean, fully functional and look nice. Service at reception and breakfast was good. A really nice hotel!
There are a lot of pigeons so you can't open the windows for long or sit on the balcony.

We were stuck in Athens without a flight back home, we booked this hotel for 2 nights. Less than an hour after the booking we were surprised to find out there's a ticket for us to fly home. We cancelled the booking, yet the property refused to refund and charged FULL price. This is pure greed. We didn't cost them anything in the minutes we had this booking. Where is the humanity? We understand why businesses want to protect themselves from last minute cancellations and money lost. But we had to cancel after less than an hour!!! Shame on you!

The hotel is not far from the center/metro station which makes comfortable to get your preferred place quickly. The placement of the hotel is not so good because of drug addict and homeless people around. Street smells terrible and everyone should be careful walking there especially at night. But anyway, the staff was so kind to us, so professional and supportive. Overall, hotel itself was clear and comfortable.

Nice hotel, comfortable room, good breakfast and fair price. Location is near the center and the Omonoia square, from where the tourist buses depart. However, the neighborhood itself is horrible. Drug addicts, prostitutes and homeless people on every corner, piles of garbage and smell of feces... Better to avoid after dark.
You love in this Hotel taxi drivers will Quickly tell you how bad the area is. Addicts litter the streets and a smell of piss permiate right outside the door.

It's located in Omonia close to Syntagma and Monastiraki
The room was clean but small for 3 persons..
The fridge excellent and freezing cold..
With a balcony and good breakfast...
Also it has a front sitting area...
Only thing missing from room a bin..
The Worst thing about the hotel is a low end area....you are bit afraid to walk on late hours

Athens Choice Hotel was a great place to stay! If you're looking for something smaller yet really nice I recommend this place. The service was also great, the employees at the front desk were super nice. I felt bad that I didn't speak any Greek and was worried this would be an issue but they were super helpful and kind.

This hotel is close to everything you want to see within Athens, by foot and metro. I am a big walker so this place was great for that.

Breakfast was also very yummy! ευχαριστώ! :)

We stayed at this hotel last week. Overall, the stay was good. The location was decent, we walked everywhere in Athens. I would not recommend walking out late at night alone, seems a bit sketchy. We had issues with our air conditioning not working but they responded quickly to resolve this. You are unable to turn the air lower than 22 degrees C which is a bit warmer than I would like. The breakfast was pretty good, they give you many options. Last thing, they advertise some of the rooms having a view of the Acropolis, however, there is not a view at all from any room. Overall, not bad.

Read very carefully. I can assure you this is 100% true what I’m about to write:
We arrived around 20:00. there were a lot of  homeless people and addicted people walking around the location of the hotel. 2 policemen were also doing a search on them.
We saw also 2 sex workers waiting in the street in front of the hotel (yes, we are 100% sure that they were prostitutes).

We enter the hotel because we were scared to stay outside any longer. The staff gives us the keys and we go up.

The room : the room was big, but the furniture was cheap (this should be a 5 star hotel) , the floor very ruined, the air conditioning BROKEN, and one window was impossible to close .

We went out to eat, but all the activities around had obviously very bad hygiene problems.

I rarely write negative ratings. But I hope that no more people have to experience this.

Don’t trust the positive reviews! They are made intentionally by other people that have interest in keeping this hotel going!

Dirty pillow cases and bedsheets. Pls see pictures. Looks like dirt stains. The hotel seems to be new but the housekeeping needs to do a better job. I found breadcrumbs next to the sofa and hair all over the floor.

I booked online and came to the hotel and they couldn't find my booking, which is fine but the lady at the reception was rude. The whole tone of voice is impolite. She asked for 1-2 minutes and only after 25 minutes the situation was resolved.
There was another tall gentleman in the lobby who offered me water while I was waiting and he clearly knew about customer service and the basics of smiling.

The hotel is at a busy intersection. I stayed on Saturday and the grocery store across the hotel kept playing loud music until 2:30am. I called the reception to notify them of LOUD music and the receptionist never got back to me. He told me he will see what he can do and get back to me but never did. I went down to the shopkeeper to tell him to turn off the music and to stop causing Boise disturbance at 2:30am. They claim to have double glazed windows and sound proofing but I could hear motorbikes passing by all night and some people speaking outside on the streets. I couldn't sleep at all.

The toilet is so small that I could barely fit inside, really small. The AC doesn't work and has some sort of a timer on and it turns off on its own.

Overall it was a bad experience. Better pay 20 euro more and have a proper accomodation. This was not good.
We loved this hotel. Great location.  Beautiful,  staff was helpful, and the breakfast was included and delicious.

The room I stayed at first floor was spacious but the balcony was closed, the walls were dirty and furniture dusty.  The choices and food quality for dinner and breakfast are poor for a 4 star hotel category. And some kitchen staff was rude, telling me that some dinner dishes were only for certain group of guests in the hotel and that my group shoul have come to dinner at later time that day.
The area around the hotel is creepy with junkies at every corner at any hour of the day.

We recently stayed at the Mansion Hotel, and while there were some positive aspects, the overall experience was overshadowed by its location.

First, the good: The service at the Mansion is commendable. The staff and the owner are friendly and accommodating, making sure all your basic needs are met. The hotel itself is newly renovated, which is a nice touch—everything feels fresh, modern, and well-maintained. The rooms, while a bit small, have all the essentials you need for a comfortable stay. Additionally, the hotel is quite affordable, making it a budget-friendly option for travelers.

Unfortunately, the location is a major downside. Despite being near a number of shops, the area surrounding the hotel feels unsafe, especially at night. There were many drunk people loitering outside, and I also noticed individuals engaging in drug use nearby. It's the kind of environment that makes you want to stay inside after dark and avoid venturing out. The hotel itself can only do so much, but the surrounding area is a huge detractor and something to seriously consider before booking.

Overall, while the Mansion Hotel offers good service and clean accommodations, the poor location is a significant issue. If safety and surroundings matter to you, I would recommend looking elsewhere.

This hotel is minimal.  It is in an area with drugs and other bad things to the right.  But you are a 20 minute walk to Acropolis.  Rooms are so small and no place to put your stuff.  Bathroom is not existent.  I would rate zero if it wasn't so close to major attraction.

Worst hotel experience of my life! Avoid at all cost. It is not worth it at all, super sketchy neighborhood and you will end up paying a lot of money for taxis to avoid the sketchy people in the street.

The hotel is very well kept and in a super convenient place. Everything can be walked to very easily. The hotel staff is absolutely fantastic. Super friendly and will give you a map and help you find anything you want. They will also offer tourism advice which was very helpful. The rooms are small but convenient. Very well kept again, and relatively modern. They have everything you would need from a hotel to have a pleasant stay. The hotel also offers a small lobby type section above reception which was very comfortable. The location didn’t receive 5/5 stars and only 4/5 because at night the street it is on is a bit run down. It is still safe, and I was assured of that, just run down and over crowded with people.

The staffs at the hotel were very nice and helpful. Reception is open 24X7 and you can ask for help anytime you need and you feel safe inside the hotel. I am a great fan of cleanliness and the cleanliness of the room was near to perfect. All the amenities listed were present and in good working conditions. The only problem was the locality we had to pass each time we went inside and outside the hotel, which was not so good. But that is not the fault of the hotel, they try their best to make the guests feel comfortable. The hotel is at a central location and all the important places are nearby and well connected.
Dirty hotel that provides poor service, but advisable for half day stays or on hourly basis.
Not good at all there is no service and not recommend it. No soap and no comfortable bed and small tv.
Great  staff always clean rooms and very cheap
Good room
Cheap and good ,but not good environment.
Great location. Fantastic new hotel. Room very well equipped. Bed very comfortable. Staff smiling and always helpful. Very good value for an enjoyable visit in Athens.

Staff is lovely and pleasant for checkin and room is clean.  I only stayed here for one night and it’s an efficient apartment for the price.  Good wifi and air conditioning.
I did not use the amenities and overheard the staff telling other guests they were out of order.
The one thing to note is the neighborhood.  Although not the most glamorous, I think if you do not plan on coming back late at night you will be fine. I felt safe in this area but I was also back shortly after dark.
There are also security men at the door at nighttime as well as a 24/7 reception.

I recently stayed at this hotel in Athens, and unfortunately, my experience was quite disappointing.

Cons:
The WiFi was extremely slow and practically unusable, which made it difficult to stay connected.
We had to switch rooms because the shower in our first room leaked everywhere. Unfortunately, we had to wait two days for a spare room, and even the second room had a water leak. It seems like the hotel’s plumbing issues are a recurring problem.
There was no reliable hot water, making showering uncomfortable.
The location felt a bit sketchy at night, and I wouldn’t recommend this place for solo travelers.
The hotel is still under construction, so there's no access to advertised amenities like the rooftop or gym, which was a letdown.

Pros:
The hotel is modern with clean rooms, and the housekeeping staff were friendly and efficient.
It’s located close to the metro, which made getting around the city relatively easy.
Overall, despite its modern design and cleanliness, the issues with plumbing, slow WiFi, and the lack of amenities due to ongoing construction really detracted from the stay. I wouldn’t recommend it, especially for solo travelers.

Recently renovated, modern hotel in the heart of Athens. Excellent communication from the service team throughout the stay. Large modern rooms with kitchen, extremely clean and high quality. Highly recommended

Apartment is new. But at the same time cleaning is bad. A lot of dust there. I have allergy for dust.
Also I found broken socket. If somebody there with children it could be very dangerous. Water in the fridge isn’t mineral but tap’s water. Sponge for dishes was used!!!

I was surprised that they didn’t give me towels, I had to go to reception to ask.

Swimming pool and gym didn’t work because it is still under construction.

Wi-fi is very slow. I couldn’t work with my laptop.

Location is bad! many homeless people. I felt very scared and unsafe.

The hotel is great, you enter and feel  secure. The location is 2 streets north, away from the better part of Athens.  If you have never experienced or walked through a rough neighborhood NYC , with the graffiti everywhere ,you will be uncomfortable. There are a few addicts loitering, but they don't bother anyone. Additional mental comfort ,the police station is the building next to the hotel, with cops always at the front of their building. Good reasonable priced food at the diner across the street. I would definitely stay there again.

Mostly wanted to write a review as my wife tried to leave an honest review, only for the owner to try and have it removed for "hate speech".
Issues with AC but we were given another room. They then gave someone else the same room. Luckily the door was physically locked so the poor man couldn't get in. When I spoke to him, he showed me our room number. I hope he was given an appropriate room or an upgrade.
Someone else mentioned their room smelling of smoke, which I think was to do with the ac as we came back to the same thing.
Staff on the desk were nice, cleaners were lovely people and so helpful. The kitchen...not sure what's going on there but they don't seem to enjoy what they do. Overcooked meats and overly salted. Don't expect to be able to choose from the menu if you book half board. They tell you what you will have for dinner and it will disappoint. The sauces on the pudding I'm 90% sure are just strawberry and chocolate ice cream syrups. No love for the food at all. You will only get a buffet if there's enough people and it will likely be the same options each time. Chicken and some overcooked pork/lamb steak.
The other staff always seem reluctant to call down to the kitchen. I think they know that's where the problem is and they must get some attitude from them rejecting orders.
Location is right next to the police station. This is for a reason. Locals warned us to stay away from the area out to the right of the hotel. Huge problem with drugs in that area and it does not look safe.
The hotel overall looks great at a first glance but a closer look shows it was a rushed job.
A lot of work to do and a new chef is definitely needed.

Upon arrival we were assigned a room which smelled really strongly of cigarette smoke. We complained and the room was changed but we should never have been given it in the first place. Rooms are extremely small but have everything you need. On day two we came home late at night to find that cleaning staff had left our room door open with our valuables inside. The worst thing about this hotel though has to be the other guests. They scream in the corridors, call the lift when it’s not needed and at the breakfast buffet they pile their plates high only to leave the food uneaten (absolutely disgusting behaviour). The best thing about the staff are the ladies who work in the restaurant. They are clearly over worked but had great patience and worked constantly. They are a credit to the hotel. The surrounding area is extremely sketchy so I’d be aware if staying here with children or older guests. I personally would not stay here again.

Centrally located, 10-15 minutes walk from cafes and restaurants, tourist areas.
Just one street down from Omonia square and the metro.
The rooms were small but clean. It has a large balcony.
Iron, ironing board and mini bar are available in the room. There is also a complimentary red wine in the room. There are all the materials you may need in the room.
Breakfast may vary according to your expectations but it was enough for me.
The staff is interested and friendly.

really nice cozy hotel pretty central. everything is walking distance.
the rooms are stunning!
breakfast is a okay not really diversified or tasty.
only issue is the neighborhood seems full of delinquent (even though a police station is right next to it)

Super hotel, clean and staff are very friendly. Ask for an iron and it's there in minutes. Location is great minutes from everything. Only downside is that Athens, like any major city has a problem with homeless & drug abuse, which is largely situated in the hotel area. My advice would be it's great for walking in the day. However, to return to the hotel at night get a taxi

average hotel doesn't deserve 4 stars
good stuff. clean room.
breakfast is mediocre.
service is below average as the standard and executive rooms don't have kettle!!! and there wasn't water refill after the 1st day in addition to the lack of slippers!!! how come 4 stars Hotel lacks that much???
the location isn't interesting and the surrounding area is filled with homeless and durg addicts but there is also police in the area

The staff were really lovely and helpful. I was a bit disappointed with the vegan breakfast options (there was fruit, peanut butter toast and muesli only) I thought there would be more options as they said they could cater to different dietary needs on their website.

Maybe adding some vegan sausages or something to the menu, and having vegan margarine to go on toast could help with that (maybe?)

Overall the hotel was nice

My stay at the hotel was not good, except for the night staff and the cleaning lady, who were nice and very professional. The breakfast was very simple, and the coffee was not good. The room was very noisy because of the cars, and the area did not feel safe. I don’t recommend this hotel.

Our room was clean , with a big balcon and had everything you need. The area around the hotel is not the best, but i will give 5 stars because the lady on the reception was really thoughtful and friendly.

The customer service was excellent. Everyone was nice, especially George in the afternoon at the reception( extremely friendly and funny). The rooms were nice. you would get your typical room service every day, including new towels and clean thw room. The breakfast was also nice. It included scrambled eggs, boiled eggs, natural greek yoghurt , cereals, a variety of bread  ham  cheese, and so on. The only downside was the area. It is located in a bad area full of drug addicts and homeless people. Although unpleasant to see, I still felt safe, and nothing happened  at all despite warnings from taxi drivers. You have a nice square near the hotel with a lot of restaurants and dessert places and  it  is also located near the underground which is very convenient.

We stayed 3 nights and didn’t sleep at all, the room next to us was making noise at every hour all night we called the front desk and they told us they weren’t going to do anything since the room next to us had children. Didn’t compensate us at all. Worst service, rooms were old and needed touch ups. The bathroom sink had mold and water damage and was leaking continuously. The bathroom door had duck tape covering holes all over it. There were cracks in the ceiling. Not the best, would not recommend.

The hotel is at a very convenient location, at the city centre. Everything is close by, for example the Acropolis is approx 30 min walk, with everything else in between. The metro is approx 3 min walk for a connection to other parts of the city.

The room is spacious, and clean. Good for two people. Fridge is available for storing cold stuff. There is a balcony but there isn't a good view anyways. However, I can't find the heating for the room, so it might get cold in winter night.

The toilet is clean with shower facilities. They provide breakfast for €5 per person, continental breakfast buffet, decent spread of food. The surrounding area is filled with graffiti so it feels dangerous to walk outside at night though. But good food from breakfast to dinner outside.
Small hotel , nice atmosphere allthough the area can be a bit confronting at night. Staff are very friendly

In an awful area. Dirty, lots of homeless/drugs hanging around. Graffiti everywhere. The rooms were very basic. The shower head kept falling off the wall. I did report it,but nothing got done. Towels were old and were unraveling. I wanted to move, but couldn't afford to. The breakfast was ok. Toast, cereal, cheese slices, weird meat slices and a bowl of apples.
I would not recommend it.

The hotel is good and the attention was as well. They offered us an upgraded room, which was great, without any charge and the breakfast is ok.

The only negative aspect is that is located in a not very nice neighborhood

Very pleasant hotel with convenient location, close to everything. Rooms are clean, modernised. Hotel staff is very friendly and professional. We had to leave very early to the train station and the receptionist prepared us a small breakfast to go, which was very nice of him. Definitely recommend for a short stay in the centre.

It’s one of the cheapest hotels you can get that’s within walking distance to a train station, the city centre, the main attractions AND the pick up point for Meteora day tours.

That said, manage your expectations because it’s very basic. If you are not too picky and travelling as a couple or group of friends on a very tight budget, this is a great choice, but if you’re used to 5-star hotels this one might come as a bit of a shock. Prepare to do some stair climbing as the lifts are slow and can only fit 2 - 4 people excluding luggage. The hotel breakfast is pretty good for the price and the staff are friendly.

Not advisable for solo female travellers as you don’t need an access card to go up the room floors and the room doors have no peephole or a chain lock.
