We checked in on 7 July 2024 the night staff with silver hair was super rude. The room was not clean ( more of a two star hotel if not less).
Location is close to city centre but walking at night defo felt dangerous and seems like a shady area.
Breakfast food was basic but hygiene was a concern so we didn't have any ; The water dispenser had some black stuff.
Carpeted floor in the room felt gross as well
Definitely NOT a four star hotel

Suitable for a short stay of 1-3 nights, though it does not meet the standards of a 4-star hotel. The rooms require improvement, and several amenities need repair, such as the elevator and shower.

The breakfast offers a wide variety of choices and is generally satisfactory. However, the scrambled eggs were among the weakest I have ever tried.

place_name: Dave Red Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: I was on a business trip. Every chain I mostly used was fully booked and I really didn't know what to expect from Dave Red. This my first time in Son of a Brown chain and in Dave Red. Check in was easy, very friendly and smooth. Room was well equipped and nice. Not a big one but very nice cozy room. Breakfast was very nice and nice variety of selection.  All staff was very friendly and helpful throughout my 12 days stay. The Hotel is in heart of the city and every mode of public transport is within 3 mins walk.  I recommend this hotel for both leisure and business stay.  Thank you for the Hotel team to make my stay nice and unforgettable.

place_name: Dave Red Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: My friend and I stayed at Dave Red for 4 nights. The staff were lovely and the hotel was great, with a gym and jacuzzi. The breakfast was included in the cost of our stay, and offered a range of nice food. The hotel’s location means everything in the city centre is just a short walk away, which was perfect. We would definitely stay again if we came back!

place_name: Dave Red Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: We loved our time in Athens! The room was clean and cosy as it was quite small, we loved the vibe of the hotel and everyone was very friendly. We were in the hot tub every night but it was unfortunately out of order on our final night :/ it was the perfect place to get to the big areas but the surroundings of the hotel were quite daunting, alot of drug addicts around and some seedy characters but had no trouble with them! if you can look past this you should definitely think about staying here :) Thanks Dave! xx

place_name: Dave Red Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: My partner and I stayed here for two nights after booking at another hotel that ended up transferring us to Dave Red Athens and we are so glad they did! The staff were incredible and helped us sort out a luggage problem and never made us feel like an inconvenience. The hotel is beautifully decorated and the bed was super comfy. They included breakfast for us which was delicious with a good range of food. We would definitely stay again if we return to Athens!

place_name: Dave Red Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text:
When we arrived for our stay, our room was upgraded which was a lovely surprise! The rooms are gorgeous and the hotel is located so close to everything.
The best thing is the amazing staff. My boyfriend unfortunately got food poisoning and we had to extend our stay another 2 nights to get the next available flight and despite going to reception at 4am in a panic, the lovely girl at reception accommodated us in extending our stay in the same room.
We cannot thank you enough❤️

The hotel was quite comfortable to relax in with no any distraction or noise nearby and had the necessary facilities. I liked the way the hotel rooms were organized, and thanks for getting our request well in advance of arrival. Regarding the service cleaning, there should have been slightly better routines, the cleaners are hardly visible to the guests in the hotel, it is good that they are doing their job, but we have noticed that they knock and open the door at the same time without us knowing when they plan to clean our room. The other negative view is that the shower could have been prepared a little better as the water runs out to the basin and onto the floor in some cases without a glass protector or shower curtain. Otherwise, I appreciate the breakfast service at the hotel, they had a very delicious and hearty breakfast that was served every morning, we really liked this and the staff who work there are very nice and calm people.

Me and my partner recently visited this hotel for a weeks stay in Athens. The room was basic and we had booked a double room but instead we were given two single beds pushed together. The comfort of the beds was fine for us both, worth noting here that we are a young couple. The shower was powerful, hot and the hotel provided clean towels. They also provided a hairdryer but the one in our room was broke, I asked the first morning for a new one and they gave us one. There was a mini fridge in the room which was great to keep a couple of bits cool and the air conditioning worked great. The staff were friendly and helped if needed. The hotel is a 15 min walk from the centre which was ideal for us. The breakfast was nice, we are vegetarian and there was enough options for variety thought out the week.

This hotel will be ideal for anybody who wants somewhere to stay while in Athens but are not looking for any luxury.

Great value for money as it's centrally located within bus stops and metro station within few minutes of walk. Staff was generous to change my room from single beds to double bed, as my booking agent erroneously booked single beds.
Note that the lane around the hotels doesn't seem to be safe and be cautious while roaming around at night

i would really not advice going out past midnight because the area is generally known for being dangerous at night. that said, at day time you’re close to the center, the metro, and the big shops. rooms were good and breakfast was great, although the coffee was super watery. they should consider getting rid of the fat balding guy at the reception though.

Cons:
- The rooms were very very basic and the cig smoke would come right into the room.
-tv wouldn't work in any other language.
-Breakfast was not good and if you're not there the first hour, it's heavily picked over
-Bedding is very itchy and towels are hard
-Location I would give a 3 out of 5
-No microwave
-Rooms could be cleaner, but thats mostly a dust issue (note, I didn't open the windows)
-Noise carries A LOT. If there are ppl in the hall, it will feel like theyre in your room

Pros:
- Spacious rooms and BIG bathrooms
-Accept night walkins
-Market/Cafe diagonally across the street
-Has mini fridge
-Air con worked well
-A bit more on the pricier side for what you get, but still not bad
-Staff were not rude. Though we didn't interact with them much

Things to note:
- This area is a bit loud
-This is an area where there are A LOT of the unhoused community. They were not aggressive at all. Some places yell at them, but I don't think they deserved it.
-The train in this area isn't great and is a hot spot for pickpockets
-To get to the busy and social area, you will realize that youre on the outskirt of the area.

At first, I was concerned. The area did not look great. But the room was decent, clean. The staff was wonderful. The breakfast was amazing and was 15 minute walk to basically everything. We did walk up to the central square at night as well as back from site seeing about 10pm. No issues for us. But still be aware of your surroundings.

We (17 people) stayed here for 7 nights in several rooms. The staff was very friendly and helpful. The rooms were clean and the breakfast was very basic but ok.

Unfortunately we had bed bugs in two rooms and 3 members of our group had bug bites all over there arms. I asked the staff to check for bed bugs and they promised they would search the room while we were sightseeing. I got a call from the manager that they couldnt find any bed bugs but changed all the towels and sheets. When we came back later in the day we wanted to make sure that there are no bugs anymore. We found them immediately after 2 minutes of searching. We then got 2 new rooms.

In another room (1st floor) it constantly smelled like someone smoked inside. My guess is that the cleaning staff smokes right next to the AC units and the smoke is sucked up and distributed to the rooms.

The neighborhood is full of drug addicts, prostitutes and dealers and there is a crazy smell of urin right in front of the hotel.

Would I recommend the hotel? If you are a male person and do not want to stay longer than 2 nights it might be ok. Don’t come here with children.

I enjoyed my time at the hotel, everything was so nice both the food and the service by Stelios, Stella, Sophia ,Vivi and Sissy. They were very polite,very welcoming and made me and my family feel like home. Especially in the kitchen I would like to thank Chrysa she was really kind ,humble and honest.(they really know how to treat customers like family)
Bed bugs! Stayed there for 4 nights first night my son was bit at least 20+ from bed bugs, he was given another room but zero compensation. Location is terrible, drug users all around the outside.

A basic hotel in central Athens. Nice location, v handy for all the touristy things. Staff lovely. Rooms very basic and bathroom v small. Go for the 4th floor as very quiet. AC which was great. Would stay again because of staff and location. A great restaurant nearby in Omania, great lamb chops!

Rooms were on the smaller size with basic necessities. The shower is quite small and there are no shelves to put your toiletries, had to keep opening the shower doors for stuff. The breakfast included in our stay was good, basic standard faire but lacking variety. The one great thing was the water pressure and temperature, very strong and consistent hot water.

Average breakfast. Rooms are a little small but not tiny. Good price for location.

Staff is polite and amazing.

We had an amazing and comfortable stay in Athens at Evita Asty! Spacious room. AC could have been a bit cooler, we found that it didn’t work consistently. Shower door also needs to be fixed. Water slips out as the door is towards the back, but perhaps be toward the front/another panel added. Also rooms aren’t very soundproof, could hear people from outside on the streets.

Great location, close to nearby metro station, Onomia. Check out Delicious Souvlaki by the station, best souvlaki we had!

The room was small but clean. Room cleaning and towel replacement regular. Breakfast was great - buffet style wide selection hot and cold stuffs, cereal, fruit, yoghurt, bread and pastries. For those who are reasonably mobile, the hotel was within walking distance of many of the main attractions. We felt safe there, but some parts nearby could be viewed as a bit 'rough'. This is a budget hotel and quite good value for money, but there were some minor irritations, The room we had was short on sockets for charging, we had to unplug the fridge to charge phones tablets etc, so take a multi-charging plug with you. There was also no key to open the balcony door, though staff were happy to come and open it for you. Staff were polite, friendly and helpful. Overall I am happy to recommend to others, and would certainly consider staying there again.

Located in kind of sketchy area in Athens. Room was adequate but not roomy. In our particular room, the shower was missing a door, we think, and when in operation, the water would run out of the shower into the toilet area underneath the shower glass. It seemed the seal was bad. For a 4 star rated hotel to not have cable television is new to me but did have wifi. Maybe my expectations are higher, but trying to get any news or any type of entertainment before going to sleep...take a laptop or tablet and make sure your netflix or amazon prime video is paid up. The breakfast was good and had more options than I  have ever had at a hotel breakfast and the food was hot and not the pre-packaged kind...they actually cooked it...which was great! Overall, I  rate this a mid-level rating due the affordability but the location was not the most desirable.

We got the room after they told us the apartment we booked at the near neighbourhood is not available.
To be honest, after we passed on that neighborhood on our way to the hotel we were glad since that street seems really sketchy and quite crappy.
The room itself in the hotel is pleasant, vintage looking, clean with great wifi and a small balcony.
The stuff were friendly and welcoming, the only thing to improve is the vegan options in the breakfast buffet.

The rooms are small but really comfortable. Air conditioning and smart TV. I really liked the breakfast with plenty of options. The view from the top floor is also really nice. I recommend it to everyone. The neighbourhood is a bit ugly if you have a car you need to look for a parking lot.

Terrible Service – Beware of the Apartment Scam: This hotel also manages apartments, and when we arrived at the reception to collect the keys for our apartment, we were told that they had double-booked it, and the apartment was no longer available (despite us booking for two nights). Instead, they put us in a hotel room that was clearly meant for staff, not guests.

After reading other reviews, it seems this "missing apartment keys" scam has happened to several other people as well, so beware if you're planning to book an apartment here!

To make matters worse, the receptionist was rude and acted annoyed, as if the situation was somehow our fault instead of their mistake.

It's disgraceful. I can’t understand how this place is still in business. On top of that, it's located in a very run-down area, with junkies hanging around right outside the entrance. I won’t be returning here, ever.

The hotel is in a less than wonderful area, with people sleeping on the streets close by. It's in a handy location to metro and bus for exploring the sights.
The rooms are OK, breakfast quite good, but cold. Don't have the buffet dinner, again cold and very limited choice. Staff were pleasant and helpful.

The only good thing about the hotel is friendly staff. Maybe also the dolmadaki we ate at breakfast. The rooms were very tiny. The bathroom is not proper. While showering, the water spreads all over the bathroom floor. No slippers or flip flops. Wet floor no slippers no hygiene unfortunately. The district in which the hotel exists is full of homeless people and junkies. They gather towards evening. We were afraid while returning to hotel in the evening. Streets were not safe. No coffee table,  no table no chairs in the room or balcony. No place to eat or drink sth in the room. No table for women to put makeup stuff in the room.
The rooms are smelly and the worst thing is the man in the reception
Hotel very basic and rundown, the worst was the the guy at the reception, never seen so rude an receptionist....
SARO000

The man at the reception is drunk, the rooms are burnt out, smelly and dirty, it's scary to think what happened there. It shouldn't be called a hotel or even a hostel. I advise you to run away from here as far as possible!!!
The attention was excellent, I paid 10 Euros for 24 hours, shower, air conditioning, TV, bed, everything was very nice.

Just if you want to save more money as possible. The building falls into pieces, as well as the toilets that must be shared with the rest of the hotel. At least the guy at reception is really friendly. They even don't have a computer to manage the reservations, everything is written on a book by hand
It's not even "basic", it could only be maked worse if the owner deliberately intended to achieve the title of "Worst Hotel in Europe".

It old building but clean
And it's staff are kind and friendly
I like it
What I like was only the price. You pay what it deserves.
ok good prices/quality

user_name: ひきこもり。高橋正臣。Masaomi Eric Takahashi.
review_text:
The finest, cleanest and most comfortable place in the entire Greece! I strongly recommend this 5 stars hotel! It’s facing the main road so you’ll not feel too lonely.
I love the soft beautiful mattress and the fluffy pillows! It’s fantastic! Amazing price!
I just have to give this place a review of 5 Stars.
Google doesn’t condone to any reviews less than 5 Stars! Otherwise, they’ll send you a shrill and condescending message!

The owner of the property in the same day told me to go to the hotel Golden city to pick up the keys. I replied ok and I will arrive by 11pm.

Surprise. Once arrived to the hotel they did not leave anything. I called by phone 3 times. No answer at all.

They left me stranded at 11pm in the middle of a dangerous neighborhood. There were many people with drug addiction problems.

I highly recommend to look the others reviews carefully and advise to where to report them to avoid it happens again to future tourists.

It is dangerous really.

The rooms are comfortable and good, but there is a flaw in that they are not sound insulated, but given that they are sunny and good in terms of ventilation. The staff is nice and energetic, and the receptionist is nice and energetic, which makes customers excited to visit the hotel again

STAY AWAY!!!
Horrible horrible horrible place. Probably the worst place I've ever spent a night.
The neighborhood is full of junkies, we're talking zombies with their arms rotting off. Feels extremly unsafe, we we're even warned by our driver to come to this part of Athens.
The rooms look nothing like the pictures, the walls are made from thin plastic sheets. The window wouldn't close so the noise from the street was horrible. We had to plug our ears with toiletpaper go get a wink of sleep. The rooms felt dirty and our bed had a huge stain from what looked like piss in the mattress. It was a nightmare and I will never go back, if you have ANY other choice don't come here.

Failure all along the line. Photos published on the Internet don't square with what we found in this place, especially in our rooms. The first surprise was the lift being so small-sized that it cannot accommodate three people – maximally two of them and they have to be almost on top of one another. Secondly, a state of rooms. Beds are crooked, toilets haven't any islanded space to take a shower so after cleaning oneself up the whole room is being turned into small pool. Wi-Fi doesn't work fast as well, it lasts a minute to load one simple website. There were fortunately clean towels and sheets, tips about our room were transparently written and sent by the owner too. Additionally, it sits in the area of drug users and other dodgy men (two of them were sitting at the door of this place) so families with children and those who travel alone should give a wide berth to this place. One of few advantages, that tempted us to book here a room, were keen prices but – as this example shows – it's not everything. This place looks as if it was going to decline.

place_name: Brown Lighthouse Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text:
Perfect location, to all attractions. Lots of taxi choices or 2 stops on metro. Really easy. Can get a 5 day metro card for 8 euros. Staff amazing and friendly. Food perfect and hotel really clean and perfect decoration for a city break.

If there was one negative- we stayed  when it was extremely hot. Only 4/5 umbrella available which meant a lot of sunbeds/ seats in direct sun. So it was a race in the mornings to get a sun bed which would be in the shade.

place_name: Brown Lighthouse Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: This is a great value conveniently located hotel in Athens beside the Omonia metro station. A smart rooftop pool and bar area, good breakfast and above all friendly staff. I must commend one of the reception staff who went above and beyond in his interactions with us.

place_name: Brown Lighthouse Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text:
Had a wonderful stay over Easter. Great location with transport links, local shops and eateries. Roof terrace is lovely with amazing views across the city.
Rooms are really nice but they are quite dark and lacking on storage space. Cleaned every day to a high standard. Breakfast is absolutely amazing! So much choice and everything was always fresh. Something for everyone.
All the staff are truly lovely and this is why I would definitely recommend the hotel to anyone staying in Athens. Kind, polite and helpful people that really want to make sure you enjoy your time and have everything you need. Special mention to the two gentlemen that run the bar of an evening - it was so nice getting to know them.
We will be back soon. Thanks for having us!

place_name: Brown Lighthouse Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text:
A great location in middle of Athens near the Omonoia square.
Room service was always good, hotel service “reception” was great too except the time when we arrived they were ok making a silly assumption.
There is a pool “not a swimming one” and a bar with a great view. You can have a cocktail while sun bathing.
Will stay there again when I come back

place_name: Brown Lighthouse Athens, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: Amazing hotel,they do think of everything! Perfectly located,extremely modern,clean,amazing decor and scent. Super rooftop bar with pool and jacuzzi looking over the Acropolis. There’s massage service and gym and restaurant with beautiful food. But above all the staff is beyond friendly and helpful,down to the last cleaning lady. Kristos from reception was so nice,he welcomed us after a long journey with drinks etc on arrival and continue to look out for anything we needed. Thank you Skylark for a great time

We were a little concerned reading some of the reviews however the hotel was great for a two night stay.
Pros - location is easy to walk to and from, metro and taxi ranks just outside. Bed was very comfy and rooms were quiet. The staff were very kind and helpful too. Breakfast… amazing. Overall for the price it was good.

Cons - yes the area is dodgy, but it’s a capital city and if you go in with that expectation it’s fine. There is security at the door and we didn’t feel concerned walking back to the hotel after dark. The rooms are small and quite dark and if you’re staying longer perhaps opt for a larger room. The bathroom does flood but we just put a towel at the shower entrance which solved the problem.

In summary, it does the job, is comfy and quite good value for money.

On the first day, the friendly staff of the hotel welcomed us by sending us a delicious wine and champagne and a beautiful letter.🥂
An excellent and luxurious hotel, very nice staff, clean rooms, great access to the center of Athens, swimming pool and hot tub on the roof of the hotel.
A pleasant walk to the ancient monuments and street markets in Athens and then back to the hotel and enjoy the swimming pool and jacuzzi and sun on the roof of the hotel with a view of the Acropolis perspective.
There are many positive points of this hotel.
Of course, if we want to judge critically, there are two things for managers to pay attention to.  The pool is small and shallow & our room did not have a closet, and of course, other rooms may have closets.
But we were very satisfied and will definitely come back to this hotel.

Not located in the best part of town. Daytime is fine but please be cautious if going out after dark. Check in at 3pm took a long time as our room was not cleaned. Rooms are painted black and very dark. We had a Jr suite with two bathrooms, which was nice. Fire alarm went off three days in a row. One of the ac units had a bad fan that would occasionally make lots of noise. Rooftop bar is decent but the pool is only knee deep so don’t expect to actually swim. Our stay was decent but if we return, we would stay in a more secure and safe neighborhood. The traffic outside the hotel is very hectic and the exhaust fumes are pretty strong. The breakfast was quite impressive so if you’re staying here, take advantage of this.

On the first night, we arrived around midnight and they gave us a wake-up call at 5.30 AM which we had definitely not ordered.
The fire alarm went off twice (once in the day and once at 1 AM).
The rooms are very dark. We did not like the lighting installations because they were bright and placed in spots which were blinding but the room would still be pretty dark at night.
One of the room types have a door into the neighbouring room which allows you to hear your neighbour very clearly.
The doors play a melody when you scan your card to open it, so you can hear your neighbours scan their cards while you are in your room as well.
The toilet was black with a plastic seat which was not very appealing. The faucet is placed weirdly so water gets all over the bathroom counter when you use the faucet.
Some of the staff was friendly, but some were a bit rude in their tone.
The breakfast does not display allergens on the cards and there is no rotation on the menu so you get the same selection every morning. The atmosphere at the breakfast buffet was nice though, with music and nice furniture and the morning sun shining into the area.

My room was a standard room on the 4th floor that was extremely small and absolutely no soundproofing between rooms. It had a small balcony door and facing another building that was carrying out construction work from early morning.

However, the staff on the roof top was amazing, the woman in the bar, I forgot her name (ginger hair) and Magdalena and the man with a beard, you made my stay! The pool is amazing, prices are pretty high but worth going up there to escape from the claustrophobic rooms.

The gym is small, but does the job. I peaked in the wellness area, but happy I didn't book a massage there, not a welcoming space between the gym and the breakfast area.

Location is good if you quickly get out of the square that is pretty dodgy.

Nice clean and spacious rooms!
Brand new hotel with welcoming staff and a rooftop with pool and acropolis view.
There is also a gym.
The cleaning of our room was perfect.
The hotel provides pool towels that you get from the lifeguard.
Overall a very positive experience!
Thank you for accommodating us.

I absolutely loved my stay here as a female solo traveler!! The hotel was very nice, clean, and spacious. The hotel staff were incredibly friendly, helpful, and accommodating. Omonia is not the best part of town but I never felt unsafe, even walking around at night. They also have reception 24/7 and add a security guard once the sun sets, but I honestly doubt they even need the security. People over dramatize how dangerous Omonia is, probably because Athens is generally so safe. I have felt so much more unsafe in Los Angeles or NYC than I ever did staying here. The hotel is very centrally located which makes getting anywhere in Athens a breeze. The hotel was very much luxury at a reasonable price. I can't recommend this enough as someone who is admittedly high maintenance!!

I would definitely recommend staying in this hotel. We stayed in the 'acropolis studio', which was really nice. I mostly loved the 2 balconies. The decoration and eye for detail make it a pleasure to spend time there.
The staff was very friendly, the hotel clean and the breakfast delivered in the room was very good. The rooftoppool was really nice on hot days.
It's very easy to reach the city center.

This hotel is very clean, in a convenient location, and the staff were extremely professional. The area is a little rough after dark, but we had no issues. We were able to taxi to a lot of the desired spots within 10 minutes or less.

Room was modern, clean, and large. They do not use key cards, instead use a keypad lock system for you to access the room. The pool has a great view of Acropolis!

During my stay at Vision Hotel in Athens, I had an exceptional experience. The hotel is excellent in every aspect - the rooms are clean, spacious, and tastefully decorated, providing all the necessary comforts for a perfect vacation.

The service at Vision Hotel was outstanding. The staff was courteous, professional, and available for any questions or requests. Their warm and personal approach made me feel welcome and special throughout my stay.

The hotel also provides night-long security service, which added to my sense of safety and comfort. I was able to sleep soundly and enjoy a worry-free vacation.

In conclusion, I highly recommend Vision Hotel in Athens to anyone looking for a luxurious stay with high-quality service and complete security. I will definitely return in the future.

We had a fantastic time staying at this apartment. It’s in a great neighborhood with lots of cafes and shops nearby. The place was comfortable, well-maintained, and exactly as described. The host provided excellent recommendations and was very friendly.

The hotel is ok for a night or two. It's not the cleanest I've ever been and the rooms are in need of general repairs, but it's cheap and situated in the heart of the city. Staff was friendly and helpful. To those who complained about weird people in the streets: Yes, they're there. As in every big metropolis. They mind their own business - mind yours and there's no problem.

We stayed here two nights. The location is great next to the bus stop and also walkable to many restaurants and attractions. 24 hour reception was a huge plus. Breakfast was included but we didn’t get a chance to have it because it only runs from 8- 10am and we were out every day before then

**Review of Diros Hotel (3 stars)**

Diros Hotel, located in a central but slightly unsafe area, offers an acceptable stay experience, though with some notable drawbacks. The hotel's structure appears somewhat rundown and poorly maintained, leaving much to be desired in terms of overall cleanliness.

The rooms, while seemingly clean, are nothing exceptional and reflect what one would expect from a three-star hotel. A critical point is the lack of daily bed linen changes, which could negatively impact guest comfort during longer stays.

We stayed for two days, and overall, the experience was okay. However, we strongly recommend exercising caution when moving around the hotel area, especially in the evening, due to the perceived danger of the location.

In summary, Diros Hotel is an acceptable option for short stays but has significant room for improvement.

The Diros hotel was just slightly out of the center but this didn’t feel bothersome - 30mins from Diros hotel to Plaka. It just means you get to get your steps in!
The room was as described (although we didn’t have any kind of view). Air conditioning was functioning which was crucial in this hot weather! The hosts were very helpful and nice!
The bathroom was a a little small, and the shower head didn’t hook onto the wall so we could only have a handheld shower. The water did get really cold though which was great!
The final thing to say is that the walls are super thin so you can hear people coughing who are in the room next to you!

Overall, it’s good value for money, clean, and well located!

There are bedbugs in the hotel. We were a group and most of them were bitten which came out after 1-2 days. We had all stayed for over a week in another place before and there were no problems. We stayed in the Hotel on Diros for one night and the next day there were already bite marks. Scandal.

I recently stayed at Parnon Hotel and had a generally positive experience. The hotel’s location is a major plus, as it’s conveniently close to many of the main attractions, making it easy to explore the city. However, the hotel itself is a bit dated, and the internet connection was unreliable in some rooms. We also noticed that some areas around the hotel didn’t feel very safe. On the bright side, the breakfast was excellent, and the service was top-notch. Overall, it’s a good choice if you prioritize location and great service.

Had a great stay here, beds were comfy, room had everything you needed, plenty of sockets, fridge, hair dryer etc And the breakfast wasn't to bad either with some hot food included. The thing that made up for it was the staff, everyone was polite and helpful, and the reception team was only happy to help and give you their time! For a three star hotel this was a lot of bang for your buck!

Conveniently located close to the metro station.  My friend & I had a wonderful stay at this hotel.   The room was impeccably clean   the breakfast was superb plus the staff were professional and helpful.   I'll definitely stay here a second time.   Thank you!

I've been in Athens for 20 days now, I've stayed in many high-class hotels, but I've never seen such service in any hotel. Wonderful service especially at the reception, wonderful people, special thanks to Angelos and Nektarios Thank you guys. If I return to Athens one day, I will only return here. Cleanliness 10/10

Amazing small hotel,
Staff are sooo friendly nothing Is to much, they give you map and show you best ways to get to places things to do
Rooms are good size balcony views are nice.
Breakfast is refreshing and someone is  always there to help

Great hotel
A very clean inexpensive vintage hotel from the 30’s. An excellent value for the location! No frills except excellent balcony views!!
Very nice people who working in reception...

Upon arrival the receptionist was nice and changed my room because it wasn't like the pictures. After that I had a bad experience. I found bed bugs the next day and they bit me and I informed the reception at 3am that day. They told me we could change the room, but I couldn't move all my stuff at 3am and I couldn't imagine that I would wake up the next day to a disaster. I slept and woke up with more bites on my body and called the reception to get medicine. He told me to go to the hospital and the hotel wouldn't pay anything because they have a bigger problem than me. They will come to clean the room from bed bugs and it doesn't matter who I am or what body the bed bugs bit. I went to the hospital and wasted my whole day there. I came back late to check out and he told me that I would have to leave the room in half an hour. I told him not to leave the room until I get to the airport. Bad experience and they won't handle my problem properly. I would not recommend anyone to visit this place.

When I first entered I was like, did I enter the wrong hotel?

This cannot be a 1-star hotel

Rooms cleaned with steam, friendly staff, location close to everything in Athens, and at an unbelievable price!

Staying here you get a 4 star experience with a zero missing in the price! Totally recommended!

The room was dirty and there were a lot of bedbugs and they attacked all my body, the furniture was in bad conditions.
Really I spent three bad nights there,
An absolute garbage dump of a hotel. Wifi in rooms doesn’t work. Paper thin windows with the loud roar of traffic 24/7. Junkies shooting up right outside. Rude staff. Avoid at all costs.

Not the very best hotel I've stayed in but for under £30 for the night it was fine.
Very basic but clean and tidy.

place_name: Brown Kubic, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: The hotel is fine with great staff who went the extra step to be helpful. Breakfast is great which a large selection to choose from and it has a pleasant roodtop terrace affording some good views of Athens, the Parthenon etc. It's location is not suitable for children. There's some 'undesirable content' in the vicinity but this is a couple of streets away. Keep a tight hold of your belongings when in this area.

place_name: Brown Kubic, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: Friendly hotel and clean rooms. Great rooftop where you can relax and take your own beers. Good choice of breakfast.  Couple of things let it down - in the room the tv picture was all muddled up and was unwatchable and the shower door handle kept falling off. The location was pretty good in relation to walking to the sights and restaurants but the area had homeless people, drugs and prostitutes on the same street. Incoming local taxi driver warned us to be careful and the outgoing one said he wouldn't live there even if he was given a free house.

place_name: Brown Kubic, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: excellent one night stay- would have stayed here longer if I could have!  The hotel had wonderful communication with us, our room was perfect, my husband loved the towels and I truly appreciate the robe and slippers!  Their employee Nefeli was a huge help and she managed to keep a clam head with lots going on around her!  We did not get the breakfast, but took a look at it and it was an excellent deal.

place_name: Brown Kubic, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: I visited the hotel on my girl's trip with my girlfriends and we all loved it there! The staff is very kind and always has great recommendations about restaurants, bars and activities outside of the hotel. They also have a gym which is available for every guest for free! The massage service is awesome and Christina working there is very friendly and easy to make conversation with. Same thing goes for everyone working there along with the cleaning ladies, the ladies at the breakfast and the front desk team whom upon arrival made us feel welcome with complimentary glasses of prosecco.

place_name: Brown Kubic, a member of Brown Hotels
review_text: Lovely hotel my room was clean and spacious. Everything was controlled by tablet which meant I didn’t have to get up to switch off lights etc .Staff are friendly and helpful and I was greeted with a glass of Prosecco on arrival. Hotel is in a good location to all tourist sites, luxury shopping and good restaurants. Would definitely recommend
Lovely  boutique  hotel. Small but very adequate  rooms, with a balcony. Much better than average breakfast included. Great location, very helpful staff. Highly recommended.

Clean room and good air conditioning. Good bathroom.
We got 2 singles, rather than a double/queen which was odd.
Nearabouts Omonia square/metro. Dodgy part of town, lots of drug use. Taxi driver told me not to walk the streets alone.
But I found it fine in the daytime, it’s actually fine. It was so central - I walked to all the sites including Acropolis etc. Perfect location that way. Just be careful about pickpockets/etc and stick to main roads, and travel in the daytime.
Breakfast was continental buffet.
And we were able to have cereal/coffee/biscuits when we needed a really early checkout.
One odd thing - encountered a hostile receptionist when returning to the hotel who demanded to recheck my room number/that I was a hotel patron… a bit of a shame as otherwise I thought people were fine.

The experience was extremely bad. Have you guys ever heard of any hotel charges extra 25 euros for bring people back. I can understand if the reception asked the second person to check in for security reasons, but what is the point for them to ask for extra charge? The receptionist said you paid for one person’s price and you didn’t pay for the second one. Are they charging not based on the number of room but on the number of person? Strange, absolutely SCAM. On top of that, the room is not clean at all. limescale in the baths and toilet, disgusting. AVOID THEM!!!

Great hotel and good sized rooms for solo travelers. The only thing I personally did not like was the view of the abandoned block of flats in the back of the hotel where my room was. But overall it was great.

They have a great breakfast variety with good coffee and juice selection. Very friendly and welcoming staff.

First of all: apart from one lady, all staff members were friendly and accommodating. The hotel is very central and there's a great breakfast buffet. There's a tiny elevator which helps you to get your luggage upstairs. The bed in my second room was very comfy (the first room looked nice but the bed was hard as a rock). Overall, good value for money. 👍

Now my issues: The first night I was in a room with a very hard bed. It was very uncomfortable and I could barely sleep. When I told the receptionist in the morning I'd like to change rooms, she first told me that all rooms had the same mattresses but eventually agreed to let me move. In the second room the bed was super comfy, so she definitely didn't say the truth about all mattresses being the same. Also, after moving (on my second night) someone opened my door around midnight even though I had locked it from the inside. It was someone who had a key card to my room, not sure whether another guest's key card accidentally worked on my door or if it was a staff member. Anyway, I screamed and the person left. Afterwards, I didn't feel safe in the hotel anymore. When I told the staff about it the next morning, she said that was not possible and didn't take my concern seriously. Instead , she implied I was making it up. Very disappointing reaction! I asked for the camera recordings of that night to prove that I was telling the truth and she told me there were no cameras upstairs. What the heck?! 🤨

Otherwise I really liked the hotel and all the other staff members were friendly, but this one lady clearly doesn't like people. 🤷
Awesome location & friendly hosts! This is gonna be the cheapest lodging you find and its comfortable. Can’t really ask for more!

Old, but clean a good value for the money.

Stayed for few days in June 2016, and had a nice experience in the hotel. The building is an old house made into a hotel. Everything is old (walls, baths, furniture), but pretty clean. There was a sink in the room, the toilet and showers are outside the rooms, and so is the switch for the air conditioner :) (which you have to turn off when leaving). The sheets and towels were very clean and nice smelling, the room was cleaned the second day (including the sheets and towels change)

In the room there were: two beds, a table with 2 chairs (+ an electric kettle, 2 tea cups, 2 teas, 2 instant coffees, sugar and a spoon), an old TV, AC, a fridge (with 1.5L bottle of water included, on the house), a sink (with mirror).

The neighborhood is not the best (but not the worst either), the hotel is at a 5-10 min walking distance from the Omonia Metro Station and, overall, the value for the price is good (compared with other hostel/hotel options found in Athens).
That's so nice

Nice hotel and easily accessible with parking almost in front so you can unload your things since it is on a main road. quite clean and to the point . fairly central in Athens, a breath away from Omonia and a few minutes from the small monastery

We had visited on June 1, 2017, the price was very good, the room was nice, but it didn't have a torch for the bedbugs and we messed up badly all night.  Nevertheless, they kept our bags for the whole day for free.

Conveniently located in the middle of a city , can easily get around with buses or metro.
Walking distance to many of the famous attractions like Acropolis, museums and many others.
Plenty of good restaurants nearby. Hence , this hotel is an ideal location to stay and explore this beautiful city, Athens.

Prices : reasonable , a very good breakfast is included
Rooms : spacious, bathrooms are quite nice.
Staff is generally OK. They would help you when you need.
Special thanks should go to their wonderful housekeeping staff. They do a perfect job !!

Family friendly: they provided a good cot bed for our toddler ( need to request)
Hotel looks a bit old , has a vintage appearance. However, it’s clean and well arranged.

If we had to stay here again , we would definitely choose this hotel.

Only main problem we faced was , the surrounding can be a bit noisy at night , and wouldn’t feel safe with various kind of people roaming around the area. But police is there all the time , hence no security issues occurred during our stay .
Efharisto!!!

I did not know that all the hotels were full. Fortunately, I went to them and there was a lady at the reception and explained my situation to him. She was really helpful with me and allowed us to spend the first night in the reception hall because all the rooms were full. On the second day we got a room and everything was really clean. Everything was there. I advise everyone to go to them because the place is also close to the tourist places. 🥇😀🙏🩵

Having stayed there for around four days, I can confirm that the negative comments you're probably reading here are either coming from clueless tourists or people who have expectations way too high.

For a three star hotel, I'd say this is the definitive choice for anyone. The prices are incredibly cheap, the breakfast is just a solid ten out of ten, it's clean and comfortable, the staff are some of the most kindhearted people I've come across and they clean the rooms often.

Only minor nitpick I have is in regards to the walls of the room, which could receive a bit of a paint job.

But aside from that? I'm satisfied.
Any issues I thought I'd have didn't happen, and others that those other reviewers seem to mention are not the problems of the hotel but rather the area itself.
It's Omonoia after all, the very epitome of crime in Athens. What the heck do you expect?! Sunshine and rainbows?!

Pros:
Price - Got a room  for a very good deal for a day.
Rooms - I had booked the Double Room but was very glad when the lady at the reception upgraded our room to  superior room.
Rooms were very spacious.
Food - Breakfast was not that great but i must appreciate that the receptionist was very kind and made arrangements to pack  food for me and my wife and to deliver it to us in advance as we had to board an early morning flight
Location - Location was just a block away from the main street which i loved as it was not so noisy but yet very easily walkable to the main junction. Lots of Taxis available all the time.

Cons:
Nothing much to comment

The staff was extremely polite and friendly !!!

The maids did an excellent job upkeeping the room and overall the quality of the room!

The hallway was nice, and they were kind enough to offer us a place to temporarily store our luggage until our ship arrived !

My huge complaint came from the room it could use a bit of painting !! It looked absolutely horrible, which made me subtract one star from the overall experience however, price to quality ratio it was worth it.

We stayed for 4 nights,  breakfast and wifi included, and we left super satisfied with the overall experience. I just wish the room was in a slightly better state. If you pay close attention to the wall picture, you can see it looks horrible.  The breakfast was also AMAZING they had an amazing selection, and my belly thanked them!!!!
The prices are expensive, the hotel is very bad and unclean, the service is very bad, and everything there is very disgusting. I feel sorry that I had this experience.

The worst Hotel, the location is perfect but it’s the only good thing about this place.
The worst receptionist that I met in my entire life, they won’t even store your luggage at the check out for a couple of hours. Everyone is rude and they won’t help you.
We even paid 5€ for the air conditioning and they didn’t give us the remote control, without any refund.
They didn’t change sheets and towels.
So please spend a little bit of more money to have a better and pleasant journey.

Without any booking we successfully
managed to find a room only in this hotel and were pretty happy.
They don't provide breakfast or any service, the  hotel is located in the nosy, dirty area, there's a permanent smoke smell in the room but we weren't going to get upset. Clean sheets, warm water are enough.
But the personel... Really that grumpy, grumbling old man at the reception... He was unpleased, impolite and didn't let us leave two small bags for 2 hours. Disgusting impression.

According to me it was not a hotel. It was like a store room. Their toilet condition was very low. The washroom was very bad. Water falls from the ceiling of the washroom. It is very annoying. There was not enough space to take a shower. Inside the hotel, next to the stairs, inside the room, the dirty smell is very uncontrollable. Their behavior was very bad. The behavior of the owner and the staff and the responsible people were very bad. In a word, everything was at a low level. I didn't like it at all.

If someone comes to visit with family or friends, I think they should not go to the hotel. It will only harass you.👎👎👎👎👎

It's just ok. Nothing special it's a place to sleep. I'm not going to trash this place. I was only there for a few hours. But it was my least favorite of the 3 I stayed in. It did have a balcony. A decent bathroom and the room was rather large in my opinion. And the internet worked.

The room was great and the bed was comfy. The room we had was recently renovated, had air conditioning, and on the 5th floor was quiet. The balcony was covered which made it a nice place to sit and relax after long days of walking. The management was super responsive and provided excellent information on how to get to the accommodation if taking public transport (which is quite close). The only downside is that the neighbourhood felt a bit dodgy. I'd still stay there again if I was back in Athens.
Clean, stylish and cozy rooms. Small kitchen is well equipped. Host is super friendly. The only downside is the neighborhood it doesn’t seem friendly ):

You may wonder why I rate my experience as 1/5 or 2/5. To start, the room itself was clean, well-maintained, and equipped with all necessary amenities. I had no issues with the room or the apartment block itself. However, I did have grave reservations about the surrounding area and the handling of my booking.

As a seasoned solo female traveller, safety is a top priority for me. Upon arriving and parking outside the hotel, I noticed that all the nearby shops were boarded up and covered in graffiti. I also encountered a highly intoxicated man walking by and peering into parked cars. This prompted me to bring all my luggage to my room immediately, as I didn't feel comfortable leaving valuables in the car. I later understood why the property recommends using paid parking with security a bit further down the road rather than parking on the street.

I decided to consult my son via video call for a second opinion, and he shared my concerns about the neighbourhood's safety. Consequently, I opted to book another hotel and left the premises. As I drove away, I encountered a group of around 6 people who were highly drug affected and rummaging through the bins, which reinforced my decision to leave.

I reached out to the hotel out of courtesy to inform them about my early check-out due to safety concerns. I acknowledged that I had already checked in and was willing to forgo the first night's fee. However, I inquired about the possibility of a refund for the second night, to which they declined. I wasn’t bothered because that is their prerogative.

What did bother me was the hotel's response, where they asserted that the neighbourhood was safe and mentioned the presence of homeless individuals as commonplace. Furthermore, they reported me as a no-show to Booking.com to prevent me from leaving a review, which I found not only disappointing but also deceitful and makes me wonder whether their review level is an accurate reflection.

Upon speaking with several local residents, I learned that the hotel is situated in an area of Athens that is considered to be unsafe, particularly at night, and not just for women and families but for everyone.

In summary, I have no complaints about the hotel itself. However, due to concerns about the neighbourhood and the hotel's handling of my request and blocking me from writing an honest review, I would downgrade my experience to a 2/5. I would urge potential guests to thoroughly research the surrounding area before booking, as there are other hotels at a similar price point in safer locations just 1-2 kilometers down the road and closer to the city center.

If you READ the Booking.com reviews, their rating might be high but most of the reviews warn people about the safety of the area.

Stayed three nights here, big spacious rooms, modern bathroom with good water pressure, our host was super attentive and kind.

As others mentioned there are a lot of junkies in the area but with that said I never felt unsafe and if you do, taxis are cheap and come quickly. This is only in the immediate surroundings in any case and you can walk to central locations in ten minutes, like the Archaeological Museum etc.

We stayed for 2 nights. They are apartments you enter with a password. Do not expect a reception area. Easy communication though booking. Room was big, spacious, sheets where smelling clean. Clean bathroom also had kitchen stuff to cook etc. we didnt used it. Not soundproofing room. BUT the location i would not recommend. Every taxi we used all warned us to be careful of the location. We didn’t know before booking, there are people using drugs nearby that’s why, felt usafe.. not that we had a bad experience, but we didnt walk at night there. Also from monastiraki is 20 minutes walking, and with Uber 4-5€ . Would recommend calling an uber to go places. Price is good.

Amazing little hotel, only a 5 min walk from Metro stations. Great buffet breakfast and the best reception staff ever!  Area has its ups  and downs but felt safe the whole time.Local restaurants are also a must.. If you love city breaks this is the hotel for you..

TERRIBLE HOTEL EXPERIENCE

Our group of 100 students had a distressing stay at this hotel.

Firstly, the hotel is located in a very unsafe area of Athens, rife with junkies. These individuals roamed the streets, frequently engaging in theft and yelling at all hours. We could even see them openly using drugs from our balcony.

Upon entering our rooms, we were met with a foul odor of mold and cigarettes. Opening the balcony door was not an option due to the pervasive smell from outside.

We were forced to order food to the hotel, as it was unsafe to walk the streets, even in the evening.

The room itself was filthy. The sheets and mattress were stained with vomit, urine, and other substances. There was hair everywhere, the mattress had cigarette burns, and there were holes in it.

Housekeeping was extremely rude and disrespectful when we requested new sheets. The receptionist was unhelpful and even yelled at us for inspecting the bed.

At least 10 other rooms reported bedbugs. Proceed with caution!

The locks one some of the rooms were not working, the doors could be opened without a keycard.

The air conditioning couldn't be controlled and ran all night, leaving me with a sore throat. Additionally, there was mold around the unit.

Breakfast was mediocre, though the food was fresh.

Overall, this hotel is a tourist trap. Avoid it at all costs!

Had a lovely stay here with the family . Staff were lovely, very pleasant and ever so helpful . Hotel room was very clean , fresh towels etc daily . Breakfast was lovely. Metro and other transport within three mins walk from the hotel itself

The hotel is located in the worst neighborhood in all of Greece. Junkies and and homeless people seen roaming arround all the time. I would feel unsafe walking around on my own. The hotel however was quite nice, with a really nice bathroom and a rather spacious room. The food selection was not the biggest but it tasted great. No issues with the staff at all.

The hotel is fine. The apartments are not. While the apartment itself is acceptable, the entrance is horrible, you must walk through a street filled with homeless individuals and filthy and unpleasant, we prepaid for 3 nights and left after the first  losing payment for the remaining nights.

Nice and clean room with refrigerator,TV, water heater for coffee & tea, and a very kind welcome @ arrival.
Excellent location,close to a Metro station and a nice Bakery & Cafè that opens @ 6am. Absolutely recommended place, very good value for the money.

The building is old, but the rooms are new and comfortable. Clean and with a great air conditioning (it was 35oC when we went there late June). It is located on a quiet street that is really close to everything, it is on the edge of a not so nice part of the street, but just don’t go left when leaving the hotel ;)
Walking distance from the main square and from there to Acropolis and others.

An ideal place to visit Athens.

Clean rooms with en-suite bathrooms, cleaned every day, friendly and helpful hotel staff and a location in the very centre are undoubted advantages of this place. Add to this the delicious patisserie-bakery just around the corner, where you can have a good breakfast, and you have a recipe for the perfect place to visit Athens.

Next to metro station, just one stop to Monastraki. Easily transport to everywhere, rooms are clean, has balcony you can smoke , netflix and youtube are installed on tv. Refrigerator and AC works well.Thank you for  friendly welcome and smiley faces.
really bad experience. Thay are really rude, specially when you ask for proper sheets. the toilets smell horrible... ! Would not recommed, even if it's cheap it's not worth it.

Rude condescending front desk, absolutely not willing to help or do a little extra work. I just cancelled my reservation because I have no desire to stay at a place that gives a terrible first impression at the front desk, I can just imagine the rest.
Nice hotel with great service. Area was a little sketchy but only because we didn't know the area and like any city, be careful.
Very good first impression. Receptions was authentic and very friendly.
WONDERFULL
Really amazing

The staff hotel is very nice and kind.  The room itself is super small but well designed.  We came with 2 big suitcases and it was hard to open them.  The location is in a bad neighborhood,  lots of homeless  and junkies.. we only came for 1 night and arrived quite late, so it wasn't worth paying more for a better hotel.

Room was small but clean. Friendly staff and simple but nice breakfast. We had a good stay but neighbourhood is stressfull because crazy people living on the streets. We got attacked by a crazy woman with a jet lighter, only 10m from the hotel entrance. Solely because of the neighbourhood, I would not go back.
The staff is the only good thing. Showers leaking, not enough space in the room. No tea and coffee sachets. Extremely noisy at night and not a great area too. Breakfast is 1 star rating.

The hotel is nice and clean and the staff is great. However, it is not in a great part of the city. It was so noisy we couldn’t sleep at night, people yelling in the streets, honking non stop. The area is a bad neighborhood of the town with graffitis everywhere, dirty and people under the influence roaming around. The windows were super thin we could hear everything happening outside. The hotel itself was nice but we wouldn’t have booked it if we knew it was in such a bad part of the city.

The hotel staff was very kind. We arrived during the night before our room was ready and we spend hours until the morning in the hotel. We were offered breakfast for free that morning because we arrived early.

The room was ok, but it is visible that is has been some time since it was renovated. It was big and comfortable though, with curtain on the window. We liked the breakfast.

The bathroom could be a bit better maintained and cleaned, the shower was broken and the sink was clogged.

There is a metro station 300 meters and the center itself is 10-15 minutes away on foot. The location itself of the hotel is not perfect because it near Omonia which is a notorious quarter. On arrival, the taxi driver entered a street where we saw people sitting on the street, and they looked like junkies. We were advised not to go there. These are the streets north of the street where the hotel is located. South of that should be fine and the street itself where the hotel is located is very well lit and we didn't see a lot of strange and homeless people in the area between the hotel and the Omonia square. We even walked near the midnight and it was ok.

When our rooms were ready, there was an electricity outage in the hotel and the elevator didn't work. We had to bring our luggage 7 floors up!

place_name: Trendy Hotel by Athens Prime Hotels
review_text: Not close to the city center, so you have to take a 5-7€ taxi to get to the areas with entertainment and dining. The breakfast buffet is nice.  The surrounding area is safe, but looks sketchy at night.

place_name: Trendy Hotel by Athens Prime Hotels
review_text: We booked it last second for 3 nights. The room is ok for two persons. The hotel in total is nothing special. The receptionist at night was not very helpful when we said there are small flies in the bathroom and killed them by her finger, saying she grew up in village and it is normal. But she gave us toiletries and did her best to provide us comfort. Breakfast is 1-2 stars. Location is almost central and you can reach center in 15-20 mins but at night (after 21) we did not feel safe in the neighborhood as two girls because of prostitution and homeless addicts.

place_name: Trendy Hotel by Athens Prime Hotels
review_text:
This was my families 1 visit to Athens, Greece yet,I would recommend for you to read our experience to decide if this hotel is fitting for your visit...
Our first day checkin was fine. Young lady up front was super sweet and helpful. We did not checkin to our room until 2 pm & she allowed us to get coffee. We assumed we could get breakfast too but as we were grabbing breakfast she said it was not included yet since our rooms would be ready in the afternoon, I’m assuming. We were charged €15 for each of the four of us, so €60 for small pick item breakfast.
Next evening, we headed to our room and a painter had painted the doors & had no “wet paint” signs,my moms bag & nice blouse got black paint on the front. we saw the painter putting up a sign AFTER he had painted the elevator. We told him it got on our bag and showed him and he comes up to us with a dirty paint towel and paint thinner to attempt to remove it. We backed away as I knew that would only spread the black.
We then went downstairs to the counter and told the lady up front. She told us to calm down even though there was no screaming or aggressive agitation, just extremely annoyed. She said we could use a towel to take it off, we tried that already and if it came out, we would not have come downstairs. My mother then says “so you would not be upset if this happened to you?” And in a snarky remark, the downstairs lady says, “no actually I wouldn’t. Our people are not like your people. This is not a big deal to us, these things.” The attitude and disrespect she displayed not acknowledging our frustration in YOUR hotel is truly  unacceptable. We then requested the managers name and she gave us one manager’s name of whom would not be there tomorrow. Which of course, is not helpful.
We went back to our room in frustration and disappointment. About 10 minutes later, we got a call to our room that they would like their dry cleaners to attempt to take out the black paint on the backpack and on the shirt. She came to our room 15 minutes after and gave us a bottle of wine and two glasses saying she did not want anybody to be upset or agitated because she saw how frustrated we were. We made it clear how it wasn’t necessarily the item that was damaged, but the place we got it which has sentimental value.
The next day we are going downstairs to get our bag and shirt. We mentioned how we noticed there were still some paint on the bag and my mom wanted it to be paid for as it was a nice shirt. The lady says we need a receipt for the shirt before she could do anything but then she will give us €300 for the shirt. My mom bought the shirt in Florida years ago, she did not have the receipt with her in Athens. Makes sense? No. My grandma said that it was fine, but can they comp us the €60 for breakfast the first day since we did not know we needed to pay for breakfast. They eventually did comp us the €60 for the breakfast the first day after continuous back-and-forth. Not to mention, they have a sign that says Tuesday through Sunday the rooftop bar is open for an extended period of time yet it was closed and when we mentioned it was closed, they defensively snapped back, saying that there were no signs even though the dog was in the table and outside the hotel door.
The point of this review is that the customer service at this hotel is terrible. They are rude, snarky, disrespectful, and dismissive. Yet, it only seemed to be the evening front desk crew as that young lady the first day was super sweet.
It is always important to read reviews and other peoples experiences as I am posting this to share how the hotel handled the situation and how you would feel or react if something similar were to occur. If you don’t care about customer service, the rooms were nice and the beds were comfortable enough. The showers are quite slippery and water splashes out of the shower, but other than that, the rooms are secure. I strongly recommend you continue your search for a hotel in Athens, Greece if this is the one you were initially considering.
Walls are also thin. Can hear everything.

place_name: Trendy Hotel by Athens Prime Hotels
review_text:
The staff were amazing and helpful
Receptionists were very kind and assisted us in any way they possibly could.
The hotels location made it easier for us to explore Athens ( walking distance in most tourist destinations )

Surely recommend Trendy Hotel

place_name: Trendy Hotel by Athens Prime Hotels
review_text:
One of my worst experiences on all levels to be frank.
Extremely expensive for what it has to offer.
Whilst the room itself is ok and it looked clean,tidy and modern at a first glance,i was shocked about what had to follow.
First off,the shower is totally blocked and after 1 minute in the shower process you had a pool underneath your feet.
On the day of check out,i woke up to a bed filled with small ants which gave me a mega shock.
I went down to eat breakfast and there were 2 young girls in the reception(31st of may) and told them about the event.
The greek one just brushed it off and downplayed the issue like it was a common event that i had to cope with.
I asked for an half hour extra for my check out and she responded that it has to be sharp 11:00 cause they have arrivals😳
This response blew my mind.I mean i just told you,you let me sleep in a bed full of ants and you give me that kind of attitude?
You should roll a red carpet to the customer that has been through that experience.
I could report them to the sanitary,health services and create a bunch of issues for you.
Whoever the manager is,just educate your staff in customer service.
Ina(if i recall her name right) working the night of check in was wonderful,responsive & attentive.
The response from the housekeeper as well was nice.We found together the ant trail,coming from the balcony and she apologized 10 times although it is not her fault.
On another note,the breakfast was very nice and that was it.

I stayed here for 2 nights in October. The staff were very friendly and welcoming. I was able to check in early because the room was ready which was really nice. They said it’s not always possible to offer early check in, I was very grateful after a long flight that this was available.

The room was small but there is a large balcony. For me travelling along the room was good but for a couple you may wish to book a larger room (there are other room options), I was in the double room with terrace. The hotel is modern and clean.

The neighbourhood is very convenient, easy to walk and lots of good food and coffee options nearby.

There are a few bars outside the hotel. I’d read reviews prior to booking about noise from the street at night. This didn’t affect me. I stayed on a Wednesday/Thursday and each night the bars were open. For me the room was well sound insulated. There is always going to be some noise when you stay in a major capital city. I wouldn’t say this a noisy hotel.

I would recommend booking this hotel and would stay here again.

Nice Hotel in urban area with friendly staff. Good size room with balcony.
Noisy on Fridays due to two bars with competing sound tracks only negative.
Good murals and shutter art near by.
Straight forward walk towards the historic centre or one stop on Metro.

Attention!! A hidden gem is here !

The rooms were toooop
The staff especially Georgia and Eirini were super kind and they were really willing to help with everything!
The location was next to the city centre
It was really clean and even on the second floor you couldn’t hear anything from the noise outside
We would definitely gonna visit again at our next trip in Athens

Thanks for everything 🩵
Amazing hotel experience. Rooms were great, clean, and very nicely decorated. Staff was always very nice and incredibly helpful. Best about all, hotel was an incredible value for its money.

The front desk staff were very friendly and accommodating during our stay. Rooms were spacious and room service was available every day. The hotel is walking distance to all the main attractions. Only downfall during the stay was that on the weekend (Friday night) you could hear the club music all night and it didn't end till 6 am. Aside from that, we enjoyed our stay

I booked this hotel through capital one. No instructions for check in were ever sent except for check in starts at 2pm. When I arrived at 330 pm no one was at the hotel working. It took me almost an 30 minutes to reach the owner on the number listed on front door. My cab driver was finally able to contact them and staff walked me through lockboxes to get to my room (remote) Huge inconvenience locating hotel and checking in. Once I got to my room I was very sweaty due to the length of time it took me to get into the building. I went to take a shower, and it was broken. The faucet was the only part that worked, not the shower heads. Since no one was working in the building I was not able to get this corrected.

Location:

Hard to find, lots of open drug use all around location. Not appropriate for families. Felt unsafe. Close to acropolis is about the only pro of the location.

Rooms were nice, and would have received higher score if the shower worked. Service was horrible. Would strongly recommend going somewhere else.

Staff here were helpful and friendly. The location of the spot was pretty decent, very walkable to the omonia train stop. And other districts are also very walkable in about 10 minutes away. The room was a little oddly configured, a little Spartan. The walls and the bed wasn't my personal favorite. But it did the trick. It was just fine. I'd recommend this place still.

Just impossible to sleep, loud music and fights or even car alarm or weird people banging on the doors or shouting until 5 in the morning - in a very ugly, dirty and creepy street. Not trendy at all!! Sadly a homeless man slept in front of the entrance for 2 nights...rooms were Ok but some dirty sheets and broken shower for my friend. No one at the reception when we arrived we were lucky to send an email to get a code and get in. Once we saw a young man (out of 6 nights) at the reception. No breakfast to go. Really we do not recommend this place - even my friend at the back could not sleep because of the noise. Strange abandonned buildings with some lights at night - but these are not Athens Lights. Bar called From dusk to dawn which is true. Very disappointed as we had no other choice. On a Positive note: earplugs are provided but you need extra headphones on top of them to fall asleep...

Great staff! Very helpful and friendly! A receptionist can speak perfect Polish! We were impressed! He is Greek! For me a big disadvantage was noise. It is a great place for people who come to Athens to party and enjoy night life 🙂
I had a very good stay here last year reception. People were very helpful organise taxi for me food and very cheap accommodation in a beautiful hotel. I recommend it highly if you’re in Athens
Very cool place with nice rooms. As a weak point, could be a bit more clean.
Very nice place

Very spacious and well equipped apartment. Clean and comfortable, really nothing to say, it's perfect.

The big plus is the location. The area is very lively, full of small bars, restaurants and other kebabs, it's very pleasant.

This hotel is listed as a 4-star, but it’s far from deserving that rating—closer to a 1-star experience, at best. Breakfast was very disappointing, with extremely limited options. On Sunday, a typically busy breakfast day, many items were already finished by 8 am. Despite several requests, no cut fruits were available, and when croissants ran out at 10 am, staff refused to replenish them, citing the 10:30 closing time as an excuse. Thank god it was my last day here.

The room itself was not up to standard. Bed sheets and pillow covers were visibly stained, which felt unhygienic and disappointing. While the ground staff were helpful, the kitchen staff were quite rude, refusing to assist even after being notified of the empty breakfast items.

Overall, a frustrating experience in such a beautiful city. I wish I’d chosen a different hotel.

What a wonderful stay.  The staff was always very polite and helpful.  Went out of their way to assist, if needed.  The hotel has a boutique feel to it, with it's location and smaller elevators, but it's a great place to stay.  The rooms are spacious and clean, with comfortable bedding, plenty of lighting and outlets, and a bathroom with an amazing shower.  Seriously, the shower was very large with great pressure and a rainfall shower head that I've missed since we left.  The breakfast every morning is probably the best hotel breakfast I've ever had.  There are great restaurants and coffee shops within walking distance to COLORS.  This was a great, great stay.

I had an issue with a cancelled flight and shout out to Tasos on the front desk who was an absolute legend and helped me sort a room for the night. Hotel is great. Nicely decorated, not that far from the train station and easy bus ride to airport and obviously close to the centre of town. Would stay again.

Everything was great! I cannot recommend this hotel more.
Nice and helpful staff, everything super clean, new and tidy.
Location is 15min walk from the centre so it’s easy to get to all places without public transport or taxis.
The breakfast is €15 and is delicious. Great food and not your typical and boring continental breakfast. This one has different savoury and sweet options for any preferences.
Honestly, we had a great experience. 100/10

My favorite hotel in Athens. The hotel is designed in a modern-humorous style.
I really like the location, close to the center but quiet.
Very comfortable (and colorful) rooms. Good breakfast.
You can find parking on the street.
The hotel staff is exceptionally helpful and cute. A perfect hotel for me.

The hotel was beautiful and clean! The staff was extremely friendly and helpful. We were able to check in a bit early and while we waited we could see the acropolis from the rooftop restaurant! We truly loved being able to stay in the heart of Athens!

Stayed here 4 nights at the start of October 2024.
The room we had was on the 6th floor. The room was nice with a wardrobe (including bath robe & slippers), desk, small fridge and a small balcony (no furniture on the balcony).
There was also complimentary bottled water, and a coffee machine. The shower was amazing, lots of space, superb water pressure and hot water. The bed was clean and comfy. We had a double room booked but it was two single mattresses pushed together which which a slight discomfort.

Staff are very friendly & welcoming. They provided a map of the city and recommendations on where to visit.

Breakfast is served on the 10th floor, the Parthenon/Acropolis can be seen from here! It is the typical hotel buffet-style, with small variety each day, overall it was pretty good. Very helpfully the staff provided me and my wife with a plate of pastry items when we overslept and missed breakfast one morning!
In the evening the 10th floor offers a rooftop bar & restaurant. We didn't eat in the evening, but the bar offered a good selection of drinks, with a great view across the city.

The location of the hotel is slightly out of the tourist hotspot area, but it is only a short walk. There is also a metro station and the city sightseeing bus stops a few minutes away. There are also some mini-markets nearby the hotel to buy any essential items or snacks.

Overall we were happy with our stay, I would rate somewhere near 4.5 stars. So I've rounded up on this occasion.

My husband and I stayed here for 2 days before going on a cruise.
The staff couldn't have been any more nicer and so helpful! Maria the manager is excellent! She checked on us often to make sure we didn't have any issues. The rooftop bar was a fabulous spot to sit and have a cocktail in the evening to the views of the acropolis and surrounding city. Manos and Venetia provided great service despite that a large group had also shown up and were quite demanding. (Probably tired and hungry! No judgement!)
Our room was clean, the showers had great hot water pressure, and the acts were very quiet!
The area was within 20 minutes walk to the markets, shopping, and the Parthenon!

My brother and I visited back in April, the hotel was refreshingly better than expected, the staff were incredible and could not have been more accomadating from assisting with tours to sitting on the rooftop drinking drinks.

Highly recommend!

3 star hotel. Yes Maria the hotel manager is nice and there are smiles on staff but, the hotel is run down, desperately needs paint and an update, almost non-functional gym, offers a sad minimal breakfast, is in a sketchy part of town and DOES NOT meet a 5 star standard. AT ALL! So as long as you’re aware that you’re getting a 3 star hotel, you’re fine. The pics and reviews are misleading. We would not have stayed here otherwise. People are very nice!

The neighbourhood is apparently known for not being very safe. We didn't feel unsafe at all. You do notice police on guard every here and there, but other than that only the cosy restaurants, eclectic vibes and ice cream parlor (Makalu, would recommend 5/5!) stood out - in a positive way.

Zoe is a very friendly host. She is available via WhatsApp and helped us with some questions. She also suggested to bring clean towels, although that was not included in our stay.

Based on the points above combined with the very reasonable pricing, I would recommend this accommodation.

However, some things to be aware of: the bed and the pillows weren't the most comfortable (although preferences can differ of course). And the apartments Zoe rents out are quite noisy, if you are unlucky to have loud neighbours as we did. If you are a light/sensitive sleeper, this place might not be for you.

Nearest metro station is a 10 minute walk away, be aware that walking in Athens can be quite tiresome because of the incline and bad pavements.
On a side note: would recommend to purchase a public transport ticket that's available for 5 days. So much cheaper than thinking you'll only use the metro once and then end up buying new tickets everyday. 🙈

Fantastic location in Εξαρχία (Hexarchia) of Athens! Post-modern flat with very good furnitures. The bathroom is very stylish! Superb non-price!
