I think I never paid a location less than this and never had such quality! I went by feet to the Archeological museum and it took me 8 minutes and in 20 minutes by feet you reach the Olympieion!
Highly recommend!! 👍🏼👍🏼 If you don't want to walk, there is plenty of taxis communicating with city center for 4€ (ain't kidding)

I don't write many reviews but I feel it is my duty to warn fellow travellers.  This area is not safe at all.  We were lucky to arrive around 3pm and could still find a different hotel for that night, but if you arrive here at night the chances of you getting mugged or pickpocketed are significant.  After the shock of walking through this area we started reading and asking around and we consider ourselves lucky that nothing happend, there are many many stories of people getting robbed, so beware of any false account reviews telling you otherwise.  Hope you stay away from the Omonoia metro station and area, it is full of junkies, homeless people, pickpockets and worse, there is nobody to help if something happens, no police no official.  Please be safe and stay in a safe tourist area.

We stayed at Miranda place past Saturday  only one night but had  really nice experience.Really clean room,perfect location .Miranda was really helpful with recommending us things we can do around area and brought us some amazing greek traditional sweets next morning ..great host :) .Recommend to everyone her place  and for sure will back if next time in Athens.

Clean apartment located in an overall central position, also considering the quality/price ratio. The only flaw is the absence of a table to possibly eat or relax. However, eating is not a problem given the wide choice in the surrounding area with more than good quality

I would have given this hotel 5 stars if it were not for the welcome we received upon our arrival.  Our overseas flight landed at 8 am and we were at this hotel by 10.  We could not get a room until 2 pm!  This was after a long day of traveling.  After speaking with other hotel guests, this seems to be a persistent problem.  Suggestions, perhaps?  Even if you could spare a glass of water and a cool place to sit would have been nice. Maybe a free breakfast or cup of coffee/tea?  I appreciate that the rooms need cleaned, but I'm sure there were some  people that left early.  An early arrival room for weary travelers would be appreciated.  Also, Theo must have been tired of dealing with weary travelers as he wasn't very pleasant.
The positives - rooms are extremely nice with a Jacuzzi tub and a shower with body sprays. Beds are super comfortable.  Rooms were clean and nicely prepared.
Rooftop pool, bar, and dining area are just beautiful to relax after a day of sightseeing.
All other staff were more than helpful and very kind.

This was our first time staying at this hotel chain and we were pleasantly surprised. The customer service was top notch, every single person was attentive and went out of their way to assist us. The rooms were very nice and the beds were very comfy. The roof that bar was really nice and had great views. I personally wouldn’t stay at this hotel again due to the location. I would stay at this chain but I’m sure there’s far greater locations. The area surrounding the hotel has lots of homeless and druggies just lingering and sleeping in the streets.

Loved this hotel. Very convenient for walks to the city centre and historical sites in Athens. The sky lounge is very popular for an afternoon swim and sun. It becomes magical at night for a romantic dinner with a view of the Acropolis.

We enjoyed our stay very much. Staff was very polite. Our room was very clean and comfortable. We liked the overhead shower but could not get our handheld to work. Housekeeping came twice a day. A nice tray of cookies and dried fruit awaited us when we arrived. We brought our own universal plug adapter but were disappointed that there were no USB ports in the room or outlet in the bathroom.
The pool was wonderful! Hot tub looked inviting but water was not warm. Food and drinks on the rooftop we're good but menu options were limited.
Breakfast buffet was Amazing!

I had a great stay in Athens—my second visit after 11 years, and the experience was just as good as I remembered.

Location: While the hotel is slightly removed from the city’s main attractions, it's only a 15-minute walk or a quick 5-minute metro ride to the lively parts of town. The Omonia metro station is conveniently close by.

Room: The room was well-furnished and spacious enough to accommodate an extra bed for our child. The bathroom was also quite large, though the hydro-massage shower felt outdated and a bit tricky to navigate as it’s quite high to climb. However, the cleanliness was excellent throughout.

Breakfast: The breakfast offered a good variety of hot and cold dishes—perfect if you want to avoid the hassle of searching for breakfast in the city early in the morning. The only downside was the coffee, which, like in other Melia hotels, wasn’t great. I’m still not sure if it’s the machine or the coffee beans they use.

Rooftop Pool: One of the highlights was the rooftop pool, a rarity in Athens. Although it’s small, it’s a refreshing escape on those hot days, with the added bonus of a bar. However, I wasn’t thrilled that non-hotel guests can access the pool for a fee, as it made the area feel a bit less secure.

Misleading photos and customer service that lets robots answer your questions.
We booked a room with a description and photos that made us dream. The reality was completely different.
One person ended up in hospital because of the damp apartment. Please note that the organization describes itself as immensely accessible, but there is no elevator!

A small studio apartment but with a great terrace.   I stayed for 3 nights in this German apartment chain during my holiday in Athens.  This property is located approximately 5-10 minutes away from Panemistimio Station.   Not exactly in the centre but pretty walkable to everywhere including many tourist attractions.

A studio apartment I chose came with an extra sized single bed with a terrace.  The indoor space was small, but it is modern and is well-equipped with necessary appliances and kitchenette.

To me it is was its spacious terrace that really did a magic.  I spent quite a bit of time enjoying coffee and the sun in this terrace (I would hardly stay indoors in this sunny city).  If you do not mind its small indoor space, I think it is just perfect for a solo traveler.

I’ve had the best intentions to rate this newly refurbished house, which business concept of individual studio units seems to taylor to professionals and digital nomads. I’ll attempt for a balanced pros & cons below:

✅ location, 8 mins walk to Omonia or Paneptismo 🚇 touching the central locations and a few restaurants in the closest vicinity
✅ friction free, key less check in, send on booking, which eliminates any need to contact the provider, 9/10 times. I didn’t have any issues with the codes.
✅amenities inside, the size of the room. Missing iron & board but having a smart 📺 hdmi cable if you need it for work
✅ 🛜 👍 I didn’t have issues but I’m also on international roaming

👋 acceptable cleaning. The floors throughout and patches of dirt on bedside table made the place reasonably clean, not spotless. You need to ask to get your room turned around

❌ beware of room 104. If you find out that you’ve been allocated to it, try to change it at all costs. The window is in a lower ground floor, looking out into a closed off space, portrayed in the picture. Feels like staying in catacombs, murky. Street noice 24/7, echoing within like if you had the buzzing right infront of your doorstep
❌ 24/7 noice. You’ll hear everything! This place has a terrible acoustic predispositions, and didn’t spend a dime on a sound proofing would it seem. I hear Neighbours chatting in their room, playing music, everything that goes on(!), mopping of the floor early morning, people going steps up and down. If someone drops something, it’s echoed and vibrates throughout the house. I’m a deep sleeper but not in this place. Not for people who need quiet to sleep.
❌ the 🛌!!! The main reason why I’d not stay again. The bed is very cheap, creaking. The mattress is awful. Very soft, unbearable. Only after a days sleep I’m considering visiting a chiropractor after my 4 nights visit. Paper thin quality pillows providing no support. I’ve had one of the worst sleeping experience in a long time.
Rooms’ bathroom sink has a reverse hot and cold tabs and the kitchen sink is wobbly (needs looking at)

My body feels broken and due to the lack of good night sleep, I’ve a feel of being in a dormitory rather than in a serviced accommodation. I’ll seek all opportunities to be outdoors as a result even-though I planned to spend some time on working from ”home”.
Even with the change of below quality standard bedding arrangement, I’m just not sure how the Limehouse Athens Solonos would tackle the obvious acoustic problem, the house presents to its temporary residents?!
Great rooms. Everything was nice and new. We loved the concept!
Clean, spacy, good locarion

The room was completely dirty. dirty. Dirty. I have never seen a place with so low cleaning standards.
When we arrived the room was a mess, with dirty chips bags all over the place, the sheets were still the ones from the previous costumer (full of hairs, food, crumbs, stains) and the bed was half made half untied. The curtains were torned and completely brown (dirty ! They were “white” on the top). The main problem of The room was in fact the door: it was broken! It would open without key.
The shared bathroom was the most disgusting toilet we have ever been. Full of hairs, insects of all kinds and dust everywhere. The shower...apart from being completely destroyed, we could see the brown marks (dirtiness) on its walls - if you stay in this place don’t forget to bring your own flipflops! Don’t touch the floor!
In the corridor there is a small fridge than can be used, but the interior was  covered in mold.. if you just need to put a bottle of water it should be alright, otherwise I wouldn’t advise.
There is no internet in the house/“hotel”. The rooter is there, but it doesn’t work.

If you want a cheap quick bed, this place is ok. But don’t expect anything else as all the pictures on booking are FAKE.

Be careful with the window, do not open it! We got stung by 5 mosquitos and we ended up with around 20 bites each - and unfortunately one of us is allergic.

Location is good.

KesTour CANCELLED my reservation upon very short notice, however, they did NOT give me a refund. Any and all attempts to contact them, with directly by phone, or via booking.com (which is the site I had booked on) failed, they were neither replying to their phone nor to emails. Beware, it could happen to you!
No no no never no. Avoid! Key needs to be obtained far away. WiFi not functional. Cockroaches. No air conditioning.

Hotel to avoid, broken lock, questionable electrical installation, moldy shower, having to pick up and drop off the keys by taxi at another location.

No hygiene facilities offered (shower gel, shampoo, soap, etc.)

DO NOT EVER STAY HERE! We arrived at 1am from the airport to find a dodgy room that was dirty stains on the bed, stains all over the bed head dirty marks on every wall! Shower was disgusting and ripped towels were made available. We stayed the night and booked somewhere else and left first thing in the morning. No refund by the owner just a rude remark saying he noted that it was dirty etc. DO NOT STAY HERE!

My stay at this hotel was incredibly disappointing. Upon arrival, we were left stranded outside as there were no instructions for check-in and no reception available. Despite having confirmation emails, we were told our reservation was "canceled" in error. The instructions provided were inaccurate, leaving us without access to our room. The room itself was dirty upon arrival, and  the cleaning staff arrived without a key, despite us being told otherwise. On the day of check-out, we were rudely expelled from our room before the designated time by an unpleasant cleaning staff member. Additionally, the noise from the nearby pub made sleeping impossible until 1 am. To top it off, the room did not match the photos advertised online. Even after the cleaning, we found dirty dishes inside the kitchen cabinet. Overall, this experience was a major letdown.

Large apartment. Very clean. Toilet wouldn't stop flushing which was a bit annoying. Easy to get to transport links or to just walk into the centre so is ideal. Provided a very good and large cot which was great as we have a big baby! We had read the reviews about the noise so we did kind of expect it... but it is VERY noisy. From the bars across the road. Ear plugs were provided and it is double double glazed! So not really sure what else they could do about it. Lift is TINY only one of us could barely fit with the pram which was more of a nuisance than a problem. We were overall happy with our stay

Great space, 10 min walk to main down town area of the city. The hot water system is extremely hot, so be mindful of that! (The hot water tank was reading at a mid level temp on the gauge, when we checked afterwards, yet still it caused my girlfriend's hand to develop blisters on her hand from rinsing the dishes, even with the tap only set to a medium hot setting.) Otherwise a very lovely stay. Comfy bed, well equipped, huge apartment, good communication from host.

Really nice apartment, easy to self check in and was reasonably priced

Pretty decent location near to ominia square

Fantastic experience through and through. Room was new, clean and came with loads amenities, particularly enjoyed the Polaroid camera and the tote bag with stickers and post cards! The online check-in and cardless doors also worked very well. Location is great for bars / restaurant and museums. Great experience overall, our only complaint is that the rooms were a little dark.

Lovely room, very clean, the shower gels were AMAZING!

We had a group of friends going on a Virgin cruise, and took advantage of being in Athens for the night. The bed was SO comfortable!

Highly recommend Bob W!! 🐻❤️😎

The location was central and the rooms were very well kept. There was a representative upon check in which I didn't expect and she was very helpful and informative. Breakfast was a nice touch with supporting local businesses. Definitely worth the stay.

I stayed there for 7 night for a business trip، do not afraid that they do not have a front desk because they are very responsive in what's app، the team vary supportive. Rooms clean great space. They have great Easterages like camera stickers. And other complimentary things. Highly recommend place to book

We had an awesome stay in a large and quiet apartment . The room was extremely clean, and they treated us very very well with all our questions. The room was recently reformed and everything felt purposefully placed and with good taste.
Combined that we loved Athens, we'll come back here, hopefully sooner than later ❤️

Beautiful new apartments, exceptionally clean, friendly hosts eager to help.
Nice towels, comfortable double bed, large TV, efficient and silent A/C, fully equipped kitchen, water in the fridge and espresso capsules provided.
Very central location, with lots of places to eat and drink nearby. A bit shady at night but quite safe in reality.
I shall definitely keep going back!

In the center, dirty streets, drug addicts shooting up downstairs. Dirty and old building, the hotel on the 6th. floor. Dirty room. As an advantage, the metro is 5 minutes away and many attractions are within walking distance. I would not go back.

Myrto was exceptional in our visit in Athens, I would recommend the stay here for anyone visiting Athens. She arranged our transit to the airport and recommended a mini breakfast box right before our flight back to the U.S. I love you Greece 🇬🇷 and warm welcomes you have my wife and I during our honeymoon.

We as a family checked in this so called 4 star hotel but were shocked to see the very average facilities.
Especially the reception people had poor communication skills and were  very rude .
The hotel room was the smallest size with the just minimal facilities and very little space available to move around.
The bath cubicle was just good enough for an average build person to stand and I hit the glass door more number of times than my heart rate while taking the bath . Water was leaking from the lower end to spoil the dry area.
We were shocked to know that even the first day water bottles were chargeable which we were not informed at the time of checking.
Even the additional coffee sachet was available @5£/ sachet ( such thing which we never experienced in the last 30 years).
We had rhe worst experience in this hotel & We strongly DON'T recommend this hotel to anyone.
Excellent option in Athens!!! Warm and kind staff. Clean room, outstanding view from the rooftop restaurant and plenty of options for breakfast! It's a 5/5 recommendation for your trip in Athens.

The location and service were nice but the rooms had such thin walls. We can hear people talking in the hallway and the room. We also heard vacuuming / cleaning from upstairs room at 7:30 am. Not sure if this is correct time to do this.

We stayed at the Polis Grand a few times during our trip in Greece. The rooms were comfortable, and the staff were all very friendly and helpful! I wanted to call special attention to Costas! He was there almost every time we were in the lobby, and he made a point to remember us and greet us with familiarity, making the check in / check out process feel a lot smoother! I really appreciated him!!

place_name: Kin Athens - A Home Away From Home
review_text: Excellent from start to finish. Stratos is a wonderful host who personally welcomed us despite the late hour of arrival. The apartments are very well furnished and laid out. Pretty much all Athens' areas of interest are within walking distance and the surrounding streets have a wonderful selection of high quality cafes. Faultless experience.

place_name: Kin Athens - A Home Away From Home
review_text: We loved this apartment hotel. It’s a small group of rooms occupying a floor of a building in the Omenia neighborhood of Athens. The owner is gracious, helpful, and welcoming and good with communication. The apartment itself was impeccably clean, modern and luxurious. Many lovely little details made the stay very comfortable for us. The building looks a little unassuming from the outside but was safe and in a perfect section of town. Because it was so central street noise is present but white noise on phone blocked it out well. Don’t hesitate and stay here if they have availability!

place_name: Kin Athens - A Home Away From Home
review_text: Very clean apartment, lobby etc. Don’t judge a book by its cover! Street view not great!  Greeted by welcoming and very friendly host! All the necessary utilities, lovely spacious rooms and separate bathrooms, kitchen, living area. Air conditioned rooms separately controlled, comfy beds. All in all impressed added to a great trip!

place_name: Kin Athens - A Home Away From Home
review_text: Fantastic, large, two-bedroom, two-bathroom (excellent showers) apartment with a kitchen and a living room. Three-zone air conditioning. Two Wi-Fi routers. Free laundry room. Safe. Clean. Great service. They left us a bottle of champagne and chocolates for our anniversary. Extremely modern yet comfortable. Nearby restaurants. Located only about a €5 taxi ride to the Acropolis. I wish this was a franchise all over the world. One of the best in-city places we’ve ever stayed at anywhere.

place_name: Kin Athens - A Home Away From Home
review_text: We stayed at Kin Athens in November, even we arrived in the appartments very early, they help us immediatly. It was very kind. The location is perfect, you can walk everywhere from Kin Athens. The coffe machine and capsules details was just perfect. It was a very good choice. Thank you to inhanse our stay

DO NOT STAY HERE! Only gave 1 star because I cant give a 0. Let me explain to you what a bug party is. This hotel isn’t for humans, it’s for cockroaches. I was greeted by 3 cockroaches in the bathroom. Killed them real quick and everything seemed fine. We come back at night and I open the bathroom door and see families of cockroaches scrambling all over the place, varying from small to large. Then in the bedroom a nice 6-7 cockroaches are enjoying their midnight nap and also a few in our bags which were left open while we were gone. If you want to stay with cockroaches this is the place for you. You can easily cuddle up with 20-30 cockroaches if you want to. This hotel should be shutdown. The owners & whoever else manages this hotel are straight up crazy. Don’t do it to yourself.

Budget hotel. Better than hostels, you can get a decent room with own bathroom. Good experience. Personnel were professional and atmosphere in the hotel was good. Area is a bit shady, but quite close to Monastiraki.

Staying here should be a safety hazard. The whole hotel smells like mold and there are cockroaches everywhere. The shower was unusable and we decided sleeping at the airport would be better than sleeping with the cockroaches. Spend an extra $20 on someplace other than this nightmare hotel.

When I checked in I was a bit confused that I didn't have to sign anything. I got my keys, got up to the room and first I recognized that the cleanliness was very bad; on the floor were crumbs or something like that, especially under the bed it didn't seemed to be cleaned quiet a while.
Then the bedposts were a bit damaged so I was able to look into it, where I saw some people left rubbish (plastik, paper and that stuff).
Then there wasn't any welcome card or "please do not disturb" sign or any information about the city or that kind of things..which aren't necessary of course, but always a nice thing to have.

I soon realized that the hotel really has got wifi, but the sad thing about this is that the room which I had was out of the Wifi's reach...and that's concerning because the Wifi was a reason for me to book this hotel.
Also there was just one hanger in the locker and the overall furniture was quiet used and held simple (e.g. there was no chair for the desk).
The good thing is that the air condition was running good and not that loud.

Therefore the living/sleeping room is in my opinion a 2/5.

Continuing with the bath; not that good.
all outflows haven't to be cleaned quiet a long time. There was a random toilet paper roll at the mirror and the sower had some damages that looked filthy, while on one wall there someone had written something on it.
The toilet was looking fine though.

I'd give it a 2,5/5, because, well, it was okay and it had everything that's necessary for a bathroom.

But the one thing I find disquisting is that once you get into the hotel room/ the bath room and turn the light on, there were insects, like small cockroaches, everywhere quickly hiding. Every time I returned to the room I first tried to get rid of all these insects but 1. they hid too fast for me to get them and 2. there were just as many the next day as it had been the day before.

That makes the experience at this hotel quiet bad for me, eventhough the people there were quiet friendly. Also the noise of the street and the near place with a metro station (the location was good) were not to hear so one could sleep good once the insects are out of the mind.
It just seems that this hotel needs to modernize.

I wouldn't recommend it as it is today, which is why I just stayed there three nights instead of the week I planned there.
Not as bad as I read from other reviews, fair enough for a 2 star hotel, sheets and towels are clean, room is clean too. WiFi is working fine and fast though am at 3F. Veranda is a consolation.

We stayed for 3 nights together with the group I had and I can say that we have all been extremely satisfied.
The breakfast is satisfying.
The rooms are large and with a balcony.
Always clean, and sheets/towels changed every day.
What can one say about the owners. Excellent people where they really bring out the feeling of hospitality.
We highly recommend and hope to meet again soon.
Thank you very much members of Sismanidis Travel.

The location is really good, the metro is close. For us it was not loud from the outside.
Sadly the shower’s water pressure was terrible.
It was a nice apartment after all. Just missing it natural lights.

3 days in this studio apartment which is very noisy during the night.. despite being sold as soundproof.. 😨 the bathroom flooded with every shower because the slope of the water drain is completely wrong.. the location is good but definitely not there we will return and we could not recommend it.. the cost for 3 nights was €310.00..

You get what you pay for. 200 eur for 6 days, you probably already know this is the cheapest option for a hotel in Athens. Location-wise it's magnificent. Next to the Omonia metro station with easy connection to Acropolis, Plaka, Monasteraki and other sites. I expected rooms to be a bit shabby, so that wasn't an issue for me. The cleaning ladies are super nice and have regularly maintained our room. Slightly hiccoughy WiFi but we have unlimited data in the EU so it wasn't an issue. Air-con worked flawlessly and it was included in the price.
Not bad, considering downtown location, you got everything, prices ok, no bed bugs, water was warm, bathroom clean, WI Fi slow though, but old school hotel is recommended...

Bed bugs. That’s the only criteria that I have in a hotel, that it not have bed bugs. I did get bit by bed bugs here and it’s very itchy & painful now, so I wish I’d stayed somewhere else.  Bed bugs aside, I encountered the following Claridge Hotel:

-there’s only sheets on the bed and an optional old blanket from WWII in the closet
-a tremendous amount of noise
-vagrants/addicts/refugees milling around the area (and pulling their pants down right out front pissing)
-a toilet that continuously ran and bathroom light bulbs that burnt out
-a paint job that hasn’t been touched up since the Berlin Wall was still up
-weak wifi that you have to compete with others to get to a website
-old key system where you leave the metal key with the front desk when you go out.

None of these things would have bothered me enough to cancel the stay except for the bed bugs. Of course I inspected the sheets when we arrived but I didn’t see any so I didn’t mind the 70 year old furniture and old peeling paint.

chances are, if you’re reading this review you already know this hotel is the cheapest option in athens that’s not a hostel, and comes with a private bathroom; you’ve seen the low star reviews & you’re trying to decide if you should chance it & stay here because maybe you think everyone else is a bunch of spoiled whiny complainers.
I’m giving them a generous 2 stars because
1. the staff is nice.  both the helpful front desk & the housekeepers that do clean
2. the convenient location near the train, restaurants, stores, museums, walking distance to Acropolis
3. the cheap price, lowest on the travel booking sites
My spouse permanently revoked my hotel booking privileges after this place.   He said “if you have respect for yourself don’t stay here”. But my kids age 16 & 26 were fine with it.

Location wise, it is very accessible, a few steps away from Omonoia Station. Not to expect inside the hotel since it’s a 2 star, good for sleeping. The Old Omonoia restaurant is just outside and the food was tasteful with a good size of serving.

The 3 receptionist throughout the day were all very kind people. The rooms were alright quality with it needing a fresh lick of paint and upgraded TVs. The air conditioning was old but I saw new boxes of air conditioning in the hallway so I think they will be upgraded. The location was also great with a lovely view of a beautiful fountain and it was right next to great restaurants.

I have led multiple groups overseas. This is a fantastic hotel. Centrally located. Excellent food rooms are clean and very comfortable. The rooftop restaurant and bar has an unbelievable view of the Acropolis. Highly recommend.

Even though the hotel is an older one, service was excellent - the staff was very attentive and friendly. Breakfast and coffee were excellent and the rooftop view was a fabulous one. The elevators are busy in the morning as can be the breakfast bar at around 8 so get to there early!

Reasonably good hotel overall despite some old outlooks and slowness — your elevator in the peak morning hours can easily take 5 minutes to come. The staffs are helpful and friendly and the breakfast area is simply fantastic. The location is pretty awesome. It would’ve been lovely if the room keeping service included the water refill, especially when we stayed for four consecutive nights.

The hotel location was awesome. The view from.the top floor restaurant was amazing. The breakfast was expansive and delicious. The front entrance is elegant but our room was run down. We had a window that was covered so really did not see daylight until we ventured out. Bed was horrible and shower was challenging.
This was the worst hotel we stayed at in Greece. Avoid room 314 at all cost. It was small, smelled like Goodwill and you could feel every coil on the old mattress. Only good thing was location
AAA design in a vibrant neighborhood with bars and restaurants all around. Great experience
Excellent
Dancing musician & smiling polite staff. Karaoli is All the Money!
They canceled our reservation less than a week before our trip, without any explanation. It's inadmissible. Do not recommend.
Amazing room in the city centre. Very clean as well! I will definitely stay again in my next visit to Athens.

Everything was amazing. The house is just like in the photos, the location is easily accessible as it is very close to the metro and you can easily move wherever you want. Mr. Andreas was very cooperative and helpful, he answered quickly and I thank him very much for the service he did me with my check in! I highly recommend the apartment!

Room is simple, modern, clean.
The location is brilliant!

We stayed for 2 days, they answered all our questions immediately. Beautiful Bor apartment with elevator, within walking distance to everywhere. The room was a little small for 3 people, although we did not use it, the kitchen was fully equipped and the bathroom was of ideal size.
Great service, nice apartment. The problem is that you can't break and throw the tape into the toilet.

Had a great experience in this hostel. The rooms are clean, the staff is friendly and helpful, the breakfast is good and you can meet a lot of people from all over the world in the public areas of the hostel. I had a great time there. Highly recommend if you wanna have a great time in Athens!

Stayed for 2 days in a 4 bed mixed dorm (weekdays) during a brief visit to Athens. Very friendly and helpful staff, common areas are pretty cozy. Accommodation was affordable and breakfast was included. Around 20 min walk to the main area. Locker facilities available separately if need to keep luggage longer after the stay.
Wouldn’t recommend walking to the hostel from the train stop with luggage. Road to the main area isn’t the best, so need to be careful but the streets are mostly alive at nights with restaurants/pubs nearby. Also means it can get pretty noisy at night, especially on weekends. Rooms are pretty small and the bathroom are pretty cramped (really).

This hostel was great in terms of location and cleanliness.
The front desk service is hit/miss. They could be REALLY nice or not nice at all. I had to clean up the stove and bathroom area thrice as the hostel backpackers had made a bit of a mess, and this was completely unnoticed but the one time I left my bowl uncleaned in the sink, i got called out on. It was  unfair that everyone else did the same but didn't get told off.
I would rather have uniform treatment to all backpackers and not unfair , negative & pejorative behaviour to American girls.
Another friend had a similar experience.
I would recommend this as a back up option and not the primary on anyone's itenirary.

I stayed here for a school trip in May. Our first night there, bed bugs were crawling on me in the middle of the night. I did not sleep at all. They did nothing when brought to their attention. Unfortunately at the end of the trip, my group had to come back here. They claimed they had cleaned everything. I thought everything as fine until our 3rd day back and I began extremely itchy and was having horrible allergic reactions. It turned out that the bed bugs were not gone, and I had more than 30 bites all over my body that continued popping up for days after I came home. I was not the only one in my group that experienced this either. We were scattered throughout the hostel both times we encountered them. The customer service was awful, and clearly nothing was done as there were several days in between our stays. This hostel is also in a gross, run down part of down that is a long walk to the city center. The showers are so small you barely fit, regardless of how little you are, and the garbage in the commons was always over flowing. Do yourself a favor, avoid the bedbugs, and stay elsewhere!

Nubian hostel was perfect! I stayed in a 6-bed mixed dorm; the rooms are spacious and clean, and there are large locked storage cabinets under your bed to store your belongings. They provide free wine in the evenings which is a great opportunity to meet others staying in the hostel. The location is ~20 minute walk or a short bus ride from the main attractions around the acropolis; the neighborhood that the hostel is in is really cute and a little less touristy than some of the surrounding areas, with really good restaurants! We thoroughly enjoyed our stay, and for the price, it seems like the best option in the area!

A great experience, the appartment (floor 6) has a large balcony with fantastic views. All the equipment in the appartment new and spotless. Small living room, large kitchen, large bathroom and shower and bedroom with also access to the balcony. The waterpressure in the shower was sometimes a little less and one or more hangers near the entrance door would be nice. The location is fantasic, walking distance to Plaka and Kolonaki area. The Asklipiou area has fantastic small bars and restaurants with outdoor seating, in the evening and night very alive and great vibes all around. Next time I would stay again in Hestia for sure.
Thank you.
A really great stay, central located, clean, modern, large apartments, with every amenity you would ever need.

We stayed for almost 2 months.
Let's start with the good: the rooms are great, they are modern and pretty comfortable. The cleaning lady is very nice and helpful.
But the service here is just awful.
The so called "concierge" support number is a joke. They answer once in a few hours, in a rude manner, blaming the guests with everything and taking 0 responsibility or will to help with anything.
Examples:
1. wifi is slow/barely working. Response: "it's a problem in the whole area, they are fixing it." which is obviously incorrect when the wifi in the Cafe next door works perfectly. And this goes on for 2 months with the same answer.
2. Fire alarm ringing crazy loud for almost 3 hours in the whole building without anyone responding or fixing it. Response (after an hour): "No one told us." again, a lie. 5 people wrote them over an hour ago, and also are you serious? Why do I need to tell you the fire alarm is on?
3. Apartment is too hot because of a sunny day with direct sunlight and AC is already on warm mode in the whole building. Response: "It's not hot in the apartment, it's cold. Open a window". Obviously window is open.

And the list goes on. I think when the support improves this would be a great place, as the potential exists.

Brand new apartments, impeccably clean, quiet and with contemporary design, many amenities and everything necessary for a really nice stay. Daikin centralised AC system, Vitra/Roca bathrooms, fully equipped kitchen with dishwasher, big fridge,  really comfortable beds, blackout doors to the French windows... They also change towels every other day.
Good bonus is the common rooftop terrace with 270° view over Athens (and the Acropolis) tables and chairs, if you want to have your own breakfast or drinks there. Definitely would stay again.
The street is packed with extremely noisy bars, you won't be able to sleep until dawn, especially on weekends

Pros:
- Good location, quiet.
- Walkable, though not super central.
- Nice rooms.
Cons:
1. Absolutely no shower pressure.
2. Towels provided are either hand-sized or very small for the body.
3. The wardrobe has only 4 mismatched hangers and no shelves ;)

Very basic room furniture, dark room with an awful smell all over the hotel probably from the Airconditioning. The TV was not working, the flush toilet was broken, the hair dryer also not working. Towels and bedsheets not super white probably are old and need replacing with new ones. The location is convenient but was expensive for the quality it offers

Warning! Service prices changing on the way
After a refundable reservation I've received an email asking if I wanted the taxi at a certain price, I replied the day after asking for the service, the I got a reply after a couple of hours with a higher price.
I called them and a woman literally told me with a rude manner: the manager has changed the price, do you want it or not?
Be aware to book with them and stay in a harsh and stressed environment as normally Greece is a relaxing place to visit for tourists.

My stay for one night was HORRIBLE. It was super hot in the room and it was so noisy from the street I couldn't sleep and I got in at 3AM. The AC also didn't work. terrible experience wouldn't recommend.

We were  coming back from the islands and had to have a night in Athens before catching the flight next evening. We wanted to be close to kolonaki as I love this area and we needed a possibility to leave our luggage for few hours after check out. So we needed an accommodation with reception. Athens Way was good price - value ratio. lots of cozy cafes nearby, good supermarket and Metro - 10 min walk.

Extraordinarily clean hostel, with large, comfy beds, nice bathrooms, kind staff, and a convenient location. It is definitely NOT a party vibe though - everyone in my room was in bed with their lights off by 9:30 pm. So, if you like to go out late, you might feel like you are constantly bothering your roommates.

Long overdue, but I had the best stay here back in July/August. The owner is really attentive and really has the best team on staff - each day they went above and beyond to help me out if I needed something, ask how I was doing or suggest things to do in Athens. You can tell everyone working cares a lot about the guest experience, shout out to all of them for showing me the amazing hospitality, humor and kindness of Greek people.

The layout of the building is also set up in a way that it's easy to meet and socialize with other travelers, I really enjoyed the pub crawl and other activities. I met some really great people working and traveling here and never felt alone or unsafe as a solo female visiting from the US.

The neighborhood has plenty of things to do and felt safe even at night, especially in summer there are plenty of people out and about and restaurants and shops to check out.

The rooms are super clean and housekeeping stays on top of everything. I lost something in a bunk one night and one of the cleaning staff tracked me down to return it personally which meant a lot.

As a result of loving my stay here I ended up extending much longer and though I have stayed at a lot of hostels in my life so far but I can truly say have never really felt so at home at one as I did here, it is really special. I hope I can return someday!

My husband and I arrived in Athens early in the morning and we didn't previously booked a room. We arrived at When in Athens Hostel by google reviews, explained our situation and they immediately helped us by accommodating us in a room, totally last minute and without a reservation, and allowing us to use the facilities of the hostel for the day, paying for one diary.
The room we stayed was shared but the beds were clean, comfortable, and the bathrooms were also very clean.
Although our stay was short, we really appreciated it.

Best Hostel ever!
This was a 5-star hostel experience.
Super clean rooms, bathrooms and common areas.
Amazing customer service & very friendly people.
Super convenient location.

I will definitely come back!

LOVED this hostel! Was kicking myself for not booking it for my entire stay in Athens! Lovely common areas, super comfy sleeping rooms, and I very much enjoyed the steaming bags protocol upon entry! Not a far walk to Syntagma, lots of great food places nearby as well! If I ever return to Athens I won’t bother booking elsewhere!
Amazing stay and comfort, fantastic hosts!!

Stayed in the Athens center property at Didotou 30 complex. Very nice accommodation, conveniently located and quiet ( slept with A/C on and closed windows). Very secure building. Clean and modern.  Gym is small but functional. Used free washer and drier in the building. We did not have a car and parking is a nightmare in Athens. Marina, the host, was very accommodating and allowed us an early check in. I a nutshell, well maintained and managed accommodation in center of Athens.

This is the only negative note:
Booked for 4 people but only 3 beds were made so we set up another one. . The positive note:
- the rooms were clean and tidy
-we liked the welcome kit and the water in the fridge.
-the house equipped with a good secure lock.
The area is certainly suitable for Young Adults.
Transport:
Nearby vehicles and everything well connected to the center, sea and acropolis.
I Suggest my Greek Vacations.

My Greek Vacations City Apartments in the Didotou address in Athens is perfectly located near the main city attractions. We walked about one mile to get to the Acropolis and Acropolis Museum. The apartment was very clean and had a nice terrace where we could enjoy a cup of coffee or a beer in the afternoon. The neighborhood has many outdoor cafes and restaurants with great food, which fill up at night with people from all ages, it's a very lively scene. Marina, the super host, was always very attentive and responded to all our questions very quickly. We surely would go back there next time!

I was extremely happy with the cozy apt. very clean and organized ,
very good location and pretty convenient.
Marina the host was very very helpful and prompt in answering the phone when it was needed. I give her excellent remark.I was supposed yo stay three nights but for business reason I had to leave earlier! I highly recommend this apartment to potential travelers .Good luck and enjoy your stay in Athens my favorite City.

Pretty nice hotel. The bathroom is a bit small and the hot water doesn’t last long so if you’re more than just 1 person, it’s hard to get properly washed. The kitchenette was handy. The gym is functional though very humid and warm, and I appreciate having access to the washer dryer for cleaning all of our dirty laundry! The hotel manager was also extremely helpful and allowed us to stow our luggage after checkout and extend checkout a little later since we had a late flight out. The hotel is also pretty walkable to a bunch of tourist sites without being in a super touristy area and getting price gouged on food or drinks.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Athens Flair Hotel and it was an exceptional experience from start to finish.
The bedroom was a true sanctuary of comfort and style. The moment I walked in, I was impressed by the elegant decor and meticulous attention to detail. The bed was incredibly comfortable, adorned with plush pillows and high-quality linens that ensured a restful night's sleep. The room was also spacious and well-lit, with a cozy seating area that was perfect for relaxing with a book or enjoying the view from the large windows.
What truly set this hotel apart, however, was the breakfast service. Having breakfast delivered directly to the room was a delightful convenience. The meal was beautifully presented and included a variety of delicious options, from freshly baked pastries to perfectly cooked eggs. The fruit was fresh, and the coffee was rich and aromatic, providing the perfect start to the day. The attention to detail in both presentation and flavor made it a standout experience.
Overall, my stay at Athens Flair Hotel was marked by impeccable service, luxurious comfort, and outstanding amenities. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone seeking a top-notch experience.

We had the pleasure of staying at Athens Flair during our three day trip to Athens, and I still reminisce about this hotel! Everything was perfect from the start, what stood out to me was their superb customer service - big thank you to Elias and his team for making us feel welcomed and comfortable during our whole stay! Location is perfect, you’re tucked away in a charming corner on the outskirts of Kolonaki, yet steps away from shops, café and top rated restaurants and only a 15 minute walk to the centre. You’re close to the main attractions but able to escape the noise! Everything was perfect, super clean, we had a beautiful balcony and a charming rooftop - look forward to my return in the near future!

Hello,
We were convinced by the location and the statement „world luxury award winner“. The hotel is lovely situated in a small alley and a lot of places to-go a in the proximity.

The hotel has historical charm. We booked a room with a whirlpool at the terrace. At the first night, we were negatively surprised by the level of noise. Our whirlpool kept droning, an outside metal spiral staircase felt like being next to our bed and overall the environment was busy. The old building isn’t soundproof and we think it doesn’t fit to the statement „luxury“, considering the price for the room.

One of the best hotels ever! Location is super, the hotel has 6 beautiful rooms. Our room was big clean and beautiful. Nice terrace and jacuzzi. Breakfast is super. The stuff are professional. We had great time. But....no elevator and a lot of stairs. So think about it.

I don’t normally write a review for a place I have stayed but on this occasion I have too!

What an amazing experience I’ve had the staff couldn’t do enough for you! The breakfast was always delicious and room kept spotless. If you need a relaxing break away from Day to day problems then don’t hesitate just book it! You won’t be disappointed by the Athens Flair.

An awesome place to stay in Athens. Its an apartment with a great view of Acropolis, & an exceptional customer service, they are not physically around but are very prompt on their reply to our inquiries. The place was super clean, & has everything we need for a family of 5 (3 adults, 2 teens), fridge, microwave & utensils. Also close to restaurants. Will definitely recommend a stay here. Bonus: laundry is available & high speed internet is phenomenal

The  location is central, up to 20 min. Walk to any major attraction. The apartment was very clean and seemed pretty new, fully equiped with everything we needed which was more than enough to accomodate a family of 4. Would definetally reccomend to stay and surely come back again.

Excellent place to stay for families with 2 or 3 kids, the rooms are spacious and well equipped. The location is a short walk to the Center in a safe district of the city. The concierge team was always online and ready to answer any questions. The roof top terrace overlooking the acropolis was the cherry on top for the stay.

Clean modern apartments close to center.   Apartment was well equipped with all that is necessary.

Lovely view of acropolis from roof terrace!
Very clean and convenient accommodation. We had a problem with the safe lock. But the concierge came to the rescue so fast. Location is very good. I recommend it for couples and solo travelers.

Central spot, one block away from metro station. Walking distance of all the main Athens attractions, yet super quiet at night. Spotless and modern facilities. Friendly and polite staff, helped us with early check in AND late check out when asked. 10/10 recommended.
Thanks for everything! Amazing apartment and super clean! The hosts are really pros!

Perfect for staying a few days in the city. The location is very convenient as it allows you to go to the acropolis area in 15 minutes on foot, there are also numerous bus and metro stops. The accommodation room was also very good.  The staff was very friendly and competent. Advised!

Large room and very comfortable bed.
Excellent cleaning.
Coffee refill every day.
The location and communication by metro is excellent

It is a wonderful studio, beautiful, clean, the location is excellent, close to the meter, close to everything, 5 or 10 minutes walk, you are Ermou Monastiraki, the master, the city, helpful, kind, whatever I wanted, he helped me!!
And one last thing, he has put extra glass from the window and I can't hear anything from the streets!!!!!
I will definitely come again soon again here I recommend it with 100000

Thoroughly enjoyed our 2 nights and Monsieur Didot.

Located within walking distance of most of the sites- but far enough away to enjoy some peace and quiet.

We booked "The Tale"- a small suite, with separate living room (sofa turns into single bed)- beautifully decorated, with all we would need. We also had the benefit of our own entrance.

Breakfast was delivered promptly to the room every morning- the orange juice was a solid 10/10.

We enjoyed an evening drink on the (tiny) roof terrace, and explored much of this funky area.

We hope to return on our next Athens trip.

Highly recommended! This was the best accommodations of the 4 hotels I stayed in during my trip to Athens. We booked 2 room types and I personally preferred the downstairs suite. The service was attentive. The breakfast was impressive and served on the atrium terrace with all fresh local ingredients.

We really enjoyed every moment at the place. Staff are very friendly and helpful. Walking distance is15-20 minutes away from all major attractions.

You also have the breakfast option: either at your own room or at the terrace. The terrace is not very big, but good enough to enjoy a peaceful and tasty breakfast. Highly recommened!
Stylish boutique hotel, with a truly delicious room delivered breakfast. We stayed in Ode Room, with its own private roof terrace. Loved it.

We cannot say good enough things about our stay at Monsieur Didot. The staff was excellent - very communicative and helpful. Our room was a comfortable, quiet, safe space with room to exist beyond just the bed. The breakfast wasn't extraordinary, but it was great to start our mornings with. The location is fantastic, too. We wouldn't hesitate to stay there again and recommend it to others!
An amazing place!! Stunningly designed to offer not only comfort but also an aura of luxurious simplicity. Perfect place to stay while visiting Athens.
A true diamond in Athens !!! 5+ star accommodation in the hotest spot of Athens , amazing modern interior design , and the most important excellent hospitality !!!
Perfect!
Value for money option. Beautiful and clean space with neat details that make the difference. Good communication with the managers.

very well equipped and quiet apartment despite the view of the street
all amenities around and for me 10 minutes walk from my work

We stayed in the attic suite and it was perfect for the three of us. The room was well appointed with coffee machine, robes and a mini fridge.  The beds were very comfortable and the check in/out process very smooth. Breakfast was delightful and communication via WhatsApp was very easy.  We loved the neighborhood- Kolonaki is very charming, quiet, and centrally located to all that Athens has to offer.  Nothing was more than a 15 minute walk and we enjoyed the coffee shops and restaurants in the neighborhood- I can’t wait to come back - this will be our spot.

Great rooms; clean and comfortable.

Location great also. Easy to walk to Ermou .

Very nice stay at Sir Athens. The room was big and nicely decorated. Staff is well organized and very friendly.
Special mention to the breakfast on the rooftop in the sister hotel at 2mn walk.
Last but not least it's well located, at walking distance of all major sites.
I'll definitely book again at Sir Athens

My family and I stayed at Sir Athens for three nights. It is a lovely small hotel.
The rooms are very spacious and the beds big and comfortable.
The design is modern and beautiful.
It is very very clean.
The bathroom is small but functional.
The service was excellent and the staff were very friendly.
The location was good.
I would have liked some closet space so I can unpack but other than that we really enjoyed the stay.
It is good place for  have haircuts in Athens.Georgino gave me best service.

Great accomodation for my family,nice decorated ,fully equipped and super clean.
Location served us the most,so close to public transport if you want to go far but even a walk in the neighborhood could keep you entertained.
Coming to Athens twice a year and from now on accomodation will be settled from the start!
Not safe. Anarchists often make attacks throwing paint and breaking the glass door at the entrance which makes it feel very dangerous

Hestia Asklopiu 9, Suite 602 with City View, Athens (2024)

The Hestia Asklopiu Apartment in Athens impresses with its modern furnishings and impeccable cleanliness. The suite is cozy and furnished to a high standard, including a practical kitchen corner with everything you need - a refrigerator, microwave, kettle and Nespresso machine are available. The quiet air conditioning is particularly pleasant and works efficiently without blowing you out of bed. The bed is very comfortable and wide, which ensures a restful sleep.

The bathroom is small, but absolutely sufficient and modern. A real highlight of the suite is the balcony with a breathtaking view of the city, Lykavittós Mountain and the Acropolis. It is a real pleasure to sit on the terrace in the evening, a glass of wine in your hand, and look out at the illuminated Acropolis - an unforgettable experience.

The location is excellent, very central and yet quiet. The area is pleasant, with many restaurants, bars and shopping nearby. It may not be the most beautiful corner of Athens, but it offers a relaxed atmosphere that is very pleasant after the hustle and bustle in Agora, Plaka or the Acropolis. If you're not too sensitive, you can even sleep with the window open.

Many of the main attractions are within easy walking distance - the Acropolis, Agora, Plaka, the cable car to Lykavittós, as well as numerous museums and shopping streets. Another plus is the cafe right below the apartment that offers great cappuccino and frapé. There are also several takeaways with good food and excellent restaurants nearby. Despite the central location, the area is surprisingly quiet, although the lively shopping area begins just 500 meters away.

The connection is also first-class: the metro, which is directly connected to the airport, is only 300 meters away. There is also a hop-on hop-off bus stop nearby. However, you can avoid the red line if you can walk well. Otherwise, exploring consists of waiting for the bus and a slow crawl through Athens' traffic jams.

A small disadvantage is the lack of a reception. Those with an early flight will have to wait until 3 p.m. for entry, and unfortunately there is no option to store luggage on site. However, this small detail does not spoil the overall excellent impression.

Conclusion: The Hestia Asklopiu Apartment is a clear recommendation - the terrace in particular is a must! All in all, a fantastic place to stay for an unforgettable stay in Athens.
Very well-equipped rooms, excellent location and communication. I recommend!

We loved our short stay here! It was a short walk to the bars and easily walkable to the acropolis. The room was very private, clean with good facilities (eg. Coffee machine). Special shout out to the staff who were fantastic - Anna Maria helped us in a stressful taxi situation and thanks to her we made our connecting ferry. Next time i am in Athens i will be coming back!

We had excellent service from Anna Maria. The room was clean, Comfy bed, Good shower, We had plenty of toiletries provided A nespresso coffee machine, kettle, water, fridge. It’s quiet and not too far from the Centre, about 15 minutes of walking. Thank you! ✨

My partner and I had a short but very good stay at CITYLUXE. Brand new place with great facilities, and meticulous detail put into it. Ana Maria is a very good host and the attention was amazing throughout our whole stay trying her best to meet our needs and definitely going the extra mile to satisfy her visitors.

Wow! Is all I can say. What a fantastic hotel. However this is mainly due to the incredible customer service we were given by Anna Marie. She was so helpful and accommodating. Thank you for making our stay and our last day peaceful and relaxing. The location is a little further away from the tourist traps, but is lovely and quiet. The rooms are 'luxe' as the title says. Thank you again!

The staff was incredibly welcoming and accommodating. The rooms are modern, clean and the beds were comfortable. Great location which the neighborhood felt safe. A close walk to restaurants and the sights in Athens.

Beautiful apartment set in one of the most stunning areas of Athens.  Definitely one of the most amazing apartments I stayed in whilst visiting Athens and definitely one of my favourite areas of Greece.  Booking and access was easy and the apartment contained everything I needed for my stay.  Loved it and would definitely stay there again.

Simply the best! Chic decorated, fully equipped, crystal clean apartment in a building of famous architects in the posh area of Athens (Kolonaki), near to all important sightseeing. Loved every corner of it!

Μodern luxurious apartment amazingly decorated and equipped, completely clean and provided with all necessary. Perfectly located in the posh neighborhood of Kolonaki, in 5-20 min walking distance from all major sightseeing of the city. Highly recommended!
Simply the best apartment of Athen's centre I have ever been! Amazing decoration, luxuriously furnished, fully equipped! Best ever!
Fabulous,highly designed,spotless clean and convinient apartment,in walking distance of all important sightseeing of Athens,plus the best host ever!We had a great time and for sure we will come back!

Spent one night, as i was in for a meeting in the area.  Pleasantly surprised.  Rooms are nicely designed.  Service was very friendly (checked in 3 hours early).  Location is in a very nice residential area (the prime minister lives nearby so don't be alarmed by the large number of policemen in the area).  It is located on a hill so be prepared to walk up a lot of stairs or steep roads to get there when walking around. Left early so can't comment on the breakfast.  Only negative - the bed mattress is very soft.  Solid 4 star, but value for money its a 5.

Great service and the view from the rooftop barpool area is amazing. Sadly the room was not very well maintained with stuck drawers blocking the mini-fridge and paint chafing. The pool is quite rundown but exists. Breakfast was nice with decent options. Overall not a bad place, made better by the helpful staff.

Friendly staff, helpful as needed (brought us an extra pillow at night) and were very kind upon check in. 20ish min walk to sightseeing areas.

Definitely wasn't the worst hotel I've stayed in but definitely not the best, and for the price I would have expected better. The site makes it seem much more luxurious and high-end than it really is.

The whole hotel smelled so strongly of synthetic perfumed cleaning products to the point where I could smell it from the street before we even walked into the hotel. I'm definitely sensitive to strong smells but it was almost unbearable and I instantly got a headache, and my partner even mentioned it (he doesn’t usually get bothered by smells). Woke up feeling badly from the chemicals.

The room was the same - strong smell and not the cleanest. At first glance it was fine, large room with separate sitting/TV area, however it was clear the room had a decent amount of wear and tear and hadn't been fixed in awhile. Not super clean, not the most comfortable bed, very odd bathroom that leaked, rusty.

We booked this hotel just for one night between airport travel from the islands and out of Athens, but if we'd been staying longer we would have found a different hotel.

I had high hopes for The Duke Hotel based on the advertising, but I was severely disappointed. The marketing paints a luxurious and welcoming experience, but in reality, it was far from that. The room was nothing like what was shown in the pictures—smaller, outdated, and lacked the amenities they promised. Customer service was also a nightmare. When I tried to raise my concerns, the staff were indifferent, unhelpful. No effort was made to address the issues or improve the experience. Overall, it was a waste of money and time. I wouldn’t recommend this hotel to anyone!

I stayed here for one night and found the service to be very lovely, all the staff were friendly and helpful.
The room was not very clean in my opinion and the whole place seemed to be a bit run down. The bar furniture upstairs and the pool seemed unkept. As well as just old build up in the rooms, I was inclined to wear my flip flops in the shower.
The internet didn’t work in my room either and there was very poor reception.
I booked this place as a treat to use the sauna but I didn’t even go, there was a strong smell down there and it looked very poorly maintained.
I personally wouldn’t say it is worth the price and I would not recommend if you appreciate a clean space.

great alternative to a hotel, in the best place in Athens. Very calm and and easy access to any point of the city, thanks to the nearby Metro station. The prices in comparison to a hotel are very good. Some people may not like the place as the interiors haven't been renovated since the opening in the 70s. I personally love the rosewood elements, it's very authentic and vintage. The apartments are spacious, with a bedroom, a living room and a separate kitchen. The balcony is enormous, with an unobstructed view to the city and mount Emitos. In front of the hotel, there's a church and office building related to it. it's really quiet.

I stayed here while looking for a last minute booking after I had to extend my stay in Athens for 1 more day. Although the location was nice and the staff were friendly, I have to say I was a bit disappointed upon entering the room due to how old the room and furniture were...and I don't mean old as in vintage classic way. The carpet had stains accumulated from 20 years and the furniture/walls/kitchen/overall interior looked like a cheap motel from the 1960's. I made the booking based on the 4-star description I saw on Google. Little did I know, those were user review 4 stars and not classification of the hotel as a 4-star hotel.

It's has good position, but it's still 40min by foot to Acropolis. The biggest problem of this hotel - it's really old: hard to open or close balcony or windows and stuff like that. But it's nice place to crash for few nights.

Large apartment, clean, well-equipped and with 24-hour reception. The location is excellent, walking distance to two train stations, and to the city centre. A safe neighbourhood. I will stay here again.

Very old. No renovation since 19xx? However Room is very big and clean.wifi. Hot water. Kitchen. And balcony over look city. Location is OK. When we stayed may 1. No aircon it's hot daytime. Big problem for us.
It convenient for me

Great house, renovated facilities, quality moments, true friendships, people with values ​​and ideals. At a time when young people today face difficulties and problems of orientation, where ideological confusion and value depreciation prevail. This particular boarding school with its staff is a real support for the young person who is preparing to spread his wings for the struggle of life. Many Congratulations!
Regarding the women's boarding school. I appreciate the help that was given to me while I was there. However, I think it is necessary for some institutions to be modernized!
I was there too, from 1995 to 1997 with Mr. Psaltakis, Panagiotis Alexandridis and........ Matoula in the kitchen.

Not just a place to stay!
They are our second family!

place_name: The Old Tailor Factory white Room hotel
review_text:
Price - quality wise a good deal! Communication was clear and everything was as promised.

Please note, toilet & bathroom are very small, there was a beeping sound, and the street can be noisy, but none of it was too bad
Very nice hotel, friendy stuff, nice location.
Nice and convenient place to go.
Excellent and clean I will choose it again !!!
Dot!!!

We had a great time, everything went well, the structure was easy to find, the keys were very simple to find, thanks to the information you receive via Booking, new apartment with a wonderful terrace, clean, everything in order and attention to detail, television with Netflix in the language Italian for some Films, excellent solution for those who need a small, clean and comfortable new apartment. Beatiful!

In a very good location. You can find a bakery, supermarket, cafe, food, pharmacy right in the same block.
Small space, clean with heating and hot water. Large terrace for the warmer months.
I wish it had a cot for infants.

A wonderful apartment with a wonderful terrace and view, new construction, right below it has very nice bars and very close to the center. It is also a very neat apartment and very comfortable. Highly recommend, would definitely go again!
Fantastic terrace, new construction very close to nice bars and restaurants. Very friendly environment and very clean place. I highly recommend it
Excellent view, clean and well-kept space!! Very welcoming and pleasant hostess!!
I really enjoyed my stay here. Everything was clean and in good shape. Metro, bus and groceries are all accessible by foot in max 10 min.

Great little apartment. It is equipped with everything you need (hairdryer, coffee machine, fridge, washing machine). Equipped also with air-conditioned for the summer. It is clean and beautifully decorated. The neighborhood is quiet. On the same street walking distance you can find bakery , coffee shops and mini market. Reasonably priced for what it is. Metro (underground) station is walking distance about 450meters away. Problem free communication with the manager.
Nice place. Near is small supermarket and 600 meters to Ampelokipi metro station.
Although the apartment is very nice, neat and clean, in a good area, and with many traffic possibilities since you can go everywhere. And many options for food and coffee in the area
Neat and clean, the rooms are a bit small, but if you're planning on sightseeing, it's a very good place.  Close to the metro and public transport
Comfortable!!!

This is the 2nd time I stay there & definitely not the last. Great location, not in the middle of hustle & bustle yet close to everything. Other hotel’s reviews mentioned doggy streets, noise from the street or the next room but it was not the issue at all. Very clean & still brand new. The staff are very friendly & helpful. Value for money is great. This will be my hotel whenever I am in Athens

The bed (wood) was horrible, made noise at every turn. Mattress was horrible, the worst i experienced ever. We discovered the mattress was kept in place by wood boards (one which was almost broken), instead of a regular mattress holder. We slept very poorly. Also, there is noise from the street (scooters etc) and the construction acrosss the street (starts at 7 in the morning), ear plugs were absolutely necessary.
Bathroom is very small, 1 sqm, we are slim and couldnt' fit more than one at once.
There is no sign for the property entrance, but it can be figured out eventually.
Location is recently renovated and appliances are all new and shiny, nice kitchinette but no stove (strange since it did come with pots and pans)

Amazing apartment, very clean and cozy, staff was very nice and helpful, my family enjoyed the stay and we recomend it. It is not in the very center, but well connected through metro line. Parking available and free.

New and extremely clean rooms in the Center of Athens. Open space parking available. Location nice and quiet. Room was spacious with nice balcony. It would be perfect if there was a small table to eat or work with a laptop.

Brand-new apartments with high level of facilities. Well located  near the center (nearby of the underground stations) . We stayed there for 4 nights and i strongly suggest it. The staff was very kind and helpful

We chose this hotel despite its location 3 km from the city centre, as the room was advertised as soundproof. Unfortunately, this wasn't the case. From the 4th floor, we could hear every noise from the street, to the point we thought the window might be open. It made it very difficult to sleep. Although we had requested a quiet room, we had to spend one night there before being moved to a quieter one the next day. The staff efficiently arranged for a room change the following day. Avoid room 406, as it faces the street and has poor window insulation, allowing significant noise from outside and heat loss. On a positive note, the reception staff were polite and helpful, and there's a car park right next to the hotel. The room was spacious. The metro station (blue "touristic" line) is 10 min. away.

I spent 2 weeks there. The part I loved the most was the staff: the waiters were kind and lovely with me; the service was optimally and the room has been cleaned several times. Moreover, the hotel has got many board games that I loved playing with my friends. I loved the place!
I spent two weeks at the Hotel. Staff is really nice and willing to help. I think cleaning could be improved. Send my regards to Giorgos.

I’ve spent 15 nights in this hotel and I really enjoyed this experience and the place although the rooms were too small to fit in 3 of us. Services were good and food was acceptable especially the large breakfast. I’ve also liked the tranquility of the people in this place. It deserves a lot.

Excellent location if you visit Athens! The staff was truly amazing trying to make your stay an unforgettable experience.  The hotel has an amazing breakfast buffet with lots of Greek delicacies. Thank you Stratos Vasilikos! We will be back soon!
I recommend this well-equipped accommodation.

Ok apartment. Extremely hot in the summer and the air only blows in the main living area, not the bedroom.  A key is needed to enter and leave the apartment. Same with the building.  Once you check out of the unit, and leave the key as instructed there is no way to get out of the building unless someone is coming in.

Horrible experience (business trip).
1. Rooms are not as depicted in the photos, are way more decadent (3 stars would be honest)
2. Receptionist doesn't spesk english. Hello? 4-stars hotel?
3. The day after I checked in, at night I casually run into the receptionist, who in a rude manner asks me repeatedly "will you pay?". The whole reservation was arranged booked and paid by the travel agency, who provided the credit card details to charge the money. After 2 hours calling the agency and someone else from the hotel who could speak english I could solve the issue.

The hotel isn't matching the expectations for what a 4 star hotel should be. The building is like  screaming 'welcome to the 70s', the furniture in the rooms is outdated, the balconies are big but the view consists of the TV antennas of the opposite buildings, the reception is closed between 11 to 8, but having said that,  the hotel is actually a good deal. Let's see...
The location is very central and easily reached via the subway.
The price is unbeatable for the area, especially for families with children.
Surprisingly, while the rooms display this mismatched furniture and totally lack of decoration, they do have a kitchenette and a jacuzzi!!!!!!!
Only if you experience the summer in the 40C Athens, you can appreciate what a bonus the jacuzzi really is!

The location was really comfortable for taking the bus or the subway. The room was really spacious. As it concerns the service, most of the time there was no one in the reception and we had to call into a specific number for service. I would prefer it if there would be someone on reception.

It is a typical Greek three stars hotel, so don't expect more. Could be cleaner, some things don't work or fall apart. But the staff was very helpful, the breakfast is much better than a "continental" Italian one, and I appreciated haveing a microwave and a kettle in my room. Thus, I give 4 starts to a 3 stars hotel.

I was very pleased with my stay at the Art Suites Athens Hotel. I have chosen it in the past more than once. The hospitality, clearness and kindness of the owners and staff makes me visit it again. One of the best hotels in terms of quality/price and location. Great deal!!!

It’s really the best place in Athens
clean, beautiful place 🫶
Very comfortable and neat apartment equipped with everything you need. In the surrounding area you can find everything. The hostess was very friendly and helpful!

I am very pleased with my stay in this apartment near the center of Athens, it was what we needed, clean, beautiful, quiet and relaxing. Very friendly hosts! I will come again, thank you for the fast service!
A wonderful hospitality very close to the city center. I hope I can visit again.
Poly aneto diamerisma me ola ta aparaithta. Eykolh prosvash se sygkoinwnies.

Great apartment, clean, modern (excellent WiFi) and full with amenities, even a hair-dryer!
Very nice view to the park just in front of the building.
I also liked the location, it is 10min from the center of Athens with bus station a few meters away. The metro station is also close.
Mary is very polite and helpful, thank you for the hospitality!

The appartment was amaizing. Excellent value of money. MARY was very cooperative and kind. Every thing was better than in appears in photos
I do highly recommend this appartment. 👌
Great stay, kind hosts, comfortable space. Definitely a place I would stay again and again. Mary, thank you very much.

My stay in this apartment was 10'!!!! it is exactly as in the photos, comfortable, clean and fully equipped!! Mary and I had excellent communication! I highly recommend it and hope it will be available again on my next visit to Athens!

Honestly, if there were 6 stars, I would easily give it.
I used this particular accommodation, during my visit to Athens, in the context of some scheduled medical examinations, at the Laiko hospital.
First of all, let me mention what you find in a strategic point regarding the specific hospital, as well as the Children's hospital.
In an Athens that was baking from the heat, the apartment was cool because of the A/C, which it has.
It is also quiet, sunny, spotlessly clean and in a good location for MMM use.
Communication with the owner was excellent.
I think that even though the photos may not do it justice, it is clearly better in reality.
I recommend it unreservedly and I will definitely visit it again, when there is a reason to come back to Athens.
Really helps children!!!
Scientific and holistic coverage of children's mental health needs
It provides very good services to children
Excellent everyone!

The owner was a friendly and flexible person 🙏 I really appreciate this!

The place is new, wifi works well, hot water comes out of the shower with great pressure, the subway is next door, there is a kettle and tea/cereal available.  We liked the place and will come again 👌
Thank you for your Hospitality. Really nice place and comfortable situated nearby metro station.
Near to Subway station Nikea👍🏼
I was passing through Athens and stayed at this hostel for the night. I liked everything - the price, cleanliness and friendliness of the host👍🏼 I wish you prosperity 😊
I liked it, it was comfortable, clean and close to the metro
The best boat parking. Fully covered. Very good solution  for tenders and also for sort stops.
Good indoor parking with cheap prices.
A family business that respects its customers as a whole. Trusted consistency and straightforwardness. There is no corresponding boat space in the area and those who enter... hardly leave.

Security & trust
Proximity & friendly environment
Highly recommended

Excellent professionals.
Enclosed and secure boat parking
The studios look amazing and they are close to the Petralona metro station, which is very close to the center of Athens (Monastiraki & Syntagma) , supermarket and restaurants.
Very nice studio and the owner is also very kind. The studio is located 7 min from Petralonica station.
Convenient place, quiet neighborhood and clean beautiful rooms. The host is amazing!

Nice place!!! , convenient location, 7 minutes from the metro, next to a park with many restaurants. Clean, lots of supplies, friendly and helpful owner! If you are in Athens for a couple of days, then I recommend staying here.

Amazing hospitality, it had everything, nice place, new rooms, all the money the yard. Good wifi. I highly recommend it and for the value for money, I thought I was staying in a much more expensive place.
Comfortable clean and very elegant apartment, Mrs. Stavroula willing and very hospitable hostess. We felt like home. I highly recommend it

This is a scam, a complete tourist trap. The apartment is stinky and unappealing. When we got there, we expressed our disappointment for what we saw and they immediately offered us a refund, which they didn't give us!!!! They tricked me into not calling booking assistance by saying that they will take care of the issue. After ignoring me for two weeks they sent me a message, blackmailing me, to remove my review in order to refund me. Avoid by all means!!!!!!

The most unprofessional behavior I have ever seen . Property was informed about our late check in since booking. Two hours before our arrival, at 2330 I received a message that they won’t be able to accommodate us . We found our selfs at midnight trying to find somewhere to spend the night.

Excellent apartment, newly refurbished, very clean and comfortable. Plenty of free parking around which is a rare luxury in Athens. Great roof terrace. Very quiet though close to a lively square with excellent restaurants. Very convenient for overnight stay before or after using Piraeus port (15 min drive).

This place is easily in the worst 3 travelling experiences of my life: after I had booked my room, somebody called me right away asking for my arrival time, to which I mistakenly replied 8pm, and saying they will send me an access code soon.
