You must first pick up the key and check in at their main building, 200 meters from this apartment.

The entrance to the apartment is from a commercial area and there are some offices on the same floor (3rd).

The outside isn’t inviting but once you enter the apartment it’s much better - cost little one bedroom apartment with the basic things you need (see pictures).
There is no balcony, if you wondered.

LOCATION: perfect for going to and from the airport or the sea port, as the Main Metro station is 2 min walk from there.
Also very central to other parts of town and sightseeing.

View of the acropolis is nice although not perfect as there is a building that blocks the mountain but you can still see everything.

THE BLINDS are not blackout blinds and plenty of light still comes in when they are closed so if you wake up from light, THIS PLACE IS NOT FOR YOU 🙈

Other than that, I think it’s a nice place for this price.
it has everything I needed, spotlessly clean with a kitchenette, incredible view as seen in the photos, in the best part of Athens. everything wonderful, I will prefer it for my stay in Athens
The building is horrible, the room acceptable, location correct.

Lovely apartment in the heart of Athens. You can reach every major attraction as well as great restaurants and bars per foot. The owner was very welcoming and helpful.

Just be aware that the apartment is in the middle of a lively neighbourhood, so it can get quite noisy in the night.

We will choose this apartment definitely next time as well. :)

It was an amazing experience to stay in this apartment which is in an ideally place in the Center of everything you want to see!!! The shopping Center is at your feet and the metro station of Monastiraki is seconds away!
Very beautiful, very safe and very very clean!!!

Perfect location, right in the heart of the city, 2 minutes walk from Monastiraki Square and 5 minutes walk from Syntagma Square.
Andreas was an excellent host, he helped us with everything we needed. We arrived earlier than check in and he kept our luggage so we could have some time while the room was getting ready and the room was ready before check in time. Also our return flight was leaving at night so we needed our luggage to be stored again, late check out was also arranged. We appreciate him for his hospitality and helpfulness.

The room was more then I expected,
It is so simple to get there and to the flat even if you arrive late.
Andreas the host was so welcoming and friendly.
The communication is super with him .
The flat was super clean with a fresh scent .
The 🛌 comfortable and the 🚿 big.
Complimentary water and coffee.
I really recommend.
Forgot the price that was great too.

Aiolos Apartments are at a very prime central location in Ermou Street of Athens.
Monastiraki Square, Syntagma Square, Plaka, Acropolis, Greek Parliament, and many other places of interest are within 10-15 minutes walking distance. There are countless restaurants, coffee shops and places to shop nearby. It's a very lively area.
The apartments are beautifully made, clean and well furnished. Our host Mr. Andreas was a very nice Gentleman. He was always available and ready to help. The apartment had everything that a family would require.
I would definitely visit again.

Thank you for a wonderful stay!

The most amazing view & centrally located Apartment with everything you need.
Pictures simply do not show how amazing the views really are! Must be seen with your own eyes!

The most incredible views out over the city to the Acropolis. The building itself is in a very central location, only couple mins walk to the train station, very convenient and very central to everything around the city. The apartment was incredibly spacious, clean and comfortable, staff were welcoming and friendly and helped with anything we needed. Would highly recommend staying here for your Athens trip

Elite Ermou Suites is perfectly located. The instructions to the location and to check in were simple to understand.. I arrived before check-in time, but they were so gracious to allow us to come and leave our bags to venture out into Athens.  The rooms are amazing with the most beautiful views of the Acropolis. Elite Ermou Suites is close to many attractions like the Acropolis, Acropolis Museum, and tons of restaurants. The Staff was so very nice and attentive. I would absolutely stay here again, and I look forward to coming back again!

Very pleased with my stay. Gorgeous views directly from the window and location location location! Room was decently priced, not cheap but not expensive for the view & area the hotel is located in.

Free laundry on premise and elevators available to bring you to the hotel which is on the 5th floor of a multi-purpose building.

Nothing super luxury, but exactly what you want if you are looking for a clean accommodation with gorgeous views and is centrally located.

More of an apartment style hotel than a full service hotel but again, exactly what I was wanting.

One thing to note, due to the location, it can get very busy and loud at night and this carries up to the rooms.

Beautiful suites in excellent location. We could see the Acropolis so clearly and right next to the Ermou pedestrian shopping mall and Monastiraki Square. The suites are well renovated and fully equipped. However, there are street noises and people shouting in the middle of the night. Despite the double glazed windows we could hear the noise in the living room but not the separate bedroom, which was very quiet.

Absolutely wonderful hotel. Nothing I can be complaining about. Lovely personel, incredibly helpful I was worried to miss my very early flight, she helped me arrange a taxi etc.

The only thing is a had difficulty finding the entrance maybe a bit bigger sign would be helpful for people like me :)

Thank you so much for a beautiful stay in Athens.  The hotel is close to everything!  It was super easy to take the train to the hotel.  Thank you Katerina for providing very clear instructions to check in.
Our room was gorgeous and we had a clear view of the Acropolis!
The Monastiraki neighbourhood is so much fun to explore!
Very cozy apartment with a beautiful Acropolis view. The location is great, next to Monastriaki Square and the metro station. Highly recommend!

We had a great stay at Feel Athens Studio. Close to Monastiraki Square and many restaurants only a short walking distance. The amenities we great. The only thing that was missing was a toaster.
Great views of the Parthenon the Acropolis and Mount Lycabettus from our balcony. We stayed in the last week of January so it was quiet near our apartment. Very clean. Orestis and Katerina was quick to respond if there were any issues.
Better rates if you pay in cash. ;)

Aircon is terrible !
Bathroom sprays water everywhere!
Lift to get up dodgy

Very nice and cozy apartment in the historical center of Athens. Great location. Nice interior. Hospitable owner. I enjoyed the place.

A minus is that there is a little bit noisy sometimes. Near apartment vocal studio exists. And you can hear their exercises.
Magnificent view! The balconies are huge and the apartment was crystal clean! Last but not least the instructions given by the host were just on the spot for everything we needed!

place_name: Mood Athens Luxury Apartments and Suites
review_text:
Never Again !! Noisy - Open Air Club in 10m distance from balcony

We had an absolutely dreadful experience at Moods Apartment in Athens. First, I want to say that we are used to noise and are aware that Athens is not exactly a quiet city. However, sleep was out of the question, as the 6 Dogs Club right next door, with a large outdoor area, was open every day from noon until 2:30 AM and on weekends even until 6 AM (see attached picture). The apartment's balcony is directly above the club, making the noise level unbearable and impossible to get any rest. Despite closed windows, the bass penetrated through the walls, keeping us awake all night.

You will definitely not find any sleep here. Additionally, the landlord was rude and not accommodating at all. Complaints were ignored, and no support or compensation is to be expected. To the booking agency, it was claimed that they had already been generous to us by allowing us to store our luggage unsecured in the hallway for 3 hours upon departure.

It seems the apartment operators' attitude is "pay and forget." If you plan to stay here, you will not get any sleep, even with earplugs.

Never again!!

place_name: Mood Athens Luxury Apartments and Suites
review_text:
We stayed here with my boyfriend for 6 days and we were happy to live here. A lot of benefits, your own kitchen, comfortable and big bed, Wi-Fi and the best location. The manager was really fast answering the questions and providing help. In our apartment the flow of the water in the shower was really slow, so I contacted manager and she called mechanic and he fixed it next day. However, the TV was a bit broken, but it was possible to watch. The view on the acropolis is nice, BUT be prepared for the loud music outside (there is bar just under the hotel). The hotel provides earplugs for that, but it could be uncomfortable for some people.
The last question is the security. We were aware of the stolen things, so we hid everything we had. However, we had some doubts regarding the Balkon door, which is really weak and could be hacked in a few seconds. We also saw one camera, but I would recommend to secure the Balkon doors, more, especially locker. Overall, the rest is fine, service is polite and friendly, there is 2 lifts. So despite these drawbacks, I put 4 stars, because it was really good place

place_name: Mood Athens Luxury Apartments and Suites
review_text: This place was great for both business and holiday! Right next to the metro but also in a prime location for sightseeing, eating at amazing restaurants and for having a piece of luxury while on holiday. The view is gorgeous, the room is beautiful and full of everything you could want/need for a short or long-term stay!

place_name: Mood Athens Luxury Apartments and Suites
review_text: The location is very good, you can go everywhere on foot. The rooms are very clean. The person in charge is very interested thanks for everything.

place_name: Mood Athens Luxury Apartments and Suites
review_text:
We spent the christmas in Athens
The apartment made our stay much more joyful

We really had a fantastic stay at Athens Design Suites! The location is unbeatable – right in the heart of Athens and close to all the main attractions, dining spots, and shops. The property was impeccably clean and well-maintained, and my room was spotless and beautifully designed. Christina and her team were incredibly professional and helpful throughout our stay, always available to answer questions or provide assistance. They even upgraded our room at no extra cost, which was a wonderful surprise!

Additionally, the staff kindly stored our luggage after check-out, as we had a late flight, allowing us to explore the city comfortably until it was time to head to the airport. I can’t recommend this place enough – from the prime location to the warm and attentive service, it was perfect. Looking forward to staying here again on my next trip to Athens!

I stayed here in the Supreme Suite for four nights. The location is perfect, walking distance to the main attractions. My room was large, neat with a modern bathroom. All the amenities and appliances worked well. You can hear through the walls from the adjacent rooms, but that would be the case of most places in the city I think. Would definitely recommend despite that.

A special shoutout to Christina - the manager for ensuring a comfortable stay and being of quick help whenever needed!

Amazing experience. Our suite was very nice. Clean, with comfortable bed and including all amenities. The location is great. In the heart of the city. Easy check in and out. Great communication with the manager. It might be a bit noisy at night but you should know that you are staying in one of the most busiest areas of Athens so it’s normal to be a bit noisy. The only thing I would change is the elevator which is very old but that something that has to do with the building so it does not depend only on them.

Clean, quiet, great location, good price. Christina was very responsive and made travel arrangements go so smoothly. Even the 'Cozy' suite was plenty spacious for a solo traveler like myself and could easily accommodate a couple. The overall building was kind of random, staircase has cigarette butts and people smoking/loitering, but the room / floor itself was very clean and secure. Would 100% stay again if I return to Athens.

Great location. Clean and comfortable rooms. 1 minute away from metro station directly to the airport. restaurants, bars and acropolis is just a walking distance away. Only i found it a bit hard to find the entrance of the building but Christina assisted me perfectly. Thank you and your team for everything.

What a wonderful stay with an incredible staff. I can’t say enough good things about the management of the property and their bar and restaurant staff.

The hotel is located very centrally in Monastiraki and just around the corner you have access to wonderful restaurants, bars, and shopping with a good view of the acropolis. You are about 14 minutes to the acropolis and acropolis museum and 3 minutes from Hadrian’s library.

The rooms were spacious, comfortable and clean with great showers. The staff was very accommodating helping us organize shuttles to and from the airport and helping us work around having a single key. Very thankful for them and would absolutely stay in the future when I’m back in Athens!
very nice rooms, modern and clean and big! super cocktails on the rooftop bar. the manager of the hotel is very kind and the communication is super uncomplicated. will be back for sure!

Nice accomodations. VERY friendly receptive and kind. Rooms were nice but the noise and music  from rooftop bar was obnoxious kind of not sure I understand why hotel guests have to endure the noise..oh well!
Quant hotel. Old world charm. Great service from the staff. Rooftop wine was great. Unfortunately it was to hot for us those few days. Would go back to the hotel again.

What to expect from a room which doesn't have a frontdesk and instead, you need to go to the wine bar downstairs?

It was perfect. The location is just 2 minutes from Monastiraki Square, and 10-15 to Syntagma Square. Rooms are tidy and come amenities like an iron.

The general manager isn't always onsite, but responds quickly via WhatsApp and has great recommendations in what to do.

And, you get a complimentary glass of wine in the bar downstairs.

Location, location, location is all I can say! Great spot near it all, literally! Very walkable area with much going on right below the apartment. Noise was never an issue. Apartment is huge which was great before we head to our boat trip. Beautiful view of the Acropolis and Markella was easily accessible! Also has an elevator which made it great for carrying luggage. Highly recommend!

An amazing place, close to everything. The apartment is gorgeous and huge. You have a great view of the Acropolis. There are plenty of restaurants, shopping and sights around. There is a Starbucks near by. The apartment is located above a restaurant, but when you close the windows it is totally quiet. There are three restrooms and three showers, which is a huge plus. Note that all of the showers are transparent, but you can close the bedroom door for each room.

This apartment was without doubt one of the most luxurious we have ever stayed in. In a great location to immerse yourself in the hub bub of athens life, easy access to historical attractions, and the always amazing view of the acropolis.
Markella was great prior to, during and after our visit, checking everything was OK and if we needed anything.
Lots of luxurious touches. Robes, slippers, air con, tea, coffee etc provided.
Felt very sad having my last coffee on the balcony the mornig we left.
I want to tell you it's so brilliant... But equally want it to be unpopular so that no one books when we want to return!
Cannot imagine a better place to be in athens.

Breathtaking view of Acropolis and right in the middle of the best part of the historical city of Athens. Everything is walking distance with lots of great bars and happenings in the vicinity. The staff was super polite and cheerful. ❤️👏

Love this place!! One of the best in athens, everything is walking distance. Very nice environment and clean place!! Nice view of the acropolis!!
Its a must try.
Everytime i come to athens i will be there

Amazing location! Close to everything - metro, restaurants, tourist sights, shops.
A/C blows very cold thankfully! Very clean, and cozy little room. Has everything you need and check in was a very easy process. 10/10!

Good apartment, in the middle of the city, close to alot of interest points, easy check in and out, the apartment is provided with kitchen utensils and basic cooking stuff, (oil, salt pepper, coffee, tea) very affordable too :) (The apartment is in a busy area which would make you think its noisy, but once you close the balcony you hear nothing)

Very beautiful house in the center Of Athens. Everything you want to see at Athens city is at walking distance from this house. Really perfect location and very friendly hosts!! We will definitely come again.

The interior of the house is really impressive as it is in photos. The location is convenient for all kind of needs. Especially for nightlife we had so many options in foot distance and didnt have to worry about metro closing early. I would totally recommend this.

Perfect place to stay while visiting this ancient city. Central to everything including sites, shops, markets, metro. Modern, clean and well equipped inside and secure from the outside. 50m or less to laundry. Host was an absolute gem. Definitely recommend this place!

We stayed there for 3 days. Perfect location. Everything is so closed. The house is big enough and so clean and the owner is so helpful.  I highly recommend it.
PS  Children loved the playstation.
A wonderful place in the most central part of Athens.amazing view of Acropolis,hundreds of bars and clubs next to it and friendly hosts!definetely suggested!we will come back!

place_name: Αmazing apartments @ Monastiraki subway stasion - Paragon
review_text: Unbeatable location, right in the heart of the city with easy access to all major attractions and vibrant local markets. Modern, clean and comfortable appartment. The host was incredibly welcoming.

Our stay at the 360 Degrees Pop Art Hotel was wonderful! From the service we received after booking all the way through our stay was top notch! They were able to arrange a taxi for us from the port upon arrival and to the airport on departure day. The hotel staff left a bottle of wine in our room for my birthday, which was a nice personal touch. The front desk team member, Liliana (sorry if I misspelled your name), was so pleasant and gave us the best restaurant recommendation for our last night. She called and made a reservation and made sure they saved us a piece of Pastichio! The location was fantastic, and everything was walkable from the hotel. The breakfast at their sister hotel each morning was very good, and the view of the Acropolis while eating breakfast from two floors below the rooftop was out of this world. We did go to the rooftop of the sister hotel for drinks a few nights, and the view of the Acropolis at night all lit up with unreal! The rooms were a fine size for two people, and had all the necessary amenities. The shower was great. The hotel does have an elevator. I highly recommend staying at 360 Degrees Pop Art Hotel if you find yourself in Athens!

We had a great stay at the 360 degrees pop-art hotel! We had mistakenly booked for a different date and on arrival the staff were very helpful and provided us with an alternative room.  A big thank you to Stephan and team! The room was very clean and a good size. The breakfast area is just across the road and has a great view of the Acropolis. There were a variety of breakfast foods which we loved. We will be back.

On balance, I would definitely recommend this hotel.

The rooms are actually a great size for the price and location. I did not expect the amount of space. Everything in the room is well maintained and works. The "mood" light above the desk is an interesting choice. But it is overall very comfortable.

There are three caveats.

First, the included breakfast is just OK. It's also a bit difficult to get to before having your first cup of coffee even with instruction: go to the main hotel in Monastiraki square, take the elevator to the top, go out, turn right and walk all the way to the end past the bar, turn right again and go down two flights of stairs, then enter the room. It is offered 7-10:30am which gives more flexibility than most places. The view is also excellent despite not being on the roof.

Second, the WiFi on floor 5 was spotty at best. If you don't have data on your phone or need to get serious work done, plan accordingly.

Third, full disclosure, there is apparently a club nearby, and you can hear the bass at night. It was fine for me, but my wife is a light sleeper. Earplugs would be well-advised if you are also. Obviously there's not much the hotel could do about this within reason and the laws of physics.

The best location in Athens if you want to experience the local culture and nightlife. The main metro line, M3 is just a few steps away. The staff is topnotch, shout out to George who provided excellent service. The restaurant at the 4th floor of the main Hotel has the best view of the Acropolis, especially at sunset. The breakfast is just fantastic. Perfect hotel to end our vacation.

Location was perfect for walking to the best sites in Athens, staff were all super friendy and helpful, room was great and the continental breakfast was truly amazing. You can't go wrong with this hotel!

My teenage daughter and I stayed here.  The room was clean, recently remodeled and the bathroom was amazing.  Very clean and quiet hotel. The staff were so helpful and friendly - they called taxis for us, gave restaurant recommendations and gave excellent directions for all nearby attractions.  The mini fridge was pre-stocked with cool great tasting water and local juice drinks - which I wouldn't have tried had i not tried it there.  This hotel does not have room service - but who wants to stay in when coffee shop is across the street and numerous restaurants are a block away???  Would definitely recommend to others and stay there again.

Stayed for 2 nights, had a great experience. Wonderful staff, good English speakers and great at communicating. Hotel appeared to be new, was very clean, rooms were big and bathroom was wonderful. Location is very good, 15min walk to the Parthenon gates. Lots of restaurants around, shops, everything you need. Travelled with my 8 year old son, he loved the room as well, beds were nice and comfy. Would definitely visit again.

Hotel staff was extremely helpful and welcoming. Location is perfect for seeing attractions in Athens, but we aware that it is in the city center and you will have sounds from surrounding businesses. Room was clean and facilities were updated. Would recommend to anyone trying to walk to the acropolis and surrounding attractions.

The hotel was clean and the staff were wonderful. Very informative staff and accommodating. You could see the acropolis from the balcony. The hotel is around everything within walking distance. I would stay there again if I’m in Athens.

Super friendly and helpful staff right in the heart of Athens. Location is extremely convenient (less than a five minute walk to the blue line) to discover all Athens offers on foot. Clean, quiet and comfortable. My new go-to hotel when I’m in Athens!

360 Degrees Hotel in Athens is a perfect place to stay for every reason you could think of! It’s in the Monostiraki neighborhood which is the center of all the neighborhoods. It is very close to shopping, good restaurants and all the attractions; Agora, Hadrian’s Library, Parthenon etc…

We checked in easily (online) and our room was directly viewing the bustling square. It was clean and had all the amenities we needed. Breakfast was 10+! Everything was neatly put out, fresh with a huge variety of items! The coffee was fresh brewed every morning and they continually put out hot steamed cream.
Surprisingly, the restaurant for dinner wasn’t that good and I’d have to rate that a 2. All food items were sub par and many people around us complained to the manager or left the food. The view of the Parthenon is amazing from this rooftop so I would highly recommend just going there for a drink and elsewhere for a nice dinner.
I would definitely stay here again as it’s the perfect location for everything you want to do in Athens! Hotel front desk staff were all very nice and super helpful!

Went to the rooftop. Terrible service. The manager, his second (bouncer?), and the waiter all had a terrible attitude. Very arrogant. The rooftop has a great view of the Acropolis if you get seated by that end. So they believe service is not important. Yet I saw at least four more rooftops around that have the same view. The tables not on the side of the Acropolis could have a great view as well, but they put some trees that block it. Definitely need a new decoration person.
They had a very poor food offering, only 5 different dishes.
I recommend going to one of the other nice rooftops around.

I highly recommend this hotel for a variety of reasons.
1. The location is amazing. You are a short walking distance from all the major sites in downtown Athens.
2. The breakfast is extremely fresh and delicious.
3. The view from the breakfast area is breathtaking.
4.The Room is spacious and clean.
5. The staff is very friendly and helpful.

However, if you are a light sleeper, you might need ear plugs as the rooftop bar does not turn down the music until 2-3 AM.
Located near the main attractions that Athens has to offer including historical sites, shopping, food, and drinks.  Enjoyed our stay, would recommend!
Very clean with good staff. But no fire safety or exits. No power in my room. Nowhere to hang towels in the bathroom. No outdoor space. Low flow shower. No coffee in the room. No bin .

I recently stayed at Nur Outline in Monastiraki, and it was fantastic! The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful, ensuring a comfortable stay.

The room was spacious, clean and equipped with a coffee maker, making mornings even more enjoyable. The bed was exceptionally comfortable, making for a great night's sleep after exploring the city.

The location is unbeatable, with the Monastiraki Flea Market, charming cafes, and historic sites just steps away. The nearby metro station made it easy to explore other parts of Athens.

Just an amazing experience in Athens!

I had the pleasure of staying at Nur Outline, a fantastic new hotel in Monastiraki, Athens. The experience was nothing short of amazing! The staff were incredibly friendly and attentive, making me feel right at home.

The room was beautifully designed, and came with a convenient coffee maker and also a very nice surprise of a bottle of wine. The bed was wonderfully comfortable, ensuring I was well-rested for my adventures around the city.

Situated in the heart of Monastiraki, the hotel is perfectly located near the bustling flea market, delightful cafes, and significant historic landmarks. The metro station which is just in front of the hotel made exploring Athens effortless.

This hotel is superb choice for anyone looking to stay in Athens.

Location and convenience! Just steps away from metro station, many convenience shops and restaurants.  Tucked away on a small street so it is quiet.  Service is great, along with housekeeping and excellent wifi and AC.  I appreciated the keyless entry; never having to worry about a key. Will definitely stay here again!

Nur Outline in Athens, located next to Monastiraki Square, offers a perfect stay. The hotel's prime location makes exploring the city’s historic sites a breeze. The rooms are beautifully designed, spacious, and incredibly comfortable. The staff is exceptionally friendly and attentive, providing top-notch service. Amenities such as a modern fitness center, add to the experience. Overall, Nur Outline combines luxury, convenience, and impeccable service, making it a top choice for any traveler. Highly recommended!

I’d give 10 stars if it would be possible. Excellent location, cozy room with double windows(!) for soundproofing, stylish interior. The staff is extra friendly and helpful, double-checking everything to make sure we’re okay. Highly recommended!

Great apartment in central location!
In addition to the main streets around, there is Starbucks down and rooftop restaurant upstairs.

Great view to the acropolis.

We stayed two couples with babies- we had concerns cause there is only one bathroom in-suite, however that wasn’t an issue - also the second one is close to the second bedroom, with the required privacy.

Best stay, recommend for couples and families!

We stayed here on our recent visit to Athens. Communication was quick and clear. They were flexible with our arrival and the place was well beyond our expectation. The location is unbeatable (10 mins to Acropolis). I would highly recommend staying here to family and friends.

Perfect location in the heart Athens. Directly above Starbucks with several restaurants and shops steps outside of the door. Pharmacy across the street. 10 minute walk to Mars Hill and Acropolis. Very clean and comfortable with a quaint porch overlooking the restaurant path below. We had everything we needed for two nights there and will definitely come back!

The location doesn’t get much better if you are in Athens as a tourist.  The apartments have plenty of space.  I recommend paying a little extra to not stay on the side with street traffic as the motorcycles can be quite noisy late into the night.  That was our only minor complaint.  Nice to have a small gym.

A gem of an apartment in the heart of Athens. The rooms are well done, with thoughtful touches such as a full blackout curtain and excellent bathrooms. The service is very friendly and the people I met were very helpful and kind. An excellent place to stay at a good price.
The one drawback here is that there is no reception which makes the check-in process cumbersome.

What a wonderful experience. Our first time to Athens. Let’s start with service. The staff of the hotel were very professional and accommodating. They were responsive, kind, and extremely helpful. We had an early flight out and they were able to arrange a car to the airport, and without us asking provided a wonderful packaged breakfast that was a delight. It was waiting for us when we departed at 5 am. Which then brings me to the food. Breakfast is offered in the stunning, modern, breakfast room or delivered to your room if you order in advance ( which we did). The breakfast was fresh, beautiful, vibrant, and delicious. On to the facility. The space is comfortable, and sexy with a chic artsy vibe. Exposed concrete structure with tasteful vintage accents that didn’t compromise functionality. The industrial touches of conduit and copper fixtures was really nice. The rooftop honor bar is a gem with a perfect view of the acropolis. We had a great time up there sharing a bottle of wine. I can’t recommend strongly enough.

Hands down one of the best hotels I’ve ever stayed at. Very hip, swanky rooms with a stunning rooftop. Our room was beautiful and had a private bathtub which was really wonderful. The included breakfast was the best breakfast I’ve ever had at a hotel. Honestly felt like you were in an upscale brunch spot. Walking distance to pretty much anything you need in Athens. The staff were extremely nice. Would 100% stay here again and recommend anyone else to do the same.

Gorgeous little boutique hotel in a perfect location to explore Athens by foot. Area around the hotel was quiet and peaceful at night.

The rooms are modern with industrial finishes. Very sexy and cool.

Breakfast is absolutely perfect and delicious.
The staff throughout are incredibly helpful and kind.

We had taxi transfers arranged for pick up from the airport and to our next hotel down the Athens Riviera (55eu from the airport).

Be sure to make use of the gorgeous rooftop. Even if it’s just for your morning coffee or a late night drink.

We had the intimate room which was fine. But it being on the first floor next to the stairs and elevator  we had constantly sounds from outside the room. It also didn’t help that we had a window to the hall which is camouflaged as a mirror but let’s so much sound through. (also no do toilet door). Rooftop was 10/10 and breakfast also fine

I wish I can give 10 stars.

This hotel is by far the best hotel I ever stayed in , the rooms are lovely to stay in, some what haunting you don’t want to leave.I recommend this hotel to every one that seeks a special experience and vibe, the peace and joy you will feel is beyond your dreams .
I’m usually not the type of reviewer but this hotel made me because of how beautiful and sereine it is .
The staff and management are incredible they know their customers too well the appreciation and treatment is 1000 stars . Thank  you for the best experience and hospitality , I certainly would come here again and again and again.
If you wanna fall in love with Athens this hotel is the place to be .
Thank you Mona !

I’m not sure what’s happening with these other reviews or if we got the one horrible room in this hotel, but there is nothing about the photos that resembles the current state of our room or stairwell. We thought we were paying a premium for staying at a charming boutique hotel, but we were sorely disappointed. The room furnishings and bathroom were in horrible condition. Everything seemed old and dirty, and there was mold in the bathroom, which was poorly designed and outdated. The shower is in the same space as the toilet (you have to open the shower door to use the toilet) and the shower walls were crumbling, leading to a large black moldy area. There was a random trundle bed taking up half the room, which we were told was put there because people frequently sleep more than two in the room. We did not request it and it only further ruined the atmosphere. There were only two very flat deflated pillows on the bed. The curtains were again old and we were wary of having to touch them. The white couch pictured in the photos was also in bad shape and stained. There was a random janky TV hanging an inch over the couch and the remote was broken. we really don’t understand how these people get away with charging close to $300 per night. There are so many nicer places to stay for less in Athens.

2 stars for: There was a small balcony with a distant view of the acropolis, which was nice, but there are no chairs on the balcony to enjoy the view. The hotel is located on an incredibly busy street with countless restaurants, but despite being light sleepers, this was not a problem. It seemed that there was decent soundproofing. The staff was also very pleasant.

To be fair I am not sure how to rate this place. It’s located in a very lively street but you will need to drag your bags for like 50m and carry them up a flight of stairs before you can access the elevator. Once you get to the rooms you will be pleasantly surprised. Clean, spacious and modern. The staff is very helpful

A small hotel which we used for our first and last night of our 10 days vacation on Naxos and Paros Islands.. It is absolutely centrally located in the old town of Athens. Both areas, Plaka and Monastraki, are reachable by foot.   It is a very lively area with a lot of restaurants, bars and hotels below the Akropolis and it's highly recommended to visit. The staff was very kind and supported us with Taxi booking and recommendations of restaurants. From Emporikon Hotel to the ferry harbour is about 25 minutes by taxi, to the airport round about 45 minutes, depending on the traffic situation. First night we got a very small standard room which was not what we would need for a longer stay, but for one short night it was okay. On the second night we got an upgrade for a wonderful suite on the third floor, which definitely can be used for longer stays in Athens. Drinks like water, wine, soft drinks, fruits and nuts are always refilled and for free. No breakfast, lunch or dinner available at the hotel.

Wonderful location in the city center, in the popular and happening Agia Irene square. 5 minute walk from Monastikari square/ metro station. gorgeous old property, well repurposed. Very comfortable and spacious rooms. Had balcony with Acropolis view. Bed comfortable and nice. Staff very helpful and kind.

The hotel is In a very central and popular area. During weekends bars and restaurants ok the area go till 1am, so it can get noisy. You should know that before booking.

Loved my stay, will return.

The staff was sooo lovely but a few things went wrong enough that i had to share a review; our suite door wouldn't lock, even after the staff attempted; so the entire stay we had to take our vauables with us and risk the rest of our stuff getting stolen behind an unlocked main door (front door stays unlocked until midnight with minimal security) and an unlocked suite door. Not ideal. Finally, we found bedbugs in the main bed. My husband was riddled with bites. Upon attempting to get a refund from the hotel based on the safety and health issues we encountered, we were denied. Sad. Not worth the $330/night we paid that is for sure.

Very nice place to stay and visit Athens. The host is very friendly and helpful. Evrything you need and more is there in this place.
Even luggage storage is possible on the day of departure.

Perfect place!!
Perfect location!!
The owner Jhon was amazing, he was waiting for us when we arrived at the apartment, explained about the area, recommended good tavernas and made sure to ask if we were missing anything during our stay.
The apartment is equipped with everything you need. There is air conditioning, hot water all the time, television, coffee machine, and everything you need to cook by yourself for those who want.
The apartment is located in an excellent place near Montraski square in a quiet and side street. We were there during the holiday and even when there were a lot of people outside and full of noise, you don't hear anything inside the apartment.
In addition to all this, the price was also accordingly.
We will definitely come back here again the next time we are in Athens.
Is near to every archeology and bar for happy night and 10 metro of metro monastery thanks for everything
Very nice room! And very close to the metro!
Excellent!

It was the worst hotel experience for us. We have traveled to many countries and stayed in many hotels, but this was the worst.In the photos there is a very beautiful view of the dirt terrace, but this is something like a small room. So it is completely photo misleading. The air conditioning is never adjusted, either it cools too much or not. We made a mistake because of the location and booking score (9.1). Yes the location is good but there are many more hotels in the same location. We stayed 4 nights and our sheets were not changed even once, the room was not wiped, the bathroom was dirty and our towels were renewed daily, so there were always missing towels. There is supposedly breakfast but it is very bad. You can get one of the 3 menus you choose in bistro 21 right next to the hotel and the menu is almost empty. You can wait half an hour until you order. Disinterested employees take and bring orders as if they are serving you by force. In short, stay away from this hotel and never trust the booking score.

We just finished an excellent 3 night stay at Athen21. It is a small boutique hotel in the heart of it all. We had the junior suite and it was great. Very spacious. Cleaned daily if you wished. Coffee in your room.The 6 th floor terrace has a great view, we went up every evening. Breakfast at cafe 21 was very adequate, not always quick but very good portions. Location, second to none. Could walk everywhere and take metro to and from the airport. The best part was the staff, they are so friendly and helpful we couldn't ask for better. Thank you for making our stay in Athens excellent. Highly recommend.

One of the best parts in our trip to Greece. The staff are super friendly and helpful. We stayed in a spacious room which is nice and comfortable. The view of the Parthenon on the rooftop is very beautiful!

Went for breakfast at the hotel restaurant. Amazing staff, very friendly and good English speakers. Excellent food, top quality,  tasty abd fast service with a smile, can't ask for more! Highly recommend!

We had a fantastic experience here. Ms. Georgia at Frontdesk made our stay a fantastic one, the Breakfast was great, and the cleaning team did a wonderful
job. Highly recommend this place when travelling to Athens. Thank you so much Team Athens21.

Great location walkable everywhere. It also half a block away from all the shops and restaurants without being on the busy street. Staff are super nice and helpful. The room is clean and modern. The breakfast is decent and adequate. I recommend it.
Great staff,very helpful. Bit noisy at night, but nothing a pair of ear plugs wouldn't cure. Perfect location and very clean. Super shower

The building was a press company, and that should tell you enough. Images of the rooms and facilities do not match reality in booking.com. When I arrived, I canceled it on the spot without checking in and lost almost 800 Euro gladly as their policy forfeits all amounts, which is another bad sign I should have caught earlier.

Rating on booking.com is misleading and deceiving. How can it be 4 stars is beyond my imagination. It should be no more than 3 stars and does not worth the money they charge customers for.

Add an extra 50 Euro and stay at Grand Hyatt 5 Star with club access and double the size of the room and much much much prestigious in everthing.

The location is excellent, unfortunately I can’t say the same about the hospitality and the room. The bed and pillow was extremely terrible we literally wake up every hour with back and neck pain. Plus we had a junior suite which was too tiny , the robes are teared up as well. Will never recommend the place.
Fantastic location, beautiful rooms. Staff and service second to none. Highly recommend

The location is perfect - it is in a very lively area, and close walking distance to everything. Despite being in a busy area, noise is not an issue. It is close to lots of shops and excellent food/dining options. The place also feels secure/safe given the 24 hour reception. We were warmly greeted each time we came and went from the accommodation. We were lucky to have a balcony and view of the acropolis from our room on the 4th floor! Other pros of this accommodation is it has a comparatively earlier check-in time. Shared bathroom is not too bad surprisingly, good water pressure and it is all kept clean.

Main con: Wifi did not work well in the room at all. There are lots of stairs to climb if you’re on the 4th floor, but this is not uncommon in Europe! They have a lift available for luggage only.

I enjoyed my stay here :) I had my own room with its own bathroom, which altogether was clean and great, the location was good and the staff were friendly. I remember breaking something in the bathroom by accident, but the staff were kind about it.

Superb location! Very good rooms considering the cost, they were clean and bigger than other cheap accommodation options i’ve stayed in. I also stayed on the 4th floor and there was no street noise at all, but I couldn’t say for lower levels. Finally, the staff were incredibly friendly, and “available 24/7 for your service.” Excellent experience and I would stay again. Highly recommend for young travellers on a budget!

Avoid!!!  AVOID 🆘🆘🆘 Wasn’t clean.  I was  abused by the male boss. don’t be fool by its good location, you need feel safe, have a clean room for a  good night sleep after a day out and back to your room with  welcoming  staffs, not missable faces  and tiny area

My room was simple (might not be to everyone's liking, but suited me fine.) Clean and the staff very helpful. The view from the 5th floor terrace was worth the hike up the stairs and to enjoy take out dinner! Well located not for from Metro station,  restaurants and the sights.

We got to the hotel in the late afternoon hours.
We got a twin bed room that wasn't Mach with our reservation in booking.com for a double room with 1 full bed.
They didn't change the room. They didn't care for customer service!
The room was dirty from dust and mold in the shower. The towels were old! Like they are from the last time I visited with my brother before 4 years.
I remember the place as good but maybe because it was new back then.
A lot of people talk about the noise from the tavern and the street downstairs, so if you get room 102, you will be in a big problem, party and music until 3 am and noise from early morning!
Luckily, we had only 1 night. If we had more, we would just take our things and move for another place!
Be careful!

Extremely dark in the room because several lights weren't working. Complained to the front desk about it. Took over 24
hours and they only managed to get 1 additional light working. Still really dark but that 1 extra light helped.

Great location and walking distance to many locations and restaurants. But it's in a busy area and it's really noisy outside.

The location is excellent. The room had a lot of noise from the bars downstairs.  If you want peace and quiet, it may cause you trouble. The shower was not working properly. Also, the room needs repairs. The breafast is served in a taverna next door, not in the property.

Located in a super busy area with amazing nightlife. It does get super loud until 4-5am though so if you are not a partier or light sleeper bring ear plugs. We loved the location however customer service was terrible. Our room had 5 lights broken, 1 in living room and 4 in the washroom. The only light working in the washroom was the mirror light so we were fumbling around in the dark the whole time and I couldn’t get ready and put any makeup on because it was pretty much dark in there, also difficult showering in the dark. We kept asking the front desk for help and The front desk staff would assure us each morning the light would would be fixed when we get back but every time it wasn’t, after a couple days we stopped asking because their promises are pointless. Would have preferred they just be honest if fixing the lights was not going to happen rather than lie to us everyday and giving a guarantee to something that never happened.

Decent hotel in which we had two rooms for a couple of days as a family stay; we were upgraded for some reason which was great; rooms clean and sufficiently equipped on suite shower, minibar fridge, iron, hairdryer, etc. Minor gripes would be that the rooms are dark and street noise doesn’t die down to around midnight.

Perfect location, short walk from anything in the city center. Very nice, comfortable rooms with great views to the Acropolis, churches, etc. The hotel restaurant is very good, we were happy to eat there for multiple meals during our stay. Breakfast on our patio was a highlight and a perfect way to start the day.
With all that said, the best part of Athens City View is the service. From the moment we arrived we were made to feel at home and at no point were we discouraged from asking a question about where to visit in the city or making a request of the kind folks operating the hotel.

It’s a fantastic location, conveniently within walking distance to everywhere.

One thing that concerned me was that when flushing the toilet, the water pressure was too strong, causing water to splash out. Additionally, sounds from the hallway are very audible inside the room.

The hotel was very well situated at a walking distance from the Acropolis and good restaurants. We were welcomed almost as family from the moment we set foot in the hotel and enjoyed our spacious room and bathroom. The restaurant downstairs offers high quality meals for a good price and the service staff is A1. I would stay at this hotel again without hesitation.

Our stay exceeded our expectations, the staff were very welcoming, helpful and friendly! The location of this hotel is also very good. The rooms are normal sized for a small city hotel but for us that's more than enough, and they were also very clean. Be aware that due to the building's design, to get to the first floor there is only stairs (with a stair lift for those who need it), after that there is a normal elevator to the other floors. There's also a nice roof garden/terrace. We will definitely stay here again when we come back to Athens!

There were some drawbacks, but they wouldn't deter me from recommending this hotel. The location was superb for sightseeing, with everything within a few minutes' walk and right in the city center. However, this also meant very noisy streets, especially on weekends and holidays (until 4am-5am). While the hotel offers earplugs, they weren't sufficient for me, so I brought my own silicone earplugs, which helped ensure a good night's sleep. Again, this is not their fault and out of their control - it’s what you get for the excellent location. Extremely convenient to travel to and from the airport too (using the metro).

Regarding the room, the decor was pleasing, but the bathroom could use improvement. The lack of a door in the shower resulted in water splashing throughout the bathroom, which could easily be resolved with a glass door. Additionally, as others have noted, the toilet had a tendency to splash water when flushed, which was less than hygienic. We learned to step away before flushing to avoid this issue.

I was a bit scared from some of the reviews I saw here but let me tell you: my experience was great. Staff at the reception was really kind, location is fantastic, and the room did what it needed to do. It was clean, quite small, and the bed and pillows were pretty comfortable.

Athens is pretty hectic at night so I was expecting noise. My room had a street view but I feel the windows did a good job at blocking the sound. I did use the ear plugs they give you, but I always sleep with them, regardless. AC worked perfectly which I appreciated a lot.

While exploring Monasteraki, I couldn’t find many bathroom options so I kept coming back to my room to leave some of my shopping and to use the restroom, pretty convenient I’d say. From Monasteraki square everything relevant is on a walking distance of maybe 20 minutes tops

In front of this hotel is a Hostel with GREAT coffee, 100% recommend to enjoy there a Freddo espresso.

On my last day, I was able to leave my luggage there with no problems at all. I did put locks on my luggage (I always do tho) but it seemed safe although they just keep them downstairs.

This is not a fancy option but I believe it delivers adequately and I would 100% repeat if I came back.

Decent hotel and the room was okay. Be mindful that the hotel is more like a bed and breakfast type thing, with shared kitchen facilities.

Gym was average but got the job done. I was a bit disappointed to note that the sauna and jacuzzi tub (key selling points for me) cost 15 euros to use.

Staff were friendly and very helpful. Location is excellent.

The location is great because it is right next to the metro that goes directly to the airport. I was only staying one night in between flights and that is why I picked the hotel. I am not new to Athens, I have stayed many other places before. I also booked this because my usual go-to were booked. I don't believe the ranking is correct. I would give it a 3 stars, top. There is no breakfast, no 24 hour front desk (after midnight you have a key to enter the building). The area is nice, but the hotel itself is tucked in a closed alley so... safety isn't the best. it is right infront of a bar: very loud too. I don't know what is the deal with the "pool" (which is an average size jacuzzi, on the rooftop). When I went up it was closed. Maybe you have to ask ahead of time? My room was on the small side, which is fine, and clean. Bathroom super small and had a window in the shower. Certainly a "no frills" room: there was barely soap. Now, if it were a 3 star, with a 3 star price, I wouldn't be saying anything. But for the price I paid I was expecting a little better. Three stars in the area or in Omonia provide very similar accommodation and they provide breakfast for half of what I paid.

Good location! Just a 3 min walk to the Monstraki metro. Rooms have everything you need and get the job done if you’re not staying in Athens for a long time. Staff let us keep our bags after checkout. Worth it if you’re in Athens and want a no frills spot!
Absolutely great location in the heart of Athens. The rooms are average and I think the cost is quite normal, especially during the summer period.

Located in the heart of Athens with a beautiful decoration where everything works and the staff are super friendly and accomodating.

To us location was the most important thing when we looked for place to stay. Mariet was perfect for this. Restaurants and metro are very close. Walk just a minute or two and you will see the Acropolis. We got nothing but good things to say about Mariet ☺️

Highly Recommend! Very clean and comfortable, excellent customer service and about a 3 minute walk to Monastiraki Square and walkable (less than  15-20 minutes) to the Acropolis, Acropolis Museum, Syntagma Square and other areas of interest. Tonia was very responsive via email prior to our arrival (I had a lot of questions) and arranged a taxi to pick us up at the airport and she was very friendly, welcoming and helpful while we were guests at the hotel with directions, restaurant recommendations, arranging a taxi to bring us to the cruise port, storing our luggage for a few hours on the day we checked out, etc. Quiet area at night – with people walking by - one night we arrived back at hotel at 10:30pm and still felt safe walking in the area and entering the building.  Free Wifi, bottles of water and a bottle of sparkling wine. Had refrigerator, microwave, table and efficient A/C.  Subway/metro stop was a few minute walk away, but did not need to use the subway system. Stayed two nights. Would definitely stay again at Mariet Athens Boutique Spa!

This place is a must to stay at. Not only does it rate due to its close proximity to Monastiraki Square & the Metro station for ease of accessibility to &  from the airport, but mostly because Tonia is the hostess with the "mostess".  Apart from her effervescent smile & personality, she made us feel very welcome & was helpful in every way . Thank you Tonia you are a credit to Mariet Hotel de Spa.  The rooms are fabulous & we felt great value for  money. Paul & Brenda🤗

Room is very clean
The host is also very nice
Very good location at monastiraki
There’s room service everyday and the cleaner is very nice too!
There’s coffee and tea even plates and cutleries it’s nice to stay in and have takeouts sometimes
Overall very good experience
Great location, extremely accommodating. If I return to Athens would stay here again

Absolutely perfect! The room was lovely, the location amazing and the staff are incredibly warm and accommodating. They helped me by storing my luggage twice and the communication was excellent. They gave great recommendations and were just all around awesome!  Truly a wonderful stay!

Like so many non hotel apartments in Athens they occupy a couple rooms in a nondescript building on a side street. Athenian Sensations is one of these. The apartment was in excellent condition just lacking a couple of items like a tea towel and dishwasher tablets.The TV unfortunately did not have one English speaking station. Other than that a nice apartment big in size with just one huge drawback - it's surrounded by roof top bars that run till 4 am in the morning so you will need your ear plugs if you want to get some sleep.

We had an amazing experience.
We were 2 adults and 7 months baby.
The pictures are accurate.
Thank you for your hospitality. We will be back ❤️
Perfect location . Quiet at night

We stayed 3 days in Athens and enjoyed it! Perfect place downtown!! Clean comfortable with its balcony!! The guys are perfect in the service!! If it had 1000 stars I would put it! Thank you very much ❤️ we will come again!
cosy central place, close to main attractions and metro, but a bit unclean and not the most comfortable bed

A lot of mess…
The towels are dirty, the room smells bad. Not worth the money.
Great location. Clean place. Same a 3* accomodation.
Good location good place

The object itself is not bad
But NO HOT WATER! But the boiler is turned off
WIFI works poorly, you could even say it doesn't exist
Room no. 5
If it weren't for that, it wouldn't be bad
A room for 3 people for PLN 120 per day in this location is a good price in my opinion
Well, it would be nice to have hot water in the 21st century...

* the lack of possibility of contact via booking also, as you can see, proves something!
** message from the owner after a few hours:
Dear Sir or madam, we found your review disrespectful and unrealistc. So our legal team just started procedures to charge you with slanderous defamation. Τhere is hot water 24hours and of course internet. As you can see no-one has ever mentioned anything about hot water in any comment.

Instead of sprinkling ashes on my head, she will tell me that there was warm water when there was none!
And WiFi that has one line or disconnects from the network every now and then is not the Internet!

Due to this attitude, I strongly advise against this accommodation! It's better to pay 10 euros extra and have peace of mind with 3 to 1*!

Boutique hotel located above a restaurant.  Unique room designs.  Wonderful staff.  You have to enter the hotel through the restaurant which is the only confusing thing about the entry.  Not all rooms are "senior" friendly with steps leading to the bathroom; safe is low to the ground.  Overall, very enjoyable stay, just let them know you are a senior.  Great location - minutes walk to all the sites.

We are a married couple 37 and 38, travelling with no kids. We are non smokers and non drinkers. It is our 10th anniversary travel trip.

Lovely location nearby to public transport, as well as being nearby walking distance to pretty much most of Athens historical sites.

We stayed in the loft room which was very lovely. Only complaints would be the fridge is not cold enough, the shower seems to wet the whole toilet area no matter what you do.

Has an outdoor jacuzzi that is nice to chill in, however there is no 'view' off the balcony aside from the other top floor room guests balcony and an alleyway.

The service was lovely for the most part, however we took a towel to the islands one day, and upon our return in the evening we were questioned by the reception staff asking why we took a towel. We explained we did not know we could not borrow a towel for the beach... we were then punished by not replacing our towels while we were away. I returned the two towels we took to the beach and requested a replacement, to which they replaced later in the evening.

The breakfast was included in our package, and when we went out too early to be able to attend the (8 or 830am) start downstairs they would provide a breakfast package the night before consisting of breads and jams and yoghurt which was nice.

We had dinner at the restaurant and found the food to be amazing, much better than the breakfasts included.

Nice place overall, great location, mostly nice staff, Jacuzzi nice but too much chlorine burns your eyes.
The 1890 was the perfect stay for our honeymoon in Athens. Attentive staff, central location and the private spa was an excellent way to unwind after a busy day's exploring. Would happily stay again.

Unfortunately we were unable to keep our booking due to a huge delay of our ferry. But although the staff was very friendly and helpful. So we are going to book again for next year and we are looking foreward to our next stay at Athens 1890 Hotel in 2025.

I will write another rezension after our stay ;)

The hotel is situated in a really good location. However you can tell this by the price I’m not sure if for what you get it’s really worth the money I don’t think I’d say Karen in the same room that I was in last time.

The breakfast here was not great. There wasn’t much of a selection and what there was was fairly plain. The coffee at breakfast was pretty awful as was the orange juice.

The room that we selected was meant to be a slightly bigger room, however, what was actually bigger in the room was the corridor, so it wasn’t worth spending more for the bigger room, we weren’t told online that when we were going to get a bigger room that all that would be bigger was corridor, so this was quite disappointing. The shower and toilet is crammed into the corner of the room, in it’s own separate room. The balcony was overlooking a pretty horrendous view at the back of the building, the balcony didn’t have any chairs. There was only one chair within the room itself.

The spa was really nice. The staff are really friendly down there. I didn’t like that we were staying for five days and only got half an hour free. It would be better off to book it individually per day so you’ve got several lots of half an hour sessions, although it was worth paying a little bit extra for another half an hour on top this is because they had lots of nice facilities such as a big pool, sauna, steam room and a nice shower.

I would not stay here again. I think I would look somewhere else to stay in the same area as the area is nice just I think the hotel could be improved on.

The host was very sweet and nice! We stayed there for one night and we asked if we could check out a bit later because our flight was later in the afternoon. The host said yes without any hesitation and was very kind throughout our stay. The room itself was very modern and tidy but also spacious with a nice balcony, air conditioning and an inside the building small yard! We loved the decoration and the small details.

The room and the host were both amazing. The host was kind and made sure we were satisfied with our stay, even though we only spent one night there. The modern decoration of the house was beautiful and clean, and the location of the building was very convenient as it was near Ermou Street, making it easy for us to go shopping.

Amazing location! The house is so comfy , clean and spacious. The host is so nice, responsible and helpful.We asked if we can stay a little bit longer because our flight was late at night and she said yes without any hesitation. I definitely recommend it!!
excellent location close to acropolis. the apartment itself was specious,comfortable and clean!!the host was very polite with us and helpful.definitely recommend it!!

We stayed 6 nights. In Room 3, the biggest one.
Location is in the centre of old Athens, close to tourist attractions. Responsive host (Debi).
There are 5 rooms. 1-3 have street facing windows and balconies. 4-5 are at rear and face a small internal courtyard.
The room was clean, spacious and comfortable.
Noise from street (can hear people talking loudly at night) and music from downstairs (only occurred one night, but lasted until 3am) may be a problem.

place_name: A Gift in the Heart of Monastiraki A
review_text: We stayed in unit A for couple of days. Lovely apartment-spacious and clean, good A/C & WiFi, fully equipped kitchen. Good and fast communication with owner. Best location possible - close to everything and nice supermarket on corner of street.

place_name: A Gift in the Heart of Monastiraki A
review_text:
My genuine advice would be to avoid this place.

Yes, the location is good. And to some extent we could have overlooked the ant infestation, the total lack of natural light, the shower that is totally unsuitable for a family of five due to the length of time the water takes to heat, and the angry neighbour who shouted and cried at us when we tried to use the advertised terrace. She told us that the owner had no right to be advertising this. We have no idea if this is right or wrong, but who wants a big argument on holiday!?

But the worst thing was the owner's reaction when we told her about all this on departure and suggested a small refund (there was no other suitable accommodation nearby to move to).You would have thought we had kicked a puppy!  No empathy, no professionalism and an attitude as if we had made it up!

Avoid.

place_name: A Gift in the Heart of Monastiraki A
review_text: Clean, well-kept, convenient (in the heart of Athens,) but without a window and with a guy in the adjacent gallery shouting and cursing every morning

place_name: A Gift in the Heart of Monastiraki A
review_text: Doll what an apartment
In the heart of Athens, nice apartment, spotlessly clean!
Great apartment in a great location

Lovely apartment with extras we didnt expect. Tea and coffee and a few condiments were supplied . Close to cafes ,transport bars and shopping. It is on a busy street and  if you not used to Athens graffitti can be confronting.  Noise wasnt an real issue .. what do you expect in a entertainment area.  Booking and communication was a breeze.

Very spacious, well equipped, comfortable place. Amazing location for exploring Athens. Having breakfast in the restaurant downstairs was an absolute treat. Staff is very responsive and accommodating.
Great stay for its price. Washroom is clean. AC worked. Bed was comfy. Room was clean!
How does anyone get ahold of their front desk?! I have been emailing, calling, even trying Whatsapp to speak with a rep & had no luck since November! Please respond back with an email that works!!

Small and cozy place to stay. Excellent location within walking distance from the train station and a popular square. If you are staying in Athens, this is the area to be. There's a cafe downstairs and they serve breakfast and lunch. Not a huge menu to pick from, but the items they have are all excellent. The room was very spacious and felt like an apartment. The staff was also very friendly and accommodating. There's also a nice small rooftop patio with a view of the Acropolis. You won't be disappointed if you stay here.

Good location BUT...
The rooms do not look at all like on Booking.com. they completely lack maintenance. The furniture was old and broken, the TV antena didn't work, the toilet was broken so that the water was running constantly, the upper part of the shower did not work and the AC that should serve as heating was not sufficient so that we were freezing.The place was not clean especially in the bathroom. We have never seen anyone at the reception to help us and they also never responded to our emails.

Apartment was situated nice and close to everything, but down a quiet alleyway. Getting into the building and the apartment was very easy.
Met the owner, he was lovely.
The accommodation was very comfortable, however, still undergoing work by the looks of things, not quite finished yet.
The bed is very comfortable and the pillows are like clouds. There was only a top sheet provided on the bed but in peak summer, that's all you need. The room is spacious with a functional kitchen. It also had a hairdryer and a clothes iron.
The toilet is quite high up on the wall- I'm 5'9" and found it almost uncomfortably tall, and slightly leaning forward/left.
The only thing I found difficult was the shower- the hot and cold are marked incorrectly so make sure you turn the handle to the opposite end of the temperature you're looking for. I stood under freezing cold water for longer than I'd like to admit before I realized this.
The other thing with the shower is water comes out faster than it can drain and it floods the bathroom. A step to contain the water would be much better but there isn't one, so make sure you put your bathmat outside the bathroom door and dry yourself in the main area.
All in all, a comfortable stay and would probably stay again.

Great value for money at the very heart of Athens. Room was exactly as pictured, sparkling clean and well equipped. Specifically, the beds are comfy, the air conditioner is super powerful (lifesaver during the heatwave...), the shower is amazing and there is plenty of room for all of your stuff. The owner was super nice and helpful, and really made an effort to make sure our stay was just perfect. Will definitely stay here again next time!

We have a long stay, at euphoria apartments.
The rooms are luxury with many amenities and very clean.
We think the best choice in a heart of Athens.
Special thanks to the euphoria team and to the transportation company gkrekostours.
See you soon !!!
The rooms are perfect. Everything is thought out. Small details in the room.vthe owner is a very nice guy. The place is brand new. Very recommend!
Very spacious and clean apartments, with lots to do and explore nearby. Highly recommend!
Great location! Walkable distance to many sightseeing spots. The room and bathrooms were very clean. A large lounge area. Greek coffee was good. AC available at night.

Lovely and cozy hostel! Each bed had its own private curtain and locker, which were big pluses. There were plenty of shower rooms, which I found very convenient. The staff were also very kind and supportive.

I really liked Athens Hub Hostel. It is bright, modern, airy, clean and has a good atmosphere and all the staff were absolutely lovely! Thanks guys! The rooms are spacious and pleasant and the terrace on the roof is a nice place to chill after a day of sightseeing. The common area is really nice.  The only downside is that there isn't a kitchen that guests can use, but they do have a bar where they make breakfast for guests and they let me use their fridge and gave me what I needed (cutlery etc) to have my own vegan breakfast which I really appreciated. The location of the hostel is excellent. Very central and right in the heart of the city, a stone's throw to Monastiraki Square, which is a very cool place to arrive at if this is your first visit to Athens as it was for me. It's also very walkable to all the sights you'll want to see, including the Acropolis, Acropolis Museum and surrounding areas.

I'd happily stay there again :) Recommended!

5 stars all round - I came here as a lone traveller for the first time, and my experience so far has been so gratifying. The main bar area is so colourful and cozy, but still feels very modern.

The air conditioning is an absolute lifesaver, and has made ducking in and out of the heat so much more bearable, especially since it’s such a good location. The beds are neat, the place is kept very clean, and the staff is lovely and warm hearted.

I feel very safe and looked after here, and will absolutely recommend it to others on their trip to Athens! (Oh and the lockers are a great size, I managed to fit all of my packing in there which was insane to me)

This review is only in regard to the coffee there. Location of this place was in front of my hotel and I realized they turned out to be my favorite Freddo Espresso with coffee.

Atmosphere is nice, barista is really kind too. From all the days I spent in Athens, I kept coming back for my first or second coffee of the day there :)

I got my personal room and bathroom here. No issues at all. Location is excellent, all the top 10 sites are within a 30 minute walk. Streets were clean and safe, and some great food and bars options nearby. The rooftop bar has a truly exceptional view and friendly service. Would recommend to other friends.

Unfortunately I can't give 5 stars because the bathroom always flooded after people had a shower. But the position is excellent, the staff is very nice and friendly and the rooftop offers you an amazing view of the acropolis and the other archeological sites. It also has a bar with music where you can meet and talk to people at night.

We stayed in a private Acropolis view room. The room itself and balcony were very nice. However, the washroom was on the smaller side and the mattress was pretty awful. It felt like sleeping on a few old springs. Staff were all very friendly and approachable with answers to all our questions. The location is great. It is in proximity to all popular tourist attractions and the Metro. We opted for the breakfast package with our stay and it was the only regret we have. It was pretty terrible. Other than the breakfast and mattress, we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
Perfect location. Quite area however there’s no soundproof in my private room and could hear my neighbours easily. Bed was very cozy. Water pressure and temperature was excellent.
It is a hostel but we stayed in a separate 2 people room. Acropolis view from the room was just amazing. You have a terrace in your room. Maybe one of the best views in Athens 🤩

Such a lovely, small studio apartment! Lively businesses in the street below kept us awake later than we hoped. Apartment was clean. Management happily gave us special requests.  (Kitchen could use a sharp knife.)
We loved the roof top patio, too!
Luggage storage, places to shop & eat, bus & metro all very close by. Taxis easy to hail.

Room"s amenities - cleaning & terrace view are satisfying enough for a solo traveller or a couple, unfortunately service of the loft was non professional at all, as our room"s shower head was shaking all the time and when we asked the male receptionist for help, he rudely stated that he is not the owner and locked the door.
Also,on the last day of our trip, we asked him if he could arrange a taxi and stated that taxi pick ups should be declared at least one day before and via message not face to face (awkward as we have used many taxi services on Athens).

I stopped in the Studio apartments the toilet is cramped and the bunkbed is way too high not enough room which was very disappointing. I have to ring up a couple of times to find out information with regards to Cuttle also they do not clean the rooms and make the bed. It is an extra charge for this. I wish I picked another room.

The location is great. The photos don’t lie, you have an incredible view of Acropolis on the rooftop. The room is nice and comfortable, windows are soundproof, the bathroom is small but it’s easy to use. AC is working. You can leave your luggage if you want to wander around the city. Great option for two!
Rooms are clean and location is the best, everywhere is walking distance. There was a little problem with hot water but staff helped even late at night.

Nice hotel in a busy touristy area with plenty of restaurants and tons of shopping.
Hotel appearance and lobby were excellent.
Room was large by EU standards. The only caution on the room is the normal EU half shower makes the smooth marble floors very slick.
The included rooftop breakfast buffet was really good with a fantastic view of the Acropolis.
Included WiFi was adequate.
Staff was efficient and friendly.
Would stay here again.

The Acropolis view from room is breathtaking. The staff is very helpful and nice. The location is perfect, it is very easy to access the metro line to Acropolis and airport as well. Breakfast is very delicious. One small minus point is the room is little old and small, but it is really not a big deal compared to other plus points. I had a very nice time during the time when I stayed here. Highly recommendation.

Astor Hotel is highly recommended for anyone visiting Athens. Its central location, close to numerous restaurants and the iconic Syntagma Square, makes it an ideal base for exploring the city. The area is lively, adding to the overall charm of the stay.

The staff were incredibly friendly, pleasant, and kind, always willing to help. Although the room felt a bit small, it more than made up for it with its cleanliness and comfort.

The highlight of our stay was definitely the breakfast on the rooftop, offering a breathtaking view of the city. Watching the sunset over the sun-kissed Acropolis was an unforgettable experience. We thoroughly enjoyed our stay here and would love to return.

Initial impression of this Hotel is disappointing. We are in a family room and only have 2 people. The set out of room feels like I'm in a caravan. Not much room to move around. We booked a larger room as I'm in a wheelchair. I can't get my wheelchair past the entrance to the room. Location is good but the pavements around the hotel are very poor for wheelchair users. We picked this hotel as it said it was accessible. It's not. Website said there was a restaurant in this Hotel. However it's only open for Breakfast and for drinks in the evening. Lunch and dinner is not served.

Best acropolis view from rooms and smack in the nicer section for market shopping and easy access to restaurants

Service was nice. Our AC unit broke and they quickly moved us to another nice room.

Breakfast is very nice with superb view of the acropolis.

Large lobby with resting area couches etc.

Elevators are a bit small but manageable.

Very good place! We received detailed instructions on how to get to the apartment before we arrived, which I appreciated as an avid traveler. The owner was very sweet and responded to texts and emails promptly. Perfect location. Right in the heart of Athens and close to metro and subway systems.

I found Lekka 10 Apartments to be great value and in a great location.

Prior to arrival I was sent clear and concise directions to the apartment from the airport. These instructions removed a lot of stress after a long flight.

As I arrived early in morning, I contact the Apartments and they agreed to let me drop off my baggage and called me when room was available, which was very much appreciated.

The room was neat and clean with a balcony.

The only downside was the small shower space but that was made up by plenty of hot water.

I would be happy to return to Lekka 10 Apartments and would recommend for short stay.

All you need to spend first/last day in Athens. Very close to bus stop to airport. The host give all information via sms/call, there are few leaflet about the closest places to have drinks/food. Clean, simple and bright room.
Floor 7 is absolutely wonderful! If you can, choose it, it's worth every penny!
Nothing less than perfect appartement at the perfect location in the center of Athens.

This hotel is special. It’s in an amazing location. We walked to everything. Never had to use a taxi besides from the airport and to the port. They arranged that transportation for us and it was seamless. Our room was so special and the breakfast each morning was delicious. I love the decor as well. The thing that really makes this hotel stand out though is the service. Everyone we interacted with gave us 5 star top tier service. Shout out to Francis for being the absolute best and bringing me the best ice coffees in the morning. I will always choose this hotel whenever I am lucky enough to be in Athens again!
