place_name: The Hadrian Wall- Historic Acropolis Villa
review_text:
This Villa is beyond incredible.  Everything from reserving our stay to dealing with the owner due to a damaging wind/rain/thunder storm was perfect.
The owner, Nikos, also owns a coffee business, so the coffee in the villa is wonderful.  The rooms and entire villa is spotless clean.  There were four of us and this place sleeps nine.  Two regular bathrooms and one large bathroom with washer and bathtub.  Plenty of room for four women to get ready in the morning!
Very reasonable rates, too!  What's not to love?  Would highly recommend as it's in the center of Plaka, at the base of the Acropolis with a rooftop terrace.

place_name: The Hadrian Wall- Historic Acropolis Villa
review_text: Wonderful stay, great host. Spectacular view of acropolis from the private rooftop. What a splendid time we had. For sure again, when we will be jn Athens, now we are hooked 😊

place_name: The Hadrian Wall- Historic Acropolis Villa
review_text:
We spent the end of year holidays, it is a very beautiful house with a magnificent roof terrace overlooking the Acropolis.
We are keeping the address for next year 👍👍👍
excellent food! The nicest stuff welcomed us. The place offers a real greek experience with the best food in town.

Spend a night all very comfortable, super close to the Acropolis monument.
In the Plaka neighborhood, very well located.

It was my first time in Athens and I loved it. Partially because of this amazing place! Right in the middle of where you’d want to be. Apartment is brand new and the host Iryna is superb. Friendly and very accommodating. I would highly recommend staying here.

I am staying at the place now and I must say it is a fantastic experience! The suites are beautifully designed and super clean as the place is new, with nice atmosphere. The location was also perfect, making it convenient to explore nearby attractions.
Iryna the host, was very kind and accommodating, ensuring that all was comfortable.  nothing more I could have wished for during my stay. You would be lucky booking this place now
Very beautiful place! The manager was very helpful the whole week i stayed! The ambiance was incredible in the heart of Athens!
Very nice hotel in the heart of Athens; convenient location and friendly staff!
Cozy place, with great location in historical part of Athens

Excellent hotel to stay in while visiting Athens. Very clean, staff very helpful and the location is perfect for dining and shopping. The Acropolis is within walking distance, uphill of course. I highly recommend this hotel. In fact we are going back next year. Staff also speak English.

This place is so centrally located, I could practically high-five the Parthenon from my window.
The staff were so helpful, they gave me great local tips on where to eat, how to avoid tourist traps, and even where to get the best souvlaki without a 30-minute wait. (Pro tip: trust their food recommendations—they know their stuff!).
10/10, would definitely stay here again if my crippling depression wasn't catching up to me.

In amongst the action. Balconied room overlooking thriving street. Great shower. Housekeeping thorough and daily. Views of Acropolis from top floor breakfast terrace. Very quick and able reception, nothing too much trouble. Great value would recommend highly.

Adrian has a great location. Near Ermou shopping, Metro is very near. Adrian is very helpful.. Can leave bags if needed.... Late flight etc. Breakfast on roof terrace with view of Acropolis is brilliant.. Really lovely. Shower room was OK... Could do with an update but OK for now. Small balcony and aircon.
Room price is v good for the budget... Book... Long weekend in Adrian is worth it.

I’m surprised by the high reviews. My room was so grubby and there were strange stains all over (peeling wallpaper, and yellow stains over the wall, red stain on the light switch, the remote was filthy, and the carpet felt matted). I felt really uncomfortable with how dirty the hotel was.

I missed housekeeping the first day. I asked for water replenishment told me to just “buy some” down the road. I would have had better amenities at an Airbnb.

Two highlights:

1. The location
2. The fresh squeezed orange juice at breakfast is excellent.

Amazing location, max 10min walk to everything. Very quiet in the evening with all the sound proofing windows. The rooms are modern, clean and extremely spacious. The roof deck had amazing view to the acropolis! The owner Konstantinos was super welcoming and helpful. He arranged all our transportation and helped storing luggage’s then placing in rooms so we didn’t have to worry a thing. I would recommend this place to anyone!!! This is the best stay we had for years beating all the 5 star hotels for sure.

Lovely spacious room in a perfect location. The owner and his nephew were so friendly and kind to us making sure we had the perfect stay. Beautiful views on the rooftop overlooking the city and acropolis. We only stayed here one night but would definitely stay here again.

On our first day of our honeymoon we arrived at another accomodation we had set up and it was not up to par for what we thought we were getting. We then searched for last minute spots and Mythodea Athens Suites came up for a reasonable price. We then walked to it as the location is excellent, being close to the Acropolis in Monasteraki. We met with a Co-owner who saw the rough day we were having and made us feel welcomed and relaxed. He showed us the room which was amazing and clean. He also showed us the rooftop terrace which was stunning. There is also a cleaning service that comes with the room. He also provided us with any information we required and recommendations for restaurants. Excellent service, would recommend to anyone.

Wonderful place to stay!  The owner was fabulous and checked in on us the day before arrival, scheduled a driver to pick us up at the port and drop us off at the airport and a representative was on the curb as we arrived to help us check in without issue.  I cannot express our great appreciation for an awesome time in Athens!

A+++++  Very nice place, completely new and just a few minutes away from important tourist sites.  Wow!!!  The owner helped us get to his hotel by offering to book for us a transport the day before we arrived in Athens.  He stored our luggage because we arrived before the check-in, so it was possible for us to start visiting Athens immediately.  He helped us book the transport for the airport too.  We strongly recommend this place.  You won't be disappointed.

We had the best time staying at A77 Suites! The staff is extremely accommodating, kind, & helpful. You can tell they want to provide their guests with the best service possible! My husband & I stayed here for one night & I wish we could’ve stayed longer. It’s in the perfect neighborhood too - easily walkable and plenty to do, see, & eat in the area. We can’t wait to come back!

Came here with my husband for our honeymoon and it was a dream! The location is absolutely perfect. While they are a boutique hotel, the service was excellent. The staff was top-notch making sure we are happy with our stay and fulfilled many requests for us including transfers and reservation bookings. I highly recommend coming to this hotel as we loved every second of it.

Stayed for one night should have stayed for more. Perfect location to explore. Staff very helpful, recommending bars and eating places nearby. The hotel itself has been beautifully refurbished and the owners/staff have a right to be proud, it’s beautiful. We had breakfast in the room which was a lovely touch. Recommend staying here and when we come back will definitely stay again. Big thumbs up!!!

Almost 2 weeks ago, I said farewell to all my friends at the A77 Suites. I stayed in the Iconic Acropolis Suite for 4 nights and all I can say is that it was magical, like a dream. This was a trip of a lifetime for me and the entire staff at A77 made my stay as perfect as it could possibly be. I could go back by myself and stay here again, in this area of Plaka, and feel totally safe. There could not be a more perfect location. I really wish I knew how to find that beautiful music they had playing on the radio in the room when I checked in! I left it on the entire time I was there (except when sleeping). And shopping at the boutique downstairs is a must; such beautiful things! Thank you to all my dear friends at A77. I hope to see you again!

The room was great (#11). Had a nice view of the acropolis and of the street from the balcony.  No noise at all! The hospitality and hosts were great!  Came with breakfast options.  Was close to everything!  We will be back!  Thanks A77!!

Excellent company with great communication. Any time I had a question, they responded promptly. The apartment I rented for month of October in Pagrati was perfect. Nicely furnished, exceptionally clean, good location with good shops nearby and quiet.

Great location, but not great service. We were having problems with the toilet backing up into the shower stall. They said they would fix it right away. We told them about the problem on Saturday. It's now Tuesday, and they haven't sent anyone, although they said they would take care of it right away.
These guys have it down. Best airbnb to date. They manage many condos and do a great job at it. Place is OCD clean with everything u could need at home. Feeling safe is an understatement.

Great location
Very clean but not really good value for money

Perfect location, beautiful apartment, outstanding host! I would highly recommend this place to anyone visiting this part of Athens. Everything from the Acropolis and other archeological sites to the center of Plaka and Monastiraki Square - all within 2-20 mins walking distance. We could not have picked a better location for a 3-day trip to Athens. Thank you to the Upstreet hosts for providing a comfortable and memorable stay.

Absolutely wonderful experience. Freshly renovated, in the heart of Athens. What a gem of a hotel. We were able to walk to all the major sites to see within 10-15 minutes. So nice. Fey, Nick and other staff were awesome, super informative and kind. This was a wonderful start to our honeymoon trip! The included breakfast was DELICIOUS!

We had a fabulous stay at Esse Athens, the staff was so wonderful & friendly, they went out of their way to ensure we had what we needed (thank you Fay & team!). Room was beautiful, well equipped and very comfortable, breakfast they coordinated as well was the best I have had ever included in a hotel stay. Location was also perfect, right by all the sites/ neighborhoods. Thank you for an amazing stay!

Amazing place - Great hospitality

Amazing place in the heart of Athens close to plenty amenities bars and restaurants.

Clean rooms, amazing a la Carte breakfast with staff always willing to help.

Fully equipped rooms with the latest technology.

Suggesting it with no doubts.

Amazing service, rooms, and staff. The breakfast is the best we ever eat at a hotel. It is served at a cozy café next to the public square. Thank you Fay, Manolis, Paisa for all your dedication to make my husband and I feel comfortable.
Brand new, clean, modern hotel in the Plaka area. The concierge & staff members were extremely nice, and the service was incredible!

Really nice hotel. The room was massive and had an amazing view of the acropolis. The location was really central, could walk to all the major sites from there and the staff were so friendly and helpful. Would definitely stay again when in Athens.

Sweet hotel in the heart of the Plaka! Maryam at the front desk was super sweet and helpful. She even went out of her way to make sure we were all set !

This area feels so super safe and secure ! Walking distance from everything you could ever want to do in Athens. We walked everywhere- we went shopping, had the best food and toured the Acropolis!

The hotel was great about arranging transportation as well!

Perfect hotel in the heart of Plaka. Dimitris and his team are awesome. They are all so friendly and accommodating. We were so pleased with our stay and we highly recommend this hotel for a stay in Athens!
Awesome place in the middle of a pedestrian neighborhood with walking distance to the acropolis. The staff is great and will help you arrange any transportation you may need. Highly recommend.
Beautiful modern and big room in a great location in Plaka. Easy check in and key code entry. We were in B02 at 99 Adrianou with a great view of the Acropolis.

We stayed for 4 nights in the apartment. My wife, two kids, and I really enjoyed the location and modern interior of the apartment. It includes a kitchen but does not have a washer. There is a lift in the apartment, which makes transporting luggage to your unit easier. The check-in and check-out process is relatively easy.

The location of the apartment is central to everything; restaurants, cafes, tourist attractions, shops, and transport options are all within walking distance. The staff are friendly, and the apartment is very clean with a view of the Acropolis.

There is no parking at the apartment, so if you plan to drive, it may be a bit challenging. However, if you enjoy walking or don't mind using Uber, this is not an issue. The Acropolis view is partially blocked by a tree on level 2, where we were located. Ideally, if you request a higher floor like level 3, the view should be better.

Overall, we had a great time staying in this unit throughout our trip. Thanks for hosting us.

Oh man. Don’t miss this one. PERFECT location in the Plaka. Larger apartment, quiet and safe. Elevator to apartment. Great communication with Jenny and Romeo to check in. Clean with two bathrooms. Reasonable cost. We were cruisers with one overnight in Athens so no time to waste. Walkable to Acropolis and Parthenon. Get tickets ahead ONLINE and go early in the morning to beat the crowds and heat.

We were excited to stay at the Fos Residential Apartments, in the Presidential apartment, for our 8 nights in Athens. We were sent instructions on how to access the apartment since we were arriving outside of the front desk hours. We really did need a bit more information on the apartment because each apartment has their own hot water tank that must be turned on at the circuit breaker box when needed. We had difficulty finding said box as it was at the top of the stairway where you would be looking down to keep from falling instead of up beside a closet.
There is a large bank of unmarked switches that control some of the lights, rolling shades and shutters. Maybe some labels would be helpful for guests. This apartment does not have a microwave, dishwasher, ironing board or laundry facilities. There is a coin laundry just a couple of blocks away towards Hadrian’s library on a side street. There are 2 grocery stores just a few blocks away. This apartment was missing kitchen items when we arrived and the front desk brought up wine and other glasses that were missing. We also noticed that there were no kitchen knives but we travel with one and a spatula would have been nice to have as this was advertised as a complete kitchen. The coffee maker leaked when left with water in it so we just emptied it when not in use. The safe needs to be large enough to hold a laptop computer. This one was large enough for passports and didn’t seem secure enough for even that.
There are actually two full bathrooms with showers although the guest one off of the kitchen is a bit small and the shower support was broken. The mirror door in the main bath had been broken and not repaired. One of the rolling shutters in the living room area was not operational. There is a large marble dining table on the terrace but one of the chairs was broken and had been pieced back together so the cushion fell through when sat upon. Some of the cushions for the other benches had been damaged and we found them in an outside equipment closet. There needs to be a bit of maintenance done to be able to call this the “Presidential Apartment”.
These are minor items that need to be checked and completed between each rental.

We stayed at Fos for 4 nights - 2 adults and 2 kids ages 4 and 9. We LOVED our stay. The location was amazing - a quick walk to all the action, but quiet and peaceful at night. The only thing about the location is the lack of a place to park/pull over for a car/taxi - so ensure that you have all your stuff ready to go for a quick entry/exit. But if you are staying in the Plaka that's likely true for all.

The rooms were clean, modern and very comfortable. The two kids slept in the main room on sofa beds that were very comfy and they loved having their own TV with a ton of channels/netflix as most of our hotels only had a couple greek channels. I know you dont go to Greece to watch TV, but its hot in August, so some downtime in the apartment in the afternoon heat was much needed. There was a nice kitchen, well stocked with plates/glasses etc and a coffee machine with fresh pods every day. I found a grocery store a few blocks away so we were able to stock the kitchen for breakfasts and snacks.

The staff were all lovely and helpful. They arranged a great taxi for us to the port as well to the airport.

Best part - we had a beautiful view of the Acropolis from our window!

Fos Residential Apartments was the PERFECT place for our visit to Athens. It is truly a unique property -- a modern, boutique apartment building with only a handful of units, located right in La Plaka with its restaurants and shopping, and steps away from the Parthenon.  Our apartment on the top level was airy and modern with big windows and ample air conditioning and hot water in the showers. The view from the patio of the Acropolis was incredible -- a stunning sight to wake up to see it lit by the sun in the morning, and to enjoy a final drink while seeing spot-lighted at night after coming home from a nice dinner.  Romeo was a gracious and generous host -- he checked in with us right before we flew into Athens, and welcomed us at the desk right when we arrived in our cab.  We would recommend it as a fabulous home base for anyone's visit to Athens.
Recommended
I ate by chance without knowing it was a hotel. The service was very unfriendly and slow but I had the best Greek salad in all of Athens.

The host genuinely cares for the guests. I have noticed they pay attention to the guests needs and requirement and have them arranged during the stay which I deeply appreciate.

Close vicinity to the Acropolis, located in the lively neighborhood.

Having stayed in fancy properties in over 30+ destinations all over the world,  I highly recommended this property for anyone visiting Athens.

A more detailed review on the way.

Amazing location and apartment. Rania was an excellent hostess and provided us with everything we could possibly need for our stay. The apartment is updated with all the amenities and is in the perfect location to sightsee. She also provided snacks and candies that were enjoyed by all of us

We had an amazing time in Athens - the flat is centrally located and absolutely beautiful and clean.
The flat is equipped with a coffee machine and other kitchen supplies; the host also provided fresh fruits, drinks as well as toast and cheese/ham/honey/jam.
As our flight was late in the evening, we were allowed to leave our bags in the flat longer.
We thoroughly enjoyed our stay.

Amazing apartment, perfect location, everything spotlessly clean & in excellent condition. Can't recommend this apartment enough. Lots of unexpected extras, food, drinks, snacks & speciality coffee's. Highly recommended, wish I could give it 10 stars. Don't hesitate in booking, you won't be disappointed. Thanks to our wonderful host Raina for bring so pleasant & accommodating throughout our fabulous stay, from Aishling & Jason, Ireland :)

Extremely spacious. Refurbished to very high standard. This is effectively an apartment that one would be happy to live in full time.

What is refreshing is that the host puts milk, water and soft drinks in the fridge. And plenty of ice cube trays in the freezer! Nespresso coffee machine.. the list goes in. And it has two powerful AC units. At night they are more than whisper quiet.

Perfectly located  2 mins from Monastiraki Square and station and 8 minutes to Syntagma.
It’s better than the photos!
Great room, very cozy and the terrace in the Zeus suite is spacious and lovely. We enjoyed the location and the street it's on is very active during night time and fun to explore!
Excellent host! Mario helped us a lot! Great balcony in the Zeus Room. Excellent location. Best offer to the price.

This suite was perfect! Very clean and great location. If you are a light sleeper, bring some ear plugs and run some white noise because it can get loud outside. The bed was more comfortable than your average hotel. The fragrance was a little strong when I walked in but that was a easy fix. I turned off the automatic air freshener. Marios responded immediately to any question I had. I would definitely stay here again.

Communication was so easy and Mario confirmed, checked in with us,  even  arranged a transfer on our behalf.  It was such a clean, cute place to stay and the ice cream shop below had a lovely young man who helped us to our room and treated us to some fresh donuts and ice cream.  Was as easy as staying in a hotel but better.   Loved our stay! Would definitely stay again.
The location was great! Marios was super helpful and nice! Definitely would recommend staying here.

Great location and spacious suite.  Water pressure and hot water was decent.  Room was clean but had a strong smelling scent that took a day or so to lessen.

Unfortunately, the rooms were a bit less than we expected.  Bed was not comfortable and we like firm mattresses.  Plywood and Ikea-like furniture. The penthouse patio had no view to speak of and the outdoor furniture was uncomfortable, peeling paint in places.  Could easily hear the outside street noise until about 9pm, especially on the weekend.
The reason we have a 2* for service is that it is a self-check in hotel so no staff on site  (how do you give a strong rating for service when no one is present) but questions we did had were answered pretty quickly via Whatsapp.
If you stay here, go with the regular room as the penthouse was not worth the extra.

**Review for The Marble Suites, Plaka**

I had a wonderful stay at The Marble Suites in Plaka! The warm atmosphere and beautifully designed rooms made me feel right at home. My suite was spacious, impeccably clean, and featured artistic details.

The location is perfect for exploring local shops and sights, and the staff were incredibly friendly, always ready with helpful recommendations. I especially enjoyed the delightful view of the Acropolis at the rooftop terrace.

If you're looking for a cozy and memorable stay in Plaka, I highly recommend The Marble Suites. I can’t wait to return!

Highly recommend!!! Vassilis was so sweet and responsive to any questions I had. The room is spacious and the location is absolutely perfect. Everything that we needed to know was communicated to us including great recommendations for local places. If you’re coming to Athens, Plaka area you should totally stay here.

We have just stayed in the Marble Suites Athens for a quick 3 night city break and WOW! Was not expecting the accommodation to be anything special as rating does NOT do this apartment justice. Our host Vassilis was attentive, a fantastic communicator and nothing was too much trouble! The apartment was spacious, well equipped and quietly air conditioned. We had a starter pack perfect for short stays as well as high end cosmetics and a treat to welcome us. The Marble suites is ideally placed for all the attractions and walking was easy. The local districts are packed with quaint coffee houses and fairy light restaurants and the whole area felt safe and inviting. To be this close to the parthenon and other amazing cultural giants was a privelidge. Cannot rate the Marble suites highly enough! You will not be disappointed! Till the next time…

Andres, the host was exceptional and was always responsive to my messages. The location is amazing and is within three minutes of everything. Highly recommend. The room was clean and provided all your needs for a relaxing stay. Highly recommend.

place_name: Plaka's Villa with Breathtaking Acropolis view
review_text: Very loud place. Couldn't sleep at all
Good location, standard studio type room, friendly host who provided helpful directions for everything and easy access.
Small boutique apartment in the perfect spot.

Central establishment in the Plaka district of Athens, a 10-minute walk from the Acropolis or the ancient agora. Small, clean accommodation, well-equipped kitchen (oven, kettle, coffee maker, toaster, etc.), very comfortable double bed, functional air conditioning. Very friendly exchange with George the owner who will give you all the instructions to enter the apartment. View of courtyard with small balcony for breakfast, very quiet even though it is a very lively area.  Free and functional WiFi. A little nugget

Location right next to streets full of shops and restaurants. Very nice host. The apartment is clean, equipped with everything you need (lots of different equipment available in the kitchen). Check in is "touchless", which makes it much easier during the pandemic :)

Great apartment in the middle of Athens in the Plaka district! We had apartment number 3 with a roof terrace and a partial view of the Acropolis 🏛️
The apartment has a small kitchen with a coffee maker, a microwave and a small refrigerator. The location is absolutely perfect - just 5 minutes to the Acropolis.
The apartment was clean and modernly furnished. We were very happy with the apartment and would book it again at any time!

This is a true gem in the heart of Athens. First and above all the host Christos is just amazing and helps with everything you need. The location is the best you can find, everything is around you. The apartment is great the room layout is smart with all amenities facilities needed.  When we arrive fruits wine and food was waiting for us. The bed is comfortable and the biggest bonus is the balcony. Very very recommended .

You cannot beat the location, it's ridiculously close to all the sites, it is very quiet and felt very safe for being in such a busy area - super walkable to Ancient Agora, the Acropolis and Parthenon, the National Gardens as well as many hops and stuff are around here). Lots of  great food and drink options.

Now, this hotel isn't really a hotel - it's more of an airbnb. There's no front desk, you text the manager - Christos - and he meets you at the hotel. It's kind of like a small walk up apartment and you are given a bottle of wine upon arrival which was nice. The breakfast included is sort of DIY, you get some granola bars, dried fruit, fresh fruit (that looked kind of old), yogurt, cheese and eggs for your stay.The room was pretty clean, though a bit outdated. The only sink in the room is the bathroom sink, and there is no dish soap - I thought the cooking utensils (like a device that I can only assume hard boils eggs) looked kind of dirty, and there was a nespresso machine but the water section had calcified and there were things floating in it which wasn't very appealing.

Christos was pretty responsive, and let us know that the day we were leaving was a taxi strike. He noted that we are very close to the subway which goes directly to the airport and shared the timetable with us. On booking, they advertised helping with transportation however once we arrived in athens he basically said to take a taxi or the subway, didn't really help coordinating that (it was easy. though, so not a big deal). Our flight was super early and we were concerned that even the earliest train might not get us to the airport in time. We had to do our own research and coordinated a taxi to take us to the airport waaaay earlier than we needed to in order to avoid trouble during the strike.

I think there are better options for the money but this was convenient.

Excellent location right in the heart of Athens - walkable to Acropolis etc. Christos is great - he kept the room neat and stocked for our entire stay - was always available via WhatsApp to answer any questions. He'll send you the room info the day before your stay. He was also kind enough to let us drop off our bags as our flight arrived earlier than check-in. Suite is equipped with coffee machine, TV, hair dryer, etc- everything you need for a great stay. Balcony is lovely for enjoying your morning coffee. Overall, great stay, highly recommend!

The hotel was our last stay on a month long trip.  When we messaged the owner to say we had been dropped off by uber on street he came and met us and helped with luggage and showed us to hotel.  The hotel was in the heart of the shopping area.  Great location.  When we went inside we were impressed by the size and layout of the rooms and the care that had been put into everything.   Breakfast was provided for each day along with many other amenities.   They even left a bottle of wine for us.  The day we checked out we were able to store our luggage and the owner was there when we came back to help us.  Could not this place at all.  Loved it and would 100% stay there again

Excellent location and excellent room.  Very Clean spacious room, right in the heart of everything.  We were there three nights and never needed transportation everything in walking distance, food, shops, Acropolis, and all….Cristos is a great host, anything you needed he provided.   He runs a great hotel, friendly and very dependable.   We would definitely stay there again.

We highly recommend WOO Suites in Athens for not only its location but its incredibly fabulous and hospitable team who looked after us like this was a 5 star hotel! From making restaurant suggestions and reservations to their friendly conversation and genuine interest in making sure we were happy and had everything we needed.  The team literally went above and beyond to make our stay exceptional!  We loved the delicious breakfast every morning whilst looking up at the Acropolis! We loved the Rooftop Area, we wished we'd used it more and will do so next visit! The only thing my wife would have loved was a Magnifying Make Up Mirror!!!!! Thank You WOO Suites Team - we'll be back!!!!!

I recently stayed at Woo Suites in Athens, and I cannot recommend it highly enough! This boutique hotel, with only 8 rooms, offers an intimate and exclusive experience that is truly amazing.

First and foremost, the location is perfect. It’s close to everything you want to do in Athens, making it easy to explore the city's rich history and vibrant culture. Whether you're visiting the Acropolis, strolling through Plaka, or enjoying the local cafes and shops, everything is just a short walk away.

One of the highlights of our stay was the delicious breakfast. We had the option to enjoy it on the roof, overlooking the stunning Acropolis, or in the comfort of our room. The views from our Acropolis Superior Suite were breathtaking, providing a perfect backdrop for a relaxing morning meal. See the photos.

The room itself was spacious and well designed, with a massive bathroom that exceeded our expectations. It's clear that a lot of thought went into making the rooms trendy and comfortable. One day, we laid in bed for hours, just gazing at the Acropolis from our window.

Although Woo Suites doesn’t offer all the amenities of larger hotels, it more than makes up for it with its charm and the quality of its accommodations. The staff is incredible, we had a very early flight, and they offered to have a light breakfast and coffee delivered to our room at 3 am before we needed to leave to the airport. If you’re looking for a trendy room with a view, Woo Suites is the place to stay. We had an incredible experience and will definitely be returning on our next trip to Athens!

I can confidently say that Woo suites rivals some of the best 5 star properties I have stayed at around the world. Their service reminded me of my time in the Maldives where every detail was cared for. From before our arrival, our concierge was accessible through WhatsApp making thoughtful recommendations for our family visit. The rooms are stunning, beautifully kept and wonderfully serviced every single day. Recently I have found that the major brand name hotels have had horrible service, regardless of whether you purchase the highest category of rooms (I.e. they don’t even change sheets or towels every day even if you offer to pay for this service). At Woo our sheets were changed every single day and the room was always made up as though it was the first day of our stay! The towels were constantly changed and they made us feel as though it was their pleasure to service their guests! This was so refreshing because it is wonderful to feel like your comfort matters when on holiday and to be around individuals who are always smiling and genuine in their offers to help you feel relaxed. We had met 5 reception hosts over the course of our stay and every single one of them was so kind. Whenever we walked past reception, our hosts would always take the time to check in and offer ways they could help including entertaining my toddler with answering his many questions. My favorite part of every single day - especially as a mom - was someone waking me up with the best coffee and breakfast every morning. Their fresh baked croissants are not to be missed - truly like being in Paris! And lastly location - when I say prime location that is an understatement - the only taxi we took was from the airport to our hotel. Every Athens highlight was a walkable distance away giving you the ability to really take in the city centre while being able to walk back to your room to take breaks whenever you felt like it (ideal for toddler naps). If you want a relaxing hassle free trip where everything can be organized for you, your room is cleaned meticulously every day, you start of each day with a beautiful view and nourishing breakfast and you can enjoy a hot high pressure shower before you step out at soak in the wonderful city - look no further. It doesn’t get better than Woo suites. Also their roof top view of the acropolis meant we didn’t have to fight our way through long lines to other roof top bars to enjoy some wine and a good view. I hope to be back and I hope this boutique hotel can open up in a lot more countries. I am their number one fan!

We highly recommend staying at the Woo Suites! The rooms were so spacious and the service was excellent. Breakfast was amazing!!! The Woo Suites are centrally located and walking distance from all of the sites. We will definitely stay at the Woo Suites again. Thank you for making our first trip to Greece so memorable.

My family of 4 stayed at Woo on our recent trip to Greece. The Woo Team made our first time in Athens very special and memorable. From the moment we booked our stay until the end of our stay, the staff was informative, accommodating, and met our needs.  The room was spacious for 4 adults. The breakfast was deliciously prepared and arrived in our suite at our requested time. Management went a step further and prepared a “take-away” breakfast the morning we had an early excursion. I can’t say enough great things about this property! Thank you, Woo Team, for making our first trip to Athens more special!

We visited the downstairs bar. Very nice cocktails and charming team.

Edit: went back a few days later and had a great dinner as well. The team was fabulous again, and so were the cocktails. Highly recommend!

Beautiful wee hotel.  Rooms clean and great size.  Staff very helpful.  Cristina is excellent in the restaurant part.  Breakfast was fantastic and they adapted it to exactly what I asked for.  Surrounding area is flat which is good for those with any kind of walking issues.   Highly recommend.  Only small negative thing I would say is some of the other waitresses were asking too much about tips and paying now!

We had the lovely upper corner room. Staff were very kind. The restaurant servers were very nice and prompt. The hotel is on a square with a lot of activity. It's rooftop bar is not yet complete, but you can go up for a view of the Acropolis. We have been to Athens many times, this is a convenient location into the Plaka and Monistiraki.

Really exceptional experience here.
So I had arrived at my original hotel nearby only to find out the reservation was non-existent and they were fully booked, so I was wandering around in the very early hours of the morning while the streets were completely dark, silent and empty except for me and my roller suitcase. I had earmarked Nine Athens earlier and it was close so I walked that way. The young gentleman at the checkin was kind spoken and helpful and the room had more amenities than my previous hotel had and I was actually very pleased at the happenstance. The quality and style exceeded my expectations, It’s a bit of a hidden gem. I’m glad to find it, I will stay again.

It is an amazing boutique hotel - all is super clean and new. Restaurants and nice garden outside the hotel.

Customer care and services were out standing - super flexibility, and anything we asded for was sorted.

The morning breakfast is like chef's restaurant level - I uploaded some pictures.

Definitely will be back again.

Enjoy

I stayed only one night in this hotel and I wish it was longer! Super friendly staff, and a super cute and cozy room. Rooms have AC and a little fridge. For me the mattress was the perfect firmness. The location couldn't be more perfect either - walking distance to the Acropolis and a crazy amount of nearby cafes and restaurants. I would gladly stay here again when I come back to Athens in the future (because I will be back, what a gorgeous city)

Perfect location for exploring the historic sites of Athens. Our room was small but clean and comfortable. We especially loved sitting on our balcony and admiring the acropolis at night. It is close to lots of restaurants, shops, and metro stations for getting to the airport. We really enjoyed our stay!

Simple hotel perfect for visiting the Acropolis. Balcony overlooking church with bells ringing on Sunday morning. Good air-conditioning. Airy room. Charming lady on reception. Did not try breakfast. Lots of places near by for takeaway coffee, ice cream,  plus restaurants and snacks

Exactly what we were looking for- No more, no less.

My husband and I stayed here for a couple of nights during a trip to celebrate our 7th wedding anniversary. Athens was the first leg of a 3-destination trip, and it was the location we had the least amount of time in, so we wanted to hit the ground running! This hotel worked out absolutely perfectly for this.

The room itself is nice, nothing special. Small, but practical and clean. It had a nice-sized private balcony with a small table and two chairs, and we had a direct view of the Acropolis. After a full day of exploring it was nice to unwind on the balcony with some wine, look at some stars, and listen to the sounds of nightlife.

The main draw that led me to book this hotel was location, location, location! You could not ask for a better area to stay! Walking distance to everything you want and need. Close enough to some nice nightlife, but just far enough away to be able to retire to your room and not be bothered by unwanted outside noise. We ended up buying a 48hr pass for one of the 'hop-on hop-off' busses that stop around the major attractions of Athens, and the hotel was less than a 5min straight walk to one of the stops, so it made getting around the city extremely convenient.

Fancy? No. Extremely practical? Yes.
Overall, it was just what we needed.

Staff are super friendly. The location is perfect, you couldn’t get any closer to the main attractions in Athens. Bathroom is not attached to the room which can be quiet annoying. Otherwise, exactly what you pay for.

Conveniently located and no worries with luggage they will help you carry it. My room was right next to the shared kitchen which sucked cause the walls are definitely thin and you hear everything. If you can get a room away from the kitchen it may be quieter but expect to hear everybody’s business. Overall staff was helpful and friendly.

Great location since it is so close to all the restaurants and shops at Plaka, but it does have some drawbacks.

At first, we had a hard time finding it with google maps since Plaka is a cobblestone area with tight shops and buildings all around so it doesn't seem like your regular squared out streets with easily recognizable  landmarks.

There is no obvious entryway or signage (a plaque on the wall). There are 2 sets of french style wooden doors with doorbells and keypads attached. The first set leads to the reception area where you fill out a form, and they make a copy of your passport. Then they lead you back out to the second set of door where you use a pin to get inside to your actual rooms.

It is a modernized older building with many of its original charm so it does have air conditioning and a modern bathroom but be ready to walk up and down a spiraling three flights of stairs to your second floor room.

I wished I had booked their room with a balcony but don't remember if it was unavailable, or maybe I just wanted a cheaper room. I am paying less than $500 Euros for 3 nights and it includes all the free 2 liter water bottles you need (they have a small community kitchen where they have complimentary tea, coffee and water).

Our room is in the corner so we have two 6 feet tall french door style windows with original wood and hardware (so be gentle when closing them since both sides have to meet in the middle to close properly unlike newer styles where you close one first and the other side seals it or others where they close independently from each other). They meet in the middle, so one fits into the groove of the other, and then you twist the window handlebar to lock it.

The room key is an actual key that you have to use on the inside in order to lock your door (no twisting mechanism for easy locking). The advantage of this style of lock is not locking yourself out of your room with the keys left inside.

The doors of both the building and rooms are old, so it did feel a bit loose.

Since we are two old ladies, we couldn't possibly take our luggage up 3 flights of a spiral staircase so the two young gentlemen at the reception were very kind to help us. The tricky part is when we have to check out, I fear we might have to bother them again for help. I feel this type of lodging is best for younger people who travel light while staying to enjoy Plaka.

Air conditioning is a split system, and it is off, so turn it on with a high fan setting if you plan to rest within the hour of unpacking. You can always leave it off and just enjoy the fresh air by opening your windows, but be careful with the curtains on windy or rainy days.

The perk is being able to see the Acropolis right outside our window, and when you open your windows, you can smell all the delicious food from the restaurants downstairs.

I would definitely pick this place again if I were only packing a backpack or duffle bag while traveling with my siblings but unfortunately this time I have my mother who has arthritis so she did prefer a traditional hotel with elevators.

We recently had a marvelous stay at the Palladian Home Hotel in Athens. I must say we were pleasantly surprised. I would consider this a boutique hotel with just a handful of rooms. Very, very quant and it just added to our overall vacation experience. The only negative thing I can say is that our room was in the third floor (tight spiral staircase) and there are NO elevators, but even with that the staff helped us with our luggage coming and going. The staff was just outstanding and in particular, Panos. He was super friendly, accommodating and so very helpful.

Highly recommend this beautiful little gem, tucked away a stones throw from the Acropolis. Staff attentive, polite and nothing was too much bother. A great base for exploring all the local shops and for getting to the Acropolis early. Comfortable bed and a gorgeous shower.

Good location, the desire of the employees to provide service is good. Everything else is bad! The staff's professionalism in providing service is not good, 4 times we checked with the staff about ordering a taxi for a certain day and they said that everything is already arranged, a minute before you have to get into the taxi, the staff does not see the order, there is no order. under any criticism. The maintenance conditions are not good, when you take out the shower tube you cut your hand as you can see in the picture.
Perfect place. Highly recommended. We will return.
GOOD

Brilliantly located accommodation for exploring Athens! The hotel is modern and the rooms are well suited to couples, with comfy beds and excellent air conditioning. Check in was super easy and all the staff were very friendly and welcoming. The room is on the smaller side, which did make manoeuvring around and storing suitcases a bit tricky at times, but what you get in exchange more than makes up for it. Our room (Athena) was on the 1st floor and directly above the entrance, so there was a little bit of noise from the door and stairs, but nothing that detracted from our fantastic time in Athens!

This place is such a great location. The rooms were pretty nice and the only odd thing was that all of the drains were slow. But ultimately it didn’t matter cause the bed was comfy and shower pressure was great. The AC worked very well too. We also had an infant / baby with us and they had a pack and play. The roof had a pretty good view of the acropolis and the bottom and top floors had a water closet for anyone to use.Would stay again!

Location is perfect for walking to most of the ancient sites & in a safe area within a very short walk to dining & sweet treats & drinks & shopping! Very quick to respond in communicating prior to check in & during our stay. Clean, modern & comfy. There is outdoor space on the roof, too. Would definitely love to stay again!

The hotel was reasonably priced, very close to the Acropolis with views, and clean. The best part was Mandy, the manager.  She was incredible - attentive, knowledgeable about the surrounding neighborhood, but also just extremely fun to talk to! Our time in Athens was as influenced by her as the Acropolis!

This may appear outlier but im just sharing my experience. We arrived late for our check in since we werent given an option to cancel 48hrs before, the room was nice and location seems great. But the washroom wss really bad. We couldnt sleep as the toilet smelled like sewage. We tried to close the door and the lid but without the exhaust being on it is not possible to stay. There is no 24hr reception so be prepared for that
Great location and nice room. However, we ended up getting a smoking room. When we asked about it we were polite told tough luck. If not for that would have been an easy 5 star.
Great clean room. Very loud outside, party area. Located in a clean safe street.

Very centric and clean room. The staff was of great help to access the room and give information regarding the entrance codes and wifi. The common area is also very useful to have a meal or a hot cup of tea.

The hotel location is at the city center, yet in a quiet street, thus it is very convenient and enjoyable at the same time. There is no reception, we could check in and out ourselfs using codes for the lock, therefore it becomes very flexible to check in and out.
Location is great, price is a bit expensive for performance.

An excellent location. We'd stay again for it's proximity tot he Acropolis and all of the walking and dining in Plaka. Easy walking to Temple of Zeus, Parliament and changing of the guard, the all-marble ancient Olympic stadium, Acropolis and Acropolis museum and more.

Rooms are a bit dated but nice. Standard European small bathrooms but everything was in working order. Small balcony and great views of Plaka and Acropolis. Rooftop lounge great for cocktails and small plates.

We took the budget room but there was no information available on booking.com that it is actually an underground room. The bathroom was actually 3 steps up inside the room making it very uncomfortable. So please don’t consider the budget room and make sure you get rooms 1st floor onwards. The location was more than perfect.
Very nice hotel in the middle of PLAKA, very hospitable and friendly staff. The owner makes all guests feel like one of the family. Highly recommended place and we will surely return.

Don’t buy the breakfast, it is not worth the €10. The hotel rooms are decent, but the bed was not so comfortable. Great location for walking to the historic monuments. The cleaning staff were very friendly.

The Good.

* Superb friendly reception staff, breath of fresh air to see genuine kindness

* Location brilliant as many have indicated

The Not So Good.

* Double room on floor 2 was so small, if we had 2 suitcases there would be zero room to stand, think TINY.

* Unfortunately one of noisiest hotel rooms I have ever been in, zero soundproofing. Noise from elevator and all bathrooms around us was horrible, beware if you are sensitive to alot of noise at night. You can hear all conversations in both corridors and surrounding rooms. We were woken on many occasions thinking someone was in room with us, those walking in corridors soubded so close.

* Yes a wet soggy mess on floor of bathroom after shower as door does not seal, beware dont leave anything on floor as it will be soaked.

* Bed is soft but with any lack of support so beware if you have back trouble

Basic but reasonably priced and clean hotel in an excellent location. The room was small but had everything you need. Unfortunately our air conditioning did not work on the second night which made for a very uncomfortable night. The hotel were very apologetic though and offered to pay for our taxi to the airport to compensate which was very much appreciated. The staff were really friendly and I would definitely stay here again. There is also a nice little cafe opposite and a terrace upstairs, from which you can see the Acropolis.

This was perfect for us, easy access to all of the main sights in the centre of Athens. Room was not the biggest but was OK, bathroom was good. A very warm welcome and introduction to the hotel services and gaining entry through the front door. Good level of room cleaning on request.

I stayed in the hotel for 2 nights. It’s very simple, but super clean, staff is very nice and location is amazing. We were able to do all tourist attractions walking. The only fyi is to consider it has 3 floors and only stairs. It was not an issue for me and my family, but something to think about if you have reduced mobility. I’d stay again 100%. Thanks for the help calling the taxi to the airport!

We absolutely loved this location in Athens! The room was perfect for our one night, the air conditioning worked well and there was even a large balcony. The service we received was also excellent- the receptionist carried our luggage up the stairs for us- and it was fairly quiet considering we were in a busy city.

I don’t understand why they say one star hotel. More like three. Truly excellent location. Close neighbour of the Archbishop of Greece. Nice terrace on the top you can use. Very helpful staff. Good size room and big shower.
Nice
Excellent apartment with all the necessary amenities. The owners are very cordial.

The Dolli: A Hidden Gem in Athens

I had an absolutely fantastic stay at The Dolli. The location was perfect, right in the heart of Athens and within walking distance of all the major attractions. The staff was incredibly helpful and friendly, always going above and beyond to ensure I had a comfortable and enjoyable stay. The food was exceptional, with a delicious variety of options to choose from. I highly recommend The Dolli to anyone visiting Athens!

We booked a room with a view and we were given a room with an interior patio shared with another room. Not worth 1k+€ per night!
Plus, you will hear the noise from the corridor all night long. Very disappointed. The view from the rooftop is great, the pool is very very small and there are no sunbeds. Very loud and annoying music. It is definitely not a luxury experience. For the rate level, there are much better hotels in Athens. We do not recommend it.

It is in the perfect location. Having breakfast overlooking the Acropolis is really something worth experiencing. And dinner too. And Lunch. And drinks while splashing in the infinity pool... perfect location, concept, rooms, amenities. So happy to be here

Spectacular views from the Rooftop pool and dining. The food was excellent. The service excellent. The rooms were very good and well appointed. A lot more than I would normally spend on a hotel, but worth every cent (euro) highly recommend

My husband and I stayed at the Dolli for two nights during our Honeymoon in Greece. The staff were very accommodating and offered us beverages and snacks upon our arrival, as well as a complimentary bottle of red wine in our room. The room was small but immaculately clean and the bed was very comfortable.  The restaurant, pool and patio on the rooftop were delightful and offered spectacular views of the Acropolis, which was beautifully lit at night.  Prices for drinks the bar are a little high but it’s to be expected at a five-star hotel.

The hotel is within walking distance of the Monastiraki market, dozens of restaurants, bars, the Acropolis and the Greek Agora. We would recommend this hotel for the service, cleanliness and its close vicinity to several tourist attractions in Athens.

This hotel was brilliantly placed for restaurants, shops, acropolis and agora. The facilities are clean and all work, the breakfast is also fabulous. To top it off, the staff are fantastic, friendly, helpful and fun.
The view from the roof garden is amazing.
We had a brilliant stay, thank you.

Very good hotel in an amazing location.  Nothing fancy, just a really great hotel with brilliant breakfast.

Most of the main attractions are walkable,  and lots of shops and restaurants nearby.

Icing on the cake is the view of the Acropolis from the rooftop bar.

Right in the prime location for visiting Athens, this hotel was clean, well run, and the rooftop restaurant offered an incredible view of the Acropolis in addition to excellent food & drinks.  Would revisit next time I’m in Athens.

I gave it 5 stars for the value of money. It really met the qualifications of a 3 star hotel. My room had a shower and bath tub. Be aware that the restaurant does not have view of Acropolis. But it was nice. The location of hotel is very good close to every where.

Comfy beds, location couldn't be better, great breakfast with lots of options, ONLY downside was very little sound proofing between rooms.
Loved the view from the rooftop restaurant. Food was excellent, but service was lacking.
Absolutely beautiful and I’m walking distance from Acropolis and shopping. The Staff was amazing! Great breakfast included! Beautiful Rooms.

This hotel is perfectly situated in Plaka and is extremely walkable. It is clean, lovely, and all around a great place to stay! Thanos in particular was phenomenal. He accommodated us when we ended up having to stay another night with no additional fees (outside of the room cost). The area is super cute and less than 5 minutes to all of the major spots! Highly highly recommend!

Wow, sums up the experience that we had at the Plaka arch suites. We booked the dream suite thinking we probably would not get it, but we did and we felt like royalty. The views of the acropolis and Parthenon are beautiful. They are so close and lit up at night. You think you could reach out and touch them. Better yet sit back in the Jacuzzi and just take it all in with a bottle of wine -Delicioso!!!  Special thank you to the blond woman I think she’s the owner not positive who was extremely nice and excited meeting us at night when we got there to let us in and show us around and then was able to find a garment that I left behind and mailed it back to me in the USA. We will definitely be back and I know where we will be staying when we do.   if you’re thinking about a trip to Athens this is where you wanna be. ❤️

We stayed 2 nights in the Rooftop Suite in August. The room was large with comfy bed, delicious breakfast, the location was awesome, and the view from the rooftop deck was surreal. We’d stay here again and refer others!

Found this gem of a hotel in the heart of Platka & were so pleased! Not luxury, but so lovely. Comfortable bed, good sized shower, clean, staff is extremely responsive to requests. We would stay there again. Perfect walkable location!
Amazing place. Super clean and the best and biggest shower area... a real delight in the centre of Athens... Purelly amazing...

Our stay was fantastic. The rooms are very comfortable and spacious and very clean. Very large and modernised bathroom. I highly recommend this location for accommodation is as very central. Parking garage within walking distance and very close to all the restaurants / cafe and shops in central Athens.
Check in was very easy and the host did everything in their power to make our stay comfortable. Five star review wasn’t enough!!

Well priced and rooms are comfortable, just be aware that the communal areas are a bit scruffy.

When we booked the room, we were asked to make a direct bank payment for one night, just so you are aware, as this involves sending money abroad, but don't worry, use Wise or some other bank service.

We stayed in two apartments, the roof terrace and the luxury 2 apartment. The balance was owed on arrival.

The rooms are supposed to be ready for check-in at 2 pm. we got there early on a Friday and were treated to drinks by Stratos in the wine bar downstairs until the rooms were prepared.

Our initial impression wasn't great as, as soon as you walk into this small apartment block, you're immediately greeted by a very scruffy storage area with a broken table and chairs stacked up which I assume were from the restaurant. The hallways and other communal areas hadn't yet been cleaned and, again, looked a little scruffy, but it wasn't a major issue. It can get windy in Athens and leaves, and empty water bottles can get blown in from the terrace on the 1st floor (because other guests don't know what a bin is clearly!).

Everything worked fine in both apartments but one annoyance was that the when you remove your room card from the slot inside the room, literally all the electricity is switched off, including the fridge, so nothing ever stayed cold.

The roof terrace door can't be locked, so anyone can walk up onto the terrace where the room with a good view is, this is where we stayed. Be warned, the roof terrace room is smaller than you might expect. There is a sliding white metallic shutter that sits in front of a sliding glass door, which is your entrance into your room, and immediately in front of you is the bed.

I complained about the fact that the exterior shutter door could not be locked from the inside, and as there is no curtain in front of the sliding glass door, anyone could slide open the shutter door and see straight into your room as you slept, if anyone did wonder upto the roof terrace which they could.

The roof terrace was very scruffy, lots of bird droppings everywhere, and that metal shutter was dirty. It's not as nice as the original pictures show on the website, it's just a bit worn.

The WiFi works fine and if you're happy being smack bang in the centre of everything then it really is a great location, just be aware that it can get noisy as a busy narrow main road is directly in front of the property, this may affect light sleepers.

Oh and excellent tip...if you're on a budget, grab your bottled water, fruit and snacks a A to B (supermarket chain) which is across the road to the left near the ice cream parlour...bottles of water are 17 cents.
This was a lovely apartment to stay. The rooms were modern and clean, shiny bathroom and light blocking drapes. The windows are double glazed, so you could barely hear any street noise.

When originally booking for a place in Athens I was unsure due to the limited google reviews Metis has.

However I don't regret booking one bit, and am pleased to be able to report that you should stay at Metis for your trip to Athens!

Rooms large and clean, luxurious finishes and feel to the place. (Apologies that I don't have better photos).
Only caution is if you're a light sleeper you might need some eat plugs as the main street is below and honks from cars and church bells will be heard.

Self check in was easy and throughout our stay we had contact with the lovely staff down at store front and also the restaurant below. Helpful and assisted us with some documents we had to print, and checked in with us to see how our stay was.

Location was central, easy 5min walk to Monastiraki station.

Overall, highly recommend place to stay in athens! Wonderful professionalism and assistance from the lovely staff.

The staff here are the best! We had a few issues (our fault) and Magda at the front desk helped us resolve all of our worries. It’s location, right below the Acropolis, was perfect. Every morning we enjoyed breakfast while looking up at the Parthenon. It doesn’t get better than that!!

We loved staying here. Such a cute boutique hotel with a lot of charm. Location is the best at the base of the Acropolis and easy access to all attractions in the city. Rooftop breakfast was a special treat and Magda takes care of everything.

We just loved this place and Lizi was so awesome for arranging transportation and information and links to get tickets for the Acropolis truly made our stay exceptional! The stairs are very steep, if you have mobility issues this will be a problem! Amazing breakfast on the rooftop was fantastic,  I'd stay here again!

The rooms are very good and spacious. Everything is in place. The reception is very helpful. I highly recommend it to everyone. If it is very popular, you can see the acropolis close to the back from the terrace. We were very pleased with walking distance to everywhere. They welcome you instead of giving you a nice welcome. I recommend everyone to go. I think we will see each other again soon. I can say that it is the only hotel I will stay in when you visit. great great great thanks guys

Kyria Boutique House was the perfect selection for our stay in Athens. First you can't beat the location. Literally steps from the gate of the Acropolis Parthenon. It's also a stone's throw from the Acropolis Museum, the Acropolis metro stop and an easy walk to the various ancient sites such as the Ancient Agora, Hadrians library etc. There are many great restaurants and shops in the area. It is lively but not as loud at night as other areas. With this location, there is no need to rent a car (and I wouldn't want to drive in the city!). It felt very safe. I was not concerned even as a woman and with children.

A highlight is the rooftop with views of the Parthenon and the neighborhood. It was beautiful at night! and a perfect way to start the day in Athens with the included breakfast buffet on the rooftop. The breakfast was very good with omelets, meats, yogurts, vegetables, eggs, mini sandwiches, coffee and freshly squeezed orange juice. Wonderful!

Magda at reception was very kind and helpful. She will answer any questions you have and gave us restaurant recommendations. She also offers airport transfers, reservations, etc. Checkin and out was super smooth. You will be well taken care of!

The room was immaculately clean. We stayed in the executive suite on the ground floor. It was spacious with tall ceilings. Charming vintage touches. There was a king bed in the bedroom and a pullout couch in the living room for the children. Both rooms had access to a private courtyard patio which was just perfect for snacks and enjoying the beautiful weather. There is a desk, wardrobe and mini fridge. It was very comfortable for the family, but I would also stay here as a romantic couple or friends as well.

I would definitely recommend Kyria for your stay in Athens, and would absolutely stay on a future trip. Highly recommended!

I would highly recommend this apartment for your stay in Athens. I have stayed in multiple places in the Plaka and this is by far the best. The Plaka is my favorite area to stay in, but unless I have stayed in a hotel, it has been too noisy. Not this place! It was very quiet and peaceful.
The place is completely renovated, and everything is updated and beautiful. It was very hot outside, but the AC worked wonderfully. It is hard to find a good apartment with great AC in the heart of Athens (in my experience).
The host is a 10/10 as well. I had issues with my phone and it deleted all the information that was sent to me prior to my stay. When I contacted to host to ask him to resend everything day of my arrival, he did so promptly. He checked in via text to make sure everything was good with my stay. He also gave me a hard copy key since my phone issues persisted.
I ended up coming back to Athens shortly after my stay at the Plaka House, but it was fully booked. I stayed nearby, but next time I will plan better so I can stay at the Plaka House anytime I am in Athens. I have not yet found a place that compares.

***Super recommend! ***

First thing to mention is that the apartment is in PRIME location! In the most beautiful neighborhood of Athens, and 10 minutes (of a beautiful and super pleasant) walk from the shopping center. I am not too familiar with Athens but I guess 90% of the most recommended restaurants and cafes are in the radius of 10 minutes walk.

The apartments are absolutely new. We rented two apartments and they were both amazing, clean obviously but also well designed and just comfortable and nice to be in.

Finally, worth mentioning that the host was amazing. We communicated via Whatsapp,  he made sure we know how to arrive and even suggested a parking location. When we needed some help he was quick to arrive, and was very kind and happy to help. He always made sure we have everything we need and made us feel very welcome which is always important.

Thank you so much for everything! If I’ll ever be back in Athens I’ll try to book Plaka house again for sure 🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼

Everything was just perfect! The host is a great and responsible person, we needed a little bit of sugar and he delivered immidiatlly. The house had everything, coffee machine with coffee pods, the fridge was full of water (but also you can drink tap water), juices, wine and some bites in case you need them after a long day of waking. The place is in the city center, less than 10 min from Parthenon. Everything was near, all of the places we wanted to see were in a walking distance from house Plaka. I highlly recomend it!

We had a good time. Very central, very clean and nice & new iSmart appartments.

Excellent Host ! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️🧿
Amazing property to stay and experience central historic Athens.

From the time we walked in the hotel, we were welcomed by the very friendly and accommodating staff.   The room we got was on the Ground floor.  We are a family of four including a 14 year old and 12 year old.

The room was just perfect for us, it has two levels, the other room and bathroom is on a lower floor accessible via spiral staircase.  Logistically, the lower room was perfect for our kids but my wife preferred us to be there.  We had no problems going up and down the staircase and we are not in our best shapes but I will not recommend the lower floor for those with knee problems.  There is a small kitchen/dining area and it has a stove and everything else you need to cook if you need to cook.  There is a coffee maker which I love since I love drinking coffee.  It has plates, drinking glasses, coffee cups, spoons, forks, knives, etc.  It is an eco-friendly hotel and I was amazed to learn that the tap water was drinkable.  I was hesitant at first but we were fine specially me who has sensitive stomach.  It saved us money too from buying bottled water during our stay there.  It was also nice and convenient that they have an iron and board available and stored in a closet that us accessible with your room key.  There is a roof access where you can enjoy the view of the Acropolis!  This is probably the only hotel where the photos were not misleading.   I had coffee up there one evening and it was relaxing.

The location of the hotel is excellent too!  It is a very short walk to the Plaka area so we had no problem finding a place for food and shopping.  It is also within walking distance of Syntagma Square and the Acropolis and other attractions.  We had to take a taxi to the Acropolis because it was very hot when we were there but we walked back to the hotel after our visit at the Acropolis and we loved it because we were able to see other nice neighborhoods.

Why not a full 5 stars rating?  I always think nothing is perfect and there is always room
for improvement.  In this case, the only thing
I can think of is that they do not have breakfast available in house, but it is really a
very minor thing.  You can order breakfast and it can be delivered to the room and as I mentioned, Plaka is right there where there are many restaurants to choose from.

It was a wonderful stay!

Good communication prior to arrival. Airport transportation provided with additional cost. Rooms clean. Beautiful roof top terrace. Great location. Staff are helpful and pleasant. Fair price.

Just fyi, there are no local free calls from hotels here or I guess anywhere in Europe. We called family in Athens from the hotel room at got a very large charge.

I’ve enjoyed staying with Hapimag ever since I was a child and once again it was a very nice stay! We had a very nice room with a balcony with the most perfect view. The room was really clean and it had everything we needed. The women at the reception are really helpful and very kind. The location is perfect, we did not use any transportation during our stay, we did all of our sightseeing, grocery shopping, going out for shopping and dinner, etc. by foot. The airconditioning works really well, which is very important since it’s 35 degrees Celsius outside. If I visit Athens once again, I will definitely stay at this hotel again!
Great location - right at the tip of the district Plaka. Easy to reach from the airport and 5min walk to the entrance of the acropolis. Nice view from the roof.
This one is up there with the best of the city Hapimags. The view of the Acropolis from the roof top is outstanding. The furnishings are relatively new and it shows.

We stayed in October 2023, and I liked it because everything was within walking distance. We didn’t need to take any buses, only an Uber to get to the airport and another to reach the ferry. The only issue we had was the noise at night; a group of people were talking very loudly, and I politely asked if they could move elsewhere or speak more quietly as it was late and we wanted to rest. Another thing that could be improved are the curtains, as a lot of sunlight came through them. The check-in was automatic with a code we received. We booked through Booking.com.

SCAM! DO NOT BOOK. We booked this room through their website months in advance, receive confirmation emails, and yet when we arrived they said the room was not available! They said there was a booking issue and to call their costumer service line in about 5 minutes. What an absolute JOKE. SAVE YOURSELF THE TROUBLE PLEASE. Luckily we were able to book another room elsewhere, again, no thanks the The Karma.

Location, location, location.  Loved it.  Stepping out the door into the heart of the Plaka district was fantastic.  The place was clean and provided exactly what we needed to rest for a couple of days in Athens.
Great apartment at the edge of the Plaka district.  Good air con, private rooftop terrace (when you book the penthouse).

Great location and rooms are clean and spacious. A cool old mansion converted into a hotel, owner is very kind and accommodating. A little loud but that's to be expected being located in such an popular area

place_name: Apartments Rastoni Athens Suites near Acropolis at Tsatsou
review_text:
Lovely place in the city centre in walkable distance to all attractions. Lots of places to eat around.

10 mins from metro station too. So very easily accessible from the airport.

Overall a lovely place in the city centre and would definitely recommend.

place_name: Apartments Rastoni Athens Suites near Acropolis at Tsatsou
review_text:
Beautiful place in a great location
It was almost perfect except the shower.
We had 2 days without hot shower and low water pressure.
Hope they will fix it.

place_name: Apartments Rastoni Athens Suites near Acropolis at Tsatsou
review_text: Great Central location and wonderful rooms in a safe feeling area.

place_name: Apartments Rastoni Athens Suites near Acropolis at Tsatsou
review_text: Great location but terrible smell of air freshener and weird green emergency exit light that was on all night.

place_name: Apartments Rastoni Athens Suites near Acropolis at Tsatsou
review_text: Exceptional service!!!

From this area is one of the best deals for that price, the apartment is comfortable, nice and practical ( not luxurious, but clean tiddy and stylish) you can cook there if you want, it have a small fridge. From the airport to there by subway is about 35 min+7 min walking. Is close to all the highlights, walking distances.
The bed is soooooo comfortable, I really loved it, the AC works good and you have power supplies close to the bed, the only thing is a little bit dificult is the bathroom is a little bit tight to shower, use the wc and brush your teeths, but is not the end of the world.

Perfect location for a couple or 3 person family to explore all Athens has to offer. Bathroom is small, but efficient for showering after a long day exploring ancient sites out in the heat. WiFi is stable and quick for any internet browsing, movies, kid games with no issues. Easy to get to and from the airport via the X95 Airport Express bus whose stop is less than a 5 minute walk to the apartment from Syntagma Square. Lovely neighborhood right in the Plaka near everything. Kitchen is your basic stove/oven, sink, small fridge, coffee pot, keurig like device, and our absolute fav the ELECTRIC KETTLE which we used frequently.  Kitchen had all the basics for cooking and a drawer/cupboard of some basic ingredients as well.  There is a Bazaar grocery shop about 3 minutes walk around the corner for inexpensive quality groceries, and a ATM right next to the apartment lobby entrance. Bed and linens clean and comfy.  Our host George was very nice and helpful, even picking us up at 11pm at night from the airport after our flight was delayed. All around a first rate experience. Hope to book again next year!
Very poor service.

Small negative points, it is not a hotel. It's a room in an apartment block.
So no one to welcome you!!!
That's why I give 4 stars.
And the bathroom is ridiculously small!
But hey, we don't live there 😜
That being said, it was very clean and quiet.
Wonderfully located! In old Athens. In typical bistros and small restaurants. Full of boutiques and mazes of small, thriving and picturesque streets. Close to the city center, the metro (Syntagma Square) and the X95 airport line as well as the Acropolis-Parthenon. About 10 minutes walk from everything!!
Excellent
Absolutely loved this place. It was luxurious, comfortable and chique. Location was also top notch and hosts were so helpful for absolutely anything we needed or any questions we had.
Fantastic location. Andreas is very professional and the room was clean and perfectly equipped. My girlfriend and I had a lovely stay, even if just for one day. Recommended!
We had a great stay! The apartment was sparkling clean and comfortable. Also Andreas our host was very helpful and responsive. I highly recommend this rental!

Love Andreas and his prompt responses to everything we need. He booked us airport transfers from and to airport so we didn't have to worry about it, even accomdated us when I booked the wrong dates! Everything was perfect, great location. Love our stay! Thank you.

Just stayed 1 night - all was perfect!
From the first contact, the airport pick-up with a cool shuttle van, the beautiful state of the art appartment … Andreas organized all our demands right on the spot.
The suites are located in a renovated beautiful building within easy walking distance of all important touristic sites.
We will definitely come back beginning 2023.
We can only warmly recommend staying with Andreas and his family.
Christian & Dimitra, Luxembourg
Very clean, organized with every need and well situated to walk to all the sights.

The location is perfect and the space of the apartment is large enough.
We were touched by the old goal, the mattress did not have a protective cover and the sheets were small, made of foam and very soft. The shower needs a door so it doesn't ski all over the floor.

Muito bem localizado, boas dimensões do apartamento. Razoavelmente bem equipado.
O box não era totalmente fechado, o que fazia com que o banheiro ficasse sempre molhado.

Apartment in a prime location. Out the door and directly on the pedestrian zone with numerous shops, taverns, cafes etc. Clean and spacious with 2 bedrooms (double beds). We felt very comfortable. All sights can be reached on foot in about 15 minutes (Acropolis, Parliament, Panathinaikon Stadium, Anafiotika). Kiosk for drinks directly under the house.

place_name: The Avant Garde - A Luxurious Rental Apartment in Athens
review_text: Apartments belong to locals, not to tourists

Do not stay here. The price is too high €450/night for the lack of air conditioning. there are 3 ac units but they just don’t do the job. They are old and they need to be replaced. The bathrooms waste baskets don’t have lids on them. It’s just not that nice for the price. You are paying for a view of the acropolis. That’s it’s.

The apartment is just like the photos. It's in a great location. Walking distance from all major attractions. It's well equipped and air-conditioned.
I gave it a 5 start because of the level of care provided by the property manager Yannis. He called me before arrival to confirm, helped me with airport rides which was very helpful. He was also there when we arrived to make sure everything was in order. Really appreciated all the good tips.
Very nice apartment at a perfect location to discover Athens. Main attractions within walking distance and also metro nearby. Giannis is a perfect host helping you  wherever he can.
I'm a bit late with this review, but first let me say Yannis is an absolutely fantastic host. The apartment was perfect with a great location.
Best place to stay in Plaka! Excellent location. Super comfortable. Clean. Strong WiFi (teenagers love it). Quaint street. Reasonably priced. 5star host - Yannis. Highly recommend!
Good .. quite place .. clean ..

Very nice apartment, many comforts and conveniences such as 2 bathrooms, 4 air conditioners, many beds and wardrobes, even a washing machine and dishwasher.
