Very basic room and hotel with no frills, but couldn't have been more pleased for what it was. Ended up being one of our favourite places we stayed while in Greece. Friendly staff, great neighbourhood and our terrace room had a certain kind of charm to it. If you are looking for a budget place to stay in a nice, quiet neighbourhood, I cannot recommend Art Gallery Hotel enough.

The Art Gallery hotel offers a cozy, family-owned atmosphere where guests are warmly welcomed, almost as if they were visiting relatives. During my three-night stay, I found the rooms to be adequately clean, comfortable, and spacious, albeit with some minor wear and tear on the room and bathroom amenities. For instance, I encountered an issue with the door lock, but the friendly reception staff promptly provided a workaround with a smile, suggesting a clever trick to operate it until it was fixed.

One of the hotel's standout features is undoubtedly its staff, who consistently go above and beyond to assist guests with a genuine kindness that enhances the overall experience. While the breakfast provided was basic, it served its purpose well enough to fuel explorations of Athens' ancient wonders, and it also conveniently provided a snack for later in the morning at nearby restaurants.

The hotel's location is another highlight, situated in a quiet area yet still within easy reach of key attractions like the Plaka and the Acropolis. Despite its proximity to tourist hotspots, the neighborhood maintains a peaceful ambiance, with plenty of excellent dining options and cafes nearby. Moreover, the reasonable pricing adds to the hotel's appeal, offering good value for money considering its prime location and attentive service.

In summary, the Art Gallery hotel provides a pleasant stay characterized by familial hospitality, comfortable accommodations, and a prime location that allows guests to enjoy the best of Athens without feeling overwhelmed by crowds.
Great location, the rooms were clean and spacious, the staff was friendly and attentive, we really enjoyed our short stay and hope to return one day

The rooms aren’t anything special, but they have everything you need and more! The staff are the sweetest, coolest people!! So friendly and warm. I stayed here with my study abroad group multiple times over our 3 week trip since Art Gallery was considered our “home base.” By the end of the trip everyone in my group referred to this place as “home.” I even stayed an extra day and a half here with my boyfriend after my studying was done and he loves it just as much as I do!! Location is SUPERB, breakfast is delicious, but the staff make this place AMAZING! I highly recommend hanging out in the front desk area to get to know the staff and cool off in the AC. I’ll be back soon! ❤️

The hotel has a fascinating history, its foundations having sheltered the current owners’ grandparents as a bunker in WWII before they made it first an art gallery and then, building upward, this hotel. Don’t expect spanking new state-of-the-art ANYTHING. Expect inimitable charm, cleanliness, authenticity, helpful staff, and an excellent location. We loved it.
Very welcoming...and spotlessly clean rooms everyday...

Great little apartment! My family of 4 loved it. 50 meters from a pedestrian only street with restaurants. Less than 10 minute walk to see the sunrise over the Acropolis for those with jet lag. Easy 15 minute taxi to the ferries if headed to the islands. Clean, quiet, comfortable, great location, perfect!!

Great location , close to all the major attractions.
You can walk everywhere.
Plenty of places to eat close by.
Clean rooms / apartments.
Good communication with the host.
Would definitely recommend.

A very late review but better late than never.
I have to pay tributes to one of the best places in Athens cause unfortunately they are not many.
Location...one of the greatest,2 steps from Acropolis and Plaka but still away & protected from the hordes of tourists.
Beautiful, spacious rooms and i saw only the ground floor studio so i guess the big room and penthouse are even better.
There is all you need in the apt so you can have a hassle & stress free holiday.
Normal size kitchen with essential cookware,a bottle of wine to accompany your food,spacious bedroom with a comfy bed on the quiet side of the building,not that the Erexthiou street is trafficked but yes, extremely peaceful.
Worth to mention is that i got the best sleep for over a year in this property despite the LED escape lights.
The service of Athens Apart was impeccable and no one,i repeat no one cleans like Tatiana.I've seen it in maximum 4-5 other places out of hundreds i've visited so it is a rare occurrence cleaning with that perfection.
Cleaning every 3d day if i recall it correctly which i think should be the norm.
There was a washing machine on the ground floor and you could take coffee pods in case they finished.
Greek service & hospitality at its finest.
I'd love to return one day soon🙌
P.S:Some paper towel in the kitchen would be a great addition and do something about the main door so it closes without people needing to slam it👍
The apartment is nice, clean,  and well decorated! We are located within an easy walk of the Acropolis and the Plaka neighborhood.  The owner was very helpful and friendly.

This hotel is a gem in the city. It is close to the train station, major tourist locations, and it is spotless clean and roomy. For the price you pay you cannot go wrong with it. Highly recommend! They even have free laundry room for every guest to do laundry. The only thing is in Greece you cannot throw toilet paper down into the toilet! Take this seriously, you do not want to clog your toilet on vacation.

The apartments are located in the very center. Everything is very clean and cozy. The kitchen has everything for cooking (oil, spices, coffee, pasta), and the bathroom also has everything. The owner is friendly, speaks good English. We went by bus to the beach every day. There is an excellent coffee shop nearby. The Acropolis is 800 meters from the apartments.
A little far from the center but nice apartment
Meh..

We stayed in this lovely apartment for 13 nights. It is spacious, with 2 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms, and very clean. The kitchen is well equipped with a full size oven and hob. There is also a washing machine.
The apartment is in a fantastic location, it is within a few minutes walk of the Acropolis Museum and no more than 30 minutes walk from all the tourist attractions in Athens. We walked everywhere but if you wanted to get the tram, metro or bus the stops were minutes away.
Despite such a central location the apartment was very quiet and peaceful.
The owner was very friendly and helpful. If you are looking to stay in Athens this is the perfect place.

It's difficult to decide which of this apartment's attributes is the best, facilities, comfort, location.
It's spacious, 2 bedrooms, plenty of storage and a fully equipped kitchen for those not wanting to eat out every night. The lounge has a small balcony overlooking the street and the plethora of restaurants and bars starting across the road and yet it was quiet with nothing to disturb sleep.
But, for most people sightseeing in Athens is most important, this was perfect, the Acropolis, Acropolis Museum and all the major attractions on the doorstep no more than 15 minutes stroll away. Plaka, Thissio and Monastiraki areas about the same. With metro, tram and bus stops also on the doorstep everywhere in Athens is easy to get to.
Final a word about the host, Tassos. He is friendly and helpful and dealt with any queries immediately.
I would recommend this place to anyone.

Great location! Comfortable and clean. Gets a little tight if you have 4 people staying in the one bedroom. Negatives are that bathroom, and especially the shower is tiny, and the washing machine didn't work.
Everything went well, we had a little lack of communication, but luckily we didn't need anything

I've stayed at Neratzia in Februari and June (3 and 6 days). If I return to Athens I do not doubt to book at Neratzia again. I would recommend my friends to go here too. The rooms, including bathroom, are clean and suit your needs. The breakfast, served at the cafe below is perfect. Personnel are perfect. Always helpful and friendly. Keep up the good work :)

Myself and my dad stayed at Neratzia Rooms recently - perhaps the best stay of our trip. The staff were great, very friendly and accommodating, the inclusive breakfast was delicious, and the room was spacious and modern. I would highly recommend to anyone looking to stay in Athens.

A hotel that is an experience, mainly thanks to the owner and staff. Everyone wants to help and provide a good experience. Yanis the owner accompanied us with good advice and a warm attitude. The hotel also has a pleasant bar with a good atmosphere. We received exceptional service and a hug from Yannis even after we left. For sure we will come back again
Merav and Hanit

Well situated and in walking distance to restaurants and shops.
The rooms are as shown on the website. Room service is every day and very good.
Breakfast and it's selection of dishes are excellent
The cafe bar is open late at  night and you need to close the window, not be disturbed by the noise when sleeping. However, you have to close the window because of  the outside temperature/AC

We loved our stay at the Neratzia Rooms! This place is truly a hidden gem. The room was adorable and very clean. The beds were the most comfortable we slept in on our whole trip through Italy and Greece. Plenty of towels, coffee, bottled water, and even a toothbrush if you need it! The owner was so nice and all the employees were accommodating. Violet especially was so kind and on top of everything. She was so helpful! The food at the cafe was incredible! We were very happy with our stay here.

place_name: Greek Aura luxury lodging in Koukaki
review_text:
Excellent downtown apartment located in Koukaki residential area. Koukaki is a pleasant neighborhood on the rise. Many eateries and shops nearby. All major archaeological sites within half a walking distance.
The apartment has two bedrooms with double beds, two bathrooms and a living room with kitchen.

place_name: Greek Aura luxury lodging in Koukaki
review_text: We spent 5 days as a family in this apartment. It is very beautiful, clean, modern, equipped with everything. There are 4 of us and there are 2 bedrooms and two bathrooms. The kitchen is open in the living room, fully equipped and fully functional. The large TV and the very comfortable sofa complete the living area. The area is residential and quiet, convenient for reaching the archaeological sites of Athens on foot. It is definitely a place to have a wonderful holiday in Athens.

Small but very welcoming apartment, quite clean and quiet.
Well connected to the center and a stone's throw from the acropolis.
It is very well located, design and comfort. I just think it lacks a little in terms of cleanliness.
Excellent location and the apartment very well equipped 🫶

place_name: Rovertou Gali 3-5 by Athens Concept Residences
review_text:
Absolutely ideal for a stay in Athens
Great location and Nikos was helpful after booking until the day we left. Helped arrange a taxi for when we departed (left early in the morning, so didn't want to risk uber etc) More than enough food for breakfast, teas, coffees. Supermarket across the road.
Really clean.
Highly recommended

place_name: Rovertou Gali 3-5 by Athens Concept Residences
review_text:
We stayed at this super convenient place for 4 days located 10 mins walk from Acropolis and minutes from the pedestrian streets with amazing busy sit down restaurants.

The place was very clean, beds smell clean, modern apartments, and very convenient.

The best part is Jenny the manager who’s is so genuine, kind, super nice and goes way out of her way to make your stay comfortable. She even walked 15 mins with us to show an amazing restaurant to eat, kept in touch, suggesting interesting places to visit for our first time in Athens and made our stay memorable.
I would highly recommend this place to anyone and we would definitely stay again just to meet up again with Jenny, the best host ever!

The only downside is the road noise at night from the street and the corridors.

place_name: Rovertou Gali 3-5 by Athens Concept Residences
review_text:
We visited Athens and stayed at 3 Rovertou Gali. These are 1 and 2 bedroom apartments located on a side street very close to the Acropolis and town centre. Location wise, this is a winner.

The apartments are very well presented. They are clean and functional. A small kitchen is also afforded for small meals if you feel like staying away from the crowds.

The owners provided basic essentials to ensure we had what we needed for both the bathroom and the kitchen. This was very much appreciated. The washing machine was a good send as we had to travel lite.

One downside was the entrance. It is very badly lit due to lack of maintenance.

Overall, we enjoyed our stay and do recommend this place. Book through the owners to get a better deal.

Every single staff member was obliging and helpful. The restaurant was amazing. The location perfect. The Divani Acropolis hotel had lovely energy..
That iced water station in the entry was the perfect touch after coming in from the scorching Athens heat!

We recently stayed here and loved the location and enjoyed the pool. The room was clean and comfortable and a clear priority. The cleaning and support staff were genuinely friendly and helpful.
The only issue was the attitude and demeanor of many of the staff members; a few front desk staffers, the breakfast servers, and bar tenders. They were short and unfriendly. It was very strange to be received this way.  We were practically yelled at when we shared our disappointment about something. Even though the problem was rectified, I felt embarrassed by the person shaming us for a complaint. The breakfast servers avoided bringing us coffee and getting our own wasn’t an option. Finally, on the last day there was a taxi strike, but we received no notification. An email would have been nice. I’m guessing this review will make little difference, but it honestly left such a bad impression, I was happy to leave.

Big shout out to Bell Captain Michael for saving our trip! We dropped our bag with all our money, credit cards and passports in it. We were 1/2 way up the hill and he came running after us to see if it belonged to someone in our group. Not only did he chase us down but also asked us to verify the contents to confirm it was getting to the rightful owner. Also want to give commendation to Maria in the restaurant who has the sweetest smile and friendly demeanor.  Such a great stay in this beautiful hotel with a very honest and friendly staff. We will stay here every time we return to Greece thanks to them.

Stayed 3 nights and was impressed with this hotel. The most wonderful experience in Athens was the restaurant on the roof of the hotel as the view of the Acropolis at night has to be seen to be believed. Don't go by the website reservations as tables were available even when they weren't shown. We visited this restaurant each of our 3 nights there and received wonderful service even if just asking to have drinks. The photos we took of the Acropolis where so good that friends accused us of using a postcard or the web. It's really that good. Breakfasts at their downstairs restaurant were also excellent. The hotel is very conveniently located to tour both the Acrpolis and the Acropolis museum by foot. I can't recommend it enough.

Michael, the Bell Captain was a highlight of our stay.  Genial and professional, he made us feel more at home and comfortable. Helpful in information about surrounding area. Here’s an asset to your hotel.

Thermis at the desk, an extraordinary ambassador! Welcoming and helpful to an all of us traveling from Hawaii!

Dinning waitstaff is superb!

Truly in the heart of Athens, just blocks from the Acropolis. Our room was spacious considering the size of the hotel, and the service has been outstanding! We arrived well before check in and the hotel staff did what they could to get us in early (our flight landed nearly an hour ahead of schedule). From that point on, the front desk staff attended to all of our needs. This excellent level of service is especially meaningful, as we are on the first leg of our honeymoon. I highly recommend this hotel for its location, quality and service, hope to stay here again on our next visit to Athens!

Hotel is in a great location convenient for the Acropolis site and Acropolis museum, but in a relatively quiet location. Many good bars and restaurants close by. Rooms are comfortable with a high standard of housekeeping. Great lounge/bar area on the ground floor and fabulous roof terrace bar and restaurant with great views of the Acropolis. Service was friendly and extremely helpful.

My husband and I had the pleasure of enjoying The Herodion Hotel on our recent trip to Athens. We had never been to the city before and were spending a short time there before departing for a cruise. Our decision to stay here was based on reviews online and we could not be more impressed. My husband and could not stop singing the Herodions praises, I lost count of how many times he would just sit back and say what a great choice the hotel had been and how much he enjoyed everything about it.
The location could not have been better. A short walk from the Acropolis, located in a quiet area of a vast city just bordering the historic Plaka neighborhood. Metro was located nearby making access to other districts simple.
The hotels rooms were absolutely pristine, with a wonderful shower and comfortable bed. We had the pleasure of booking a room with a view of the Acropolis and enjoyed morning coffees on our balcony. We used the hotels service to book transportation from airport to hotel and arrived before check in but they staff was wonderful. From the moment we arrived and had a member of staff rush down to the street to help with our luggage, to the front desk securing our bags while we took lunch and explored the area prior to check in.
We were told we could also utilize the hot tubs (which have fantastic views as well) even before our room was ready if we liked.
We had lunch in the conservatory room which was delicious and dinner at the rooftop restaurant which was probably the most delicious food I had on my vacation.
When we left the hotel we were able to once again enjoy the ease of having the welcoming staff arrange our transport to the Port.
We can not recommend this Hotel more, from cleanliness to hospitality there was no fault to be seen and we plan to stay here on any future stays as well as recommend it to family and friends.

We had a great stay at this hotel. Very friendly and welcoming staff. The room we had was great and could fit a crib in for our little one. Easy walk to Acropolis (5mins) and plenty of restaurants and bars close by. They also offer a great breakfast in the morning. Would definitely stay here again.

The hotel staff have been wonderful the entire stay. Every staff member is warm and exceptionally friendly. No task is too difficult and every request is met with a smile. The hotel restaurant on the roof top has phenomenal views of the Acropolis, with a spacious rooftop bar and restaurant. Our party of 4 enjoyed gourmet food, with each dish looking more like art than the last. The rooms are stylish and well maintained. We would highly recommend the Herodion hotel, we could not fault it. Overall, an exceptional experience.

We haven't even left and have voted this accommodation to be spectacular.
Homely, clean, and well equipped.
The view, what can I say, but mind-blowing.

We were very happy with the apartment. It was very clean and we felt like home from the second we came in. The beds are very comfy and the furnishing is complete. Just the bathroom is not on the highest standard and worn.(but still very clean.) The owner is very lovely and willing to help with any problems.

We stayed here for one night only, the day prior to boarding a cruise. The location is perfect, so close to the Acropolis and Plaka.  There are also plenty of restos and cafes, and it's right beside a souvenir shop too. They are very responsive as well. We also arranged our transportation from them (pick-up from airport and to and from Pireaeus port) and everything was a breeze. It was overall a great experience dealing with them.
My visit June 2019 was great. This is the best location in Athens if you want to visit the Acropolis. Host is very accommodating—will pick you up at the airport for a reasonable price.
Clean rooms, helpful host, location and view are the best.

Our daughter and friend stayed in an Acropolis view room with balcony to celebrate their combined 30th birthdays (they loved the room!). I emailed the hotel to ask if it was possible to have champagne and glasses waiting for their arrival. The staff were very happy to arrange this - with a note, a little birthday cake and snacks on a lovely tray with the chilled champagne and glasses. The birthday girls were in tears at the lovely surprise and enjoyed the balcony and view with their champagne. Thank you Philippos Hotel for making their stay even more special.

Great location near Acropolis, Museums and La Plaka. Gracious, hardworking and helpful staff, gave restaurant recommendations, scheduled tour and taxi. Excellent Greek breakfast. Stayed here 15 years ago and the quality and value are consistent. Rooftop terrace with direct view of the Acropolis, wine available.

Amazing location, spotless clean, but missing a few basic amenities (e.g. plug socket on only 1 side of bed, no plugs at desk or light around mirror), staff were really friendly and very helpful. Rooftop space gives fantastic views

Great hotel in a central position close to the entrance to the acropolis. Rooms were clean and all seemed to have a balcony . The buffet breakfast was very good with a wide selection, including Greek cakes and yoghurt. The only slight downside was the smell from the drain in the bathroom but hey it's Greece and the drains always smell in Greece

This hotel was amazing. we were not meant to stay, but after getting late from the aeroport ( 5 in the morning ) we went inside asking for a room ( even tho we knew it was nearly impossible. The man saw that I was really anxious for being outside in the dark, and let us stay. It was really nice and also, we were so close from the Acropolis that we could go walking. Also the hotel super clean.

Huge apartment for couples or families, with beautiful decoration and all the necessary equipment you need for your daily needs. The location is obviously the big plus of the apartment as it is a stone's throw away from the Acropolis museum and the Parthenon.

It was great the owner accommodated us with storing our luggage at the main office and the when we arrived to the apartment greater us there. Showed us around the place and the use of the home. From the Washing machine to the hot water for baths. We did report to the owner that the door nobs to several rooms had a issue of falling off or the latch spinning. They responded right away but we let them know that we can work with it until we leave and then they can come fix it for someone else. Over all it was a great place close to many tourists locations.

It was like staying at a 5-star hotel, but it was an entire apartment, walking distance from the Acropolis!

Akis went above and beyond for us. Everything was perfect. He was very kind, provided food, big TV with netflix in 5 different languages, big beds perfectly made, plenty of towels, air condition of course and the apartment was bigger than expected!

Couldn't recommend this place enough and even more so for the customer service. Thank you Aki, we will be back again!
Great location.

The apartment has an ideal location, convenient parking - there is a paid parking lot next to it, and free parking on the street. It is clean and very welcoming. The host is kind and responsive, he had left us breakfast in the fridge.

Excellent well-kept and clean apartment with every service, excellent location for visiting the city. Akis is a fantastic owner, kind and provided us with all the necessary information. I highly recommend it to everyone and if I were to return I would have no doubts. Thank you and congratulations again Akis 👍👍👍

Our stay at the Acropolis Penthouse was wonderful!  Yannis was a wonderful host - friendly, helpful, knowledgable.  He helped with transfers to/from airport and the ferry, as well as recommending some nightlife places.  The Penthouse was very clean, well stocked and very comfortable.  Great location (15 minute walk to the Acropolis).  We highly recommend this place for anyone visiting Athens.

Absolutely our best choice for the trip.  If you travel with more than one party (or needing more than one room) to Athens, you don't need to look further.  The place is stylish and centrally located; within 5 minutes walking you will be at the entrance to the museum which is right next to the entrance of Acropolis.  We also found the supermarket, cafe, and bakery (supposedly the oldest in the city) all within one minute of walk as well.

We even bought leather goods right at the street floor of the building.  So convenient!

Thank you for a beautiful property in Athens. The apartment is centrally located close to tourist attractions, dining and transportation. Three large bedrooms, two full baths, and a great kitchen, living room, and dining area. Excellent customer service at the property to ensure everything went well. Highly recommended.

The five star reviews tell it all. The  host, Yannis, was very helpful. The space was modern and accommodated five guests with ease. There were two full bathrooms for convenience. Everything worth seeing was within walking distance.  The restaurants in the area provided excellent meals. We were very happy this was our first stop in Athens.

We had a wonderful stay at this penthouse. It was centrally located with great cafes and restaurants nearby and  was in walking distance to the Acropolis and the heart of the city. Our host, Yainnis, was especially great at making us feel comfortable, giving recommendations for the city, and being responsive to our needs. I would strongly recommend this to anyone looking to stay in Athens!
Modern well-equipped apartment. With a good price-performance ratio. Please note: The apartment is in the basement. (basement)

Without hesitation, a wonderful place to stay for a few days in Athens. Nearby stores, shops and interesting restaurants. Also, historical sites such as the Acropolis and the Acropolis museum. Clean temperature controlled for those hot Athens days!
Great Airbnb! Close to the major heritage sites.  Literally 2 min walk from the acropolis museum and not much farther to Metro or the acropolis itself.  Very new, secure, and we'll managed.
Amazing location. Great room. We had a hot tub on the roof with a stellar view of the acropolis

WOW 🤩, you guys treated me like royalty and your entire building is state-of-the-art!
Centrally located at the foothills of the Acropolis & Parthenon! Close to all the city center of Athens has to offer and lovely restaurant and shop owner neighbors!

If you are going to Athens, book THIS place!!
Rooftop view is breathtaking : )
Visited some friends who stayed there. The rooftop has an amazing view of the Acropolis

An impeccable spacious room with a kitchen and living room. The location is excellent near the center of Athens, you are next to the Museum, the Acropolis, the plaque and the Syntagma, of course the room has a perfect view of the Parthenon. But what I want to focus on is that our room it smelled musky and has a great layer. For sure, whenever I go to Athens it will be my first choice, it's hard to find such a neat apartment in the center and in a quiet area
Great place. Balcony overlooking the Pantheon. The facility is well-kept and tastefully done. I recommend it 👍

If you’re lucky enough to hear about this place, simply stay here. It’s a wonderful apartment, beautiful, exceptional value for money, peaceful and located perfectly for Plaka, Acropolis and 2 mins from the metro. It’s the place to stay in Athens.
Super
Close to all.

Liked · The apartment was very nicely furnished, tasteful, simple, very clean. Although in the center, it was very quiet. The location is excellent. Communication with the host was very easy. We will definitely choose it again next time for our stay in Athens.
Excellent stay. Safe location and great accommodations, I highly recommend.
Excellent accommodation, impeccable aesthetics, spotlessly clean and with smart applications.  Just 1 minute from the Acropolis Museum.  I highly recommend it.
Nice, central apartment and location.

one of the BEST places i have stayed,
owner was very polite, helpful and welcoming.
the place was superb, clean, tidy, comfy, had a nice atmosphere.
we could feel like home or better. its in the center of Athina and near to every place which facilitated us to go around and explore without loosing much time. everything is in reach by walking. just 2 mins from the Museum and from the entrance to Acropolis.
Owner did welcome us in a way that no one ever has, they are so polite and educated that you feel like a king/queen.

great location, nice large appartment.
the towels smell by cigaretts (maybe stored in smokers room), wifi did not found even after restart

Perfect romantic getaway at Kinglin Luxury Living - A must for couples!

From start to finish, our stay at Kinglin Luxury Living in Athens was absolutely perfect. As a couple visiting Athens, we were looking for a place that would offer both comfort and convenience, and Kinglin Luxury Living exceeded all our expectations. The rooms were spotlessly clean, creating a cozy and inviting atmosphere for us to return to after our daily adventures.

What really made our stay memorable was the staff's friendliness and their constant availability online, which ensured that any request or query we had was promptly and efficiently addressed. Their warm hospitality made us feel truly welcomed and cared for. They even arranged a birthday cake for my girlfriend. It was beautiful and delicious!

The location of Kinglin Luxury Living is ideal for couples seeking both tranquility and accessibility. Situated in a quiet, peaceful area, it provided a serene retreat away from the bustling city. Yet, it was close enough to all the essential sights and experiences Athens has to offer, making our exploration of the city effortless and enjoyable.

We highly recommend Kinglin Luxury Living to any couple visiting Athens. Whether you're here to soak in the historical sites, indulge in the local culture, or simply enjoy a romantic getaway, this place will undoubtedly add to the magic of your visit. We can't wait to return!

Amazing, what can I say. From the first class location, walking distance to the Acropolis and Museum, to the cobble stone streets with restaurants at Plaka and the best frappe at Avila Coffee a 5 min walk away. Modern rooms, quality finishes, beautiful bathrooms. Effie and the team are very hospitable, they can book your tours, suggest restaurants and promptly answer any questions. The rooms are very modern. Beautiful finishes and lovely bathrooms. The rooms are serviced daily and the breakfast boxes with orange juice, cheese & spinach pies, muffins and biscuits are a lovely touch. The 7 of us have been so comfortable during this heatwave, winter would have its own beauty too. Thanks so much Kinglin.

Amazing place to stay and enjoy Athens. Luxury interiors, very kind personnel and fresh snacks every morning 😉 Walking distance to the most important places like Akropol an Akropol Museum.
I highly recommend!

The suites were perfect for a multi-day stay. The bed was very comfortable, the living room and bathroom were very functional. The small kitchen would not work well for cooking. But best of all was Effie who provided great service and helped us with airport and ferry transfers and restaurant recommendations and reservations. I suggest visiting Meno Male, the Italian place right on the corner if you desire a break from Greek food, which is excellent.

An absolute pleasure was to stay at this hotel. Luxurious and comfy space, with modern deco. And the hotel is placed right next to the key landmarks like Acropolis museum, shopping streets and is surrounded by cafes, cocktail bars, restaurants/ tavernas.
Special mention to the host, Effie for the welcome and amazing local recommendations.
Great place to stay at for a few nights in Athens.
Everything was spotlessly clean very happy.... And with the host there was communication when I needed him, he answered the phones..... And of course I will prefer him again

Very satisfactory apartment, very comfortable and in the heart of the center. Only minus the wooden balcony doors that, as long as the restaurants are open, let a lot of noise inside. We will definitely prefer it again.

I cannot say enough good things about our stay at Urban Stripes. Our stay was enjoyable and effortless. We had clear communication with our host the whole time. We landed in Athens around 0900. Our check in time wasn’t until 1400, but we received a message from our host the day before that we could leave our bags at the hotel once we arrived so we could explore Athens without them while we waited for our room to be ready. We received a call as soon as our room was ready (about an hour before our official check in time). When we arrived our host gave us a map with sights on it that’s common for tourist, she took the time to highlight areas and routes that we should take and areas to avoid! The room itself was great! It was VERY clean and VERY spacious for the two of us. It was a 2 level apartment with a living area, kitchen, dinning room table, balcony, and restroom downstairs. Upstairs was the bedroom, closet hallway that lead to the restroom with a shower and toilet, and an additional balcony! The location was extremely convenient as well. We were just enough out of the super crowded area that our room was quiet but a quick 5 minute power walk to the area surrounding the acropolis with all the cute shops and delicious restaurants. 10/10 would recommend Urban Stripes to anyone staying in the Athens! I would book here in a heartbeat when I return!

Very pleasant stay, I definitely recommend it.
Great communication with the host, clear instructions for self check-in, as we arrived late on a Sunday night.
They offer you a city center map along with great tips on what to visit and how to get there.
Beautiful view over the Acropolis, especially at night when the site gets illuminated.
Nice neighborhood and safe for parking your car, in case you need it.
Very clean with nice breakfast served in the room.
Close to the Acropolis - 10 minutes walk, close to city center and surroundings.

Everything was perfect. Maria did a great job with the check in and check out, she helped with transportation and recommendations and she is very kind. The apartment is amazing, very modern, comfortable and it has the most amazing view of the Parthenon. Location is superb, walking distance to every point of interest and great restaurants are located nearby.  For sure i will return to this apartment next time in Athens.

We booked this hotel literally at the last minute while we were in transit, and the host made sure everything was taken care of within an hour. By the time I landed in Athens, they had given me all the instructions for check in. I am honestly impressed by their quality of service! Location Location Location! This property is 5 minutes walking distance from Acropolis museum and probably 15 mins from Acropolis hill. There are lots and lots of restaurants within 5 minutes walk, and Plaka is within walking distance as well. The rooms are spacious and clean. The area felt safer as well. The office is downstairs where they hold your bags after check out. We were in a different hotel at the end of our trip, but we literally missed being here instead! Definitely recommend this property!
We had a wonderful two bedroom apartment with a view of the Acropolis. Rooms were clean and comfortable. Location was close to everything. Definitely would like to stay here again.

place_name: A skip away from the Acropolis museum by Athenian Homes
review_text:
Perfect place to rent and stay in Athens. Fully equipt appartement, with the option to cook yourself or go down to one of the resturants closeby.
Easy to find the key and get inn.
There are some small markeds close to the place to go food shopping.

place_name: A skip away from the Acropolis museum by Athenian Homes
review_text: Clean and comfy

place_name: A skip away from the Acropolis museum by Athenian Homes
review_text: The apartment is super central, located on the first floor by stairs. It has all the comforts. Anna gave us directions and was very kind. I recommend it for couples traveling to Athens. They are going to enjoy it.
A dream vacation in a luxurious and luxurious apartment with an amazing view and location .

We stayed here for a long weekend break and it was absolutely perfect for us. We got the studio as all the other rooms were full and it is a small room but perfectly functional for a couple. Bed was very comfy, room and bathroom modern and clean.

Breakfast was served outside on in the seating area just outside the hotel lobby and served from one of the bars. We had perfect weather for this but not sure how that would be if it were cold and wet. Also there were a few birds that kept flying in to finish off peoples left overs when they were too lazy to move their plates to the clearing area.
Breakfast was great for us with a choice of hot or cold food and drinks.
The staff were very polite and helpful too.

Location is excellent as it’s just a five minute walk into the Main Street next to the acropolis museum. Loads of amazing restaurants and little shops right on your doorstep.

Loved this place and great pricing. Staff explained everything and were friendly upon arrival. Amazing views from room of the acropolis. Clean and spacious room. Location fantastic to walk to many sights. Loved our stay here. Only downside was the cleaners make noise in morning (I was trying to sleep in well past 9am) but they have a job to do and I am probly the only one sleeping that late 😅 great place!

Sooooo, interesting experience. We needed 5 beds, so our room had a Hostel feel. 1 bedroom with queen/bunk, and 2 single beds in the studio area. 1 bathroom, which isn't ideal with 3 teenagers.
Staff were great. Location was excellent. That's why we stayed here. Within walking (10 min) to Acropolis entrance.
Parking was an experience. Drove around block of hotel 4x until I could park.

Almost a perfect stay! The hotel truly has an incredible location, offering quick access to a lot of restaurants, and sites. The staff was very kind and the breakfast was good.

Unfortunately,we got bitten by a bedbug which we found and killed on our bed during our second night at the hotel.

Hoping the administration takes the necessary measures to inspect and sanitize the rooms properly. And a reminder to travellers to always check your rooms no matter where you go.

Wonderful position at less than 5 minutes walk from the Akropolis and the lively nearby area where very good restaurants are waiting for you.
Rooms are a bit small but clean and comfortable and the bathroom is bigger than you would expect.
Staff is very kind and gentle and shows a genuine will to help make your stay better.
Defnitely the hotel of choice for short stays in Athens.

Amazing apartment with fantastic views of Acropolis.
Very comfortable bed, great size TV, nice bathroom, friendly host, hot tub, sunny balcony etc etc. What more can you ask for :)
Excellent Apt, great location and extremely good service, one of the best places to stay in Athens
The apartment is fully equipped right next to the Acropolis museum, I will definitely be back soon.
Great apartment with an amazing host who gave the most helpful list of recommendations around the city.
Anny, thank you for the best experience and excellent customer service, it felt like home while we stayed there, very comfortable and close to everything, I would recommend this location 100%
Nice apartnent in proximity of all most important historical places

Very clean every room...
Nice owner
I would like to come again.  It takes about 10 minutes to walk to the Acropolis
Highly recommended!!

Ultra-luxury apartment in the heart of Athens with all the comforts a customer could be looking for! As for the hostess, my anny, we have no words for you, your kindness and help are indescribable! Thank you very much for everything!!!! You made our stay great!

Anny and her colleagues were amazing. The place was in a prime location to everything.  The place was super clean and anny was so accommodating.  They even arranged for a van to pick 6 of us back to the airport at a much better rate.  They were seriously the nicest people to rent from.  Anny will send you great recommendations for restaurants as well. I could not say enough about her and this place that we rented

Beautiful and modern apartment
Anny and Emilia made us feel welcome.
Walking distance to  restaurants and Acropolis.
We really enjoyed our stay.

We traveled as a party of 2 without our kids in June for the first time to celebrate our anniversary and we splurged on the Double Athenian Panorama Room. Wow, the wall of windows that overlooked the Temple of Zeus was breathtaking day and night. The room was very spacious and clean. It had a very comfortable bed, a sofa and seating area, a desk area, a large wardrobe, robes and slippers, a large marble bathroom with a luxurious double tub (which I’d never seen before), and a flat screen tv. It was perfect for our 3-night getaway in Athens. It was convenient to all the central sites, as we did a self-guided 12-hour walking tour and never needed a taxi. The breakfast included was good and the rooftop terrace restaurant was quiet and convenient. The staff were pleasant and helpful when we needed them, which we really didn’t except for a transfer to the airport. We didn’t try room service or the pool so we can’t comment on those amenities. We got everything we paid for regarding our accommodation here and couldn’t be more pleased. A great value. We’d stay in this room again in a heartbeat!

A lovely hotel in a perfect location, just 10 minutes walk to Acropolis southern entrance. Good sized, very clean rooms, nice sized bathrooms, with shower over bath. Top floor bar overlooking Acropolis and the park. Breakfast was good with lots of choice and good service, also located in restaurant on top floor to enjoy the views. Would certainly return here.

The room was clean and comfortable. It was huge and a very nice room. The bathroom was amazing. We enjoyed our time there. The breakfast was part of the room and delicious. The staff was helpful. The view is good but not as amazing as the hotel photos. The area is decent but I wasn't impressed with most of Athens.

Large well appointed rooms with big bathrooms.  Everyone at the hotel was friendly and eager to offer assistance. The rooftop bar and restaurant provided lovely views and friendly service. The bar did their best on cocktails....stick to the menu and you won't be disappointed.

Elegant hotel with fine furniture appointments. Satisfactory rooms, neat, clean, luxurious towels. Room lighting was low, no tea or coffee provided in the room, cental temperature control with delayed response. Helpful staff. Elegant roof top dining with panoramic views.
Central location, everything is easily accessible! It is a bit noisy even at night due to the heavy traffic.

Great place and super value. Make sure you spend some time in the evening on the balcony to see lit up Zuess Temple and Parthenon. Walk to both. Nice pizza and breakfast place next door. AC works and is quiet. TV is good. Comfortable beds. Large family room.

Best view you can buy at this price! I got the middle apartment and i spent much more time at the accommodation  then I normally would in a city break just because the terrace is amazing. Very clean and top location in a safe part of the city center. The bathtub needs some work. Highly reccomend

This was by far the worst experience I ever had!!!
First day of arrival as I was in the room 4 strangers entered the room at 10p.m. Imagine!! The owner sent the entrance code of my room to other people! It's a matter of privacy and security!
Of course I immediately informed the owner that I would leave as I could not feel safe anymore.
The owner did not even mention of returning funds for this huge mistake. I requested a refund and never received anything. She does not even reply to my calls and messages.
Not professional behaviour at all! Very disappointing!

Don't waste your money!!

We stayed for 4 nights and it was awful. Firstly, you expect privacy when paying for accommodation, however we had workmen on our "private balcony/terrace" who peered into our bedroom and lounge area every day. This meant we had to keep the shutters constantly closed so the "view" was pointless. They also started work at 8am every day and were very noisy, banging, leaning ladders against our door and shouting to each other - the chance of a relaxing lie in on holiday was impossible.

Not only this but they spilt drinks and food on the balcony. (Photos attached)

The shower also did not work. There was no shower holder and the bathtub had no plug which worked. There were no curtains around the shower either so you had to wash yourself sitting down in the bath (which had peeling paint all over the base) to shower whilst hand holding the shower head. (Photos and video attached)

The shower also turns scolding hot when someone uses the tap/toilet and this burnt me.

The images of the bathroom were nothing like it and it was very below the standards of a hostel, let alone a booking.com place.
The company offered us a partial refund but they have not paid this and I wouldn't have even paid that half for the experience we had.

Absolutely awful.

We felt like we had the best view in town! Lovely apartment with massive terrace. Def recommended ♡

Liked · Stunning traditionally decorated apartment at the top floor of lovely building in perfect location in city centre. Massive corner terrace facing 2directions - both stunning views - sunset of Acropolis ♡ Big dining table on the terrace. Small functional kitchenette, 2good AC units in apartment. Modern bathroom.

Disliked · Lack of storage space to open suitcases. One of the advertised photos wasn't the actual apartment we stayed in.

If you want a stress-free holiday, Living Yard Acropolis is the place to stay. We have some small issue with the Air conditioner, and the hotel help changed our room right away. Plus, very nice location and helpful staff.

Great hotel with an amazing location!!
But the staff is what makes this review a solid 5/5
Really professional and caring.
I highly recommend them

I strongly recommend, close from city center, everything close by/walking distance, couldn't be better located.
Hotel is small but if you plan to go visit more than enough. It is clean, modern, well maintained and friendly staff.

Great location to visit the city.
The Room was beautiful, cosy and clean. Very quiet at night.
Bathroom is very clean and functional.
The staff is very friendly and careful.
We Will definitly Come Back here for our next visit to Athènes.
Perfekt locaition between Acropolis and the Trainstaiton.

I was one of the first guests to stay at this beautifully renovated apartment! Very stylish, impeccably clean, absolutely comfortable, and fully equipped! The location is amazing: you are steps away from everything! The host is very polite, knowledgeable, and accommodating! I highly recommend it for business and leisure alike!

Nice and spacious. There is an AC unit in every room. You can kinda see Zeus's pillars from the balcony. It's within walking distance from the Acropolis and the metro. When you walk out of the front door go right and take your first right, it will lead to the metro or keep walking to the Acropolis. There is a pizza place across the street and grocery store on the street to the metro. They even have Netflix for you for free.

This apartment is just a stone's throw from Acropolis, so you can go by walking almost everywhere (museum, Plaka, Syntagma). It has been completely renovated and is equiped with comfortable and cosy furniture while the decoration is really unique and fabulous! The host also is very caring, available and helpful!
Huge disappointment! So noisy we couldn't sleep at all,  not very clean and everything (bed and sofas) extremely uncomfortable. Don't trust the pictures! Not worth the price.
Lovely and newly renovated apartment, perfect location, next to everything: sites, restaurants, metro. Highly recommended.
One of the best apartments I have stayed in Athens. And John *aka* Nakos is such a gentleman and awsome host
It was indeed luxury and the location was Perfect! Highly recommended!
Nice owner with good hospitality
Overall good

A very good apartment to host a group of friends/colleagues in its 3 rooms. All the rooms are more than spacious, and the fact that they have high ceilings gives a special touch and nobility to their spaciousness.
The beds are "imperial" both in size and in comfort, and of course with spotless linens. The bathroom is modern and spotlessly clean, with all the necessary personal care materials. Only after a shower, some little water came out on the floor from the shower which is not completely enclosed with a cubicle.
The living room was comfortable, but the oval dining room table was more convenient for our late-night review of work, accompanied by fine wine.
In the kitchen there were all the necessary utensils, espresso coffee maker, toaster, etc. as well as fridge freezer, electric stove and dishwasher.
The "minimal" decoration is pleasant and the lamps are beautiful.
The view towards the Pillars of Olympian Zeus, and Lycabettus in the background is excellent. Good window frames do not allow street noise to pass into the interior of the apartment to the extent that it disturbs.
Final conclusion: a wonderful accommodation, in a privileged location, overlooking one of the great monuments of Athens, suitable to accommodate a group of 6 people in particular comfort and at a reasonable price.
Many thanks to Giannis, who, among other things, was interested in a mechanical problem with our car, he even called us after we had left to find out if the damage had been repaired!!!

The property is absolutely pretty and central to everywhere in Athens. HOWEVER, reason for 1 star is due to the frustration and inconvenience caused by the check-in and check-out process. During check-in, there was no one at the property, you will need to contact them for them to make their way to the property to hand over the key - this was still acceptable.
The major frustration came when we checkout and left the luggages at the property for them to transfer it to their storage place as they told us that late check-out at 12.30pm was not doable which is understandable. We had to change our airport transfer to pick us up from a different address instead. We left the property at 9.40am and texted the guest relation person ASTERIS that we will pick it up from the storage at 12.30pm. At 12.40pm when we went to the storage place to pick up our luggages, there is no office and we had to call ASTERIS again. That’s when I was told that our luggages were still at the property. When I asked why was I not informed earlier so that we could have changed the airport transfer pick up, he simply brush it off by saying that it was a busy day for them and luggage storage was a free service. If you had bothered, it will be a simple WhatsApp or Call. We had an unpleasant argument over the phone while standing around waiting for our airport transfer to be sorted as the storage place and property was 20mins drive away although walking takes around 7mins. But imagine us having to walk to the property and drag the luggages back to the storage just to board the airport transfer, not to mention that we are already running late for the pickup. This is an absolutely unpleasant experience made worse by ASTERIS. If you can’t manage your guests, don’t be a guest relation person, you are supposed to help solve an issue and not argue to insist your point.
Thankfully, Harry, another staff of the property came and spoke with our airport transfer driver who eventually spoke to his company and mange to drive us back to the property to pick up the luggages. We arrived at the airport late but managed to still catch our flight. I will definitely think twice about staying at this property in future.

Amazing hotel. Well-decorated with modern architecture. The living room and master bedroom offer the view of Temple of Zeus, the rooftop also provided Acropolis view with temple of Zeus on the other side. Centrally located, close to Metro and shopping area. Fully-equipped and well-prepared with full amenities. The host is very friendly, well-communicated and super helpful. Definitely I will stay here again when I revisit Athens.

This hotel was AMAZING. We stayed here on a family vacation in a group of seven. The entire apartment is beautifully furnished, easily one of the nicest places that I have EVER stayed. The best part is that it's in walking distance to many of the local attractions (Plaka, Acropolis etc.). The property manager George was very attentive and when my mom fell ill towards the end of the trip brought medicine for her.

Highly recommend!

Awesome apartment in Athens.
Highly recommend. We just stayed 4 days in Athens, and this was great with 3 bedrooms and 2 1/2 bath apartment. John was very helpful and nice. Couldn't ask for better location and place to stay.
Beautiful apartment with lots of space for a large family or group of friends, exceptional location and top level comfort! Would absolutely stay here again, especially for the reasonable price!!!

Great hotel and great location. This was recommended by a Youtuber. The location is in close proximity to Acropolis, with beautiful views, comfortable beds, and just enough space for 2. The restaurant adds an elevated aspect to the whole experience. Their free breakfast is amazing, they offer so many fresh and delicious options to include fruit, pastries, eggs, ham, etc. Going to the restaurant in the evening allows for a beautiful view. Would def. stay here again!

Mixed Feelings with Great Potential

During my recent week-long stay at the hotel, I experienced a mix of highs and lows that shaped a unique visit. Here are my thoughts, which I hope will be helpful for both potential guests and the hotel management.

Pros:

1.	Exquisite Dining: The restaurant is truly exceptional. The culinary team offers delightful meals that rival the city’s finest. I highly recommend trying the dinner menu for a pleasant surprise!
2.	Convenient Location: While not centrally located, the hotel is well-situated with easy access to key attractions, making exploration convenient.
3.	Accommodating Management: The hotel manager deserves special mention for their dedication and kindness. They consistently went out of their way to assist guests, adding a personal touch to the experience.

Cons:

1.	New Section Feels Disconnected: I was accommodated in a newly opened section of the hotel located in a separate building. Unfortunately, this area lacked the traditional hotel vibe and felt more akin to an Airbnb. Given the price, I believe guests might find better value elsewhere.
2.	Reception Challenges: Interaction with the reception staff was often less than pleasant, marked by a confrontational attitude that detracted from the welcoming atmosphere typically expected at a hotel.

In conclusion, while [Hotel Name] has several strengths, particularly in its dining and management, improvements in staff hospitality and the integration of new sections could enhance the overall guest experience. I look forward to seeing how this hotel evolves and addresses these mixed aspects in the future.

I have mixed feelings about my stay at this hotel. On the positive side, the staff was friendly and welcoming, which helped improve our experience. However, several issues left us disappointed. First, the pictures on the website were misleading. While the metropolis was visible from the rooftop, the location itself wasn’t as romantic as it seemed online.

The room size was another drawback—they were really, really small. While that might not have been a huge issue, the cleanliness definitely was. There was noticeable fungus in the bathroom, which made us uncomfortable. We were only staying for three nights, so we didn’t focus on it much at first.

What really disturbed us happened on the last day. We had traveled all the way from the U.S. and were exhausted when we first arrived, so we didn’t check if the sheets were clean. During our stay, we spent most days exploring Athens and only realized a major issue on the final morning. As we were packing, we found bloodstains on the side of the bed where my husband had been sleeping. This blood was not ours—my husband checked himself, and I am physically unable to have periods, so it was clearly from a previous guest. It was shocking and unsettling to think my husband had been sleeping on bloodstained sheets for days. When we approached the front desk about it, the receptionist seemed skeptical, almost as if questioning if the blood was ours, which made the situation even more frustrating.

Finding someone else’s blood on our bed was not only gross but also potentially dangerous. It raised concerns about hygiene and safety that shouldn’t be overlooked.

We feel that when we approached the front desk to address what happened, the hotel should have immediately offered a sincere apology. Instead, it felt like they were questioning our account of the situation. At the very least, they should have apologized and offered some kind of compensation, whether a full refund or another gesture to make up for the discomfort. I even considered pushing for a refund, but given how uncomfortable my husband was with the whole experience, we decided to just leave.

No, I don’t recommend this hotel. And, NO! This is not a 4 stars hotel!!!!

Bad experience in this hotel, we were accommodated in an uninhabited building 50 meters from it.
We arrived at the hotel at 9 pm, we liked the entrance and reception and the nightmare began as soon as the room was paid: the employee told us that the room was outside the hotel but that we did not worry, that it was safe. They accompanied us and our surprise was that it was an uninhabited building, without lights to climb, the broken elevator, there were no cameras ... See my photos as a test that reaffirms what I say. It was scary if you go up just because it seemed like there were squatters in the building.
Well, after 3 hours of discussion we got us to give us a 20% discount. They wanted to ignore everything and the worst of all is that a room is reserved and as they see fit you are lodged inside or outside the same hotel.
Luckily, we stayed only 1 night. The room was new but where it was was insecure. Breakfast quite well and its helpful employees. The best thing about the hotel is its location, it is located right next to the Plaka neighborhood and the Parthenon of Athens: the neighborhood is safe but it passes a main avenue where you have to be careful to cross it if you don't want to be hit by cars.
I don't recommend it, be careful with this hotel.

A really good place, super comfortable and the rooms are super good compared to other places in terms of space, the views are super nice, I recommend that if you can, you try to get a room on the 6th floor with a path to the Acropolis.

Apartment is very nice, spacious and clean, the location is really perfect!
Taxi was arranged, Konstantinos was great, helped us on our way right away, thank you!

I am posting this for my wife, who is not great with technology but wanted to be sure to tell the world how much she loved this hotel.
She says:
I can hardly begin to say how wonderful the Athenaeum Hotel is.  Yes, it has everything you expect from a five-star hotel -- great service, fabulous food, a beautiful room with a great view, and every amenity. But it is so much more, because of the beautiful spirit of everyone I met who was working there.  It all felt like family, and I was as sad to leave as I would be to leave my family after a visit that felt like it was too short.  Congratulations for creating such a very special place -- and THANK YOU!

I stayed at The Athenaeum Hotel in September 2024, and it was a most excellent experience.  I have traveled for 50 years in my business and stayed in many hotels around the world.  Big one and little one, very fancy ones and modest ones.  Many good ones for sure.  However, I have to say that my stay at the Athenaeum Hotel was a truly a top-notch experience, everything was lovely, quality rooms and amenities, but the quality of the staff was amazing. Everyone from the gentlemen who carried my bags into the reception, the gentlemen and women at reception, the women who kept my room beautiful and the staff in the restaurant.

Our stay at this hotel in Athens was absolutely incredible. The staff, the food and the rooms were fantastic. But the highlight was the view from the restaurant… Wow. And if that wasn’t good enough, I forgot my watch and Bill… The wonderful man that he is… Took the time to send it to the next hotel that we are staying at. I truly give this hotel 10 1/2 out of 10. Sincerely.Hank Arnoldussen Canada

Great stay in Athens - the rooms are clean and well appointed. The rooftop breakfast is good. What really shines at this restaurant is the staff who are amazing. Every person we came in contact with went above and beyond in every interaction.

I've traveled to Greece several times, but this is by far the best experience I've ever had. The staff is extremely helpful and very flexible. The hotel is quiet, comfortable, and close to center. The man at the reception, named Andres, is very cooperative and polite. Even the restaurant staff is very friendly, the food is delicious with plenty of options.

Service was impeccable, rooms were clean and beautiful. We stayed in a temple of Zeus view room. We wished that the balcony had some furniture, but other than that, our stay was truly amazing. The FOOD is so SO incredible!!!!! Breakfast buffet was to die for and we had a dinner as well which was just amazing.
The hotel is also within a 5 minute walk of the Acropolis and Acropolis museum, along with many shops and restaurants. we will definitely return next time we are in Athens. Thank you for a lovely stay!

First place visited off the plane and it was wonderful.

The staff is amazing.  One of our group was missing luggage, so we congregated on the rooftop bar to commiserate, and after some beer and ouzo and amazing views of the Acropolis, all our worries were far away.

I can't say enough how wonderful and accommodating the staff is.  (I would say 5 stars ++)

Breakfast was delicious with a wide range of options.  Eaten in the rooftop patio...again, what a view!

Perfectly located for exploring.  The Acropolis is near as is the Parliament, shops and other landmarks.

It was a wonderful stay

I recently stayed at this fantastic hotel in the heart of Athens, and it exceeded all my expectations. The highlight of my stay was the breathtaking view of the Acropolis, which could be enjoyed right from the breakfast restaurant. It was truly a sight to behold, especially at night when the Acropolis was beautifully illuminated.

The rooms were incredibly comfortable, spacious, and well decorated. The bed was plush and provided a perfect night's sleep, and the room was equipped with all the modern amenities one could need. The attention to detail in the room's design and cleanliness was good.

Breakfast at the hotel was another standout feature. Served in a charming dining area with equally stunning views of the Acropolis, the breakfast buffet offered a wide variety of delicious options, from fresh fruits and pastries to traditional Greek dishes. The quality of the food was excellent, and the staff were attentive and friendly, ensuring a delightful start to each day.

The hotel's central location made it incredibly convenient for exploring Athens. Major attractions, shops, and restaurants were all within walking distance, allowing me to make the most of my time in this vibrant city.

Overall, my experience at this hotel was exceptional. The combination of the prime location, comfortable rooms, spectacular views, and outstanding breakfast made it a memorable stay. I would highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Athens.

Absolutely top notch from the very beginning. Front desk staff helped us sort out a crooked cab driver. Rooms and location were great. Free breakfast at the roof top lounge has some amazing views. Would 100% recommend to anyone.

Book the rooftop restaurant for dinner!!! Please pleeee please do this as soon as you make a reservation here. Having dinner under the acropolis was our highlight. Not to mention, the food was tasty!! So worth it.

We loved how close it was to everything. Literally everything. You could walk to all the things you needed to see in Athens and you were also close to the train station.

The hotel felt very safe, so did the area.

The staff were amazing. We had the nicest girl
Greet us and the nicest man say good bye to us when we left. They gave us lots of tips not just about Athens,  but for the rest of our trip around the island.

The room was  clean and exactly what we needed.

We were recommend this by a friend and we will keep recommending to more friends.

We stayed here end of August 2024.

We arrived early but were able to get into our room which was appreciated after an overnight flight.  Travel agent booked the Newell Acropolis so I can’t comment on price compared to other hotels in the area.  The receptionists were all very friendly and helpful. Our transportation to the ship port cancelled at last minute so reception immediately got us taxi service (for 8) and ensured the rate was reasonable.
Room was clean, sufficient for our needs, excellent location for walking and sightseeing.  We faced the street and it was quite noisy all night (hence the ear plugs in the bathroom !)  Breakfast was good, interesting selection of foods.

Newel Acropolis is truly deserving of a full 5 stars. From the moment of arrival, the staff is incredibly friendly and helpful, creating a welcoming atmosphere. One of the standout features is the convenience of purchasing city sightseeing bus tickets directly at the hotel. The staff goes above and beyond by assisting in choosing the best ticket option to suit various needs.

The rooms are modern and impeccably clean, equipped with a smart room entry system that adds a touch of sophistication and ease to the stay. A delightful bonus is the availability of free snacks in the room, making the stay even more enjoyable. Each morning, the delicious breakfast offers a good variety of options, setting a positive tone for the day ahead. Additionally, the hotel's location is perfect, being very close to the main attractions and making it easy to explore the city. Overall, Newel Acropolis provides an exceptional experience and is highly recommended for anyone visiting the area.

One of the worst experiences I have ever had in a hotel. Ruined my vacation. The reception staff either wasn't there or, when they were, were very rude and unhelpful. The rooms where dirty and noisy. I was transferred there after an even worst experience on a different hotel of the same company.

This was a great spot for our 48 hours in Athens. I’m really glad we upgraded to a room with a private balcony — they are off the back of the hotel further from the street, so the noise that many complain about wasn’t bad & it was nice to be able to relax on the balcony (we never ended up using the hot tub). The hotel was very accommodating when we arrived early from a redeye and let us into the room right away. We decided not to eat breakfast there, it didn’t look very substantial.

Our recent stay in the Penthouse, admittedly in a heatwave, was marred by an inadequate air conditioning system. The system is for the whole hotel & if one room/suite mistakenly pushes the wrong button, the whole thing shuts down. At 1.00am, with no reception staff and no after hours number to call, we had to try to sleep with the bathroom window & sliding door open to get some relief. The location whilst convenient to all the tourist locations, would have to be at the busiest, noisiest intersection in Athens. The guest wi-if is pretty dodgy, again not what you’d expect in the penthouse!
On the positive side, the staff were amazing! All credit to them; the breakfast was adequate, a little underwhelming overall, the location great, but the inadequacies of air con, wi-fi and noise put a huge dampener on our last few days of holidays, and our impression of Athens. Overall not very good value for money.

I want anyone to read this a a generally positive review, although there are going to be some comments that might make you wonder about it.  It is one of the better apartments I have rented.
First, it has a rather unbecoming entry.  The neighborhood supports a number of motorcycle sales and repairs shops, and the entry is buried among them. Frankly, they are very neat, but some may find them off-putting.  So, take the leap and wait to judge the apartment until you get inside.
The entryway is not updated, and will make you wonder if you have picked the right door.  It in no way reflects the apartment. The elevator is small - two people with luggage  will barely fit.  However, it works.  The apartment is on the third floor  (the entry is floor zero).
The apartment is clean and nicely updated, with good air conditioning, good appliances and nice bathroom fixtures. It has a very modern feel.  The rooms are spacious and comfortable with good storage space.  The furnishings are very nice, with good quality comforters and sheets and towels.
The bathrooms are nice, but for Americans, you need to realize that paper does not go down toilets in Greece - it goes into a small trash can next to the toilet and you dispose of the plastic bagful daily at a large trash dumpster out the front door. If you need to flush, you probably should go to a very expensive hotel.
The description of the apartment describes a 360 degree view - I am not sure of author's geometry knowledge.  There is a very nice balcony that wraps on two sides of the apartment, but in no way is there a 360 degree view.  And, it has to be noted that Kallirois is a busy street - there is a lot of street noise.  My party varied on sensitivity to the noise - some thought it was like Times Square, others not particularly bothered.
The apartment is very nicely located, less than 20 minutes walk from the Acropolis and Acropolis museum, and even closer to the edges of the Plaka - a little bit of a hill climb, but nothing too hard.  There are lots of coffee shops near the apartments; a pharmacy and ATM are directly across the street.  We walked everywhere without problems.
In terms of service, we had one problem - we were assured of early access, and when we showed up, we could not get in, first for two hours, and then for an additional hour - there was clearly a miscommunication about cleaning the apartment.  On a second check in (there was a cruise in between), everything worked out well.  The managers were, for the most part, very responsive to concerns expressed, and were also very helpful around travel arrangements as well.
Don't be deterred by the dinghy building, once you get into the apartment its v nice - newly renovated and comfortable. Responsive service too.

It is a recently and tastefully renovated apartment, and it has all the comforts you need. Large, spacious, with three bedrooms and 3 full bathrooms with shower and screen. Large balcony. It is strategically located: 10 minutes walk from the Acropolis, or 10 minutes from the center of Athens, Monastiraki Square, etc.
Excellent location, a 10-minute walk to Monastryaki Square, a beautiful apartment with its accessories, highly recommended

Amazing location, only 150 meters from Acropoli Metro Station. We felt very secured there because there are 2 security doors (with codes) in the building before you enter your big and comfortable room. Even if the building is located on a very busy road, we didn’t hear a thing which was something that amazed us. Definetely will be our only choice next time we visit Athens.

Very lovely suite - we had the outdoor hot tub suite. It's somewhat tucked away, so it can be a bit confusing at first glance. It's also upstairs on floor 3(?) I believe, as the building houses other apartments/businesses. The only complaint is there wasn't enough toilet paper so we had to buy more. Other than that, it's good value and well air conditioned, comfy and clean :)
Beds and billows are comfortable. Early logges storage was helpful idea. Unfortunately, the floor and bathroom were not clean dusty, and around the bathroom chair were short hair everywhere.
Large room with everything you need. Easy access to Acropolis.

There is not a single thing to complain about. The rooms are very clean. It is modern, fair priced and located in the very center of Athens. The staff was able to fullfill every request (even spontaneously). For example earlier check-in and luggage storage. It's a perfect place to stay when you are planning on doing sightseeing in Athens.

We are in the door to check in for 40min, waiting in the street with luggages because there is noone in reception!! Also there is no phone number to contact them!! Incredible. Very poor service and experience. No information in the booking confirmation. How are we supposed to get in?

Amazing stay, 10/10 views, clean, modern, spacious and only a 5 minute walk to the centre.
Highly recommend!

The rooms are newly furnished. Clean and hygienic. The rooms had a coffee machine, hair dryer, iron and ironing board, TV, microwave oven, kitchen, plates, glasses, forks, knives, spoons and pots. The location of the hotel is very good. Walking distance to everywhere.

Everything was incredible, wonderful apartments, they are new and the room with a jacuzzi was perfect. The girl at the reception is very very friendly. I only have one complaint, check out is 11am and the cleaning girl tried to enter our room at 10am, waking us up without permission when we were still in bed sleeping…

The service was really good, they let us in earlier than allowed and excellent service. The only downside is that we have check out at 11 and the girl tried to enter the room several times even though we told her that she cannot enter at 10... the rest was excellent.

place_name: Athenaeum Acropolis Residence By Elite Signature Collection
review_text: I would not call the accommodation “luxury” - it is basic. However, the location is amazing, the space is nice and comfortable and there is everything you need. Fully equipped kitchen and living area to relax. I would highly recommend!

This loft is wonderful! Very clean and modern, with an amazing porch view of the Acropolis. The owners are extremely helpful and friendly, and they even gave me Greek cookies and a loaf of bread when I showed up.  Coffee and espresso were available in the room as well.

The location is right next to a metro stop and within easy walking distance of all of the shops/attractions/restaurants. Highly recommended if you're looking for a nice place to recharge before hitting the sights of Athens.

the loft is very nice, new, modern and contemporary design and well equipped even for longer stays!! the view is amazing and you can easily chill on the big balkony!! the location is great as it is close to the metro and a walking distance from Acropolis and the city center! moreover the hospitality was very nice and the hostess was very helpfull and always stand by to help us!

Excellent location. There's a lift, saves you climbing stairs to 6th flr. Great views, from top floor. Lots of fab cafes and restaurants nearby. Lovely apartment - comfortable, warm, small kitchen so you can prepare simple meals. Complimentary snacks - toast, honey, nutella. Owner was helpful and available through whatsapp.
Fabulous location and gorgeous apartment. Super clean, very pretty and close to all major attractions in Athens.
Beautiful apartment. Well situated. Only negati e is a couple flights of stairs to carry luggage not accessible by elevator. Staff very pleasant and helpful.
Very central, everything we needed and affordable. Will be comingback for sure

A big scammer made us save in Villas the house and took out the Virgin Mary to finish the work with a thousand begs and put the cheap material on the thermal facade if you don't know about these you step on it also ate our money because he had said he would refund us a amount we gave him when he takes it from my savings and he never gave it back to us. The door we changed and was charged more broke down in the first year and you have to have a warranty and they don't pick up our phones. Be careful not to be trusted at all.

After cooperation in settlement, issuance of electronic building identity and energy performance certificate for drawing up a purchase and sale contract, the services provided were excellent, with prompt response, scientific competence and completeness.

Το ίδιο μου έκανε και εμένα όσον αφορά τα χρήματα, "και καλά μόλις πληρωθώ θα στα επιστρέψω". Εγώ απ ότι καταλαβαίνω ήμουν από τους τυχερούς που δεν του έδωσα να κάνει την ανακαίνιση και του έδωσα μονο τα χαρτιά όσον αφορά το πρόγραμμα Εξοικονομώ.
Rented out an apartment. It's absolutely amazing. Very central location.
Excellent stay! Exceeded expectations!
The very best!!
Spectacular home, everything very new, very comfortable furniture and beds and an unbeatable location for walking through the center of Athens.
A really great apartment. I would book again at any time. Very close to the center and fully equipped. 5*

We arrived in Athens in the evening around 10 o'clock and stayed only one night.  So after a walk until 2 in the morning, the sleep was very good.  In the kitchen I found coffee and in the fridge I found water, a very pleasant surprise.
The only problem I had was finding a parking spot - even though street parking is free, I think after 10pm it's virtually impossible to find a spot.  And an SUV with a roof box doesn't really fit into most of the covered parking lots.  Otherwise, the property has a garage available free of charge, where a normal car, even with a roof box, can enter without problems.

Visited March of 2024

This was a phenomenal suite that was perfect for my wife and I. Price was very very reasonable for its location...less expensive than any other place around. If you need parking like we did for our rental car then all you have to do is let them know ahead  of time and when you arrive you will be given a garage door opener for a secure parking place to park in behind a door that is only accessible by you.
