It was big enough and had a very comfortable bed. It was walking distance to every where we wanted to go in the city center. Acropolis, Agora, Stadium...all was close. I would absolutely recommend this as a great place to stay while site seeing in Athens! Thanks for a wonderful stay!

The location and price were good thats why we choose to stay here.
Staff was polite, they send us all onstruction for check in on time.
The bathroom is disapointing. Very very small bath tub but even smaller space for toilet.
Also the curtains are so thin that every light can go through and wake you up every morning.
To rich the elevetor tou must walk 4-5 steps which is not comfortable with travelbags and children.
In general it is a very old building that needs renovation and not just to paint the draws or kitchen staff.
Also please note that there is an additional charge for towels and bedsheets.
The rooms are fine, but the service was a bit unsettling. The maid made us feel uncomfortable each time she was around, also make sure you know that they charge extra for towels prior booking

It was a nice surprise to arrive in front of the Suites and find a reserved parking space waiting for us. The rooms were nicely apportioned and we were really close to many of the major attractions here in Athens. I wish we would have stayed longer...

Do you want to be within walking distance to Athen’s main attractions, feel comfortable, safe and appreciate excellent service?  The 11 Enteka Acropolis Suites checks all the these boxes and I think is an excellent value as well.  Our modern, well appointed room made us feel comfortable after a long flight from California.  When we woke the next morning we found restaurants close by and the Acropolis Museum practically next door.  Anna, the hotel manager gave us excellent advice for restaurants, helped arrange transfers from the airport and the cruise port.  She’s a delightful person who even gave us some lessons on the formation of the island of Santorini.  I would not think twice about returning and think after one stay you will too.  All the best to Anna and her staff, thanks for a wonderful visit to Athens. NOTE: If you use Google Streetview to view the surrounding buildings, don’t be put off.  The area is safe, close to everything and the hotel building is modern with every convenience.

The rooms are clean and check in / out is incredibly easy with the codes.  The location is superb and walking distance to acropolis and the main sites of Athens.  They let me leave my bags after checkout to explore the city.  The communication was efficient when I had a question

We stayed here for two nights. The hotel was perfect. We had a suite that accommodated 4, with a loft bed upstairs. The rooms were new, clean and comfortable. The hotel was walking distance to everything we wanted to see. It was  a two min walk to all the shops and restaurants. Ana was an absolute pleasure to work with. She arranged our pick up, transport and our tour guide for our stay. She was very helpful and sweet. We definitely recommend this hotel !

If you want to have a real good place for a stay in central Athens ... take this one!
Nice and comfortable rooms. Acropolis and the Plaka is reached within 5 minutes by foot. A metro station is located just 150 m away.

Stayed for 2 nights at this hotel May 2024. My group had 6 bookings at the same time in 4 different types of rooms. The Deluxe Room was quite small, however the Junior Suite and the Two Bedroom Suites (Grand and Regular) were quite spacious! Air conditioning worked great, comfy beds, great walk-in shower.

It's a very small hotel with a very small elevator. Located literally just steps from the entrance to the Acropolis and Acropolis museum. Many restaurants within walking distance.

We cannot praise Anna, the hotel manager, enough for how helpful she was to our group. From helping locate lost luggages at the airport, to organizing multiple transports to/from hotels and cruise ports, she was such a huge asset to the enjoyment of our trip. Communication was very easy through WhatsApp prior to and during our stay. We hope to visit Athens again and will be thrilled to stay at this wonderful hotel again!
Excellent location. Comfortable, nice decoration and clean. There was a lack of better communication when arriving at the place. We couldn't find it and we hadn't received the entry code.

place_name: Anthology of Athens member of The Leading Hotels of the World
review_text: Anthology of Athens has potential. The location is good, some rooms are lovely and the support staff  (especially the ladies at reception, the servers and the head housekeeper) are charming and eager to please. But, and this is not a rant but a plea for improvement. No one seems in charge. We encountered a different manager each day. The result is confusion and a bit of chaos. We were assigned an unsuitable room, (never stay in #102). We requested a change. First we were told none would be available at all during our stay ( our friends on same room type reservation who checked in with us received a larger room on a higher floor), then we were told maybe the last night, then finally we were moved on the second night. The room, while delightful leads to the second  set of concerns, design  and function.  The vanity in the smaller room had no towel rack.  You had a choice to place your toiletries  on the vanity or the towels. Not both. The L shaped  knobs on the shower are flush with the glass. You cannot open the door from inside if you have wet hands. The first floor does not offer the view of the temple  that inspired you to book the room in the first place. Do not open the window or the exhaust from the street will overwhelm. The new room, was lovely, but the sliding closet doors cannot slide if something thick (think bathroom) is hanging inside.  The breakfast room is on the top floor; the kitchen in the basement. Pity you if your order is missing an item, the server has to return to the basement for it. Fast though she may be, your eggs will be eaten or cold by the time the missing item arrives.  In sum, this could be a lovely place if management can organize and if the designers actually stay in the rooms they designed and corrects the many silly flaws. For the sake of all employed  at Anthology, I hope they do.

place_name: Anthology of Athens member of The Leading Hotels of the World
review_text: What a great place to discover Athens. From the rooftop restaurant and the pool you have a straight view to the Acropolis. The guest Team  is extremely friendly  and the rooms are really comfortable and stylish. It well deserves 5 Stars. We will definetely stay here again when we are back in Athens .

place_name: Anthology of Athens member of The Leading Hotels of the World
review_text: Room with Very nice decoration, style and sound proofed. The staff are super nice, friendly and ready to help you. The restaurant on the roof is very pleasant and with nice views. The food is absolutely delicious. As it is a new hotel, the manager is collecting feedback to try to have everything spot on. Highly recommended

place_name: Anthology of Athens member of The Leading Hotels of the World
review_text:
Let me start by saying I spend 13-16 nights a month in hotels for work. This hotel and its staff have been nothing short of amazing! They have been so welcoming and attentive to every aspect of our stay. The property and rooms are second to none…

Our check-in process was seamless and we were greeted by name the second we walked in the door. The hotel manager was able to accommodate our early check-in and escorted us to our room and then invited us to the beautiful rooftop restaurant for breakfast.

The hotel itself is new and the rooms are beautiful. While we were on the second floor and on the “main road” side of the hotel. The windows and sliding glass door completely blocked out any road noise. All the attractions of the Acropolis and  Plaka are just steps from the door of the hotel.

The linens, towels and bedding are all very high quality. As were the fixtures and amenities in the room.

The staff also made dinner reservations for us at an off property restaurant, arranged transportation to the port at the end of our stay. Every time you come and go you’re treated as a friend not merely a room number.

You could spend twice the money and still not receive half the service that you would at Anthology of Athens.

If you’re comparing this hotel to another prior to booking. Just stop, pull the trigger and book this hotel. You won’t be disappointed. Thank you to the staff and management for making our stay absolutely superb.

place_name: Anthology of Athens member of The Leading Hotels of the World
review_text: A truly perfect escape in the heart of Athens. Hospitality, food, and rooms are top tier. Perfect for those interested in touring the Acropolis, it is a stone’s throw away!

I had a pleasant stay at this hotel, and there are several positive aspects to highlight.

Firstly, the location is excellent—it’s close to many tourist spots, which is a big plus for those looking to explore the city. The rooftop is really nice, offering a cozy view where you can even see the Acropolis, making it a perfect spot to enjoy the sunset. The hotel itself is nice, with well-distributed facilities and a decent breakfast. I also appreciated having access to the fridge, which came in handy.

However, there were a few issues that affected my overall experience. On the day of checkout, I was leaving early at 6:30 AM, but the reception was closed, and there was no staff available to assist. I wasn’t able to retrieve my food from the fridge, which I had prepared for my travel day, and the same thing happened to two other guests heading to the airport. There should definitely be someone available for early checkouts, even if it’s just for small tasks like greeting or farewelling guests.

Another minor inconvenience was the towel fee—while it’s common for hostels to provide towels for free, the 2 euro charge wasn’t too bad, but still, it’s worth mentioning.

Lastly, I noticed that the bedsheets had an unpleasant smell, like they were damp or not properly washed. I had the same sheets for three days, and the smell was quite strong. I recommend checking the linens carefully before using them for guests to ensure they are fresh.

Overall, it’s a good place to stay, especially for solo travelers looking to meet new people. The hotel offers a friendly atmosphere, and the breakfast is definitely worth it. However, some small improvements could make the experience even better.

Great location, my room was clean and secure, decent breakfast. As far as amenities, I've stayed in much nicer hostels, there's hardly any common area, and the best feature of the hostel, the rooftop, doesn't open till 6pm. Maintenance and cleanliness/supplies in bathrooms could be improved. But if you're looking for a cheap, safe stay near the Acropolis, a quick walk from the Metro, this is a good option.

This hostel has a great location to public transport,  attractions and restaurants. The rooms were clean and a good size with locker space for backpacks. Beds were comfy and had a night stand with plug. The AC was amazing in the heat aswell. Staff were polite but not overly warm. Breakfast was basic but substantial. They did allow us to leave our bags at reception prior to check in which was helpful.  Overall would recommend.

Stayed here in late May 2024.
Location is perfect for accessing the metro (Akropoli station), Acropolis Museum, and majority of the ruins (Acropolis, both Agoras, Hadrian's library, etc - buy the multi access ticket from the Hellenic Heritage website). A lot of food nearby and the Plaka neighborhood is not far for touristy shopping and more food.
Rooms are an okay size, lockers roomy enough for a backpack, BYO lock but also they sell them for a high price in the lobby vending machine. Multiple people's luggage cases can take up all the floor space. Bedding is not great and there were stains on the blankets. Bathrooms were clean but they do not clean out past guests' toiletries that were left behind, so it piles up, and they desperately need better ventilation and some towel racks in there.
Some staff are nice and helpful, some staff are incredibly rude. One person said we could fill our water bottles in the breakfast area, another yelled at us for trying to do it before the breakfast area was 'open.' No clear policy it seems. Breakfast is okay, more of a continental / UK breakfast fusion style.
The lobby is also the breakfast area and it's gated off at night so if you are trying to work remotely, there is virtually nowhere to sit. There is a rooftop bar with a sem-decent view of the Acropolis but the cheapest and most uncomfortable plastic chairs.
Okay for a one or two night stay/budget traveler, otherwise try one of the small boutique hotels in the neighborhood.

Location is great. The night time desk worker is creepy and mean. Made me wait outside for my 3am cab and there was a group of men being rowdy outside. Didn’t make me feel safe. Esp when I could have waited inside the seated area.  The linens smelled like cat pee. The location is great tho. And for what it is, it’s not bad but don’t expect great service.

The apartment is quite spacious, bright and very well equipped. It has two bedrooms (1 x double bed, 1 x 2 single beds). In addition, there is a sofa bed in the living / dining room. There is a small bathroom with a toilet and a shower (which could use a few more storage options). In addition, there is a separate shower and another separate toilet with a - in comparison - larger sink. The kitchen is very well equipped with a stove, microwave, oven, kettle, coffee maker, dishwasher and washing machine. There are tabs for the machines, tea bags and coffee capsules. If there would be some salt and oil, it would be perfect.
There is a small balcony off the kitchen facing the courtyard with a laundry rack. There is also an iron and ironing board. Along the living / dining room and bedrooms there is a corner balcony - of course with a view of the Acropolis. There are balcony doors in these rooms accordingly.
The apartment is modern and smartly decorated / furnished - without a lot of frills that just collect dust or break. Overall, the apartment is in good condition. In some places (especially in the bathroom) the paint is flaking off, or it shows signs of wear due to less careful handling by previous tenants. For my taste, everything could have been a bit cleaner, especially the oven and microwave, but I am admittedly very picky.
The TV in the living / dining room is oriented towards the dining table with bench seats, which are not insanely comfortable in the long run, but who comes to Athens to watch TV. And there is also a TV in the bedroom. The Wi-Fi reception and throughput was very good.
The reception at the apartment and the short introduction to everything you need to know was nice.
A really nice apartment, but unfortunately with one big drawback: Vourvachi is a busy street, so it is very loud!
Convenient to the Acropolis. People are nice and the air conditioning worked well. The beds were a bit firmer than I like, but all in all good
Good 👍
Nice view and very close to historical sites
Good

Some of the best service we experienced during our time in Greece! We arrived before our room was ready and were able to store all our baggage. Were offered infused water when we got in and our bags were all brought to our room. Rooftop bar/restaurant is very cool and the breakfast spread was incredible. Room is nice - the shower is in the middle of the bedroom which is a bit strange. The ground floor has partially glass flooring which allows you to view ruins below which is unique and intriguing.

The Niche is a boutique hotel that is elegant without being fussy. The front desk and housekeeping staff are friendly and helpful while remaining completely professional.

The rooms are somewhat spartan but nicely appointed, comfortable, and spotlessly clean. The bed was comfortable with four different pillow types. If you liked one specific type, a pillow menu made it possible to request that type of pillow. Because the hotel is on a busy street, the rooms are somewhat noise early at night. If possible, request a room on the 4-7 floors.

The rooftop restaurant and bar, with both indoor and outdoor seating, provided an excellent view of the Acropolis. The breakfast buffet was outstanding. The dinner menu offered unique dishes that highlighted Greek cuisine. The bar offered a selection of signature cocktails (try the Philosopher's Whisper) in addition to beer, wine, and other beverages.

The hotel is just blocks from the Acropolis and many of the archeological sites. All of the sites shown in the city pictures are within relatively easy walking distance. The entrance to the Acropolis is about five blocks from the hotel. Entry requires tickets to enter and the climb to the top where the Parthenon and other ruins are located is quite steep and strenuous but definitely worth the effort. Excellent dining and shopping options are within a few easily walked blocks.

Niche Hotel has been such a beautiful surprise! We stopped in Athens at the beginning of our trip to the islands. This hotel is simply perfect, the location, the cleanness but most of all the kindness, and professionalism of the people working there.
A special thanks 😊 goes to Nicholas and George that have been far and beyond. Their advices have really made the difference in our stay that was just supposed to be a plain stop before the real vacation. We strongly recommend this hotel and its human beings if you have to pass by Athens.

Our stay at the Niche Hotel in Athens was fantastic! The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, making us feel right at home. The rooftop restaurant was a highlight, offering amazing food, drinks, and a breathtaking view of the Acropolis. The lamb risotto is a must-try!

The hotel's location is perfect, with a short walking distance to all the major attractions. The breakfast included was the best we've ever had at a hotel—so many options and everything was super fresh.

The rooms were beautiful, clean, and equipped with modern amenities. Despite being in a bustling city, we found the rooms to be quite soundproof with the windows closed, and street noise was not an issue at all.

Overall, we highly recommend the Niche Hotel for anyone visiting Athens. The combination of great service, exceptional food, and a prime location made our stay unforgettable!

My mom and I had a wonderful stay at the Niche during our recent mother daughter trip!  The hotel staff were extremely welcoming and friendly.  They made us feel right at home.  The breakfast buffet was delicious and the rooftop view of the Acropolis was incredible.  However, the best part of our stay was our massages with Chrysa and Ireye at the spa.  The spa was located below the main check in and was nestled among ancient ruins that were uncovered when the hotel was built.  The space was truly incredible and created a beautiful environment which left me feeling relaxed and connected to the earth and it’s rich history.  Chrysa and Ireye were beyond incredible and gave us the best massages either of us had ever had.  I highly recommend booking with them as they made our trip even more memorable.  I am beyond grateful for the wonderful experience they provided us and cannot speak highly enough of them both!

Great stay at Petal Apartments. We stayed in the two bedroom unit on the 1st floor. It was in great condition. Very clean, quiet, and comfortable. Good kitchen with full appliances,  great bathroom, AC worked well. Good spot for families looking for extra space with a living room and kitchen.

The location is a quiet, local neighborhood that is an 5 min walk to the busy tourist areas of Athens. There are a couple of nice restaurants and shops nearby.

It is not a serviced apartment (no daily cleaning and no breakfast). Overall it’s a good spot that we would recommend.

LOVED our little apartment in the heart of Athens. Great food, markets, and pubs in close walking distance and of course the Acropolis is just a walk away! Strongly recommend to anyone who wants to stay in the city without all of the unnecessary attention and crowd that comes with a hotel. 5 stars.

We stayed in a 2 bedroom ground floor apartment. Marios met us on arrival & was helpful with information on local cafes and sightseeing tips. The apartment was new & clean, and everything you need is taken care of. (Refrigerator, kitchen, microwave, washing machine, hair dryer, wifi & TV etc. The Acropolis & Syntagma are an easy short walk, or you could also take the tram. Plenty of good restaurants close by also. Highly recommended 👍.
Large space.  Clean and tidy..good terrace. Comfortable bed.. just needs some essentials like kitchen utensils.. missed not having a coffee plunger...

Check-in process poor. You need to specify your arrival very precisely. The apartment is definitely for two, not four people. Microscopic living room. Eating area and table outside. The kitchen is very poorly equipped. The cutlery and dishes were dirty and needed to be refreshed. Dirty mugs with visible traces of tea on the inside. The blanket in the living room on the couch smelled distinctly. In one bedroom, cigarette foil next to the bed. There were visible stains on the floor in the bathroom. Overall dirty. Location very good. The apartment is definitely for one or two nights, but not for a longer stay.

Super convenient location. Walking distance to the Parthenon and the museum. Half a block to tons of shopping and dining options.  1 block to the metro is a huge plus, too.

The hotel is well staffed with very helpful employees who truly want you to have a great experience.

The rooms are clean and well kept if slightly dated. GREAT water pressure and almost instant hot water!

The breakfast was good with lots of options to choose from. Cereal, pastries, bread, meats and cheeses, and of course fruit. With coffee, tea,orange juice,sour cherry, and milk to drink.

We would absolutely stay here again.

This hotel is in a perfect location and for the price you can’t beat it! Just one block from the Acropolis south entrance and the best walking street with so many amazing restaurants all around. The breakfast is phenomenal the staff are wonderful. They have the best deal in the bar for happy hour from 4pm-8pm BOGO drinks for €6.50 and the bar area and lobby are beautifully decorated. The rooms are basic but cozy and spacious. Overall had a very nice stay!

Great location, literally a step away from Acropolis. The MVP is the AM shift manager, was nice enough to give me the room as soon as possible which was a lifesaver after a 12hr red eye flight. The breakfast was good too. The only thing is the rooms are quite dated.

Exceptional hotel with excellent service, one of the best breakfasts ever at a hotel (included in rate), great location and expansive rooms. Location is central to major historical sites, a plethora of stores and restaurants, metro/bus/tram stops. We walked to everything except for our cool tram ride to the Greek Riviera. Front desk staff, housekeeping staff and breakfast staff are top notch. In the morning, you can treat yourself to a large variety of breakfast items, freshly made, hot and cold. Great communication prior to and during our 3 day stay.

This hotel is in a perfect location to walk to all the sites! The street is not noisy or too busy. The staff was very welcoming and helpful! The room was clean and had all you needed; the fixtures and linens are a little dated, but again all you needed. Nice breakfast choices too. Thank you!

This is a very nice looking apartment in a convenient but busy part of town. It is one block from a very busy street that is quite noisy. A lot of the walking and bike tours meet right outside your window downstairs. The speakers of the yogurt shop right below you are mounted in the ceiling, and you hear the bass from the music in the apartment all day. We also had to check out of the place at.

The inside of the apartment was very nice and the beds were decent. The entry and the hallways into the apartment were a serious step down. The front entry door to the building does not close and lock so they have guards that sit down stairs but are not helpful. The door slams every time someone goes out and you hear it upside in the apartment.

Over all it was inexpensive for a large 3 bedroom apartment which is this nice in this area. There is a top end hotel next door that charges about the same for one hotel room.

12 Keys is an ideal base for any length of stay within Athens. I stayed for four nights, and the location, service and amenities were all excellent. Walking distance to virtually everything, close to the tram and bus stops, and apartments are well equipped with all you could want for a comfortable stay. The top floor apartment has a private rooftop an amazing view of the Parthenon, an excellent place to lay for an afternoon nap in the sun or to watch the sunset. Would happily return for my next visit.
Parking on the streets can be challenging as it is a busy area, but you do not need a car in Athens.
Great overall location - close to a train stop, close to acropolis and other attractions. Room was clean, bed was comfy, A/C was great. Room was a bit small but manageable!

Service was excellent. The girl at the reception was very kind and helpful always. The rooms were clean and nice. It's a little bit far from the most touristic places, but for the price and quality it's good.

I can't say enough good things about this lovely place.  The room was extremely spacious and had everything we needed.  Giannis was the best host I have ever dealt with in all my travels.  He went above and beyond to make our experience in Greece a fantastic one.   He sent me maps, even took the time to print out the washing machine manual instructions in English.                            Aside from impeccable hospitality, the location is perfect too.  You can walk to most sites easily and conveniently within less than 15 minutes.  I'm already plotting my next visit.

An excellent stay spent in an apartment with a roof terrace. The apartment is modern, well-equipped, very clean. The terrace and balconies are great. Located close to attractions in Athens, 5 minutes to the metro station. staff very friendly. Thank you very much and I hope to come back there🫶
Perfect place to stay in Athens! Room design is perfect. Balcony is huge. Entrance is via passwords, you don’t need to worry about carrying keys around. I wish I could stay longer.

We stayed in the first week of October for 4 days. We had a pleasant stay except for the minorities issue with AC. Check in is keyless and directions provided for the same were helpful. Everything to visit was within walking distance. I wish I would have stayed longer.

I cannot say enough about Ilias who went far beyond being helpful. Started with an accidental cancellation of booking. Ilias managed to get out booking back. Arranged pick up from airport and to ferry terminal. The list goes on with always a pleasant manner and willingness to help.

The apartment itself is fantastic with everything you need. Position is perfect for getting around in foot in the lovely area close by the Acropolis, restaurants, hop on hop off bus.

Would recommend this very reasonably priced accommodation. Thankyou Ilias for making the start of our holiday so stress free!

Large, modern apartment equipped with all comforts. All operations are carried out in self check-in. The only not too positive note is the external noise of the splendid terrace: barely perceptible inside, but a bit of confusion outside; however absolutely normal being a stone's throw from the Plaka.
Excellent structure.

The room was great and comfy, and the shower was amazing, definitely what we needed after travelling through Greece for two weeks. The location is very convenient, close to the sights and restaurants around the corner. We received the information for the check-in the day before, and it was clear and easy to enter the apartment.
Amazing location! Very convenient to move arround.. the apartments were just great with high end finishing! Customer service top! Definitely we will stay at A&N next time!
Good 👍

place_name: AD Athens Luxury Rooms and Suites
review_text: Small boutique hotel perfectly located 5 min walk to Acropolis Museum and Plaka district at the base of the Acropolis. We had a room with a large terrace and a view of the Parthenon.  Super clean. Excellent AC. Great staff. Roof top patio where we had a continental breakfast with a great view of the Parthenon.  This is a fabulous hotel for a couple of nights at the heart of old Athens. Highly recommended!

place_name: AD Athens Luxury Rooms and Suites
review_text: Stayed for 4 nights.  They accommodated an early check-in.  Our room was on 5th floor with a view of the Acropolis and large balcony.  Everyday started with breakfast with a large assortment to choose from and included any type of coffee made to order.  Everyone was very friendly and accommodating.  Once you leave the metro is literally 50ft away.  The Acropolis and museum are an easy 5min walk to the entrance.  Every food and drink imaginable is within a few blocks of the front door.  It doesn't get any better than this.  Thank you for allowing us to stay.

place_name: AD Athens Luxury Rooms and Suites
review_text:
Spent a night here before heading home.  The ppl at the desk watch the door and are very nice.  Speak good English and are fun to talk with about the city.  We had to get a 4:30am cab next day and they fixed us up 2 breakfast boxes.  They were delicious!

The room was 2 twins put together, very clean everywhere, new modern bathroom with fancy multihead shower, espresso maker, waters, and small fridge.

6 floors with rooftop bar and an elevator.  Nice selection of drinks. You can see the lit Acropolis from this rooftop bar. Took a cab here so parking is likely a nightmare based on my observances.

Highly recommended.

place_name: AD Athens Luxury Rooms and Suites
review_text: What an amazing hotel! Great rooms with good AC (we needed it when temps were 104). The location was top-notch - about 1 block from a Metro station (needed because the taxis were on strike) and very close to the Acropolis. There are also a ton of wonderful restaurants very close by (1 block) with shopping better than the super touristy parts of the Plaka. The included breakfast at the hotel was excellent and the view from the rooftop breakfast area was absolutely amazing (see photos). This seems to be a family run small boutique hotel and it was outstanding.

place_name: AD Athens Luxury Rooms and Suites
review_text:
What an amazing experience! Iphigenia is incredibly kind, friendly, and helpful. The breakfast team prepared delicious breakfasts for us and served them with genuine care. We could even enjoy the view of the Acropolis from where we had breakfast. Dimitri, who worked the evening shift, was very helpful and kind. The location is most central yet quiet. The room with the balcony facing the Acropolis offers the most stunning view imaginable. The hotel is impeccably clean, and the jet shower is a luxury you don't often find, even in 5-star hotels.
The hotel is definitely more like a 4-star boutique hotel and not as classified!

Excellent location being a short walk to the Acropolis with many food, market, cafe and bar options close by. Avoid plaka and the tourist trash cafes and go the other way away from the Acropolis for better options. The room was well appointed and super clean. Coffee pods, a welcome bottle of wine, bottled water and free laundry included in our stay. Would definitely recommend this highly functional hotel for your next stay in Athens if you are a tourist.

My husband and i have only words of praise for the owner and the apartment. We contacted the apartment before arrival because our flight came very late. Communication was via whats app with quick responses. Mrs. Cleopatra and Mr. Yannis arranged for us a taxi that was waiting for us at the airport despite a delay of almost 1 hour. We were in front of the apartment at midnight, Mr. Yannis called us despite the late hour to welcome us and give detailed instructions. The apartment was very clean, comfortable and spacious. The location of the apartment is excellent, only 5 to 7 minutes walk from a street full of restaurants where you can have your morning coffee or dinner overlooking the Acropolis. If the road brings us to Athens again, we will definitely stay in the Great Living Apartments.

Stayed here for a night while in Athens. Close to the Acropolis and the Acropolis Museum, and nearby to food and drinks. Clean and comfortable rooms, and they were accommodating with leaving our bags after checkout.
Very attentive service! The room was perfect and the location is quite convenient, within ten minutes walking distance from famous attractions.
Wonderful location to visit Athens city center, 3 min walk to the Acropolis. The room was really nice and comfortable, modern design, good WiFi, and friendly hostess.

Excellent apartment in a very quiet area, and yet walking distance from the center. Very big apartment 3 bedrooms great for families. Takis the owner is so helpful and welcoming. He will help you with what ever you need. Very recommended 👌🏼👍🏼
I strongly recommend this place and I am glad I found it. Nothing to complain about.

Perfect for families! Excellent location
Very clean, spacious and comfortable

Unfortunately I found a cockroach in the room and it was a bad experience. I didn't like the lack of security due to the very old door but also the old kitchen and windows. It gave me something negative about cleanliness. The internet was very bad in terms of signal and you couldn't work. Definitely needs a wifi signal boost.
On the positive side, the convenient area and the communication with the people in charge of the area.

The best thing is the location, the heating and the mattresses 🤗🤗🤗 you can rest well ... but you should invest a little in updating the bathrooms and the kitchen 🤗🤗🤗🤗 ... in general it is quite good for what is needed after hours walking through the streets of Atena!!

If you’re going to Athens, this is absolutely the best place to stay. The staff were very friendly, accommodating, knowledgeable, and prompt, and professional. The hotel and room were modern, well maintained, clean, well equipped. The breakfast was incredible and honestly, one of the best parts of staying here. The location CAN NOT be beat either! I could not recommend this place more! Best place we stayed in our 3 weeks trip across the Mediterranean.

Our 3 night stay at your hotel capped a wonderful 2 week vacation in Italy and Greece. Our room was clean, the beds comfortable and the daily room cleaning service was much appreciated.  The front desk personnel were very courteous and helpful with a special shout out to Margarita! The complimentary breakfast featured a variety of quality offerings as well as very helpful and gracious service. Of course, the view from this rooftop location was amazing! Your personnel handled our luggage on arrival and departure and also arranged for taxi pickup from Piraeus and on our departure day to the airport. We had a wonderful stay at your hotel! 5 Stars all the way! Thank you so much! John and Sue Delapa

We just spent 2 nights here, and it was incredible. The room is quiet and so comfortable and nice. The staff was super kind and helpful. The breakfast is fantastic. We had dinner one night at their rooftop restaurant - the food is great, and it provides uninterrupted views of the Acropolis lit up at night. The entire stay was magical, and we would 10/10 stay here again.

Service was A+. Friendly front desk and bell staff.

Breakfast was included and the restaurant was great. Big menu, fast and friendly service, great food.

Rooms were clean and comfortable. Nice balcony with chairs too. Shower was a bit tiny, and awkward to use due to size , but not a deal breaker.

Location can’t get much better. Right near most of the attractions.

If you’re looking for a quiet, restful night, avoid this hotel at all costs. After a long day, I was hoping to get some sleep, but I was informed that the bright LED lights on the balcony, which illuminate the building, cannot be turned off. The lights are glaring, and the room stayed brightly lit all night. When I raised this issue with the staff, they suggested using the three layers of curtains in the room as a solution, which shows that management is aware of the problem but clearly uninterested in the well-being of their guests. While the staff really tried to work through the problem and even offered me a bottle of sparkling wine for the inconvenience, it just demonstrated the tone-deafness of the organization. If you value your sleep and want a peaceful hotel experience, this is NOT the place for you.

The location is amazing, you’re in the middle of everything and a short walk to the Acropolis and some awesome restaurants. There is a 24 hour restaurant across the street if you’re an early riser and I had one heck of a view. The folk that run this place are super helpful and friendly. I’m definitely booking here again when I come back.

A truly nice place for a short stay in Athens. The Mini-apartment has a small kitchen and a separate sleeping room,  and a sofa in front of the television. Everytthing is super clean, modern and nice aestethically, and you are only 5 minutes walking distance from the Acropolis. I did not give 5/5 to the room only for the fact that it is on a noisy street, but with the windows closed, which are of very good quality, it is quite enough. The instructions you get by email from the owners are extremely clear and professional and the apartment access experience is flawless.

We had a really nice stay here. The rooms are very clean and the shower was really nice. The location is in the center of Athens everything within reach, whether you want to use public transportation or just explore the city. It is all close. They have a luggage room where you can store your luggage when you are checked out but you leave midday.

The place we stayed was not as described, dangerous, and had roaches.  Main bedroom has no walls and door. AC did not work. Unlockable outside door, insecure main door.  Curtains broken so people could see inside.  It was clean and modern, but has roaches.

Response:  Thank you for replying to our review.  We noticed the sign saying all external doors and windows needed to be closed and we did that.  The door to the patio was closed both top and bottom
There was no sensor where I had the cable so no way the AC was an issue due to that.    Please take my concerns to heart and do better.  You definitely have an eye for internal design.  I hope this review will help others decide on this place.

I was very disappointed with management this time.
+ perfect location, room looked clean till I found next day not my hair on the bed sheet
- not flexible with check in time. No one will inform you if it’s ready before 15:00 and where you can leave your luggage before or after your stay
No one is there, no reception as they call themself like apartment ( with pretty much hotel price).
6.09 no electricity from 6:30 in the morning. No a/c ( so we woke up directly), no opportunity to make warm formula for my 5 month baby . All day I been contacting them and saying that it’s very important for me as I have to feed my baby. No conditions for comfortable day sleeps as well. They couldn’t help and put the blame to providers and couldn’t answer when finally we will get electricity. They said approximately around 14:00.
At 16:53 they texted me for the first time with info that there is an issue ( thank you, I am very aware about it).
As still it was no information if it is coming at all this day, no help from their side ( like hot water, or anything). We left to another hotel at 18:00. No more patient, I count see how my daughter is sweating.

They said that they got electricity like 18:30 ( if it’s true). Also there was a message that they couldn’t assist and bring me hot water or smth. Guys, if you want to assist and provide a good service you could find a way, you could apologize and order a coffee to your guest ( as long as I didn’t even touch you complimentary capsules and been not able to use coffee machine) and just hot water from any coffee shop near by ( I could put it in my thermos). You prefer not to do anything, and only write you polite messages which doesn’t help me. If you are interested to help - you do your best and take responsibility. In the end 0 euro has been refunded. Anything
👏Μπράβο σας
It was a great experience. All the positive reviews were actually true. I advice everyone and especially families of it. Thank you for welcoming us
Wonderful place to stay.  Great service.  Will stay here next time. Great location.
Right next to Acropolis, a great apartment to stay!
Great hosts. Everything was perfect.
yummy

The location is remarkable. In the Plaka.. close to the Acropolis and Museum. Fine restaurants around. Quiet place though crowded and busy area. A free WiFi, spacious rooms, clean and cozy with big balcony. Expensive paid €788 for 3 nights ( hot season). Close to metro. Loved it and will be visiting again.

Apartment 10/10
Location 15/10
Host 20/10
Strongly Recommended place!
The hotel went above and beyond by offering all necessary amenities, including a toothbrush and toothpaste.

Very convenient place, at the heart of Athens. It was exactly as depicted in photos and according to description! Very clean, easy to access and the amenities were perfect! The host was very kind and willing to guide and help us out! Would definitely visit again!
The best place for a short trip stay in Athens. Great location, central but in a quiet street. Comfortable bed and very clean space.

Everything was absolutely amazing!! Very comfortable beds and great aesthetic!! The property is in a perfect location, next to lots of stores for eating, drinking etc. and also very very close to Akropolis and Athens city center. Worth visiting!!
Great place for a short stay down town in Athens.
So beautiful, clean and very well decorated apartment! Love it!

June 2023
A nice enough apartment centrally located and convenient. Perhaps a bit on the cramped side but the rooftop terrace view of the Acropolis was incredible. The owner Sylvia seemed tired and stressed though and wasn't particularly friendly or as helpful as previously described. This took a lot of the gloss off the experience. One hopes Sylvia recovers her joie de vivre.
The other issue was bedbugs. More attention needs to be paid to this and unfortunately more fumigation is necessary. My wife was heavily bitten and I killed two of the little beasts myself.

July 2024
Sylvia suggests in her response to this review that the bites could have come from mosquitoes or other insects. Unfortunately this is not the case. As I mentioned I was able to kill two of them and positively identify them as bedbugs. It is reassuring to read that further fumigation was carried out.

Beautiful house, amazing location and friendly hosts.

The area is very safe and there is an underground right across the road. From
The roof balcony you can see the best view of Acropolis too

Will definitely be using again next time I visit Athens 😊

Stayed here with my wife and our 15 months old baby.

The communication with the hosts was excellent. They arranged for a cot for our little one and welcomed us with a smile.

The apartment itself was spotless and very comfortable. It has a little kitchen area with appliances and the bed is extra large and comfortable.

If you want to stay in the heart of Athens this is the place to be!

We traveled  to Athens for 4 nights and wanted to stay close to the Parthenon and the historical part of the city.  We stayed at the Parthenon Apartment for our first night and at the Acropolis Apartment for the folllowing nights.  Both apartments are well equiped. Parthenon has a garden and Acropolis has a balkony.  Both are spacious and cosy. I would recommend both to anyone visiting Athens for the first time. Don't forget to see the top floor terrace of the building. There is a fantastik view if Acropolis especially in the evenings. Our hosts Raphael and Sylvia were very helpful and they answered all our questions. Highly recommended for anyone  visiting Athens.

It's so comfortable and relaxing. The owners are so kind and helpful. You feel just like home. In addition, the area is entertaining and the view from the building's roof is such adorable at the sunset.

Fantastic apartments for our family in a great location. We had power outage issues due to the extremely hot weather (all across Athens) and the owner was always in contact to ensure we were ok.
The view from the balcony was a wonderful bonus too
We cancelled our stay well ahead of time but they still billed us after cancellation. refund was very long process and lost money in exchange fees.
Fantastic location. 3-5 min walk from metro station, in the heart of SO MANY restaurants and cafes. Wow! Clean and cozy!

A spacious, clean and comfortable apartment right next door to the Acropolis Museum. The apartment was very quiet even though it’s close to everything. The view of the Acropolis was stunning. The owner was friendly and very helpful. I couldn’t have been happier and I’ll be back.
Exelent location. Midle of Athens. Plaka under the windows. Sintagma 1000 m. Acropolis 300 m. Acropolis museum 200 m.

Great appartment in Athens. Perfect for a family or a group of friends. The roof top is amazing with a view to Akropolis. There’s plates and wine glasses for more than 6, really a plus for a Big family. There’s also candles and everything you need when staying in Athens.

We stayed in apartment 6, the building is well presented and stylish on entry. Our apartment was a good size and the terrace was a nice bonus, a good addition would be a full length mirror in the room as there's only one in the bathroom. The room was comfortable and we were really pleased with our stay. The apartment had everything we needed and was lovely and clean on arrival.
We had booked the apartment for 5 nights and our only miscommunication was that they don't clean the rooms/refresh towels unless you request this, which we didn't know so were disappointed this hadn't been done. However I messaged the owner and he corrected this immediately, brought us fresh towels and arranged a clean for the following day. So definitely request this if that's something you'd want.
Location was perfect, a short walk to a lot of attractions and popular areas and not too far from the airport either.
The Peruvian fusion restaurant nearby (Huaca Nikkei Resto Bar) was amazing too, definitely recommend.
Antonis the owner was very friendly and so helpful, especially when we realised we had left some valuable items in the drawers once we had checked out and he got them and shipped them to us, we're very grateful! Thank you so much!

The best place to live in Athens.

Amazing rooms - amazing staff.

Kind Regards,

Theodoros

This is a fabulous remodeled structure in a very cool part of the Mets neighborhood. The apartment we stayed in was well provisioned, clean, and tastefully designed. The owner was very gracious and responded right away. I would highly recommend staying here as a couple, family, or group of friends.
Very good accommodation! Close to city center, quiet area.

Fantastic apartment, only thing it was missing was a toaster oven. The terrace outside was so nice to sit and relax at, barely spent any time inside when I was home. Not very noisy at all aside from basic city noise.

Highly recommended. The rooms are glamorous, high end finishes, yet homely. With amazing views on the Acropolis. Easy access to all of Athens, and close to the hottest club in town if you want to party the night away. But what makes this place really special is the care and service provided by Giorgios….in one word: WOW! (and I recognize I’m not an easy person 🙈).

We already booked our next stay at The One, can’t wait to be back ❤️❤️❤️
Wow. George & the team are exceptional with rooms to match. I proposed to my girlfriend and George couldn’t have helped more or made it easier. Truly unforgettable. Thank you endlessly

Outstanding service and hospitality. George is a master of hospitality and anticipated all
Of our needs. He really went above and beyond. Had an amazing time and had a very first class experience and  stay. The beds were also comfortable and high end. The whole space was very upmarket and had loads of amenities. The view was amazing.

The place lives up to the hype. Rooms are clean and spacious as displayed in photos. We travel a lot and George and the entire staff are the best ever. Very accommodating and make you feel at home. We will definitely return.

Stayed many times and in all types of rooms. The suites are on 5 star hotel level with all the amenities you will ever need. The views of acropolis and lycabetus are incredible especially from the penthouse. The service and treatment is always good and George the front office manager is a true professional and always available with solutions to any issue. I highly recommend The One Acropolis for business travelers and couples that are looking for luxury and safety in the center of Athens.

It’s a perfect place with a fantastic 360 degree balcony. Very calm area and 15 min to walk to syntagma. The airbnb is very clean and new. I really love the modern bath. I would come definitely again.
Great airbnb, clean, tool set up
Excellent choice for accommodation in Athens! Unique professional service, incredible large modern spaces, huge roof overlooking the Acropolis and the sea, truly a gem in Pagrati behind Kallimarmaro!

place_name: MA MAISON ACROPOLIS MANSION N°6 & N°7
review_text: An exquisite apartment: well-furnished and well-kept. Located in a quiet corner of central Athens, it has all facilities needed for a happy stay. Every place of interest is within walking distance. The view of Acropolis is amazing. The management team along with Yanni were warm and efficient.

place_name: MA MAISON ACROPOLIS MANSION N°6 & N°7
review_text: The flat was spotlessly clean, modern, and fully equipped. It was like staying in an IKEA showroom. The water pressure was excellent. Yannis was easy to reach via WhatsApp and very accommodating. We plan to stay here again in future.

place_name: MA MAISON ACROPOLIS MANSION N°6 & N°7
review_text: Simply incredible!  I highly recommend Ma Maison Acropolis for its comfort, beautiful views, perfect location and gracious hosts. I just wanted to stay longer!

place_name: MA MAISON ACROPOLIS MANSION N°6 & N°7
review_text: Everything was wonderful! As a reward our team stopped in Athens for some site seeing by day and city night life. We were lucky to find this beautiful location near outdoor cafes, restaurant, and the best night life Greece has to offer. The airbnb was clean, roomy, and had everything we needed. The perfect location for visiting the Acropolis, the Acropolis museum, and the cutest shops with handmade jewelry and art. We highly recommend for a perfect stay. Yannis helped us with everything we needed and Toli transported us too and from the airport with ease. Thank you for the stress free stay, we will definitely be back soon!

place_name: MA MAISON ACROPOLIS MANSION N°6 & N°7
review_text: AMAZING experience!! The home was beautifully kept and well appointed.  Keen attention to detail in every aspect including coffee, tea, water, shampoo, etc.  The home was spotless, decorated beautifully and inviting.  The location was superb!  Just steps away from a shopping market, coffee shops, local restaurants, and wine bars.  The neighborhood was clean and safe.  We never felt uncomfortable strolling around.  The City Centre was just a brief 10 minute walk and filled with numerous shops, restaurants, and museums. The rooftop terrace offers the most incredible views and was perfect for relaxing after busy days exploring Athens. Our stay in this home was the beginning of a wonderful vacation.  Yannis is an incredible host that was accommodating, responsive, thoughtful and considerate.  He even provided resources to help us plan and navigate our stay.  I highly recommend this property and host.

Great balcony view of the Acropolis
Wondeful place to sit.
Apartment had everything needed incl washing machine, fridge, iron and cooking things.

Host helpful.
Bedroom nice and comfortable. Bathroom very nice.
Kitchen and living room are very small but you are coming here for the Balcony living space really.

What an horrible experience… The manager’s communications skills are lacking, to say the least. More importantly, our booking was cancelled by the manager before the agreed upon meeting time at the property. No calls, no messages. Just a closed door after a 8 hrs flight. There are great hotels in Athens, anyone of them is a better than this one.
Couldn't stay in a nicer apartment
A nice view of Acropolis !!!

Μικρό σχετικά προσεγμένο με όμορφο μπαλκόνι με θέα την Ακρόπολη.
Περισσότερο όμως για καλό καιρό ώστε να μπορείς να καθίσεις έξω.
Εύκολη πρόσβαση στο κέντρο σε μια ήσυχη γειτονιά.
Great place.
Spotlessly clean space, fully equipped, with a unique view.

I had an amazing stay at this Airbnb! The apartment's charming garden was a true heaven, offering a peaceful and relaxing environment that was simply delightful. The cleanliness of the place was impeccable, and the host went above and beyond to ensure I had a comfortable stay. With its convenient location near Athens city center, I was able to explore the city's attractions with ease. I highly recommend this Airbnb to anyone looking for a tranquil and well-located accommodation!

Such a cozy spot! The place was clean, comfy, and had a really nice vibe. Great location too – close enough to everything but tucked away in a quiet neighborhood. The host was super helpful and made sure we had everything we needed. Would definitely stay here again!

Secret Garden Downtown exceeded all my expectations! The apartment's enchanting garden created a serene ambiance that instantly made me feel at ease. The cleanliness of the place was remarkable, and the host's warm hospitality made my stay truly special.
Being situated near Athens city center was a huge advantage, as it allowed me to easily explore the city's vibrant atmosphere. I highly recommend this place for a memorable stay!
Lovely place quite centrally located clean safe a
That will be an amazing stay🔱✨️💎💫🌞
Absolutely loved my stay! The apartment was spotless, beautifully designed, and in a perfect location. The host was attentive and made everything seamless. Highly recommend!

Wow!!! What a place. This is a top quality hotel in the centre of Athens. Designed by a local architect the features use all natural materials. Dark wood and stone are strong features. We stayed in a city suite which was spacious, comfortable and clean. The bed was huge and we had a basket of complimentary goods waiting for us. The stand out feature is the hot tub on the roof overlooking the acropolis - you can’t get better after a long day in Athens!

Stayed for 2 nights to finish off our 2 week Greece trip. By FAR the nicest accommodations we had. We stayed in a beautiful room with a view of the acropolis. Service is accessible at anytime and all you have to do is WhatsApp the hotel. Extremely accommodating. Included breakfasts and complimentary snacks when you arrive. I will stay here again the next time I'm in Athens!

This is a little gem of a hotel close to the Acropolis.  The staff is so accommodating and helpful.   The rooms are beautiful, and the rooftop jacuzzi overlooking the Acropolis is so relaxing. I highly recommend this hotel for any travelers

We have stayed here twice - it was perfect both times! The rooms are beautiful, clean, and have everything you need. The location is right in the center of the city and the rooftop view is breathtaking. Wouldn’t stay anywhere else any time I’m in Athens.

This boutique hotel has many standout qualities, and a few drawbacks. The staff is absolutely fabulous. Warm, caring, helpful and professional. Can’t say enough good things about them! The hotel itself is clean, modern and the views of the Acropolis are spectacular. We stayed in the penthouse suite with sweeping views, a large outdoor deck, and private, jacuzzi soaking pool (that was so nice to have but could have been a little cleaner tbh.) The room itself was large and had great AC and a kitchenette. I like clear surfaces and little clutter, so my one critique would be to clear some stuff out and make it more simple. There’s also a shared rooftop deck with another soaking tub for all hotel guests. The drawbacks for me mainly had to do with the streetscape around the hotel. There are no sidewalks or easy place for a car to stop on the street which is a very busy road. While the area is walkable, it’s dirty and depressing to walk along the streets and often times there are little to no sidewalks. There’s also some sort of ghost kitchen café right next to the lobby door which had people whizzing up on and parking bikes to deliver food and smoking cigarettes. I wish I could pick the hotel up and put it in a better location.
Great apartment. Although older equipment and furniture, everything is fully functional and equipped. Accommodating host.

The room is nice but tight.  I loved the body soap. The bed was really comfortable. Communication is not the greatest. When we first pulled up with the cab, I thought we were in the ghetto.  I did get in contact with someone, and we were able to leave our luggage in lockers to explore the Acropolis while we waited for our room. There is a hot tub, but you have to reserve a spot, and it looks old and crusty. It would be nice to have a couple of sun chairs on the rooftop.

Great hotel with a wonderful stuff clean rooms and a nice rooftop with an infinity pool and a bar. The breakfast was quite generous. Even though we got to the breakfast half an hour before it was finishing, they continuously topped up all the foods until the very last minute. The checkout time was 11 but they allowed us to check out two hours after without asking us for any money.

Stayed here for a week with family and friends.  We had 2 rooms with different layouts, both comfortable and clean.  We all felt very safe during our stay as it is a coded gate for entry and rooms are equipped with cameras.  If they weren't booked, I'm pretty sure we'd stay on our return trip this weekend.  Staff was very friendly and helpful during our stay.

There is NOT a lobby or any personnel to greet you on site.  If you need to check your luggage, there are lockers, but be aware that you will need to communicate with Luxe&Easy personal by phone.

The customer service was good once we connected with the reservations personnel.  I just wish that we would have been more aware of there nature of this spot.

Now that we know what to expect (I would
Not call it “Easy”), we would consider coming back to this “hotel” if we decide to return to Athens.

This is really more like a high end ARBB or VRBO.  Rooms are private and great.  Clean, updated, beautiful and safe.

The view from the Roof Garden of the acropolis is beautiful. The neighborhood is similar to most downtown areas of a big city.  Expect graffiti on the walls and some older buildings in disrepair.

Walking distance to most sites are reasonable.

Location central to your main tourist attractions like the acropolis, which is only 5-10 mins away driving.
Rooms spotless, and room service quick and efficient.
Lots of added amenities and loads of room.
The hot tub is lovely with the garden included, multiple seating areas and lots of room for both of us.
My only downside was the hot tub only had one side working but otherwise very good.
We recommend paying the parking fees as it is secure and street parking is limited. The entrance for the parking is very narrow with other cars parked on the street so would recommend only smaller cars.
Being close to a main road it is quite noisy but you get used to it quickly.
Very nice spacious place, close to the center, many options for food and drink with easy access even on foot. Suitable for families.
Spacious, bright and clean apartment in the center of Athens!!! Excellent choice and very helpful hostess!!!
I highly recommend! Very clean and comfortable! The hostess very helpful and communicative!
Great host, super friendly and nice. The apartment we stayed at was clean, comfortable and had everything you need for a pleasant stay.

Great location:walking distance to all main attractions!Really beautiful and modern apartment -spotlessly clean!
Also the garden was a hidden gem!

Super location!!
Brand new rooms!!
Very clean!!
Great hostess ❤️

Excellent ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
All the rooms are Brand New and clear!!!
In the Heart of Athens.
You can visit in 1km the Acropolis Myseum,Kallimarmaro Stadium ,the old location of Plaka & Anafiotika ,The temple of Olympian Zeus ,National park of Zappion and many many more places!!
Excellent location, just a few minutes on foot from the center of Athens! Very clean and new Suites! I absolutely recommend to visit them!!

Awesome location only 12 -14 minutes walk from the Acropolis and all the cafes and tourist shops near the Acropolis. It is away from the hustle and bustle of central Athens but close enough to the city centre. The rooms are clean - with complementary slippers , snacks and coffee pods. We had a room with a kitchen which was awesome . And the views from the roof top were just gorgeous of the Acropolis. So you can enjoy a swim in the plunge pool and watch the sunset come across the Acropolis. Pic attached of roof top view. Air con was awesome. Reception was lovely and nothing was ever too difficult.

Absolute gem of a hotel with the most amazing facilities. And at a very affordable price. Located within a short walk of all the things you need to see and restaurants. Clean and modern with everything in perfect working order. The most amazingly helpful staff. Especially George who helped out with excellent recommendations and advice throughout out stay. I can only recommend this hotel.

I loved this apparement style accommodation which provided a kitchette, living room with a TV, and bedroom with a view of the Acropolis. What more can one ask for! The bathroom and living room spaces were nicely decorated with marble or marble like motifs and very modern decor. State of the art door lock technology and really enjoyed the fact that I had my own private balcony too! There a rooftop pool and lounge where you can read or do work but no restaurant. Loved the stay overall! Would return. Staff were nice, kind, and tried to help me with whatever I was doing.
Perfect hotel for a weekend in Athens. Very nice room and amazing view from rooftop with small swimming pool. Main attractions within walking distance

We stayed at Athensense for the beginning of our honeymoon to be close to the city center of Athens. A really comfortable stay with a trendy boutique vibe. Super cute, friendly staff, great amenities like a pretty rooftop with a small pool and Acropolis view, and a nice gym downstairs. Rooms were lovely, nice balcony with tables and chairs and loved the newly renovated bathroom and great soap products. Definitely impressed and would stay here again!

Great place near cafes and restaurants but still very quiet. So clean and luminous.The owner take care of every details that make a stay comfortable,we found everything we needed. Nice terrace and view and Chrissa is a nice and warm person.
Amazing place and good host. Though breakfast could have been much better.
Nice n comfort stay

New modern loft with details, nothing is missing, completely renovated, penthouse with views, everything new, small mini kitchen, but with the essentials, located in a quiet residential neighborhood, next to the stadium, 15 minutes walk from the center along a very pleasant, illuminated and very safe walk, in the area where the loft is located there are restaurants, supermarkets and all kinds of services, so if you decide to stay and enjoy the penthouse after dinner, you will not need to go downtown.
The Sintagma metro is the best stop.
The owner is very professional and goes out of her way to help her guests.

Small room but ideal for two people with a view of the acropolis, the treatment has been very good, although the best of the whole neighborhood where it is located, very young atmosphere and the Greeks themselves.

Nice details!!! A little chocolate on your pillow, Nescafe, tea, drinks, air freshener, everything new, well thought out, I liked it. The wifi works perfectly, the hot water wonderful, the mattress divine. Cons, the bathroom gets all wet and when you come back in after showering, it's a disaster.
So clean and cosy studio at one of the hot spots of Athens!
I loved my time in this apartment!! ❤️

A very cool spot just outside of the city centre. The hang out social space is awesome for cooking and getting to know the rest of the guests and closes at an appropriate time to allow everyone to sleep comfortably.

The staff were very helpful and welcoming. George and Terry were always up for a chat and full of information and ideas about Athens and life in general.

I stayed for 3 nights at the end of a cycle tour through Greece and it was the perfect way to finish my trip. The rooms were well set up, had plenty of fans and locker space. Bathrooms were clean and there are laundry facilities if you need them.

I highly recommend this place and will be visiting again!

I recently stayed at this hostel in Athens, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. The place was cozy, friendly, and exactly as described online. Most of the staff were kind and helpful, but I had an uncomfortable experience with one employee named George.

George seemed to have a habit of drawing attention to himself, interrupting guests, and engaging in what felt like a strange flirtation with younger female guests. While these behaviors were off-putting, the situation that most affected my rating occurred on my last day.

A girl had fainted in the kitchen, and I stayed to help her until the emergency patrol arrived. George, however, insisted that I leave the room. I understand that the kitchen was small and crowded, so I eventually complied. However, the way he demanded it was disrespectful and abrupt, which made me feel unappreciated despite trying to help.

When I was leaving the hostel, George approached me at the exit door, and we began discussing what had happened. I calmly explained that his attitude might discourage guests from returning. In response, he violently slammed the heavy metal door in my face. While I can understand reacting emotionally in a stressful situation, such violence is inexcusable.

I initially planned to give this hostel a 4-star rating, but due to this incident, I’m giving it 2 stars instead. I hope George takes the time to work on managing his emotions and avoiding such aggressive actions in the future.

I’ve stayed in over a hundred hostels during my travels, and this is the first time I’ve ever experienced something like this.

Great hostel! Easy to get to with public transport or walking from the centre of Athens. The staff are super friendly, approachable and easy to talk to. Rooms are clean and it’s not noisy at night. Would definetly stay here again!!!

one of the best hostels i’ve stayed at. The staff really makes the place. Me and my friend stayed over a year ago and still regularly wonder how george and terry are doing. (hence why i’m leaving this review)

I recently had the pleasure of staying at Delfini Hotel and I must say it was an exceptional experience. From the moment I arrived, the staff greeted me with warm smiles and made me feel right at home.

The rooms at Delfini were simply luxurious. Immaculately clean and tastefully decorated, they provided a peaceful and comfortable retreat after a long day of exploring. It was evident that great care had been taken to ensure every detail was perfect.

One highlight of my stay was the delicious breakfast served at Delfini. The variety of options available was impressive, and the quality of the food exceeded my expectations. The dining area was well-maintained, and the attentive staff ensured a delightful start to my day.

Overall, I highly recommend Hotel Delfini to anyone seeking a memorable and enjoyable stay. The polite and friendly staff, combined with the clean and luxurious rooms, made my time there truly special. I am already looking forward to my next visit.

Thank you, Hotel Delfini, for a truly exceptional experience!

Warm regards,
Ben

place_name: The Aristotelian Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:
Not sure where to start. Location: Was close to some of the bus lines so that was nice. Not much else is close by. Neighborhood was quiet and seemed safe.
Service: The cleaning ladies were very helpful with helping us check in which was great since there was not a reception desk and we were given no instructions upon booking (we booked through our credit card company so not sure if you book directly with them if you get instructions). We talked to the hotel and then I had to send them my ID over WhatsApp and then they sent the codes for everything. The service was was very quick.
Room: AC was working which was great. The place was spacious. Everything was new but not the greatest upkeep. Some floorboards were pulling apart. There was some hair in the shower. There wasn't hot (or warm) water in the shower. The toilet was broken (but my husband fixed it). There some stains on the floors (not sure if they were permanent or just not cleaned). Overall I would not stay here again. I think it would be good if you are on a hostel budget or looking for those vibes (but with more privacy).

place_name: The Aristotelian Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:
The pictures on the website are NOT representative of reality. A dark and dingy hovel, dirty sofa, rickety furniture, zero decor. The two sparse bedrooms were located in a basement which smelt of bins. We were a family of five and the bed for the fifth person was not prepared nor was there sign of where it would be… praying not on that dirty sofa.

We could have perhaps tolerated the poor location if anything else had been good.

We stayed long enough to put our bags down, look around, take out our phones and book into a central hotel.

I guess you get what you pay for in the end but the photos and reviews gave us false hope.

would not recommend

place_name: The Aristotelian Suites by Athens Stay
review_text: This place is a dump, and it's being rented as a premium suite. It was disgusting, nothing worked, total disrepair, dead bugs and the Athens stay people didn't care to do anything about it after multiple complaints! The washing machine didn't work, no basic supplies and it was just dirty and broken everywhere!

place_name: The Aristotelian Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:
In one of the apartments now. Very happy with it. Modern and well-equipped. Staff are accommodating.

It's in a good location close to bus routes. Lidl is a reasonably short walk away but there's a small supermarket close by, too. Easy walk to where it's all going on in Athens.

One tip to the manager/owner is some balcony furniture could be a useful addition.

Overall, great place. Very comfortable.

place_name: The Aristotelian Suites by Athens Stay
review_text: The apartment is beautiful, and well laid out. It was very comfortable for two of us but could easily have taken more. The apartment was perfectly cleaned and fully equipped with everything we needed. Everyone was very welcoming and left thoughtful items in the apartment. Overall, we had a really great stay and would certainly stay there again!

user_name: Dr. Radu BALANEAN, PhD, MSc Med, BSc,MSc Psych
review_text:
ROMANIAN/ ENGLISH:
I booked this apartment on airbnb. It was hard to choose but honestly I don't regret it. The apartment has a nice living room, a bedroom with a large and comfortable bed, a kitchen with all the necessary equipment.
The apartment is modern and very finely finished and cared for.
It has a gorgeous terrace with table and chairs, but also beautiful plants and modern lighting for a gorgeous atmosphere in the evening.
ENGLISH:
I booked this apartment on airbnb. It was hard to choose but honestly I'm not sorry. The apartment has a nice living room, a bedroom with a large and comfortable bed, a kitchen with all the necessary equipment. The apartment is modern and very finely finished and cared for. It has a gorgeous terrace with tables and chairs, but also beautiful plants and modern lighting for a gorgeous atmosphere in the evening. The bathroom is modern and has a smart washing machine. It has Wi-Fi similar to the internet in Athens. The host is kind and responds promptly to requests. The price/quality ratio is very good.

Discover new renovated 2-bedroom apartament just a few minutes walk from the Acropolis area. Floral House can became your own home during your trip to Athens. It will be ideal place for your business trip, for a couple or for the family with children.
Very nice apartment! It contains everything necessary for a comfortable life. Recommend!

The place is very decent with a great value of money. Yiannis was very helpful and allowed me for an early check in and also helped me find a place on the day I was leaving. The apartment was very clean and spacious.
A very clean, calm street, recommended for couples,
Good place to stay, near to a lot of touristic places. I like this flat and spent one week here.

Nice, spacious apartments equipped with all comforts. Excellent both for a short stay and for something longer. The non-central location doesn't favor it, but you can get to the center quickly with a walk. The value for money and the possibility of arriving at any time once you have received the entry instructions are also excellent.

place_name: Supreme Luxury Suites by Athens Stay
review_text: If I could give this place zero stars I would. I’m honestly shocked it has 4 stars. The apartment was filthy when we checked in, it smelled of urine and mildew.  We didn’t feel comfortable walking with our shoes off. There were scuff marks all over the walls. This place is advertised as a hotel but it’s run more like an Airbnb. The whole vibe of the place was really unsettling. Save your money and stay at a real hotel.

place_name: Supreme Luxury Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:

place_name: Supreme Luxury Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:
Horrible experience.
Booked this thinking I’m going to a hotel. It’s a private building. We arrived early thinking we could leave our bag somewhere. The guy at the front desk said he doesn’t have us in the list and I must call some phone number to check what’s happening. It all seemed like a scam. I called company I booked with they told us to call at 3pm some number as we can’t check in before it. At 3pm we called. They sent code for the door. The apartment was just okay. Nothing luxurious about it. So disappointed in the whole booking. Also they only provided body wash- no shampoo no conditioner. Just poor service after all.

place_name: Supreme Luxury Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:
Very good room with nice views of the acropolis.

Quiet ambient and service very up to the standards.

The area looks a bit rough at the beginning, but people around is very friendly.

place_name: Supreme Luxury Suites by Athens Stay
review_text:
The main photo is from the roof which I assume comes with the penthouse and perhaps slightly misleading as a result, but these are nice and big apartments in Athens. It’s easy to get around from here to the heart of town by Uber for £3-4. This is a lot bigger than standard hotel rooms and I totally recommend staying here instead of a hotel.

The staff are helpful and a phone call away, it’s a basic apartment which is clean and cleaned by staff on a daily basis.

Don’t worry too much about the location - you’re getting better value for your money here instead of a hotel in town.

My only complaint would be that there’s some dogs in  both the apartments next door and that poor dogs seems to go nuts when people come in.

Pros: The only good for me was that the room was clean.

Con:
1) The location has some foul smell of sewage or something that made it impossible to stay in the room without air conditioner.
2) The location was far from metro station. The location did not offer anything except some food court around 5 minute walk.
3) There was nobody from the staff or owner side to resolve any issue, all contact numbers including emergency number gone to automated recorded message so it was a horrible experience at the property on first day because another guest was in my room by mistake because she said the owner didn't tell her room number and she couldn't reach them over phone so she stayed in the room.
4) The room looked dated with almost all items looked like they were purchased at the flea market when available cheap because the room furniture etc didn't match in style at all.
5) No complimenty water bottle was provided and water taste wad not good so i had to buy water from nearby supermarket.
6) The bathroom didn't had cold water from faucet, I had wrote to the owner about it but didn't get any reply.

The owner only want to make money out of the rooms but they are not available at the property nor available on phone. They don't even call to ask guest on arrival if everything is okay or you need anything or get anything fixed, don't even wrote back. I will never ever book such stay again, better to be in some airbnb or proper hotel with help desk. Athens is not safe also,you can get robbed and when you'll need some assistance from hotel, you won't get any. Worst stay till date and I have stayed around in numerous hotels etc in Asia and Europe.

Very very disappointing experience. I would not recommend anyone staying. The communication is awful, the hosts were unreachable, the flat is lacking a lot of things, there is absolutely zero sound blocking in it (4-5 rooms in total), so zero privacy and quietness as well, and overall it was just a bad choice for me.

We enjoyed our stay here. It’s about a 15/20 minute walk to the Acropolis, and the price was fantastic. Coming from the airport, we took the subways to the “ΣΤ.ΣΥΓΓΡΟΥ-ΦΙΞ” stop. The experience is roughly a shared home with a full kitchen and shared bathroom. Checkin was very relaxed - someone met us outside, brought us inside, and gave us the keys.

Comfy and clean place with decent beds in quite a calm neighborhood. Depending on room-type there's a shared toilet & shower (male/female) and a common kitchen area with necessary items. Doors isn't that isolated so you can hear when others arrive or leave.

5-10 minutes walk to the nearest metro station which is convinient. Bars, restaurants and kiosks close by. Easy to walk to/from central parts of Athens.

Alegria house is a budiling divided into rooms located in the heart of Athens. Just 20 mins far from the Acropolis by walk.
