Amazing place, perfectly clean and completely cozy! Alexia and Manos went far and beyond our expectations! Thank you both, we'll definitely return for a longer stay!!
Really cute apartment, grear location and super clean. Thanks for the great stay!
Justice
Amazing
Everything is wonderful, clean and thought out! I recommend
Cozy, comfortable, and accessible Airbnb. The hosts were amazing and accommodating. I would recommend everyone to stay here as it is walkable to all the attractions in Athens.

“MomentsAthens B&B was an amazing find! The location is unbeatable for exploring Athens, the rooms were spotless and comfortable, and the hosts went above and beyond to make the stay enjoyable.Would definitely stay again!”
An outstanding place 5⭐️ by all means. Excellent location and super clean. Hosts are amazing ready to help with any request in no time. Once you stay here it’s difficult to stay anywhere else!

My stay at Manos and Fay’s place was wonderful! The apartment is in a great location and was extremely nice and clean! The hosts are very responsive and helpful, and I would happily stay there again :)

Wonderful Airbnb with amazing hosts.
I felt totally comfortable.
For every concern and question, Manos and his wife were always available.
Thank you for the wonderful hospitality, highly recommended.
I will come back again!

Amazing brand new Apartment - Penthouse for couples with a spectacular view of Acropolis. It is located on the 5th floor with a large balcony and hot tub outdoors. Within 5 minute walking distance you from the Acropolis musuem & metro station, Temple of Zeus , Olympic Stadium and plaka region.
Thank you Christina and Stefania for all your help during our stay.
Beautiful location super close to the Acropolis and super comfortable location. Location is about 15 mins from the Acropolis walking time and in the center of Plaka and everything else. Loved it
Excellent View and amenities
Excellent!!

This hotel is one of the best I e stayed KKb, second only to the Taj in Mumbai.
Hotel staff were friendly (they were great!), food was outstanding, and the room was lovely.
I have so many good things to say about this hotel that I don’t have time to write it down. Just stay here!

Beautiful and spotlessly clean hotel in the center of Athens with an incredible staff always smiling and willing to help in anything..thank you very much Elli Konstantina Ioanna Penelope the crazy Cretan and George for the perfect service...😉 😉💯 💯 💯

Wonderful service; all the hotel staff were very friendly and very attentive to everything we needed; they are very polite and warm people. The location of the hotel is spectacular; you can clearly see the amazing Parthenon from the bar, and certainly the facilities are wonderful. The breakfasts served are indescribable; they are a true Greek delight. Besides being buffet, there are options for all tastes, and all this while enjoying an unforgettable, beautiful scenery. An excellent hotel for visiting an amazing city. A hundred percent recommended!

The rooms are not as nice as the photos, quite run down and small. No where to lay out a small case. The breakfast is just ok, aside from breakfast you can't get any food here. I asked for my bed to be made up as a double as it was a twin room but it was never done (the booking was for a double). The staff were friendly and the location was good. Overall not good value for money.

Loved our stay here. Small rooms but lovely and clean.we had a wonderful balcony that had a perfect view of the Acropolis. There wasn’t a kettle for tea or coffee but when we asked about it, in less than 5 minutes we had one. All the staff were amazing and made us feel like we were friends. Very close to everything including transport. Can’t wait to visit Athens again and we will be making this our go to hotel.

Location close to metro and 10-15 minutes from the Plaka. Nice variety of choices for breakfast including fried eggs upon request. Very friendly and helpful service, especially Anna at the front desk. Rooms are moderately sized. Roomy corners shower with plenty of hot water. Highly recommend the room with the balcony. Excellent view of the Acropolis, especially front the 6th floor.
We had a great stay! Really clean rooms, very helpful and hospitable stuff. There is a rooftop bar where they serve complimentary, continental breakfast.

A fantastic apartment to stay in!

The view will amaze you and you won't stop gazing at the Acropolis.

It is worth mentioning that the apartment is for 2 people.

The space is spotlessly clean and very nicely designed!

We only stayed one night & loved it!

We will definitely visit again!

PS The lady who owns the apartment is very welcoming and kind and answers you immediately for anything you need!

Whenever you come to Athens or if you want to stay somewhere with a view of the Acropolis, stay at Acropolis view Homey#2 and you will definitely be satisfied

**Review for B4B Signature Hotel, Athens**

I recently had the pleasure of staying at the B4B Signature Hotel in Athens, and I can confidently say it was an outstanding experience! The hotel is perfectly situated in a central location, making it incredibly convenient to explore the city's most popular attractions—all within walking distance.

Safety was never a concern; the hotel is in a secure environment, and parking options, both on the street and underground, made my stay even more hassle-free. One of the highlights was the rooftop bar and restaurant. The breathtaking views of the city, especially the Parthenon, were simply unforgettable. It was the perfect spot to unwind after a day of sightseeing.

Breakfast was another standout feature. The selection was not only delicious but also catered to various dietary needs, including dairy-free, gluten-free, and vegan options. It’s great to see a hotel that prioritizes inclusivity in its menu.

What truly made my stay memorable was the staff. They were incredibly friendly and helpful, offering valuable information and recommendations for sightseeing, dining, and transportation. Their local insights really enhanced my experience in Athens.

Overall, I highly recommend B4B Signature Hotel to anyone visiting Athens. Do not hesitate to book your stay—you won’t be disappointed!

I wanted to take a moment to express my heartfelt gratitude for the incredible experience I had during my recent stay at your charming hotel.

From the warm welcome at check-in to the exquisite attention to detail in every aspect of my visit, everything was simply perfect. Your team's hospitality truly made me feel at home, and the comfortable accommodations exceeded all my expectations.

The unique decor and ambiance of the hotel added a special touch, making my stay even more memorable. I particularly enjoyed the helpfulness of the entire team, which made my trip even more enjoyable.

Thank you once again for your outstanding service and for creating such a lovely atmosphere. I can't wait to return and recommend your hotel to friends and family!

Good hotel in walking distance from the Acropolis of Athens. Clean and nice rooms. Delicious breakfast.
Beautiful view from the rooftop bar. Very romantic and cozy rooftop. Very friendly and helpful staff. Absolutely recommended to stay

A great place to stay while we explored Athens. The staff was extremely friendly and accommodating. The rooftop bar was excellent with a great view of the Acropolis. And the breakfast had many options and was very delicious.

We had a one night stay at this fantastic hotel and wished we had made it a longer stay. The greeting on arrival was very nice and along with an arrival drink, was very welcome.  All of the staff were so friendly and helpful which is great as we felt comfortable talking to them and asking questions.  Nothing was too much trouble.  The location was very good and a 15 minute walk to the touristy areas.  The room was very good and clean and we loved some late evening drinks on the roof terrace overlooking the Acropolis.  All in all a great stay

Beautiful, clean, tastefully furnished accommodation. for a great price!!! We had a really great time! Accommodation is about 5 minutes from the metro station. About 10 minutes walk to the Acropolis. The same goes for all other monuments. The owner was nice, accommodating, gave advice and always explained in detail. A shop or pharmacy is about 2 minutes away. There is a nice park in front of the house where you can sit. I highly recommend and we will definitely stay here again on our next visit to Athens ❤️👍🏻🏛️

Excellent location at a short distance to a metro station..easy parking around the local park..very clean accommodation and very good communication with the owner..we will definitely prefer it again✌️
Theara 🪄

Rooms solid, everything you need for a short trip, if you don't have too high standards.
The hotel is located on a busy road, nothing for "Silence-Lovers".

Roomkeeping friendly and clean.

Employees friendly, helpfull and very well english speakers.

Breakfast sufficient, fresh and tasty.

Location good, next to metro, bus, tram and near to touristic hotspots. You can reach everything by foot or puplic transportations.

All in all: perfect hotel for a citytrip.
I.like the room stylish, very clean! Service is the best! Pur room cleaner is the best! Super nice! location 6 min from Acropolis and walking distance to restaurants.
Stayed here for 2 nights after a garbage Booking.com location across the street, very clean rooms, friendly staff, and great location,  a very nice boutique hotel.
Unfortunately we had to cancel our reservation because of a medical situation and they helped us to get a refund without any problem. I hope we will stay here next time!

Really gorgeous rooms and the staff was off the chain helpful..a bit noisy but the location was incredible, about 15 minute walk to the acropolis . Only problem was make sure you tell your driver the acropolis hotel not the other

I had a very late flight out of Athens. The reception staff were very warm and caring looking after my luggage and giving me a quiet place to relax until my transfer arrived. The decor is beautiful, and the hotel is situated very close to the Acropolis. They opened the rooftop bar before I left and made me a snack. Simply breathtaking!! 🥂♥️

Stayed for two nights post a cruise.

Good location, rooms a good size and I enjoyed a bath.  We were in an executive room.

Breakfast was good.

Unfortunately we didn't get to the rooftop due to a wedding.

Easy walking distance to Acropolis and Plaka.

Staff were very friendly and helpful.

This hotel is absolutely outstanding, especially in terms of the service provided.  From the moment we checked in, every single person we encountered was incredibly friendly and helpful.  We especially appreciated the help with our luggage, something that is rare these days unless you are staying in a much more expensive hotel.  The rooftop restaurant has a beautiful view of the Acropolis, and the staff there was amazing as well.  I would definitely recommend this hotel to anyone staying in Athens.  You will not be disappointed.

This hotel meet our needs and is located in an area that allows you to seek out and see many of Athens tourist highlights. It is located approx. 30 minutes away from the Ports and approx. 40 mins from airport dependent on traffic (there is a lot of traffic in Athens). Rooms were very clean and appointed nicely with the bed mattress being comfy. The staff were both pleasant and helpful, making sure we had a box breakfast on the morning of an early morning departure to catch a ferry. The restaurant and bar on the roof top with a spectacular view of the Acropolis at night is worthy of at least a cocktail visit.

We decided to have dinner at the Athenian Callirhoe Hotel Restaurant while visiting Athens. The staff was friendly and even though we did not have a reservation they seated us at a table with a view of the Acropolis and the Parthenon which was absolutely beautiful.  The menu included lamb chops, fresh fish--tsipoura (sea bream) and salads; we ordered one of each and we were not disappointed! They were delicious and plentiful! Will definitely consider it again when we find ourselves in Athens.
It was a really amazing experience. I did a e-bike tour with George, the best guide ever. Totally recommend.

We rented 2 bikes for 3 days and it was great! The bikes are so comfortable and perfect for a city tour. We wanted to keep the bike for an additional day and it was super easy as we just called the office and they said there was no problem at all. Kostas at the office is a very nice and kind man who assisted us from renting the bikes until the day we dropped them. I will definitely use them again.
Thank you - Claudio
We had an awesome e-bike tour experience at the coast of Athens! George is an excellent guide with cool equipment. I highly recommend this place.
I did  costal  bike tour on an e-bike in May 2023. Took sometime to get used to it. Great experience. I need an eletric bike in my life!😀
Had a lovely time renting a bike from this place - very kind and relaxed service, no stress. €30 for the day.

We stayed in this hotel while visiting Athens but it was a bad idea.
Although we enjoyed the excellent location and the safe neighborhood, we could not imagine the traffic noise would be so loud. The hotel is located on a heavy traffic road. We could hear motorcycles and cars even at 4 am. We did not really get accustomed to such hectic nights.
Also make sure they send you all the instructions including the entrance code before you arrived. We had not received the code upon arrival. As there is no front desk, the online assistance was difficult to reach. We waited outside for more than an hour to try to call somebody for help. No one answered. We finally called the assistance of the reservation platform who tried several times to reach them and a few minutes after they finally called us back.
In a nutshell, if you plan to stay in this hotel, don't forget your ear plugs!
Except from those issues, all went well.

10 day stay. Best thing here is the price. Clean modern rooms. Everything you need, except maybe a toaster. Nice bed, nice swower and bathroom, good A/C, cable TV, fast quiet elevator. Close to supermarket, coffee shops, bakeries, restaurants. Just over a mile to the center. This is not a hotel, its more like renting an apartment, no front desk, so you're on your own, checking in and adking questions by message, which is pretty timely, you have to do your own cleaning and laundry. The cleaning person I encountered in the hallway was rude, and indifferent, they won't lift a finger to help you with questions or anything, and absolutely ignored me like i was a ghost, when I tried to talk to her. There is a balcony. You can't hear your neighbors much, but the road noise is  constant, as it's right on a busy street. Parking is not available, and although not impossible, would be very difficult. It's an OK place to stay for the money.

Enjoyed my stay at WYZ appartements! I had a lovely studio, very confortable with calm and relaxing ambiance with a balcony. New furniture and appliance. Practical location, near the downtown but at the same time far from the city noise. 10 minutes walking to Syndagma. Would love to come back for every stay in Athens.

My husband and I stood at WYZ Apartments in the week of July 8th, 2022. Very clean  modern and comfortable place! Their Customer Service is on point. We needed to get in touch with them in the middle of the night, and they responded and assisted us promptly! Conveniently located to walk distance from Acropolis! The caffe and supermarket so close it is a plus! Very inexpensive place but with high quality!
Love it!!!! I recommend this place 100%. The only thing I was disappointed about was that they don’t change sheets or towels during your stay! My recommendation to WYZ management is to provide this service to be on point!
Ana and Marcos

Don’t come here, bad location
Very poor service, not answering if there is a problem and there is a lots of problems
Electricity problem, water problem, elevator problem
Stay away for your own good!!

I had a fantastic stay at Yantra. The property was beautifully decorated and equipped with all the amenities needed for a comfortable stay. The location was perfect, close to local attractions and restaurants.

The highlight of my stay was the host, Apostolos, who was incredibly friendly, always available and went above and beyond to ensure I had everything I needed, making me feel truly at home.

Highly recommend this property for anyone looking for a great place to stay with an exceptional host!
Excellent place nearby downtown, metros, supermarkets museums etc! The best spot for visiting Athens

Avoid getting the bus in Athens unless you really need to!! It honestly was one of the worst experiences of my life, pushed up against the windows the bus was so full, people just kept getting on! No air conditioning & very dirty! Do not recommend! Only plus was that it was free however that was still overpriced for what we had to go through!

I had the opportunity to stay in this rental room for a few days and I can say that it exceeded all my expectations. The room was perfectly clean, spacious and very well equipped, with all the amenities one would need for a comfortable stay.

The bed was extremely comfortable, offering a restful sleep, while the room decor was warm and thoughtful. The space had natural lighting and gave a sense of calm and relaxation, which we greatly appreciated after our activities during the day.

The location is ideal, with easy access to local attractions, shops and restaurants. In addition, the neighborhood is quiet, which adds to the sense of calm and safety.

One of the biggest pluses was the friendliness and helpfulness of the owner. He was always available to answer any questions or give suggestions for local places worth visiting.

Overall, this is an excellent choice for accommodation in the area and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a combination of comfort, location and good service. We will definitely be back in the future!

Our stay at the hotel was excellent! The room was comfortable, clean and tastefully decorated, offering all the amenities we needed for a pleasant stay. The location was ideal, with easy access to the area's points of interest. An added bonus was the bus stop right outside the house, which in just three stops took us straight to Syntagma, making our commute particularly easy and quick.

We would like to thank Christos for his wonderful hospitality. He was always available to help us with anything we needed and made our stay really special. We will definitely be back and highly recommend to anyone looking for an unforgettable experience!

Our stay was perfect! The room was spotlessly clean, comfortable and well equipped. The location is also excellent, with a bus stop outside the house that takes you to Syntagma in three stops. Many thanks to Christos for his excellent hospitality. We will definitely come again!
The room was fantastic, very clean and very beautiful! The one who had the back was very good! Well done

Do not order from this store, be careful. they send products in duplicate, but not the ones you ordered in the hope that you will not go through the return process, filling in a product return form, etc. This form is definitely impossible to fill in because it is disabled

I stayed at the Poseidon Residence in Athens with my friends.
The flat was very nice and the landlords helped with minor problems within hours. The location is very central and the kitchen has most of what you need. We felt very comfortable and would come back again.
We had a wonderful time. The accomodation is spacious and clean, and within walking distance of most of the main sites of Athens.
It's in a walking distance from the centre. There was some issue with the cleanliness - it could be much better. Free parking is available on the streets around the block.

Rooms look great, very cosy atmosphere in the backyard - everything was wonderful 😍
Our host Carolina is a very nice person ☺️ Thank you for all your advices! All questions were answered:)
Highly recommended!
Great place to stay,  I highly recommend it !
New building, comfortable accommodation. I recommend this place.

Better location than our previous time in Athens, we were able to find a bar after 11pm when the hotel bar had closed. Room was spacious, comfortable and lovely shower to freshen up.  Not the best breakfast yet sleep was more important

This was the first hotel of our trip. The staff was very helpful and pleasant. I can't rave enough about the breakfast. It was amazing! Hats off to the chef! The room was clean and had everything that you needed. The slippers provided were a nice touch. Thanks to the staff for making this a great first impression of Greece!

Excellent hotel!
My room was spacious and clean the whole stay, the room service was impeccable, the breakfast met expectations.
The reception staff were kind and I got an extension at check-out until 2:00 PM.
There is a beautiful yard, a cute lobby and a small gym.
Near the hotel there is a bank, an Italian restaurant, a cafe and even a supermarket.

Thank you very much.

Service is essential for me, and my recent experience was disappointing. During check-in, the receptionist was unfriendly and had a bit of an attitude. When I asked for help with my large bag, I received a rude response: "No, we don't provide assistance with carrying bags to the room." Instead, they provided a trolley for me to load up my bags myself. If I have to do everything on my own, I wonder why I'm paying a high price for my stay. I could have chosen another place for less cost and received better service. I've stayed at many hotels, and this is the first time I've encountered such a refusal. It seems standard for hotels, especially a well-known brand like IBIS, to assist with luggage. Due to this poor service, I will not be returning to this hotel. Additionally, the interior feels outdated, and the location is a bit far from major attractions (not within walking distance).

Nice hotel but it was not in a very nice area, I wouldn't feel safe walking out at night on my own. The real down fall was the hotel only did breakfast and there was nowhere in walking distance to get a hot meal so I had to have a taxi to take me to a restaurant on the three evenings I spent there. My travel agent did the booking as it was a two night stay before a cruise and a one night when I got back before flying home. I am a pensioner and felt it was a stupid place to put me. I will make sure I will not stay there again.

We had a really good time at Irene's apartment! It's clean, spaciouse apartment for 2 people, very well equipped, there was everything you need like coffee machine, toaster, equipements for cooking. The neighbourhood is calm and safe! The hisotrical city center is half an hour walk. The metro line 2 is a 5 minutes walk. Irene was really communicative and helpful! The self check-in and out is easy to handle!

We had the three room apartment on the first floor with balcony, perfectly equipped kitchen and living room. Everything was clean and nicely arranged. The elevator was appreciated for the luggage. Only 5 min from metro station an 1 min from bus stop line 227, the apartment was perfectly quiet during the night, when windows were closed. Very efficient mosquito defence for all windows. 20 min to Akropolis by metro, 30 min by foot. Irene was very kind and helpful with our questions and needs. Thank you very much for this pleasant stay.

Nice accommodation and a warm welcome from Greece:) Irene was super nice and eager to help with any questions about my stay here in Athens. Facilities were well equiped and i was surprised to see a wine in the dining room☺️and it was so great to have prepared icy water in the refrigerator after one hot long trip back from the outside!

We enjoyed two weekends at Casa D'Irene rustic appartment. There's plenty of space, so you feel like at home. By the way, it's full of details, like the wine everyone mentions. Irene also makes everything easy to help you with your stay.
The area is very quiet, and although it may look a bit scary at night, seems to be safe, at least, we didn't have any trouble, and we could see other people walking alone, and didn't look worried at all.
It's very well connected by metro line, and quite close to the city center, so you can get to the Achropolys in about 20 minutes on foot.
We are really happy to have chosen it for our visit to Athens!

A cozy and very nice appartment, located a bit away from strickt city center of Athens, is a very quality choice in case you are looking for staying in appartment during your holidays or other obligations you may have in this ancient Greek city. The apartment has two bedrooms, quite nice balcony (with a street view), bathroom with bath, and spacious living room presented together with kitchen. The appartment is clean and has all neccessary equipment for comfortable stay and cooking, together with some food items and spices you can use right away with your meals preparation - the owners are very accessible and open and are in general taking care that in appartment is all that is needed - a nice welcome touch from them is also presented as gift for newcomers in a form of a bottle of home made wine.
Otherwise, the appartment is just 350 m away from the nearest metro station (there is also a bus option), so the journey to other spots in city is simple and not far from main city attractions. In neighbourhood you can find bakeries and small shops with basic food and other basic equipment, while for those who are more demanding, the visit to supermarket would be essential - unfortunately there is no larger supermarket in vicinity of appartment, so you will have to search for it within at least 10 - 15 min walking distance.
The appartment is otherwise in the building on the first floor, and is easy accessible by stairs or by elevator, while the main entrance in the building itself is however a little bit clumsily, especially for people with disabilities, who could have some difficulties with entering to building.
The apartment has also an AC unit and free WiFi system.
In general, highly recommended for staying if you do not mind to not be in strickt city center of Athens.

Τι να πρωτοπώ; Το κατάλυμα είναι μια αθηναϊκή ρετρό όαση με καλλιτεχνική ατμόσφαιρα, που συνδυάζει μοναδικά την αισθητική του παρελθόντος με την άνεση. Η τοποθεσία του είναι εξαιρετική, πολύ κοντά στο κέντρο και στα βασικά αξιοθέατα, ιδανική για εξερεύνηση της πόλης. Κάθε λεπτομέρεια του χώρου αποπνέει μεράκι και φροντίδα, δημιουργώντας ένα ζεστό και μοναδικό περιβάλλον. Ο ιδιοκτήτης είναι εξαιρετικά εξυπηρετικός και φιλόξενος, κάνοντας τους επισκέπτες να νιώθουν σαν στο σπίτι τους. Ιδανική επιλογή για όποιον θέλει να απολαύσει την αυθεντική, καλλιτεχνική πλευρά της Αθήνας σε ένα φιλικό και ευχάριστο περιβάλλον. 🌼

Numa Lofts is incredibly welcoming, immediately making guests feel valued and at ease. The lofts boast a pristine cleanliness that speaks volumes of their meticulous attention to detail.

The staff at Numa Lofts are not just helpful; they go above and beyond to ensure that every aspect of your stay is taken care of. Their assistance is always delivered with a smile, adding a personal touch to their professional service.

Location-wise, Numa Lofts could not be more convenient. Nestled close to the metro, it offers effortless access to the wider city while being a short distance from the vibrant heart of Athens. This makes it an ideal spot for those wishing to soak up the local atmosphere without straying far from the historical center.

Numa Lofts is more than just a recommendation for those visiting Athens; it’s a top choice for anyone who wants to experience the city’s authentic charm coupled with the comfort of modern amenities. Whether you’re here to explore ancient ruins or enjoy the contemporary culture, Numa Lofts provides a perfect home base.
In essence, Numa Lofts is a must-stay for travelers seeking a clean, friendly, and strategically located accommodation that perfectly captures the essence of Athenian hospitality.

Very disappointing hotel. No reception after 3 p.m. so you have to manage to find your room number and map at the café next door!! You have 15 cm to pass between the foot of the bed and the wall while leaning to avoid the TV. The view of the courtyard is lousy, moreover as the bed is mounted on a platform and the window sill reaches you mid-thigh, hence a significant risk of falling from the third floor. Dirty window.  Don't plan on taking a shower morning and evening because hot water is rationed. No explanation of how the air conditioning works, which turns off and on at night depending on power outages. Lots of flashiness but zero service. TO AVOID.

Disconcerting hotel at first glance. It is in fact an old building converted into a hotel, hence the entrance is difficult to find and the absence of reception.

Nevertheless, very good value for money, the rooms are well equipped, new and comfortable.

The location is very good, close to the metro and the city center.

Very good service to note, we had an unexpected event the day before departure and the team was very understanding and flexible regarding the checkout time.

I recommend for those looking for good value for money

DO NOT BOOK HERE. In addition to most of these photos being fabricated, the listing lies outright about the availability of WiFi. This is not a hostel, but an apartment crammed with as many beds as possible. It is dirty and dark, there is no lockbox, no towels, no evidence that the bathroom has been cleaned in the last 6 months, and the bed I stayed in was so jammed into the room that my head was next to the door handle that opens out into the street. The proprietoress is an opportunistic grifter with no interest in hospitality, only making money off tourists and young kids who don’t know better. If you are unfortunate enough to be dissatisfied with these conditions, you will not get your money back.

Had an amazing time here. Staff was so friendly and helpful. Pretty easy to get from hostel to center of city with busses. Very close to cafes and restaurants. Amazing views of the sea/ aegina island and also the surrounding mountains. Beds were very comfy and everything was kept very clean regularly.

Most of the times reception is deserted. Beds in dorms are uncomfortable and without any blankets. We were charged 6 euros only for waiting for an hour and not allowed access dorm beds.  Staff hardly speaks English. Washrooms are very uncomfortable to use with indefinite hot water.
Good place to stay as it is very near to the city centre. But there is no air conditioner here. So, think twice if you are looking for a dormitory with an air conditioner in the summer.

only on certain parts of the building
Cleanliness is not good
television old model and hubby have only one room is not smart tv
Everything is perfect, thank you!!!!

I am very grateful to the staff of the UNESCO hostel for their hospitality. They are very friendly, take good care of their guests, are generous with food and smiles.
I recommend
It is very grateful for the hospitality of UNESCO Hostel. They are very friendly, take good care of guests, generous with food and smiles. Recommend.

Nice clean rooms with helpful staff located in a residential area. Not close to Acropolis but has good connectivity to Piraeus port,  Airport by express bus and site seeing places.

To withdraw cash:  check this ATM... https://maps.app.goo.gl/hQGqcB3VxaWuanQZ8

Near by there is a Big super market where you can buy water for 0.24 Euros and other required items like sun screen lotion

For food:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/ELEUSzQRXo2wf6CC8

Go to this place. There is lot of local and fine dine restaurants. There is a church, kids and local hang around this place till late night

Give the review a thumbs up if you found it useful. Thanks and Enjoy

The area is very calm and feels safe. Public transportation is near (tram/bus/trolley bus).

Clean and spacious rooms, given the price level. Make sure to tell the reception when you want the room cleaned, it is not done automatically. Clean towels are delivered packed in plastic. The air condition worked well and there was always enough hot water. There was a small fridge with a tiny freezer that worked well. The rooms had some signs of needing maintenance and there could have been more places to hang used towels and shelfs for placing clothes etc. Nice balcony! Drawback that the smell of smoke would come from the balcony.

The staff working during our visit was friendly and helpful. The reception is open 24/7 and the staff recognize everyone staying there so it feels rather safe. Very practical that one can buy snacks and drinks at any time. The breakfast was always the same: toast, an egg, a chocolate croissant, a glass of juice, a glass of water and a choice of coffee/tea/cold milk. There are many cafeterias nearby where you can also eat breakfast.

It is easy to take public transportation to places like Acropolis and beaches. There's a direct bus from the airport (X96). The mini market right next to the hotel sells tickets. If you plan to make more than 6 single trips in Athens it is cheaper to buy the five day pass.

All in all the hotel is a good choice given the price. The biggest drawback is the constant smell of smoke/e-cigarettes in the reception area. Not sure if this is a common issue in Greece, but if you're very sensitive to smoke the hotel is not the best choice.

Great hotel for the price. The room was clean, we didn't take breakfast, but there are plenty of options around. Definitely worth it for a couple on a budget stay in Athens.

Easy access to acropolis by bus 40 or 5 (30 min away).
Excellent stay in this hotel and the women who work there are great! I recommend ! Thank u again for everything girls !

Has a private parking area. Very polite and helpful staff. The room was very clean and renovated. Beds comfortable. Huge bathroom with a very big shower area. Silent room, no noise during the night. Everything was perfect. Late check-out available with extra charge. Only the shower head and water mixer in our room (107) could use a replacement. And one power outlet was half blocked by the bed so impossible to use. But that's just small details which didn't impact much. For the price and the category of the hotel its perfect!

The rooms do not look like in pictures.
The rooms are very small, no space where you could put your things. For 1 week we had the clothes in the luggage.
The rooms are in a very bad shape ... we had a bit of mold begging to show in the bathroom.... old holes that were bad covered up and they where showing.
The shower was not delimitated from the rest of the bathroom and when you were taking a shower the whole bathroom had water in it.
The mattress of the bed was to soft for our liking and you were falling out of the bed. There were 2 small mattresses in order to have a big bed, thus we were falling in the middle.
The location is nice it's close to the beach but your are far from the metro and center of the city.
I would not recommend this hotel.

Rooms are very small , noisy and limited housekeeping services.
All staffs are extremely rude with unhelpful fake manners which are also reflected in others' reviews.
Worst is the bathroom which reminiscent a prison's bathroom in you could bump into walls while taking shower.
Very tiny and bad design.
Would not recommend at all. Avoid! Doesn't even live up to a two star hotel in any standards.
If you want a nightmare this is surely the best option!

I would have been happy with the room if I had paid around 50$ instead 90$. The quality and comfort of the room are not worth close to the price I paid. do no expect anything amazing from this place, we just used it for its proximity to Piraeus Port which was its only positive aspect with the nice service at frontdesk

Hotel close both to Athens center and the beaches. There’s public transportation nearby. Bus stop in front of the hotel’s door, tram less than 5 min away by walk. Amazing location. The neighbourhood is safe (I’m super picky about this since I normally travel alone) and there’s some really good restaurants nearby. The staff is very friendly. The rooms are clean and the room clean service is excellent. Been there for a month in 2022 when I first move to Greece and I wish I have stayed more. 100% my first choice if I ever need to stay in a hotel again in Athens!

I usually travel several times a year due to my job and this was the worst hotel I have ever been. If you want to miss your home, this hotel is perfect for that. Let's enumerate the main problems:
- The fridge did not work, and when I asked about that the answer was that none of the fridges were working.
- The room smelled of tobacco.
- I asked several times to clean the room, but for some reason, the cleaning teams had already left (several times)
- There is no space to put the clothes, I need to leave them in the bag.
- the breakfast was very bad, and although they have an allergenic sign, when you ask for gluten-free food, it is a problem.
- Only one member of the staff was nice.
- You can hear people talking in other rooms.
I booked it and when I arrived it was closed for renovation... Please be careful...

We were a group of 6 with 3 different apartments.
Can agree with the latest comments made in general, but generally I am sharing my experience with my room.
The shower is a disaster. Water leaking below the sides, drain is not working as intended. Temperature handle coming off constantly.

If you press the toliet flusher too hard it gets stuck.

Pool area seats are destroyed, sunbeds are indeed somewhat dirty. View is nice though

We got a  used nespresso capsule to our coffee machine

Dreadful, stay away!
Ants and bed bugs throughout apartment. Pool disgusting dirty with live electrics showing, stairways dangerous. Plumbing issues as water never went down plug holes. We left and cancelled second booking of which refused refund.
Blood on sheets.
I would give no stars if I could.
Area is awful

Nice swimming pool, but it wasn't clean.
The lightbulbs all over the rooms had different types of white light (especially annoying in the bedroom). And it would've been nice if the electric box was covered with a simple frame or something. But overall a very nice stay! 👌🏼

Third time we visit this place! The apartment is clean, with a nice design and good view! We travel often to Athens so this place is tge first one that comes to our mind when we want to book.
The only small problem is the parking but like the rest of Athens and Piraeus.

they cancelled my booking 10 days before the trip with any alternatives.
bad experience.
Feels deluxe, lovely location near marina, loads of restaurants and access to port! Love everything about it!! Super clean, quiet and wonderful beds and decor!!! A hidden gem in Athens!!!
Don't be put off with it's two star rating...immaculately clean..interesting  decor, in a really super spot,few minutes to wonderful restaurants..
A great, not expensive, peaceful hotel with very nice staff and clean rooms. Excellent location!

Besides the location being terrible, the windows in the room were not possible to open and the AC barely works. We were unable to breathe inside the room. The bathroom door is designed in such a way that you cannot close it? It's not broken, just like that. Weird
The hotel's location is best for exploring the city of Piraeus. It is close to some good seafood restaurants.
From what we understood, a hotel rented by the state for the illegal immigrants who have filled our country to stay, food, sleep, internet, cleanliness!! Stay away!!!
polu kalo exei kai prwsopikh katharistria
GAMATW KSENODOXIO💯💯🔥🔥⚡
Great stay with everything you need!

Comfortable
and fully equipped for an unforgettable stay on the 6th floor in beautiful Kallithea! The host was excellent and very very kind and helped us with everything we needed. I highly recommend it!!!
Good small room, nice view, good security but.. no roller blinds and .. curtains..
It was perfect. The host was very kind and helpful. I highly recommend it!

place_name: Cozy Apartment n Stavros Niarchos Park
review_text:
We had bugs in the apartment all over the beds and walls. No refund and no comment from the owner. We booked a whole week and in the first evening left the apartment.
Don't book this apartment! Beware!!!
Well located and comfortable
Dot

Easy check in, rooms are compact but have everything you need, well presented and comfortable. Breakfast is fairly basic but good. The roof top pool and bar is the real selling point, it's lovely. We used this hotel as a relaxing few days before our sightseeing truly began and was perfect for this otherwise would have been a lot of taxis back and forth from the main areas.

The rooms are very modern and well kept. Decent size as well but could be larger. The roof top pool is very nice. It's very busy so it's difficult to get a place to sit in the sun. You really aren't close to anything here and not close to the subway. There are a decent amount of local restaurants to walk to that are very affordable.
I realized after 1 nights stay that they put me in the wrong room. When I spoke to the front desk they said I had to come back to speak to a manger which doesn't make sense to me. I had to come back the next day. I told them that I booked a king size bed and they said that they don't have any available.
I over heads someone having the same issue. It seems they book people with a nicer room and then give them a regular room and don't say anything and don't offer any compensation. That's not a good way to run a hotel.

Short stay in Athens, initially own error when booking, as misdirected on website: Reservation was registered for another location. After discussion we found a compromise, for which we were very (!) grateful.
The hotel, which is still quite new, is very pleasant, the view from the rooftop restaurant is great: thanks to the location halfway between the city centre and the coast, the Acropolis on one side and the sea on the other. The pool above the rooftops is also great, just a little shallow. The staff were courteous. We would love to stay again. And above all, many thanks for the constructive, customer-friendly approach!!!

Loved this hotel. New, modern and clean. Rooms were spacious and comfortable. The rooftop pool and restaurant were very nice and the gym was nice. Not a huge gym, but enough for a workout. The staff were very nice. The location isn't central, but it was easy enough to get an uber. I'd definitely stay here again.

There were some good and some bad points  about this hotel, but overall, we wouldn’t stay there again, especially for the price.

Negative Points:
The location was the biggest letdown. We should have done more research, as there’s nothing within walking distance. It took at least 30 minutes to reach anything near the center, and the area around the hotel was dirty and felt unsafe, especially at night, with homeless and disoriented people in the area. We ended up using taxis for every trip, which cost 7-10 EUR each way, adding around 20 EUR extra to our daily expenses.

The rooms were another disappointment. The first room we booked was so incredibly small that we upgraded to a Premium double room, but even that wasn’t much bigger. After one night, we had to switch rooms again because the walls were so thin, making it feel like we were sharing the space with a stranger. We could hear every word and sound coming from next door. All the rooms we saw were very dark, with no natural light or view. A large building was just 2-3 meters away from the windows. Also, there’s no separate bathroom—the sink is right next to the bed, and the shower is just a step away, meaning the room constantly felt humid. When we moved the armchair, we saw mold on the walls. I also found the smell in the room unpleasant—it had a damp, tobacco-like odor. When I mentioned it to the receptionist, I was surprised to learn that it’s the hotel’s “signature scent,” intentionally used in all the rooms.

The pool area was generally nice, but it felt awkward to be half-naked while people in the restaurant watched, as the dining area and pool are in the same space.

Positive Points:
On the upside, the staff was very friendly and helpful throughout our stay. The gym was well-equipped with high-quality Technogym machines, and the breakfast was fantastic, with a great variety of fresh food, including plant-based milk options like oat and soy, as well as fresh vegetables, fruits, and pastries.

Conclusion:
Overall, given the price (over 1,100 EUR for 4 nights), this hotel did not meet expectations. The location, in particular, was a huge inconvenience, and the quality of the rooms was below what we expected.

Knowledgeable, professional and attentive, GYC provides excellent service to all who want to charter a yacht in Greece. They work hard to find your particular needs and make your yacht experience as unique as you are!

Greece Yachts Charters is a 100 % guarantee when it comes to cruising around the Greek Islands and making your dream itinerary come true.
The places we have seen onboard our charter were truly idyllic and beautiful. Our charter itinerary was a dream come true!
Awe inspiring & magical. Thank you GYC! :)
dirty, air condition not working, heavily smelly bathrooms, no storage room, no WiFi (although we paid for it), bad organization,  unfriendly staff, just the skipper was nice
Not helpful at all

When we came in the room there was a smell of cigarettes like someone was smoking a lot in there. I am asthmatic i can’t sleep there, so i called immediately straight to the booking and asked for refund. I didn’t stay in this apartment. They didn’t answer to booking on the request with the refund. They didn’t answer to my messages. The worst experience ever! Room doesn’t look like on the photos they have in the profile and plus you will get a smell of cigarettes in NON SMOKING ROOMS

Been here 3 times. Biggest double room on the upper floor very comfy and alot of space! Big balcony! Everything you need! But sound from rooftop makes it for light sleepers on highest room not great.  Room 103 also beautiful designed....but only thing to mention is it was not so clean. Hairs around and bathroom was smelling like mold :( this hotel is good located, easy checkin without reception, rooms are modern and comfy....but they could work on the cleaning a bit.

Convo 212 is a very nice located place. If you want privacy. This is a good stay. No reception. All goes via phone. Even the payment. They all speak very good English.

+ I had the penthouse. Lots of room. But for 3 persons is best ? (Not 4) you have 2 levels :-)
+ big screen TV and a sofa
+ kitchen

- the road noise. It’s on the free way. For me not a big problem, after all. It’s Athens
- the penthouse. On top of the roof is the airco. If the switch on you hear a humming sound.

In beginning I had issues with warm water. But that was fixed with a phone call.

I even asked for late check out. No problem.
Great stay. Good price. Close to centre

Liked:
- Good location
- fast access to restaurants and supermarkets

Disliked:
- the hosts weren't prepared for my arrival even though they had plenty of days ahead to get the room cleaned up and to prepare my access key

- I had to stay more than half an hour in a phone call with the receptionist to get an access key. Because the key wasn't in the locker in the end I had to take a temporary one.

- at my arrival the room was dusty, finger nails on the floor and smelly and one of the windows wasn't closing properly. Because it is located near a large boulevard the noise from cars and scouters made it impossible to sleep.

- they call the room a studio, but is actually a regular small room where they crowded a a bed, a so called sofa, a desk and kitchen cabinets. When you enter the room you have to pass through a 30-40 cm space between the desk and the bed.

- after a few days the shower drain has blocked and and all the water from the shower got into the room. Me and my wife spent two hours trying to absorb the water with towels. After I called the reception to ask more towels and a mop until somenone will fix the shower, nobody provided extra towels that night. The next day I had to call again for this issues and a receptionist was polite enough to give us another room and he actually made an upgrade for us.

Overpriced and poorly serviced.

Room was of good size but furniture has damages and floor was stained.

I arrived late and had to call many times until someone picked up the line and gave me entrance codes.

Positives:
- good location
- pool was good
Negatives:
Everything else
The breakfast was inedible
Rooms were disgusting and bathroom had mould
I was expecting noise as it is close to the airport however the walls are paper thin and you can hear everything on the entire floor
Mattresses are rock hard
Dietary requirements are basically ignored “ask us about dietary allergies” so I asked about gluten free options “no”
Stay somewhere else

Great price, great location, great staff… There is also a restaurant downstairs but I only ate there once. It was really good though and reasonably priced. Though I did see ashtrays in hallways, I did not smell any smoke at all. With the exception of some loud talking from the people across the hall a few times, the place was very quiet. There was a heavy plastic “shade” door(which made the room pitch back in the middle of the day) and a heavy interior sliding glass door that drowned out most of the street noise. The parking situation is pretty bad. It’s in a neighborhood that is all street parking. It’s free and has very few no parking areas which is great but it can be difficult to find spaces. I did not deduct any stars because this is hardly the hotels fault. Just something to keep in mind if you intend to rent a car as I did. To be honest, unless you’re making a day trip at some point, you probably don’t need a car. Everything’s pretty close. I intend to visit again someday and I will absolutely look here first.

We were very disappointed with our room for the 3 day stay! AC didn't work, the very small bathroom fan didn't work, the vent pipe was broke in bathroom causing the whole small room to smell like sewer. We had to open our Slider to air out everything when we came back to the room. The room and the small balcony were in need of many repairs. Just was not a good start to our first Athens vacation. Remember, you get what you paid for!
How true. We would not recommend this place to anyone traveling.
I don't know how it was rated at  3 1/2 stars???

It's worth the money you pay. The hotel is dated and there are deficiencies all around. In one room the fridge did not work and in another the safe didn't work. The street facing rooms can be noisy-- especially if you keep the balcony door open, but there is an A/C unit in the room. Floor surfaces need replacement in and outside the rooms. The rooms are clean and with adequate linens. Friendly service and good breakfast.

Had to change multiple rooms to find the least odorless in terms of smoking, looks like smoking is permitted in the outdated rooms. Toilets and cleanliness are the worst. Would have changed the hotel but I had to stay due to location and proximity to my relatives. 1 star just for the acceptable breakfast and morning Greek coffee.

Young couple for a 6 night stay.

Checked in hassle free, earlier than check in we received an SMS with instructions on how to download an app to be our room key onto our phone so check in was almost optional.

The service throughout the whole stay was very professional and friendly. We don't normally turn off the do not disturb sign until the room needs cleaning and the staff were attentive to this and would even offer to clean the room as late as 2pm.

The room is very fresh and open. Feels very modern and the air conditioning works very well. Microwave oven and fridge/freezer means self catering is a breeze. Some small breakfast items and drinks were provided when we checked in to last untill we got to a shop.

Location is very good. 25 min walk to the Acropolis and the most we had to walk from the furthest attractions was 1hr. Normal in Europe.

Lots of restaurants around either 5 mins across the avenue or 20 mins in one of the more commercial aereas. Plenty of small shops, one 24hr one nearby too. Ordering food for delivery was no problem too.

++ high tech room, clean and big room, polite staff, near to Acropole ( 30 minutes by foot), parking on the street or 10 eur per day if you wish safe parking lot.
Cofee capsules, tea, water on the room
Cleaning was done every day
Luxury shower gel, towels.
Best accommodation , I recommend them
Price / value 👌  perfect
Small market at 5 minutes
Elevator for luggage
This accommodation have everything that you need. The best 👌!!

very pleasant and luxurious apartment with advanced technologies.  parking is in the garage and is charged €10 per day.  There is free parking around the apartment and plenty of free parking.  the apartment is highly recommended.
Beautiful and very high quality finishes! The staff is very nice. If you don't care about being far from the center or the beach, then it's great. Metro stop also far.

Neat. I didn’t know about asking for special room service- towels were not replaced even though she said it will be replaced the next day. Our beds were made but not really dusted or even shaken. There were no real place to dry our clothes after the towels were all laid in the bathroom to dry, not even a hook pending as it was 4 of us who stayed.

It said that Check in starts after 3 on Booking.com and we got out of airport at 4. At 5, the receptionist was about to leave when we just arrived. But there’s no information on this. Best way to reach them is call. I didn’t get any replies on Booking.com.

Overall nice stay though as the location is not too remote, quiet and family-friendly. You can get Uber easily. Also, just a few minutes walk you will access main road.

The room was always on optimal temperatures. You need an app to access the doors, so no worries of losing access card or forgetting pins.

Service I wouldn’t say luxurious.

Very comfortable hotel. Spacious and clean room. The breakfast was amazing for variety and quality. Very useful car parking included (beware of the roof which is very low in the underground levels). Good position if you arrive late into the night at Piraeus port and you need a one night stay before your plane (check in available 24h). I don't know if it's located in a good position to reach the center

Try to avoid at all costs. The hotel is located in one of the worst locations (nothing around the hotel except strip clubs) and the rooms are extremely dirty. I had to stay here only for a few hours ahead of my flight and I truly regret not booking another place. The staff is also not very friendly which I found to be quite rare compared to anywhere else across Greece.

Although this place was recently renovated it happens to be located in a section of Athens surrounded by strip bars and studios as they call them here. We booked this trip through Costco and I’m kicking myself about not doing a little more homework. The housekeeping department in my view after three nights has a lot to learn. No face cloths, one bath towel for two guest, they don’t replenish the soap or toilet paper and a toilet that ran all day and night. The bedding has a flat sheet over the mattress pad and no top sheet. Just a comforter? Room is very small. My advice would be look in another direction for booking a hotel.

20 to 30 mins ride from the main attractions. Beautiful to look at, but beds in the suite were uncomfortable for all 3 of us. The front desk staff was nice when we arrived, but not as helpful the next day.

I stayed here for one night prior to my cruise which is the reason I was able to tolerate it. Had my stay been longer my family and I would have gone elsewhere. The check in staff was friendly but the breakfast staff was rude and clearly hated her life as if she woke on the wrong side of the bed. She would not accommodate families sitting together in open empty tables and was extremely nasty without providing any explanation. It seemed as though she was only catering to the Greek tourists. The rooms are TINY and NOISY. The windows don’t have a good seal in any room as my family had occupied 5 rooms in total and each room had loud traffic noises emitting from
Every window as if they were open. My bathtub was arranged so awkwardly that it was impossible to shower without getting water outside of the tub since there was only a half glass door and the shower head was in the middle of the tub. I also found dirt on the floor of my room which looked similar to mouse droppings. AVOID at all costs and spend a little more and stay at the Hyatt (which we would have done if they weren’t sold out) up the block.
Beautiful apartment, fully equipped, clean and well located. The pictures were as shown in reality. It only needs to maintain the bathroom shower and install some electrical points.
Εxcellent communication with the company, the house was spotless and beautiful. I recommend it 100%. Thanks for the great experience!
Although very nice location, next to everything, and the house is excellent. The guys at Gtrip were very knowledgeable.

Definitely a nice hotel here in Athens. Breakfast that was included with the stay was very nice, great selection. The view on the rooftop is absolutely breathtaking, was worth it to stay here for that alone. In addition the staff was very helpful + the rooms were clean and modern. Fair prices as well even without a reservation. The only  downside was the elevators were very small and a little sketchy, but seems to be common in Europe:).

Exactly what we were looking for. Mr. Dimitrios is an excellent host, caring, resourceful and knowledgeable. A place away from the hustle and bustle of downtown Athens, with an impressive view, clean and located in a not so touristy area but with excellent access. The service is impressively efficient, warm and helpful. The rooms are spacious and comfortable. Lovely views to the Acropolis from the terrace. Breakfast is delicious.Yes, you are next to a highway but it is well insulated and the noise is minimal, at least on the fifth floor. Highly recommended. Warm, welcoming, and comfortable. I’d come back anytime. Thanks to all the staff for your support and kindness.

We had an amazing experience with this hotel. Right when we came in, lobby is clean and the receptionist, his name is Demitrius. He's such a patient person as the group before us don't have a reservation yet he tried to be patient and accommodating to these group of elderly people.

Location is great! Close to city center like 7-10 mins via cab, same minutes going to one of the mall.

Our room is very clean and not only that, we're graced by our own view of the Acropolis.

It was very smooth and lovely experience for us. Thanks to Demitrius for making a good impression and it definitely lasted even after we checked out.

Will recommend this hotel and will sure come back to stay once in Athens!

Quick review. Why to book this place? A)Location proximity to nearly all touristy things B) Knowledgeable assistance at front desk.
Why to not book this place? A) in a night club area. Crystal Queen is a club across from the hotel that plays music until 4am which shakes the building from across the street. B) you can forget sleep because it is on a busy freeway.
Make a smart choice.

Very clean, modern and beautiful hotel. The room has modern decor with hard wood floors. The roof top terrace has cafeteria that is wonderful. The breakfast is very good with lots of choices for vegetarians alike.

Small room,but great service.  We forgot our plug adapters & receptionist lent us one. Asked for coffee late at night & coffee machine was activated just for us. Cant fault this place. Breakfast is plentiful & All the receptionists we met are amazing. Always helpful.

Quite far from center, but possible to reach center by 20 minutes walk.
Impossible to sleep at night because it is very noisy from street and from night club.
Breakfast without coffee because coffee machine was broken.

Arrived late difficult entry due to stairs no ramp for assistance with luggage  greeted by Alexander, big smile , very friendly,  assisted us with location of eateries, arrived at our room, two single beds were expected, we had one queen bed, notified Alexander at reception who promptly resolved the situation, well done Alexander, you are an asset  to Athenian Montazza Hotel,

Not sure how this place gets all the good reviews. Incredibly overpriced, I paid over 85 EUR/night during low season for a tiny room with only a bed and desk, old and tacky curtains, and a room key that worked 50% of the time.
Expect to hear music from the nearby ‘clubs’ until 3/4am. When I asked the front desk about this, I was told it’s basically everyday.
I was also kept awake by students slamming doors and screaming in the hallways past 2am, and instead of apologizing for it the staff told me it’s a school trip.
The water in the shower was maybe 80% normal heat.
Nothing special about the location, it’s off a highway and although you can walk to the city center, it’s not at all a nice location.
Overall this is more of a nice hostel than a hotel. Not at all worth the prices they charge.

Room is clean. Staff very friendly.
Close to the public transportation although the metro is 15 mins walking distance but there is a bus at 5 mins walking. Breakfast is good with a good variety. Even though there is a club next to the hotel, the room was very quiet but we were on the other side not facing the club.
Inconvenient: The room is a bit small

Great location, right between the centre of Athens and the sea. Bus stop 1min from hotel, amazing helpful stuff, they helped us with transport, told us everything important. They also sell bus tickets at the reception. Everything we needed was within 5min, market, bakery, restaurants... Clean modern rooms, quiet neighbourhood, definetely will come back.

Superb!!! Nice clean apartment with very comfortable mattress and minimal decoration. Felt like home. All personnel I met, couldn’t be more helpful, friendly and kind!
I would be happy, to stay there again. Highly recommended!

It was an excellent experience. The people working there were extremely helpful and eager to help with whatever you needed,very well mannered too.Everything was clean and the place was hygienic.Overall I loved the place and I left satisfied.

There are no words for this hotel ! Everything is new, very very clean, the location is just perfect, and the staff very very friendly! They really helped us a lot ! We highly recommend you to stay here if you come to Athens !
Spacious rooms, comfy bed, good room amenities.

Erta made our stay warm and very inviting, nothing was too much trouble, The rooms were spacious and clean, bathrooms and kitchenette were fully stocked. Very comfy bed and couch and the cafe up the road even delivered breakfast at a discounted rate through a deal PNOĒ has with Peggy Sue. If I could give more than 5 stars I would.
Thanks for having us :)
-Mel

Gorgeous Hotel with a stunning view! The rooms are big and everything is so clean. The staff is super friendly and helpful in any matter. We really enjoyed our stay here and will definitely pick this hotel again for our next visit in Athens!

This place is absolutely fantastic!  Brand new with a modern aesthetic, super clean, and spacious rooms. The staff is outstanding. They were extremely helpful and friendly, always greeting us with a smile and asking what they could do to improve our stay. I'd give them ten stars if I could!

We stayed for three nights and had a great time. The service was excellent; the staff is extremely friendly and goes out of their way to assist as much as possible. There are cafes and restaurants nearby, and the hotel is generally very accommodating! I wholeheartedly recommend it!
Such a beautiful place. Spacious rooms with a connection to a local place (Peggy Sue’s) to deliver a beautiful breakfast to your room

Our stay at the Grand Hyatt Athens was nothing short of spectacular! The hotel exudes luxury from the moment you walk through the doors. Our room was beautifully appointed, with modern decor and a comfortable bed that made for a restful night’s sleep. The rooftop pool and bar offer breathtaking views of the Acropolis – a perfect spot to relax after a day of sightseeing. The breakfast buffet was outstanding, with a wide variety of fresh, delicious options. What truly set our experience apart was the impeccable service; the staff were attentive, welcoming, and always ready to help. If you’re looking for a luxurious stay with exceptional amenities in the heart of Athens, the Grand Hyatt is the perfect choice!

Wow what a great hotel! The location is ideal it’s out of the busy acropolis area of the city but still close enough to walk to most of the sites if you wanted. They have a complimentary shuttle bus that will take and pick you up to the national gardens area from there you can hit everything pretty easily. We found the food and beverage offerings to be outstanding from the food to the service it was top notch. The breakfast buffet is one of the best I’ve had in Europe, seriously, it’s pretty incredible. The dinner menu is equally amazing it has a little bit of everything even sushi. The views from this place are worth just coming up for a drink but I would definitely eat here again. We also ate downstairs at the bar/lounge which had even more offerings and it was delicious. The service and the food at these places make it one of the better options to eat at in Athens we stopped by a few spots in the city and felt the offerings at the hotel were much better. The hostess at the dinner restaurant 9th floor was very kind she made sure my wife’s birthday was acknowledged which we really appreciated. The food and beverage manager was also very much on top of things we were very impressed with him and his team. The rooms are really nice great balconies with views of the city it was clean and well maintained. Reception was helpful and nice to deal with each time as well as the bell/valet team. When we’re back in Athens we’ll be staying here again for sure.

Thanks for the amazing event at Cyclades Hall yesterday!!! Beautiful and elegant space! The large screen and the high ceiling was giving the feeling of luxury and simplicity at the same time!!! The banquet exquisite, in compliance with the standards of Hyatt!!! But, above all, the employees were the key to the success!! Excellent service, all the staff was extremely polite, helpful and to the point! All the departments, from sales to banquet, from IT to the reception and the concierge! Congratulations to all the people of Grand Hyatt Athens!

What an amazing location!  The hotel is very lovely and the views from the restaurant on the 9th floor AMAZING!  The rooms are extremely ample, clean and the mattress and pillows outstanding.  The staff we encountered were very friendly and helpful. Don’t pass up their restaurants…Best Greek salad ever, breakfast buffet has the largest selection and the food extremely tasty.  Worth the stay for safety, location and service.  Memorable stay!!

For the price you really cannot beat this hotel. It's got a great modern vibe, huge luxurious rooms, and an absolute A+ spa that is free to visit (pool, jacuzzi, sauna, cold plunge etc) - comes in very handy after long days of walking and hiking). Service is really amazing, Erinda at the front desk was so nice and helped us get settled when we arrived a bit early before check-in. You're about 20 minutes from the city center but they have a free shuttle that gets you there and back in 5.

I stayed 3 nights in Athenaeum Grand Hotel.

Room was so spacious and very clean.
Also, it has a balcony to see a magnificent view of Greece.

Breakfast was American style, but it provides a lot of selections.

Staffs are very friendly and I personally thanks to the male staff, helping me to search the lost cellphone for my colleague.

I’ll definitely use the hotel again.

Our room was amazing. Great view. The staff were on point
The complimentary breakfast was a full buffet. Would definitely stay again. Five mile drive to city center will cost about $5 for taxi

What a beautiful hotel, but what makes its more amazing is their staff! So courteous, friendly, professional, and kind. I can’t say enough great things about all of their staff. We will definitely stay here again.

The staff were fantastic, very helpful and accommodating. The room was nice with a fantastic view, as promised.  What I didn't realize when I booked the hotel was that it sits between 2 "Gentlemens Clubs".  So it felt a bit sketchy at night.

First the positives. The breakfast area and the breakfast itself were great. The beds were very comfy and the bathroom looked classy. The staff were all very friendly.

Now the negatives. We had a room with a veranda but unfortunately it stunk out there of sewage so we could not even use it and had to keep the door always closed. We opened the window on the veranda to try and get some fresh air in but it didn't help. A complete waste of money upgrading to that room.

Also, the air conditioning always seemed to blow out very cold air despite trying to increase the temperature. The worst part was when we turned the air conditioning off, there was a constant buzzing sound coming from the air conditioning which was strange and very annoying. So it was either freeze or have a constant noise at night...

The hotel location was also in a pretty seedy area with strip clubs all around it and fair walk away to the closest Metro station. Whilst we never felt unsafe in the area, it was not a nice vibe to be in unless you want to be around that sort of thing.

This is not somewhere I will be staying again...

Rooms slightly on the small side, not as quiet as they could be, overall good.
Service from the staff was very good, they were pleasant and friendly.
Location is easy to get to the central sites, nice views from the roof top breakfast bar, it is unfortunately on a main road.

Don't stay there!  We stayed on a Friday and Saturday night and couldn't sleep at all.  The front desk claimed that a university across the street was having a function.  Loud music and a Dj talking from 9 p.m. until 6 a.m. both mornings.  The hotel was well aware of the noise and has done nothing to accommodate their guest to prevent hearing the noise.  Their attitude is that it has nothing to do with them.  If you are aware of a problem then you should do something to fix it.  I can not recommend this hotel because of noise and the hotel not caring about it, or doing something about it.
Clean and modern rooms. Amazing, friendly and professional staff. Everyone was very welcoming and polite, especially Sofia at the breakfast!

I have loved my experience here. My wife and I stayed here for 9 days and loved every day of it. The people at the front desk are awesome and very helpful. The views are gorgeous, and it really puts you far enough from the Acropolis to be perfect for a cultural and tourist experience. The breakfast was lovely. Every morning, we would go to the rooftop and enjoy a delicious meal. The girls that ran the breakfast area were very kind and made us feel welcome. Side note, I would be eating with the amazing view of the Acropolis, and all I could focus on was this fat bumble bee flying to eat flower on the rooftop garden. It was a sense of beauty, relaxation, and a calming pleasure that made my stay so much more enjoyable. My stay was amazing. Thank you to all that made it possible.

Very good hotel for price and location.  Don't understand why there was just a standing mirror in the room and that too hidden behind the blinds - so you had to open the blinds to use the bathroom mirror.

We asked the owner in advance if we could leave our luggage prior to check-in. He told us yes. We arrived in the morning to leave our luggage, after an intense and long trip. The owner greeted us, very angry. I told him that he had told us we could leave our luggage and very reluctantly, he tells us to follow him. In a garage, he tells us to leave our luggage and leave quickly. I urgently needed to go to the bathroom, so l asked him for that. While he looked at us to leave. He told me not very aggressively and shouting, making a gesture with his hands in the shape of a cross, and that we should leave. We left very uncomfortable due to his treatment and waited outside to find somewhere to go to the bathroom. The owner comes out after 10 minutes and tells us to come with him. I thought he was going to lend me the bathroom, but he took us to the top floor of it, to show the view it had of the Acropolis. My partner got upset and asked him again that we were not interested in that, that we had only asked for the bathroom (the owner was trying to be nice and show off the amenities of his apartment), but when my partner asked him that, he got upset and. He rudely took us to the bathroom, while waiting next to the bathroom door for him to enter and exit quickly. I felt very uncomfortable, so without entering, I told him that I did not expect such treatment towards a human being and that it no longer mattered, that we could look for other accommodation. He got so upset that he didn't even let me explain anything to him and started yelling at me to keep my voice down, because other people were sleeping, while he told him that he was speaking softly and couldn't treat me like that. To which he begins to silence me with his finger. When we got down, I told him that he was a terrible person and he waved us off. I called booking, with poor handling and wasting all my cell phone minutes, I still haven't seen them respond to me at such a bad time we are experiencing. His name is Sergio

The shared bathroom on level 2 is very unclean and unhygienic, lots of mould on the ceiling. But for the low price the accommodation is decent and very fine for a short stay. The rooftop is a nice addition with views of the acropolis! :) Also a very nice man who received us at check in.
If you want cheap price this is the accomodation, the room of fine but very poor in cleaning, kitchen is very dirty…has everything but dirty.
The person in charge is really kind and helpful😊 big and comfortables rooms

hospitality 5/5  rooms 5/5 price 5/5  location 100% safe
it is the best.
some will say it is not  perfect clean, but its only 20/night. for its price it is the best it can be.   clean sheets towels the room is clean enough for the money you pay
Magnificent stay in Greece thanks to the quality of the accommodation and its owner, Maria. Efkaristo polite!

Athens Chic House is one of the best places I have ever stayed! The apartment is super nice! fully equipped, clean and very cozy! You can feel at home ! Maria and Themis, the owners, are wonderful people who have helped me in everything! they inform you about everything in detail. In addition, they recommend and inform you on how you can enjoy your stay in this wonderful city of Athens.
The apartment is in an excellent location, you have access to supermarkets, bus stops, tram, bars, pharmacies, bakeries, cafes, typical Greek restaurants!
100% recommendable! I would come back again without hesitation !

This place lives up to the name.  There is everything you need to stay for any period.  The apartment is very cozy, like a small palace.  Absolutely calm and quiet area.  Security is ensured by outdoor video surveillance.  Transport infrastructure in the nearest access.  The hosts are very welcoming, smart intellingent persons, and are always ready to give advice on what to do in Athens and are very attentive to guests.  The hotel is new, everything that is offered to guests works perfectly.  Next time I hope to stay here.

Clean and spacious apartment, with kitchen supplies and everything you need for your stay. The hosts were extremely helpful and kind! What is more, on their website there is everything you need to know about Athens, where to go, what to eat and what to visit during your stay!
A great place for a comfortable stay.  Everything is clean, comfortable, polite.  Excellent quality for your money!  We came back here, and I hope to come back again.
I spent a month in here and it was incredible. Amazing and comfortable apartment. Highly recommended ☺️

If you're looking for a more inexpensive hotel in Athens, this is the place. It's about a 30 minute walk to the Acropolis or a 5 minute/6 Euro taxi ride. The rooms are simple but nice, restaurant is great, and service is excellent. It's right on the avenue so it's a little loud with the cars and bells from the church across the street but that was no issue for me. There are some excellent local spots nearby to eat that are way cheaper than in the downtown Acropolis area as well. I would definitely stay there again!

Carpets and walls of rooms were disgustingly dirty . Hotel needs a complete refurbishment . Beds were like sleeping in boards.   I complained to the girl at the desk and was given a plate of fruit and wine as an apology .   But this hotel is definitely not 4 star .

This hotel is good a lot ! the location is conveniently close to all that we wanted on our days in Athens, and the interior and atmosphere of this house is gorgeous! The rooms are cosy The bathroom are GOOD, the water in the bath are wonderfully warm when that is needed, and the breakfast is all okay great also Truely recomendable !

If you want to avoid any disappointments, avoid this hotel when you travel for business.
I have no idea who gave them 4 stars, but I would give them 3 with difficulties.
Breakfast I can rate as even 1 star hotel.
And by any chance, if you have to leave earlier, they will ask you to pay the full reservation, without any understanding that when traveling for business some times the plans are changed rapidly.

Terrible hotel. Really outdated and unkept. The room carpets are disgusting. There is no air-conditioning and the staff were very impolite. This definitely should not be a 4 star hotel. This hotel is also not suited for families.

My wife and I love this place. This is our first time in Greece and in this hotel. We were staying for two nights at room 901. The hotel is small but cute. The hotel is clean, modern, and service is fantastic and simple. From my room we can see Acropolis. We love it. We strongly recommend this place. Yes, it was a little bit pricey $350/night compared to some hotels around here. But as we all said, you get what you pay for!

Our stay in Athens at this hotel was wonderful.  Staff was very helpful and friendly.  We were able to walk to the Acropolis and interesting neighborhoods for great local food.  We were about a 10 minute walk to a local brewery/taproom if you like that sort of thing (I do).  The rooftop restaurant was amazing with a great view of the Acropolis.  We had dinner there one night, and went for drinks another.  Both experiences were fantastic.  Grilled squid was excellent.

Amazing hotel! Very clean and modern and great location in the Acropolis.
